crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on confidence, progress, and a vision for a prosperous future, which are affirmative and aspirational themes rather than threats requiring urgent response.

paragraph_text: At the same time, tragically, countless victims fell in Kosovo, in Central Africa, and in Iraq, while disasters devastated entire regions in a matter of hours, as we recently witnessed alongside Central America. Here at home, unemployment and poverty, whether material or moral, have not diminished as much as we had hoped. And we have witnessed the rise of globalization. A world, it is true, where crises, particularly financial ones, spread very quickly. But also, and above all, a world that is increasingly open, where everything circulates: people, wealth, information, knowledge – a world full of energy, full of vitality, rich with new forms of fraternity to invent. These changes sometimes worry us. And yet, they will be a source of progress if we can not only master them, but above all, if we can humanize and civilize globalization. This commitment to a more humane world, where law and fraternity prevail, is the mission of France. It is my mission. We are fully capable of succeeding, because we will do so together.
model_reasons: disasters: refers to general humanitarian or regional catastrophes, not explicitly climate-linked events
paragraph_summary: The speaker addresses global conflicts, domestic socioeconomic challenges, and the need to humanize globalization through French leadership and national unity.
reason: It connects international crises and the phenomenon of globalization to the domestic mission and responsibilities of France.
crisis_reason: The speaker frames global conflicts and domestic socioeconomic challenges as urgent threats to the nation, necessitating a specific response through leadership and unity.

paragraph_text: This is how we can play our cards effectively in an open European space. Europe already has a long history. It is still a long road ahead. Increasingly, it will be a part of our daily lives. The creation of the Euro marks a new era. The Euro will change Europe, and first and foremost, it will change people's attitudes. Tonight, I want to pay tribute to you. The Euro is first and foremost the result of your efforts and your successes. It is also an expression of your clear vision and your openness, because if you had not wanted it, if you had not made it possible, we would have remained outside this great adventure. For us, the French, it is an opportunity. The Euro will bring us more choice in our purchases, lower prices, new market opportunities, new investment possibilities, and therefore, more jobs. It will bring us greater stability in an uncertain world, and more strength in the face of the major economic and political forces that are developing around the world.
paragraph_summary: The speaker highlights the economic benefits and strategic importance of the Euro for France and its citizens.
reason: It discusses the impact of the Euro and European integration on the domestic French economy and job market.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on the positive economic benefits and strategic value of the Euro, framing it as an achievement and a source of stability rather than a threat or an urgent problem.

paragraph_text: Even as the world changes as never before, modernity must not divide us. It must benefit everyone. We will succeed. We will succeed because we have made decisions that commit us and guarantee our future. We have chosen, together, to build a stronger France within Europe – a Europe that guarantees peace, a Europe that allows us to have a greater influence in the world. We have also chosen to participate in globalization, to embrace it fully. But a globalization that is controlled, organized, and respectful of the environment, capable of addressing the aspirations of people and capable of reducing poverty. This will be the purpose of France’s efforts in the major negotiations to come.
model_reasons: environment: refers to the general socio-economic and global political environment
paragraph_summary: The President outlines a vision for a stronger France within a unified Europe and advocates for a controlled, environmentally conscious approach to global integration.
reason: It discusses France's role in Europe and globalization as a means to improve domestic outcomes.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on a vision for the future and a strategic approach to integration, which are presented as positive policy goals rather than urgent threats or dangers.

paragraph_text: We have become more aware of the fragility of human life and the inequalities in access to healthcare. Consider the example of AIDS. It affects more and more people around the world. Our societies must, more than ever, remain mobilized, active, and vigilant. Yet, the treatments available in the West remain inaccessible to those suffering in developing countries. This injustice must be fought. We are also aware of the fragility of peace, when misunderstanding, passion, and anger lead to violence in so many regions of the world.
paragraph_summary: The speaker addresses global health inequalities regarding AIDS treatment and the persistent fragility of international peace.
reason: It links global health and peace issues to the responsibilities of domestic societies.
crisis_reason: The speaker frames global health inequalities and the fragility of international peace as persistent, urgent threats that require ongoing attention and action.

paragraph_text: But we are also aware of the power and brilliance of human ingenuity, when medical advances offer so much hope, and when gene therapy allows children facing serious disabilities to be born and grow up in good health. We are conscious of the momentum, the dynamism, and the many opportunities offered by global growth. We are aware of a more open future for young people, and of new areas to explore, new technologies, and new practices that are gradually changing professional life and our relationship with time.
paragraph_summary: The speaker highlights the potential of medical innovation, global economic growth, and technological advancements to improve quality of life and professional practices.
reason: It connects global technological and economic trends to changes in domestic professional life.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on positive developments, potential, and improvements in quality of life, which are explicitly excluded from the definition of a crisis.

paragraph_text: Finally, we are aware of the duty of solidarity – solidarity between the North and the South, essential if we want globalization to benefit everyone. Solidarity within Europe, if we want to build a European citizenship that guarantees peace and democracy on our continent. Solidarity among all members of our national community. The social action of public organizations is necessary, but beyond that, it is the support of associations, the outstretched hand of a family member or neighbor, that truly makes solidarity tangible. Progress is nothing without fraternity.
paragraph_summary: The speaker emphasizes the importance of solidarity at the global, European, and national levels to foster progress and fraternity within society.
reason: It discusses the necessity of solidarity across international, European, and national levels.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on positive values like solidarity, progress, and fraternity as aspirational goals for society rather than framing a specific threat or danger that requires urgent intervention.

paragraph_text: With the introduction of the euro, we are about to experience a historic moment: 300 million Europeans will now share the same currency. This is Europe moving forward, Europe progressing. We view this Europe differently after the terrible events that struck America on September 11th. In a world where there are so many sources of hatred and misunderstanding, where inequalities are growing, and where a sense of injustice is so prevalent, we recognize more than ever the importance of Europe asserting itself, amplifying the actions of nations, and contributing to the evolution of the world. The euro is a victory for Europe. After a century of divisions, wars, and setbacks, our continent is finally asserting its identity and its power, in peace, unity, and stability! We can be proud of this, as it is also a success for all French people, a reward for the efforts they have made. Of course, everyone will need time to adapt, to find their bearings, to learn the prices, and to become familiar with the new currency. Some may experience difficulties. But with patience, attention, and mutual support, you will see that new habits will soon take hold.
paragraph_summary: The President discusses the introduction of the euro as a symbol of European unity, progress, and identity following the events of September 11th.
reason: It discusses the introduction of the euro as a European project with domestic implications.
crisis_reason: The paragraph frames the introduction of the euro as a symbol of unity and progress rather than as a response to a threat, focusing on identity and achievement rather than the danger posed by the events of September 11th.

paragraph_text: But you know well that it is not simply about replacing our francs with euros. The euro is an additional opportunity for France and the French people. It is also a new way of being in Europe, of living in Europe – an everyday Europe that must truly become the Europe of its citizens. Throughout its history, France has sometimes been tempted to turn inward. Together, however, we have chosen to move forward, to have confidence in ourselves, to broaden our horizons, and to open up to the rest of the world. Six years ago, thanks to the considerable efforts of the French people to reduce our public deficits, we committed ourselves and put ourselves in a position to qualify for the euro. Everyone knows that this was not a foregone conclusion. But we did it. We achieved it, and this choice is now definitive. We chose this so that every French person would have all the cards in hand for the 21st century. In this way, we have fulfilled our part of the European contract. The euro is not an end in itself. For us, it will mean greater growth, more jobs, greater purchasing power, and more opportunities for exchange. A stronger France. But above all, it must be a tool to serve the Europe of people that we are building. Now, it is up to us to take advantage of this! Because if the euro is an opportunity, it is also a challenge. We have many assets that will allow us to make the euro a powerful engine of energy and prosperity. Our economic and social model, which combines economic development with solidarity and protection against the risks of life. The dynamism of our businesses. The strength of our agriculture. Our geography, at the crossroads of all European routes. Our infrastructure, our public services, the quality of our training and research. Our language. Our culture. But for the euro to live up to its promise, for it to allow us to improve our standing and our position in the world, we must still launch major reforms to modernize society, the economy, and the state, to remove the obstacles that hinder our progress. And I am confident that we will do so.
paragraph_summary: The speaker discusses the adoption of the euro as a tool for French economic growth, modernization, and deeper European integration.
reason: It frames the euro as a tool for both European integration and domestic economic modernization.
crisis_reason: The paragraph frames the adoption of the euro as a positive development for economic growth and integration, rather than as a response to an urgent threat or danger.

paragraph_text: We must be clear-sighted, because we live in an uncertain and dangerous world, where the threat of war is compounded by the risks of terrorism. It is a world where the misuse of scientific progress can undermine the very dignity of human beings. France must be at the forefront in defending peace, justice, and ethics. Here in our own country, we face temptations of division and isolation. Violence, though reduced thanks to the government's efforts, has not yet disappeared. Our environmental balance is increasingly threatened. Our national unity, often strained, needs to be strengthened. The employment situation, financial difficulties, and social exclusion remain major concerns for us.
paragraph_summary: The president addresses national challenges including social division, economic hardship, environmental threats, and the need for France to uphold global peace and ethics.
reason: It discusses both international threats like terrorism and domestic issues like national unity and employment.
crisis_reason: The paragraph frames social division, economic hardship, and environmental threats as urgent national challenges that require active attention and a commitment to upholding ethics and peace, indicating a threat-focused rhetorical posture.

paragraph_text: My dear fellow citizens, this is a year of action, a year of resolute and fair action, that awaits us, for our future and for that of our children. The more dynamic France becomes, the more it will be heard. In a united, soon-to-be-reformed and expanded Europe, it will speak with greater strength. In recent months, it has demonstrated its ability to convey its message of peace, balance, and solidarity with developing countries. In 2003, France will continue its efforts with the United Nations, despite the challenges, to uphold the principles that underpin its commitment and its vision of the world. To my fellow citizens, both at home and abroad, let us approach this year of resolute and fair action united and determined. From the bottom of my heart, I wish you all a very happy New Year. Long live the Republic, long live France.
paragraph_summary: The President outlines France's commitment to domestic reform, European integration, and international diplomacy through the United Nations in the coming year.
reason: It balances domestic action with France's role in the European Union and international organizations.
crisis_reason: The paragraph describes routine governance, diplomatic engagement, and policy objectives without framing them as urgent threats or dangers.

paragraph_text: My dear fellow citizens, I am delighted to be with you tonight to warmly wish you a happy and prosperous New Year 2004. Many of you are gathered here for New Year's Eve, sharing the joy of being together. Others are alone, unwell, or facing hardship. My deepest thoughts and compassion are with them, and I want to tell them, from the bottom of my heart, my wishes for hope and solidarity. My thoughts also extend to all those, here in our country, who this year have been cruelly affected by the fires and floods. And how could we not also think of the tragedies beyond our borders, and in particular the devastating earthquake in Iran, for which our medical and rescue teams are working tirelessly.
model_actions: Dropped count of 'fires' | Dropped count of 'floods' | Dropped count of 'earthquake'
model_reasons: fires: refers to general disasters without explicit climate change framing | floods: refers to general disasters without explicit climate change framing | earthquake: geological event not linked to climate or environmental policy
paragraph_summary: The President extends New Year's greetings while expressing solidarity with those suffering from personal hardship, natural disasters in France, and the earthquake in Iran.
reason: It addresses domestic hardships alongside international tragedies like the earthquake in Iran.
crisis_reason: The paragraph is primarily ceremonial, focusing on New Year's greetings and expressions of solidarity rather than framing these events as active threats requiring urgent state response or mobilization.

paragraph_text: My dear compatriots, from mainland France, from overseas territories, and from abroad, we are all deeply saddened by the terrible catastrophe that has brought death, horror, and devastation to the Indian Ocean. Tonight, my thoughts are with all the victims. They are with the families and loved ones of our compatriots whose lives have been tragically lost on December 26th. They are with the injured, and with all those who are anxiously awaiting news of a loved one. On behalf of the entire nation, I want to express my deepest emotions and profound solidarity.
paragraph_summary: The President expresses national solidarity and condolences following the devastating Indian Ocean tsunami that caused significant loss of life among French citizens.
reason: It addresses a global natural disaster while focusing on the impact on French citizens.
crisis_reason: The paragraph frames the tsunami as a devastating event involving significant loss of life, which constitutes an acute threat to the safety and well-being of citizens.

paragraph_text: The government has immediately mobilized to provide assistance and support to those affected. And I commend the remarkable generosity of the French people, who are actively engaging and responding in great numbers to the calls of humanitarian organizations to help the populations affected by this tragedy, a tragedy that is also our own. Because today, we all feel how deeply, beyond all distances, we form a single humanity, whose destiny is inextricably linked to that of our planet.
paragraph_summary: The President commends the French public's humanitarian response to a recent tragedy and emphasizes the interconnectedness of global humanity.
reason: It links the domestic response to a global humanitarian crisis through the lens of shared humanity.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on commending the public's humanitarian response and reflecting on global interconnectedness rather than framing the tragedy as an ongoing threat requiring urgent action or vigilance.

paragraph_text: In 2005, you will have the future of this Europe in your hands. I have decided that the European Constitution will be submitted to you, by referendum, before the summer. Thus, you, the sovereign people, will be called upon to choose your own destiny. By approving the European Constitution, you will enable Europe to be more democratic, more proactive, and more powerful. You will enable it to achieve faster economic and social progress, and you will allow France to have a greater influence within the Union. Between retreat and openness, the choice we make will be decisive. This choice engages the future of France and Europe. It must not be altered or diverted by any other consideration. This is a great responsibility for all of us.
paragraph_summary: President Chirac announces a national referendum on the European Constitution to be held before the summer of 2005.
reason: The European Constitution referendum bridges domestic democratic processes with international European integration.
crisis_reason: The paragraph describes a routine political procedure regarding a national referendum, which is presented as a standard governance action rather than a response to an urgent threat or danger.

paragraph_text: My dear compatriots, from mainland France, from overseas, and from abroad, as we stand on the threshold of this new year, I extend to each and every one of you my warmest wishes. And at this time of celebration, my thoughts first turn to those who are facing illness, loneliness, and hardship. I also think of our soldiers, serving for peace on every continent. And I want to say to the family of Bernard Planche that the government is fully committed to securing his release. The year 2005 saw the expression of tensions and questions that run through our society – the referendum result and the crisis in the suburbs bear witness to this. Underlying these issues is the question of globalization: how can we remain true to ourselves in a world that is changing so rapidly? But 2005 was also a year of success for France. With the Airbus A380, with Ariane 5, with the Galileo satellite navigation system, and with ITER for the energy of the future, our country has positioned itself at the forefront of global innovation.
paragraph_summary: The President reflects on 2005, addressing social tensions, the challenges of globalization, and France's achievements in technological innovation.
reason: It combines domestic holiday greetings and internal social issues with international military commitments and global technological innovation.
crisis_reason: The paragraph frames social tensions and the challenges of globalization as ongoing issues rather than urgent, active threats, while balancing them with positive achievements in innovation.

paragraph_text: The Republic also embodies a spirit of ambition. We will harness globalization to drive our growth and create jobs. Because we want to strengthen our employment advantages and guarantee a high level of social protection, we will launch a reform of its funding. Today, companies that lay off employees, that relocate production, and that pay fewer taxes. Our system of employer contributions must favor companies that employ people in France. Furthermore, working with social partners, we will establish a genuine system of professional security, based on the right to support and training, for everyone, throughout their working lives.
paragraph_summary: The President outlines plans to reform employment funding, incentivize domestic hiring, and establish a lifelong professional training system to strengthen the French economy.
reason: It links the global phenomenon of globalization to domestic employment protection and national tax reform.
crisis_reason: The paragraph describes routine policy measures and economic development strategies rather than framing the employment situation as an urgent threat or danger.

paragraph_text: We will also win the battle for globalization and employment through innovation, by embracing technological progress. Today, we are simultaneously facing two major industrial revolutions: energy, with the transition beyond oil, which is the great challenge of the century, and the digital revolution. I have decided to make these two priorities of our industrial policy. France will be a pioneer. It will lead the way for our European partners in building an industrial Europe.
paragraph_summary: The president outlines an industrial policy focused on leading the energy and digital transitions to drive innovation, employment, and European integration.
reason: The paragraph connects global industrial revolutions to national industrial policy and European cooperation.
crisis_reason: The paragraph describes a proactive industrial policy focused on innovation and growth, which is framed as a positive development and routine governance rather than a response to an urgent threat.

paragraph_text: Third, we must recognize that France has particular responsibilities in the world. France’s vocation and its honor are to assert its voice with strength and independence for peace and justice. It is also in our own interest. Therefore, acting as we do, to support the development of the world’s poorest countries, is, of course, a fundamental moral imperative. But it is also a way to prevent an influx of people from their countries, seeking hope elsewhere.
paragraph_summary: The President emphasizes France's global responsibility to support developing nations as a moral imperative and a strategy to manage migration.
reason: It connects international development aid and foreign policy to the domestic issue of managing migration flows.
crisis_reason: The paragraph frames support for developing nations as a strategic and moral imperative rather than an urgent threat or an active crisis, focusing on international responsibility and policy management.

paragraph_text: Fourth, there is Europe. For a year and a half, we have chosen to advance the Europe of projects: research, energy, security, and immigration. Let us never forget that Europe is a guarantee of peace and democracy on our continent. It is therefore our future. The 50th anniversary of the Treaty of Rome, on March 25th, offers us the opportunity to give a new impetus to European construction: for a political Europe, for a Europe of economic ambition and social progress, for a Europe that protects us. France will naturally play its full part.
paragraph_summary: The speaker advocates for strengthening European political, economic, and social integration to ensure peace, democracy, and protection for the continent.
reason: Discusses European integration and its impact on French national policy.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on the positive goal of strengthening integration to ensure peace and democracy, rather than framing the current state as an urgent threat or danger.

paragraph_text: With the same spirit of openness, with the same commitment to fulfilling my promises, I approach this new year, despite the international economic crisis, where the first results of our efforts are beginning to be felt. Much remains to be done, and I am well aware of that, to ensure that the measures we have implemented translate into tangible improvements in your daily lives, to meet the expectations you have expressed, and to restore France's standing and role in the world. At the end of 2007, a first stage is coming to a close, a stage of urgency: the need to overcome old partisan divisions. The urgency of fiscal and social reform, to restore confidence and support economic activity, which has allowed our economy to better withstand the slowdown. The urgency of improving purchasing power. The urgency of greater autonomy for universities. The urgency of reforming special regimes, and of liberating and rehabilitating work. The urgency of providing a minimum service. The urgency of modernizing the state, a process that is finally underway. The urgency of reforms that have been delayed for 20 or 30 years. The urgency of making France a leader in environmental protection, quality of life, and sustainable development. The urgency of a simplified treaty to unlock Europe, a Europe that I have never ceased to believe is essential. The urgency of France re-engaging in dialogue with the world, so that it can play its rightful role in promoting peace and stability, serving those who suffer, the children and women who are victims of violence, the persecuted, and those who are waiting in prisons, hoping that France will speak out and act on their behalf.
paragraph_summary: The President outlines his commitment to economic recovery, domestic structural reforms, and strengthening France's international role and influence in the coming year.
reason: Lists domestic reforms while linking them to France's role in the global economy and international diplomacy.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on economic recovery, structural reforms, and strengthening international influence, which are framed as positive goals and routine governance rather than urgent threats or active crises.

paragraph_text: So, let France show the way! That is what all the world’s peoples have always expected from her. That is what we will do when France assumes the presidency of the European Union, starting July 1st. That is what we want to achieve with the Union for the Mediterranean, a great vision of civilization. That is what we want to do throughout the world, to restore hope to those who have lost it. And, of course, overcoming our doubts and anxieties, that is what we must do first for France itself. Our old world needs a new Renaissance. Let France be the soul of that Renaissance! That is my dearest wish for this coming year. I sincerely wish that it will be a year of happiness and success for France, for each and every one of you, and for all those you hold dear. My dear fellow citizens, Long live the Republic, Long live France!
paragraph_summary: The President outlines his vision for France to lead a global and European renaissance while extending New Year wishes to the French people.
reason: Connects France's international leadership ambitions with domestic national renewal.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on a vision for the future and extends holiday greetings, which are explicitly categorized as non-crisis ceremonial and affirmative content.

paragraph_text: Fellow French men and women, my dear compatriots, the year that is drawing to a close has been a difficult one for all. No continent, no country, no sector has been spared. The economic crisis has imposed new hardships and new suffering, here in France and elsewhere. I am particularly thinking of those who have lost their jobs. However, our country has been less affected than many others. We owe this to our social model, which has cushioned the impact, and to the decisive measures taken to support economic activity, and above all, to ensure that no one is left behind. But it is to each and every one of you that the greatest credit belongs. Tonight, I want to pay tribute to the resilience and courage of the French people in the face of the crisis. I want to particularly acknowledge the social partners, who have demonstrated a remarkable sense of responsibility, the associations that have provided assistance to those most in need, and the business leaders, many of whom have strived to save jobs. Together, we have averted the worst. But we have also prepared for the future.
paragraph_summary: The President acknowledges the economic hardships of the past year while praising the resilience of the French people and the effectiveness of national social measures.
reason: Discusses the domestic impact of the global economic crisis and the effectiveness of the French social model.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on the resilience of the people and the effectiveness of past measures, framing the situation as a recovery or achievement rather than an active, ongoing threat.

paragraph_text: My dear compatriots, even though the challenges are not yet over, 2010 will be a year of renewal. The efforts we have made over the past two and a half years will bear fruit. At this crucial moment, we must remain united, as we have shown that we can be. It is this unity that has enabled us to take the initiative and lead others. The ideas that France defends will be able to take hold in the search for a new world order: one that is more balanced, more regulated, more just, and more peaceful. These ideas impose on us a duty of exemplary conduct. Let us respect one another, let us make an effort to understand one another, and let us avoid words and actions that cause pain. Let us be capable of debating without tearing each other apart, without insulting one another, and without becoming disunited. A united France, confident in itself, looking to the future as a promise of achievement – that is the hope I have for our country.
paragraph_summary: The President calls for national unity and respectful public discourse to foster renewal and promote France's vision for a more balanced global order.
reason: Links domestic unity and national conduct to France's influence on the global order.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on national unity and a vision for the future rather than framing current conditions as an urgent threat or danger.

paragraph_text: Europe, facing a storm, has managed to weather it, perhaps not always completely, and often not always quickly, but Europe has held firm, and Europe has protected us. Do not believe those who propose that we leave the euro. Isolation for France would be madness. The end of the euro would mean the end of Europe. I will oppose this with all my strength, this backward step that would disregard 60 years of European construction, which has brought peace and fraternity to our continent. I say this with particular conviction, given that I have always championed the principle of community preference, and I have always fought for the protection of our industry, for reciprocity, and for an end to naivety in commercial negotiations with our main partners. Europe is essential to our future, to our identity, and to our values.
model_reasons: storm: metaphorical use referring to political or economic challenges, not weather
paragraph_summary: The President defends France's continued membership in the euro and the European Union, arguing that European integration is essential for national prosperity and security.
reason: It discusses the role of the Euro and European integration, which directly impacts French national sovereignty and economic stability.
crisis_reason: The paragraph frames European integration as a positive, essential foundation for prosperity and security rather than as a response to an urgent threat or danger.

paragraph_text: My deepest conviction for 2011 is that we must relentlessly continue to strengthen our strengths and address our weaknesses by becoming more competitive, by better training our young people, by working harder, and by reducing our public spending and deficits, otherwise we risk seeing our independence gravely threatened. Let us look at what has happened in Europe. Countries that have sought to live beyond their means, without considering the future, have been heavily penalized. My first duty is to protect France from that prospect. France will therefore honor its commitments by balancing its accounts. I will not compromise on that objective.
paragraph_summary: President Sarkozy outlines a commitment to fiscal responsibility, deficit reduction, and increased competitiveness to ensure France's economic independence and stability.
reason: The text links domestic fiscal responsibility and deficit reduction to the broader context of European economic pressures.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on routine economic governance, fiscal responsibility, and competitiveness as a means to ensure stability, rather than framing the economic situation as an urgent, active threat or crisis.

paragraph_text: My dear fellow citizens, the year 2011 is drawing to a close. It has been a year of significant upheaval. Since the beginning of the crisis, which over three years has repeatedly brought the global economy to the brink of collapse, I have never concealed the truth about its severity, nor about the consequences it could have on employment and purchasing power. This crisis, which marks the culmination of 30 years of global disorder in the economy, trade, finance, and currency, is unprecedented. It is undoubtedly the most serious since the Second World War. This crisis is not over. It has swept away countries like Greece, Ireland, and Portugal, as well as powerful nations like Spain and even Italy. Throughout this turmoil, you have suffered. I know that the lives of many of you, already strained by two difficult years, have been tested once again. You are finishing the year with greater anxieties for yourselves and your children.
paragraph_summary: President Sarkozy addresses the ongoing severity of the global economic crisis and its negative impact on French citizens' employment, purchasing power, and personal anxiety.
reason: The text connects the global economic crisis to its specific, severe consequences for the French population.
crisis_reason: The speaker explicitly frames the economic situation as a severe, ongoing threat to the livelihoods and well-being of citizens, emphasizing the urgency of the negative impacts.

paragraph_text: My dear fellow citizens, we must be courageous, we must be realistic. What is happening in the world indicates that the year 2012 will be a year of both risks and opportunities, a year of hope, if we are able to meet the challenges ahead. It will also be a year of dangers, if we remain inactive. Delaying decisions, however difficult they may be, is the worst possible option. When decisions are made too late, the price to pay is higher, and the suffering greater. In 2012, the destiny of France could once again be at a turning point. Emerging from the crisis, building a new model of growth, forging a new Europe – these are some of the challenges that await us. I want to tell you my conviction that, united with our European partners, we will be stronger to face them. But these challenges are imposed upon us. We cannot reject them, nor can we postpone them. We cannot ignore this new world.
paragraph_summary: The President outlines the necessity of decisive action to address economic crisis, growth models, and European integration during the challenges of 2012.
reason: It discusses the necessity of forging a new European model to navigate global economic challenges.
crisis_reason: The paragraph explicitly frames the economic situation as a crisis and emphasizes the necessity of decisive action to address it, indicating an urgent threat to the economy.

paragraph_text: My dear fellow citizens, before I extend my New Year wishes, I want to talk to you about what we have in common, and what is most precious to us: our country. The year 2013 was intense and challenging. It was intense because France took on its responsibilities during serious international crises: in Mali, Syria, and the Central African Republic, most recently. It was intense because Europe, at long last, managed to overcome the financial turmoil it had been experiencing since 2008. It was intense because the government implemented reforms to restore our public finances, improve the competitiveness of businesses, modernize the labor market, and strengthen our pension system, taking into account the physically demanding aspects of certain jobs. It prioritized education – that was my commitment – and it legalized same-sex marriage. However, 2013 was also a difficult year for many of you, and for the country. Because the crisis proved to be longer and deeper than we had initially anticipated. And we felt the consequences with weak economic growth and a series of social plans. The state of the country required me to ask for your efforts. And I know what those represent. Taxes have become burdensome, too burdensome, having accumulated over many years. In 2013, unemployment remained at a still-high level, although the trend in recent months is improving. Results take time to emerge, but they are there. And I have confidence in the choices I have made for the country.
paragraph_summary: The President reviews France's 2013 international military engagements, economic reforms, and the ongoing challenges of unemployment and taxation.
reason: Reviews a mix of international crises and domestic economic/social reforms.
crisis_reason: The paragraph frames unemployment and taxation as ongoing challenges, which are presented as persistent problems requiring attention and reform rather than as an acute or urgent threat to the state's integrity or safety.

paragraph_text: My dear fellow citizens of mainland France and overseas territories, next spring, you will be consulted through two elections. The upcoming municipal elections will elect officials who will partner with the government to drive our country forward, but within a framework that needs to be clearly defined. A new decentralization law will grant elected officials greater responsibilities and will simplify the territorial organization of our country, which has become overly complex and costly. As for the European Parliament elections, they should provide an opportunity to promote a political majority focused on employment and solidarity, rather than austerity and national self-interest. We will not build the France of tomorrow by undermining Europe. On the contrary, it is by strengthening it that we will better protect ourselves. I will not tolerate those who deny Europe’s future, who want to return to outdated borders, believing that they will offer them greater security, or those who want to leave the euro. I feel a deep connection to all the generations who fought for Europe. Therefore, starting next spring, I will be taking initiatives with Germany to strengthen our Union.
paragraph_summary: The president discusses upcoming municipal and European elections, advocating for decentralization reforms and a stronger, more integrated European Union.
reason: Covers domestic territorial reform alongside European Union political strategy.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on routine political processes like elections and standard policy goals such as decentralization and integration, without framing these as urgent threats or dangers.

paragraph_text:
My dear fellow citizens, I am addressing you tonight with a message of confidence and determination as I extend my best wishes for the new year. I am aware of the challenges we face, I recognize them every day, and I am thinking tonight of the families who are worried about the future of their children, facing unemployment and sometimes even exclusion. And I want to put an end to negativity and discouragement. France is a great nation; it is the fifth largest economy in the world. France takes its responsibilities whenever peace is threatened, thanks to the commitment of our soldiers in Africa and in Iraq, and I want to salute them wherever they may be; they do honor to our flag. France is a proactive diplomacy, constantly seeking solutions to conflicts, such as in Ukraine, where I personally became involved, or in the Middle East. France is driving Europe forward. It is championing economic growth with a 
315 billion investment plan, which will be launched by the European Commission in 2015. France is recognized for its innovation, its culture, and the talent of its entrepreneurs, creators, and researchers. It was honored this year with two Nobel Prizes. Therefore, we have every reason to be confident, but on one condition: we must move forward, demonstrate boldness, reject the status quo, and avoid regression. I have chosen the path of the future, while remaining faithful to the values of the Republic and our social model. France is not about nostalgia; it is about hope. My duty, alongside the government led by Manuel Valls, is to do everything possible, to undertake everything, to prepare France for the future, to give our all for our country. I believe in perseverance, consistency, and long-term commitment.
paragraph_summary: The President addresses national economic challenges, military commitments abroad, diplomatic efforts, and the need for bold reforms to ensure France's future prosperity.
reason: Balances France's international diplomatic role with domestic economic and social goals.
crisis_reason: The paragraph frames economic challenges and the need for bold reforms as urgent threats to the nation's future prosperity, indicating a crisis-oriented posture.

paragraph_text: My dear fellow citizens, France will move forward next year, in all areas, for everyone. That is the commitment I have made. I will pursue this fight to the end, against conservatism – and there is a great deal of it – and against populism, which is dangerous. When it comes to unemployment, we will succeed not by standing still or isolating ourselves from the rest of the world, but by taking initiative. Let us reject the deceptive and misleading rhetoric that abuses the people. With Europe, we will not convince anyone by dismantling what exists or by pretending to leave the euro zone. Instead, we must improve our own competitiveness, mobilize all economic levers to escape stagnation in Europe, and give the euro zone a democratic foundation.
paragraph_summary: The President outlines his commitment to economic reform, competitiveness, and European integration while rejecting populism and isolationism to address unemployment and stagnation.
reason: Addresses domestic economic challenges in the context of European and global competition.
crisis_reason: The paragraph frames unemployment and stagnation as urgent problems that require a rejection of populism and a commitment to reform, treating these economic conditions as threats to the nation's stability.

paragraph_text: French people, the events we have experienced have confirmed to us: we are united by a shared feeling. That feeling is a love of our country. Our country is the invisible thread that connects us all, French people from here or from elsewhere, citizens of all backgrounds, of all faiths, and of all origins. And that country is at the heart of my commitment – a country that is open to the world, that respects others, that promotes equality, and that has confidence in the future, the future of France, and never in isolation, closure, discrimination, or nostalgia. Our country is the foundation upon which we built Europe. Europe has achieved great things. But it has also revealed shortcomings that today put it in peril. Faced with the influx of refugees caused by conflict, Europe must be able to secure its borders and to welcome those who seek asylum, while also, with dignity, returning those who do not qualify. It is a major challenge, or else the walls we believed had been dismantled by history will rise again.
paragraph_summary: The President emphasizes national unity and calls for a balanced European approach to managing refugee influxes and border security.
reason: Discusses national identity in the context of European integration and the international refugee crisis.
crisis_reason: The paragraph frames the refugee influx and border security as a challenge requiring a specific, balanced approach to manage, but it does not explicitly characterize these issues as an urgent threat or a crisis demanding immediate defensive action.

paragraph_text: But I owe you the truth: we are not yet finished with terrorism. The threat remains, and it remains at its highest level. We regularly thwart terrorist attacks. Therefore, my first duty is to protect you. Protecting you means addressing the root of the problem: in Syria and in Iraq. That is why we have intensified our operations against Daesh. Our actions are having an impact; jihadists are retreating, so we will continue as long as necessary.
paragraph_summary: The president emphasizes the ongoing threat of terrorism and outlines military efforts against Daesh in Syria and Iraq to ensure national security.
reason: Connects domestic security against terrorism to international military operations in Syria and Iraq.
crisis_reason: The paragraph explicitly frames terrorism as an ongoing threat to national security, which necessitates active military intervention and vigilance.

paragraph_text: My dear fellow citizens, tonight, it is the last time I address you as President of the Republic. It is a moment filled with emotion and gravity, and I want to share that with you, with all French people, regardless of your background, beliefs, or religion, from mainland France and our overseas territories. First, I want to speak about what you have endured over the past year, as our country has been struck by terrible attacks: the attack in Nice on July 14th last year, but also those in Magnanville and Saint-Etienne-du-Rouvray. At this moment, I think of the victims, their families, and the injured who are suffering, both in body and in spirit. I also understand the anxiety you feel in the face of this ongoing terrorist threat, as tragically demonstrated by the events in Berlin just days ago. It is my duty, alongside the government led by Bernard Cazeneuve, to ensure your protection. I am dedicating all necessary resources to that end, and I want to pay tribute to our police officers, gendarmes, and military personnel who dedicate themselves, even to the point of sacrifice, to ensure our safety.
paragraph_summary: The President addresses the nation regarding the impact of recent terrorist attacks in France and Germany while emphasizing the government's commitment to national security.
reason: Reflects on domestic terrorist attacks while acknowledging the broader international threat.
crisis_reason: The paragraph frames recent terrorist attacks as a direct threat to national security, which the government must actively address, fitting the definition of an acute crisis.

paragraph_text: However, we are not yet finished with the scourge of terrorism. We must continue to fight it – both abroad, as demonstrated by our military operations in Mali, Syria, and Iraq, where I will be traveling in two days to greet our soldiers. We must also fight it within our own borders, to thwart attacks, to neutralize dangerous individuals, and to prevent radicalization. Be assured that in this fight against barbarity, our democracy will emerge victorious.
paragraph_summary: The President outlines ongoing domestic and international military and security efforts to combat terrorism and radicalization.
reason: Links the domestic fight against terrorism to international military operations.
crisis_reason: The paragraph frames terrorism and radicalization as ongoing security threats that require active, sustained military and security efforts to combat, which constitutes a chronic threat framed as an urgent danger.

paragraph_text: At the end of this year, what we once considered to be permanent – democracy, freedom, social rights, Europe, and even peace – all of these are now vulnerable and potentially reversible. We have seen that in the United Kingdom with Brexit, in the United States during last November’s election, and across our continent with the rise of extremism. There have been periods in history when everything can shift. We are living through one of those periods.
paragraph_summary: The President warns that democracy, peace, and European stability are increasingly vulnerable due to rising extremism and global political shifts.
reason: Analyzes the impact of international political trends like Brexit and global extremism on domestic stability.
crisis_reason: The speaker explicitly frames democracy, peace, and stability as 'increasingly vulnerable' to threats like extremism, which constitutes a chronic threat requiring urgent attention.

paragraph_text: In just five months, my dear fellow citizens, you will be making a choice. It will be a decisive one for France, for its social model, which you cherish because it guarantees equality for all in the face of life’s uncertainties, particularly healthcare. It concerns our essential public services, and especially the schools of the Republic, where so much is at stake, particularly for our young people, who represent our hope. It also concerns our country’s ability to seize the major transformations that are the digital revolution and the energy transition, and to turn them into drivers of growth, well-being, and employment, rather than sources of precarity and instability. Finally, it concerns our values. France is open to the world, it is European, and it is fraternal. How can we imagine our country retreating behind walls, reduced to its own internal market, returning to its own currency, and, on top of that, discriminating against its own citizens based on their origins? That would not be France! These are the major issues at stake.
paragraph_summary: The President outlines key national priorities ahead of the upcoming election, including social welfare, public services, economic modernization, and the preservation of French values.
reason: Discusses upcoming national elections in the context of France's European identity and global openness.
crisis_reason: The paragraph describes routine governance and national priorities in the context of an upcoming election, lacking any language framing these topics as urgent threats or dangers.

paragraph_text: In 2018, we will first and foremost undertake several crucial initiatives and determined actions on the international stage: the fight against Islamist terrorism in the Levant, in the Sahel, and within our own country. In that regard, I want to pay tribute tonight to our military personnel who are currently engaged in those theaters of conflict. I think of those comrades who have fallen this year. We will win this battle against terrorism. I also want to pay tribute to our police officers and gendarmes who, every day, fight against Islamist terrorism and protect you. But we must also secure peace internationally, which means the essential work to ensure our security, and because it is our universal mission, the work to guarantee the stability of nations and ensure respect for all minorities. That is what we have done in Lebanon, what we are doing today in the Sahel, and what we will continue to do in Syria, in the Middle East, and in Africa. We need to reinvent a framework for peace and hope across many continents.
paragraph_summary: The President outlines France's commitment to combating Islamist terrorism internationally and domestically while promoting global stability and peace.
reason: It addresses the fight against terrorism both within France and in international conflict zones like the Sahel and Middle East.
crisis_reason: The paragraph frames Islamist terrorism as an active, serious threat to both domestic and international security that requires ongoing commitment and combat, which constitutes a crisis framing.

paragraph_text: I also want to prioritize fraternity. Fraternity is what unites us, what has made us one, and what holds us together. I believe in success, in achievement, but what is the value of those successes if they are, in effect, only the successes of a few? If they fuel selfishness or cynicism? Nothing truly lasting. So many nations are crumbling because only a few succeed! We must, therefore, rethink a major social project for our country, and that is the project I will be rolling out during the coming year. It must inspire our health policy, our policy for those living with disabilities, our housing policy for the homeless, and our social policy to support the most vulnerable. Without that, without that humanistic commitment, our country will not remain united. That requires rules and rigor, and I am not underestimating the ethical tensions that may arise, nor will I shy away from them. I want us to be able to provide shelter for all those who are currently homeless. The government has made significant commitments in that direction in recent months and has made considerable progress, but there are still situations that are unacceptable, and I will not accept them any more than you will. We will therefore continue this essential effort to fully honor the commitment I made to you. Count on my unwavering determination in that regard. We must also welcome those men and women who are fleeing their countries because they are threatened because of their origin, their religion, or their political beliefs. That is what we call asylum. It is a moral and political duty, and I will not yield on that. We will respect that right, and we will continue to welcome these men and women because France is their homeland. However, we cannot welcome everyone, and we cannot do so without rules. It is also essential that we be able to verify the identity of each individual. When someone arrives on our territory and does not qualify for asylum and has no chance of obtaining French citizenship, we cannot accept that they remain in a precarious situation for months or years, a situation that is neither good for them nor for the country. Therefore, we also need simple, respected rules, and rigor. I am committed to ensuring that our country upholds this balance of humanity and efficiency.
paragraph_summary: The President outlines a social project focused on national unity, homelessness, and a balanced approach to asylum and immigration policy.
reason: It addresses domestic social policy alongside the international issue of asylum seekers and migration management.
crisis_reason: The paragraph describes a social project and a balanced policy approach, which are presented as routine governance and constructive initiatives rather than urgent threats or existential dangers.

paragraph_text: As you can see, we are experiencing several unprecedented upheavals: the ultra-liberal and financial capitalism, so often guided by short-term gains and the greed of a few, is coming to an end; our sense of unease within Western civilization and the crisis of our European dream are evident. So, should we despair? I do not believe so. It is an immense challenge, and all of this is, of course, linked to the unease felt by our country, but precisely, we have a role to play, a vision to offer. This is the path I have been charting since the beginning of my term, and I intend to continue it: to put people at the heart of this contemporary project. This requires a great deal of constancy and determination. But I am deeply convinced that we must invent a response, a project that is profoundly French and European, to address what we are experiencing both within our country and beyond our borders. We must, once again, as we have always done, take our full part in the renaissance of our world and our daily lives. That is why, my dear compatriots, this year, 2019, is, in my view, decisive, and I want to make three wishes for us.
paragraph_summary: The President addresses global economic and societal crises while proposing a French and European vision to foster national and international renewal in 2019.
reason: It links domestic unease in France to broader Western civilizational crises and the European project.
crisis_reason: The paragraph explicitly identifies global economic and societal issues as crises that require a strategic vision for renewal, framing them as active threats to the nation and the international order.

paragraph_text: Finally, I want to make a third and final wish: a wish for hope. Hope in ourselves, as a people. Hope for our shared future. Hope for our Europe. I believe that we have within us a powerful energy if we can rediscover our trust in ourselves and in each other. I believe that France embodies a unique project: a project of education for all, a strong culture that unites us, a project of industrial ecology, a society based on new forms of solidarity and dedicated to serving people. And behind that, what we deeply desire is to regain control of our daily lives and our destinies. To stop being subjected to forces beyond our control. That is what must guide our choices for the country and the major decisions for the year ahead. It must also guide the renewed European project that I will propose to you in the coming weeks. Regaining control of our lives means choosing our food, ensuring fair taxation, protecting ourselves from our enemies, investing to innovate, and providing a common response to migration. I deeply believe in a Europe that can better protect its people and restore hope to us all. Next May, we will have the opportunity to express ourselves on this crucial European choice. We want to end the feeling of powerlessness at all levels. This is a task of unprecedented scale, but it is within our reach. I am confident that we are capable. And that is where hope for 2019 lies.
paragraph_summary: The President expresses hope for France and Europe, emphasizing the need to regain control over national and European destinies through collective action and reform.
reason: It connects domestic aspirations for control over daily life with the need for a renewed European project and migration policy.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on hope, collective action, and reform as a positive vision for the future rather than framing the current state as an urgent threat or danger.

paragraph_text: Tonight, for the first time, a country, the United Kingdom, is leaving the European Union. A few days ago, we finalized an agreement to organize our future relationship with it, defending our interests, our industries, our fishermen, and our European unity. The United Kingdom remains our neighbor, but also our friend and ally. This decision to leave Europe, Brexit, was born from a sense of malaise within Europe, and from many lies and false promises. But I want to be very clear with you: our destiny lies within Europe. Our sovereignty is national, and I will do everything to ensure that we regain control of our destiny and our lives. But this sovereignty must also be achieved through a stronger, more autonomous, and more united Europe. That is what we have built in 2020. Because we have been able to drive the necessary transformations and strengthen our credibility, we have been able to convince Germany and then the other members of the European Union to create a unique and massive recovery plan, and to agree on a common and solidarity-based debt, to better prepare for our future by committing to ecological and digital transition, and by creating as many jobs as possible for our young people.
paragraph_summary: The President discusses the United Kingdom's departure from the European Union and emphasizes the importance of European unity, sovereignty, and the post-pandemic recovery plan.
reason: The text discusses Brexit and European integration, which are international issues with direct implications for French national sovereignty and economy.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on the post-pandemic recovery and the political transition of Brexit as established facts or ongoing processes rather than framing them as active, urgent threats requiring immediate defensive action.

paragraph_text: Romain is a gendarme in Tende, in the Alpes-Maritimes. On the night of October 2nd and 3rd, floods threatened to sweep away a retirement home. At great personal risk, he managed to evacuate more than 70 residents, saving their lives. Together with the police from Nice, who stopped an attack on the Basilica of the Assumption, and with Arno, Cyrille, and Rémi, gendarmes who were killed while assisting a woman who was being abused in the Puy-de-Dôme, and with Tanerii, Dorian, and Quentin, soldiers who died last Monday during an operation in Mali, they are heroes of the nation.
model_reasons: floods: refers to a specific natural disaster event without explicit climate change framing
