With development comes opportunity – which can be used wisely or unwisely. Technology is advancing rapidly, presenting us with dilemmas that we may not be ready to face. The pursuit of short-term gains can stand in the way of making good choices that serve the best interests of individuals, society, and the planet. And research increasingly shows that we have long exploited the planet's resources at a rate that it cannot sustain. We need prudence in the face of all the new opportunities that are being presented to us. We need time for thoughtful conversations about important issues in a time characterized by rapid change, a harsh debate climate, and global unrest.
model_reasons: climate: refers to the tone of public debate, not Earth's climate
paragraph_summary: The speaker reflects on Norway's post-war development while emphasizing the need for prudence, sustainability, and thoughtful public discourse amidst rapid technological and global change.
reason: It connects domestic challenges and national development to global unrest and planetary resource issues.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on reflection, prudence, and thoughtful discourse as general principles for navigating change rather than framing specific threats as urgent dangers requiring immediate crisis response.

paragraph_text: At the same time, we need to be challenged by the impatience of young people. We need all of this to avoid losing ourselves and each other, and to avoid losing all that we have created together. The question is: What best serves humanity and our shared future? This touches and concerns us all. We have different answers and may deeply disagree. But we must continue to seek answers together on these great questions – around kitchen tables, in lunchrooms, in classrooms, in politics, and on international stages. Because together, we can solve them. We must learn to live with our differences. We must tolerate uncomfortable truths. We must learn to look beyond our own small corner of the world. We must dare to admit that our worldview may not be the only correct one. And we must be prepared to be challenged – even hurt. That is what it means to live together – both in small and large communities. A society that values diversity must be built on the principle that all people are equally valuable. As a stark echo from the war, we know what happens when societies are built on an ideology that claims some people are more valuable than others. If there is one thing we should take with us as we celebrate the peace that finally arrived, it is this: All people are equally valuable. It may seem obvious. But if we truly live by that principle, transformation can occur. We can then come together with hope for a good life for all. I wish everyone at home and all Norwegians abroad a Happy New Year!
paragraph_summary: The speaker emphasizes the importance of mutual respect, diversity, and the fundamental principle that all people are equally valuable for a cohesive society.
reason: It encourages looking beyond national borders while addressing domestic social values and diversity.
crisis_reason: the paragraph focuses on positive societal values and principles of cohesion rather than framing these topics as an urgent threat or a danger to the state.

paragraph_text: Our country has historically been built on a strong will to survive and to be independent. I believe that our nature and our history have shaped us as a people. We have had to live alongside the forces of nature and create livelihoods despite them. We have, at times, experienced being under foreign rule – and we have had to fight for our freedom. This has, among other things, taught us to value equality and human rights – both at home and abroad. We must not forget our history, otherwise we risk squandering the freedoms and progress that we have painstakingly earned. And we risk forgetting our solidarity with those who are still in the midst of the fight for fundamental rights. This also applies to the fight for equality and for the best interests of children. This work has been driven by the strong will and perseverance of individuals. Gradually, the entire society has supported this work and followed suit – so that together, we have made significant progress as a nation. But society is constantly changing, and the work is not yet done. We must remain vigilant so that no groups are left behind. We need both boys and girls, women and men, from all backgrounds, with all kinds of experiences and personalities, to create a good society where everyone can contribute on their own terms.
paragraph_summary: The speaker reflects on Norway's history of independence and equality, emphasizing the ongoing need for national solidarity, vigilance, and inclusive societal participation.
reason: The text connects Norway's historical struggle for independence and domestic equality to broader international human rights values.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on national values, history, and the general importance of solidarity and participation as a routine civic ideal rather than framing these as a response to an urgent or specific threat.

paragraph_text: In Åse-Marie Nesse’s poem, it continues: "It draws grapes from the lava stone, It lets thirsty palms bear fruit, It sows sunflower seeds among thistles. " The Creator’s work is full of life, which we humans have been given to steward. Knowledge is the key to acting wisely. For the will is there! This will, for example, manifests in a burgeoning green technological revolution that is currently underway. Almost no one creates anything today without considering the environmental impact. That alone is a significant step forward from just a few years ago.
paragraph_summary: The speaker highlights the role of human stewardship and knowledge in driving the current green technological revolution and increasing environmental awareness.
reason: The paragraph links local technological innovation and environmental awareness to global trends.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on human stewardship, knowledge, and positive technological progress, which are framed as achievements rather than threats or urgent dangers.

paragraph_text: Another wish, written by a girl and a boy together, said: "I wish that we make it safe for women to live in Norway – and that we don't have to ask for that again. " Our society must be safe for everyone. We must especially work to protect those who are most vulnerable and exposed. In 2015, world leaders adopted the new sustainable development goals to create a better world. One of those goals is to combat violence against women and children. On this issue, Norway acknowledged that we need to make an extra effort in our own country. To create a safe society for everyone, it is important to speak out about what is happening, and those who have the ability to help must be attentive listeners with strong action-taking abilities.
paragraph_summary: The speaker emphasizes the necessity of combating violence against women and children to ensure a safe society for all in Norway.
reason: The text discusses domestic safety for women while referencing international sustainable development goals.
crisis_reason: The speaker frames violence against women and children as a serious threat to societal safety that requires active combat and intervention, rather than treating it as a routine policy matter.

paragraph_text: One of the finest wishes I have heard this year came from a police officer who works with young people in trouble. The conversation between him and one of the young people was featured on the news program this fall. When the young man said that the police officer was only dreaming when he believed in a change for him and his friends, the police officer replied: "Perhaps. But my dream starts with you. " The young man fell silent. My dream for you. If we have good dreams for each other, if we care about each other – then so much can happen. It is good for us to help others to feel well. Both in our local community, in Norway, and as global citizens. I would like to extend a special thank you to all of you who are serving outside the country – and who may be missing family and loved ones tonight. Thank you for the efforts you make through diplomacy and humanitarian organizations, through the police and the military – for peace, stability, health, and safety for our fellow human beings.
paragraph_summary: The speaker emphasizes the importance of mutual care and dreams for others while thanking Norwegians serving abroad in humanitarian, diplomatic, and security roles.
reason: The text addresses both local community support and the contributions of Norwegians serving abroad in international roles.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on gratitude and the expression of national values and mutual care, rather than framing the work of those abroad as a response to an urgent threat.

paragraph_text: Dear all, The Queen and I are both turning 80 this year. For us, it feels a little unreal. As so many older people have experienced before us, you rarely feel as old as the number suggests. It brings us great joy to meet so many people who leave a lasting impression, who inspire us with new ideas, and who paint a picture of the new Norway – with all its opportunities and challenges. We hope and believe that these people will be able to build upon the values of trust, community, and generosity. People who will shape our society with their positive influence and extensive knowledge. Life has taught me that what is most important to us humans doesn't change very much over time. We need people who care about us. We need to feel useful. We need to be seen – and recognized – for who we are. We need someone to take the time to listen and be present. We need to feel that we are contributing to something, and to someone, beyond ourselves. Therefore, I am pleased to see this note fluttering on a branch of the Wishing Tree: "I wish for a Norway that is rich in its diversity, with the ability to love its neighbor, where people can be who they are. A country with genuine freedom! " In the new year, I hope that Norway, Europe, and the world will be characterized by this: that we, who share this Earth, recognize that we are, above all, fellow human beings. That we can unite to work towards a healthier world. That we choose to combat evil with good. And from the Wishing Tree in the Slottsparken, comes one final hope: "That all the wishes on the tree may come true. " Happy New Year!
paragraph_summary: The King reflects on aging, the importance of human connection, and his hopes for a Norway defined by diversity, trust, and global solidarity.
reason: The paragraph reflects on domestic values while expressing hopes for a healthier world and global human unity.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on personal reflection, social values, and aspirational goals rather than identifying a specific threat or urgent danger requiring immediate action.

paragraph_text: After the terrorist attacks against Jews in Paris and Copenhagen at the beginning of the year, a group of young, Norwegian Muslims took the initiative to create a protective ring around the synagogue in Oslo. It was a simple and very effective action that demonstrated courage and commitment by caring for others.
paragraph_summary: The speaker highlights a protective initiative by young Norwegian Muslims at an Oslo synagogue following terrorist attacks in Paris and Copenhagen.
reason: It discusses a local response to international terrorist events.
crisis_reason: The paragraph references terrorist attacks in Paris and Copenhagen as the catalyst for the initiative, framing these events as acute security threats that necessitated a protective response.

paragraph_text: The massive influx of refugees this autumn has brought us face to face with a major challenge. The entire European continent is experiencing a major challenge in welcoming them in a good way. People across the country have collected and distributed clothes and food, taught Norwegian, and opened their homes to those in need. These are spontaneous actions – and an expression of human kindness. In this way, people have been seen and important communities have been built.
paragraph_summary: The speaker highlights the challenges of the European refugee crisis and commends the Norwegian public for their spontaneous acts of kindness and community support.
reason: It addresses the local impact and societal response to the international refugee crisis.
crisis_reason: The speaker explicitly identifies the refugee situation as a 'crisis' and frames it as a challenge requiring a response, which constitutes an active threat to social cohesion and stability.

paragraph_text: Tonight, I would like to extend a special thank you to Norwegians who are in other countries, helping people in need, and contributing to the work of peace and stability. The men and women in the armed forces, police, diplomacy, and humanitarian operations and organizations are making an important contribution that we can all be proud of. My thoughts tonight also go to the families and those affected by the avalanche on Svalbard just before Christmas. Volunteers and emergency services also made a significant effort to help those affected.
paragraph_summary: The speaker acknowledges the contributions of Norwegians working abroad in humanitarian and security roles and expresses sympathy for those affected by the Svalbard avalanche.
reason: It combines praise for international humanitarian efforts with condolences for a local tragedy.
crisis_reason: The mention of the Svalbard avalanche refers to a specific, acute natural disaster, but the paragraph frames it as an expression of sympathy rather than a call to action or a warning about an ongoing threat.

paragraph_text: Norway has experienced unprecedented economic growth in recent years. Our country's significant access to energy and the riches of the sea have given us great wealth. However, it is not just our natural resources that have given us these opportunities – but, even more so, our shared human resources. Because so many people participate in the workforce and can therefore contribute to the community, we are all working together to create the prosperity that everyone enjoys. Many people today are concerned about what the future will look like. In recent times, we have experienced trials – both as a nation and as individuals. It is natural to feel uneasy and to express those feelings. Norway is closely linked to other countries. Therefore, events on the other side of the world can have significant consequences for our daily lives here at home. Some industries are threatened, and for some employees, this can have serious consequences. We have recently heard of layoffs in several places. I believe that many people tonight are worried about what will happen to their employment situation in the months ahead.
paragraph_summary: The speaker discusses Norway's economic prosperity driven by natural and human resources while acknowledging public anxiety regarding global instability and potential job losses.
reason: It explains how global economic trends directly impact the domestic Norwegian labor market.
crisis_reason: The speaker acknowledges public anxiety regarding global instability and potential job losses, framing these as active threats to the nation's economic security and social well-being.

paragraph_text: Every day, we see human drama in the faces of all those who are trying to find a safe haven in Norway. Many are fleeing from terror in their home countries. The horrific events of the autumn in Paris and other cities have been a terrifying reminder that the values on which our society is built are under pressure. Terrorists know no boundaries, and their goal is to create fear throughout the population. They want to deprive us of our freedom – our daily lives. We must fight against this – using various means. While terrorists attack our lives with bombs, our best defense is to use values as weapons.
paragraph_summary: The speaker addresses the threat of international terrorism and emphasizes the importance of defending societal values against attempts to incite fear and restrict freedom.
reason: It discusses the domestic threat of terrorism linked to international events.
crisis_reason: The speaker explicitly identifies international terrorism as a threat to societal values, fear, and freedom, framing it as an urgent danger that requires active defense.

paragraph_text: The world has become so small, so fast, and so open. We can scarcely speak of our own country in isolation. When it comes down to it, we are part of a large, global community. Together, we must resolve human conflicts, address environmental challenges, and overcome financial crises. And together, we must find a way for different cultures to coexist peacefully, now that we are so closely connected. I believe that in Norway, we must continually re-evaluate what we mean by the word "integration." People should be different. However, I believe that a prerequisite for different cultures to thrive together is that we respect each other's differences, agree on fundamental human rights, and have the opportunity to talk to one another. Individual people should not be allowed to undermine the efforts of the vast majority who seek community. Individual people should not be allowed to restrict the lives of others. Individual people should not be allowed to dismantle what pioneers with great courage have fought to achieve. We stand firm on these principles. But if we can live by human rights, different cultures can coexist and enrich one another – even within Norwegian society.
paragraph_summary: The speaker discusses the necessity of global cooperation and the integration of diverse cultures within Norway based on mutual respect and fundamental human rights.
reason: It discusses the challenges of integrating different cultures within the context of a globalized world.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on the positive values of cooperation, integration, and mutual respect rather than framing these topics as urgent threats or dangers to the state.

paragraph_text: I hope that we can increasingly seek out what unites us as human beings. The actor Svein Tindberg had a great success this year with his performance, "Abraham's Children." In it, he draws inspiration from the three major world religions – Christianity, Judaism, and Islam – all of which have Abraham as their "ancestor." The play reminds us that these three religions share a common origin, and use many of the same stories about what it means to be human. About faith and doubt, about animosity and reconciliation, about the bright and dark forces that exist side by side within us. When two parties are entrenched in their positions, believing that they alone are right, conflict often arises. We find this kind of certainty in individuals within all religions and cultures. But within those same environments, we also find people with a completely different mindset – those who are able to meet differences with respect. I believe that what may appear on the surface to be a religious conflict often, at its core, is about a person's ability to tolerate differences.
paragraph_summary: The speaker advocates for religious tolerance and mutual respect by highlighting the shared human experiences and common origins found across different faiths.
reason: It uses international religious themes to discuss the universal human challenge of tolerance.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on promoting positive values like tolerance and mutual respect as a reflection on shared humanity, rather than framing religious differences as an urgent threat or a crisis.

paragraph_text: “Where are all the heroes going?” sings Jan Eggum. I believe I constantly meet everyday heroes. Some of them are honored with the King's Meritorious Service Medal and get to visit the Royal Palace. But the vast, vast majority are never mentioned on the front pages of newspapers and may not even receive much recognition. These are people who likely have their own worries and busy lives, just like everyone else. But they use their lives to realize what is written on Albert Schweitzer’s gravestone: “Seek out where you can invest your humanity! ” Our history is full of examples of individuals who were willing to get involved and make an effort for their fellow human beings. Next year, we will commemorate one of them: Bjørnstjerne Bjørnson. He dedicated his life to making a difference. “Good deeds save the world, ” Bjørnson said, and he followed through by becoming a passionate advocate for humanism and human dignity in Europe. Even today, Norwegians are leaving their mark around the world. Tonight, I want to commend all those who use their lives to help others, whether it's in their neighborhood or through humanitarian, diplomatic, or military missions around the globe.
paragraph_summary: The speaker commends the contributions of everyday heroes and humanitarian workers who dedicate their lives to helping others locally and globally.
reason: The paragraph connects local Norwegian everyday heroes with international humanitarian and diplomatic efforts.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on gratitude and commendation for humanitarian efforts rather than framing a specific threat or urgent danger that requires immediate action or sacrifice.

paragraph_text: This New Year's Eve is a difficult and challenging time for many, and it is especially hard for those who have lost loved ones. Now, it is important that each of us shows compassion and provides care and support to those who need it most. Right now, we must pause and take the time to hold each other close. From my own experience, I know how important thoughtfulness and care are during difficult times. Tonight, I send a greeting to you all, both here at home and abroad, and especially to everyone who is in some way involved in the tragic disaster, with a few words from our recently deceased cultural icon, Erik Bye: “The child dreams of tomorrow, Not of the years that have gone by. The world stands watch, alert and awake. Spin us a shield against the wolf and the goblin. Blessed are those among us Who are born for wakeful nights. You who govern, keep a hand Over all who do not sleep. ” Happy New Year!
paragraph_summary: The speaker offers condolences to those grieving after a recent disaster and calls for national compassion, care, and support during difficult times.
reason: The text addresses a global tragedy while offering comfort and support to the local Norwegian population.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on the aftermath of a disaster and the need for compassion and support, which frames the situation as a serious threat to the safety and well-being of the population.

paragraph_text: We are also making a major shift in immigration policy. The number of people choosing to come to Sweden is decreasing significantly, while migration is increasing in other parts of Europe. Reducing immigration is necessary if we are to have a chance of successfully integrating those who have already arrived.
paragraph_summary: The Swedish government is reducing immigration levels to improve the integration of individuals already residing in the country.
reason: The paragraph discusses domestic immigration policy in the context of broader European migration trends.
crisis_reason: The paragraph describes a policy adjustment as a routine governance measure to improve integration rather than framing immigration as an urgent threat or crisis.

paragraph_text: At the same time, we are significantly strengthening Sweden’s defense. We have just signed a historic defense agreement with the United States, and we are now rapidly increasing Sweden’s military capabilities. Thousands of young people are now completing their military service, just as two of my daughters did last year.
paragraph_summary: The Swedish head of state discusses the strengthening of national defense through a new agreement with the United States and increased military service participation.
reason: The paragraph links national defense strengthening and international military agreements to domestic security.
crisis_reason: The paragraph describes routine policy measures such as a new defense agreement and increased military participation as part of national governance, rather than framing the security situation as an urgent or active threat.

paragraph_text: Differences can be both stimulating and enriching. They encourage, challenge, and release resources. The same applies to other human relationships, whether in the family, at work, or in broader society. In a multicultural world, we must learn to appreciate each other's differences. We must be willing to challenge ourselves with new ideas and perspectives, and we must dare to think differently. Fear of the unknown often blocks exciting thoughts and visions. As long as we have a secure foundation, we can dare to explore new paths. We create our own future. Therefore, compassion, justice, and tolerance are essential and fundamental values. This applies both to our close relationships and to us as global citizens.
paragraph_summary: The speaker emphasizes the importance of embracing diversity, tolerance, and new perspectives to foster personal growth and a better global future.
reason: The paragraph discusses universal values of tolerance and compassion in both personal and global contexts.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on positive values like diversity and tolerance as a means for personal and societal growth, rather than framing these topics as an urgent threat or danger.

paragraph_text: With simple actions, we can contribute to influencing reality – if not for everyone, then at least for a few. Even if we cannot solve all the world’s problems at once, it is wonderful to do something! I commend those who, year after year, take on a voluntary commitment and use their free time to benefit others. We see this in children’s and youth work, in sports, and in cultural activities. It is also deeply gratifying to see individuals and organizations taking the initiative to collect money and clothing, sending fully loaded trucks across borders to those in need. There are countless ways to show solidarity and care, whether in our own communities or in other parts of the world. Ultimately, it is good to be able to bring joy to others.
paragraph_summary: The speaker commends individuals and organizations for their voluntary efforts and charitable initiatives that demonstrate solidarity and care for others.
reason: It discusses local volunteerism and community service that extends to helping people in other parts of the world.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on commending positive social actions and solidarity, which is an affirmative and celebratory framing rather than one identifying a threat or urgent danger.

paragraph_text: During the next six months, Norway will assume leadership of the international forces in Kosovo. For many years, Norwegian military personnel have demonstrated impressive efforts in peacekeeping operations. Our defense forces are undergoing a significant transformation, which we must expect can have major consequences for a number of local communities and for many of the Defense Force’s employees. It is my hope that the upcoming changes will ensure that our country continues to have a military defense that protects our national security, while also contributing to international peace and security. I thank the Defense Force personnel for their efforts, both at home and abroad, and wish them the best of luck in the challenges that lie ahead.
paragraph_summary: The speaker discusses Norway's leadership in Kosovo, the ongoing transformation of the national defense forces, and the impact of these changes on personnel.
reason: It links Norway's international military commitments in Kosovo to domestic defense transformation and local community impacts.
crisis_reason: The paragraph describes routine military transformation and leadership roles as part of standard governance and organizational development, rather than framing these as responses to an urgent threat or emergency.

paragraph_text: Today, we do not feel the same sense of enthusiastic optimism that prevailed a century ago. Many no longer believe in automatic progress, having witnessed that science and technology can be used for both good and evil. Bloody conflicts continue to rage. Hardship and uncertainty mark the daily lives of many people around the world. Nevertheless, efforts to promote human rights and democracy have gained greater support than ever before, and environmental issues are now a global responsibility. However, we have fallen far short in terms of binding cooperation and solidarity across national borders. This is where we, both as a nation and as individuals, have a responsibility. We must all, within our abilities, contribute to finding solutions to the problems we carry with us across the millennium, however great or seemingly insurmountable they may be.
paragraph_summary: The speaker addresses global challenges, including conflict and environmental issues, while emphasizing the collective responsibility to foster international cooperation and human rights.
reason: It addresses global challenges like human rights and democracy while emphasizing the shared responsibility of the nation and individuals.
crisis_reason: The paragraph frames global conflict and environmental issues as challenges requiring collective responsibility and action, which constitutes a threat-focused framing of urgent global problems.

paragraph_text: More than anything, it is technological development that distinguishes our century from previous eras. With the help of new knowledge, we can travel to the moon, pump oil from the sea, and give a person a new heart. Technology also allows us to bring people together across continents – in ways that previous generations would have considered impossible. Human intelligence, curiosity, and creativity have given us the car, the airplane, the telephone, the radio, and the television. Travel patterns and working lives have taken on new forms. Most notably, the new information technology has revolutionized our daily lives. Future generations may view the technology of our time as outdated and incomplete. Each new generation of researchers builds upon the knowledge passed down to them. It is important to remain open to the opportunities – and the dangers – that this represents.
paragraph_summary: The speech discusses the transformative impact of technological development on human life, society, and the importance of navigating its associated opportunities and risks.
reason: Technological development is discussed as a global phenomenon that has fundamentally changed daily life in Norway.
crisis_reason: The paragraph frames technological development as a broad transformative force with both opportunities and risks, rather than presenting a specific, urgent threat that demands immediate crisis-level intervention or sacrifice.

paragraph_text: The spoken word prevails. Good evening. We live in a time of great change. We had barely put the pandemic behind us when Putin sent troops into Ukraine. There is once again war in Europe. An energy crisis. Sky-high inflation. Rising interest rates. And many are struggling. We are entering a year of economic uncertainty, and unfortunately, also with a risk that unemployment will rise. When the clouds gathering above us are darker than we would like, we must stand together and find strength and hope in community.
paragraph_summary: The speaker addresses the challenges of the war in Ukraine, the energy crisis, inflation, and economic uncertainty while calling for national unity.
reason: The paragraph links international conflicts and global economic crises to domestic struggles.
crisis_reason: The speaker explicitly identifies the war, energy shortages, and inflation as challenges and sources of economic uncertainty, framing them as urgent threats that require national unity to overcome.

paragraph_text: Not far from here, Ukrainians are now fighting for the eleventh month to defend their country against a Russia whose cruelty we find difficult to comprehend. Families in a freezing winter, without heat, without electricity, without clean drinking water. Doctors operating the wounded by the light of a mobile phone. It is unbearable. But also a people whose courage and will have surprised everyone. And a Europe, a West, whose unity – also with other good allies – caught Putin off guard. There is light in the darkness. Denmark continues our steadfast and loyal support for Ukraine. At the same time, we must prepare for the fact that we, too, face increased threats here at home. In Europe, we tend to imagine that the rest of the world thinks and acts as we do. For many years, we believed that trade and growth would almost automatically lead to peaceful coexistence. We disarmed while others armed themselves. And in many areas, we have become too dependent on others. Now we see that we were too naive. That we stand on the threshold of a new era, one that will be difficult. It was a moment of pride for Denmark when we last year wholeheartedly joined the European cooperation on defense and security. Europe must become stronger on its own. And Denmark must contribute more to NATO. We must increase our investments in our defense and security, so that by 2030, we reach the 2 percent that is needed and that we have promised our allies.
model_reasons: freezing: refers to winter weather conditions, not climate change or environmental policy
paragraph_summary: The Prime Minister addresses the war in Ukraine, the necessity of European unity, and the requirement for increased Danish defense spending and NATO contributions.
reason: The paragraph discusses the war in Ukraine and its implications for Danish defense and NATO commitments.
crisis_reason: The paragraph frames the war in Ukraine as an active threat to security, necessitating urgent responses such as increased defense spending and NATO contributions.

paragraph_text: * * * We are facing major decisions and difficult compromises. We are a small country, but that shouldn't prevent us from thinking big. On the world stage, we will uphold our responsibility for peace, security, and a green future. At home, we will create a welfare system with more care and dignity. Education that provides freedom and opportunities for all children and young people. Let us meet adversity with foresight. Seriousness with hope. And let us remember, in our hearts, even in uncertain times – especially in uncertain times – that the world is a beautiful place. Happy New Year!
paragraph_summary: The speaker outlines national priorities including global responsibility for peace and climate, alongside domestic improvements to welfare and education systems.
reason: The speaker balances international responsibilities with domestic welfare and education goals.
crisis_reason: The paragraph describes national priorities and improvements as routine governance and positive developments rather than framing them as urgent threats or dangers.

paragraph_text: On July 10th, we will celebrate the 100th anniversary of Southern Jutland's reunification with Denmark. Reunification Day became a landmark day, not just for the people of Southern Jutland, but a day that involved the entire Danish population. Thousands of people from across the country flocked to witness King Christian X ride across the border on a white horse. Perhaps many today feel that it feels distant, but ask the people of Southern Jutland: they know that a new chapter began in Denmark’s history that day. We can certainly celebrate this anniversary, and we will. Last summer, I visited Schleswig-Holstein, where I received a warm and festive welcome, which helped to reaffirm the strong neighborly relationship and good friendship between Germany and Denmark. It was also a truly memorable meeting with the Danish minority. You did not return to Denmark 100 years ago. But Denmark is and remains your homeland. It moved me to hear that, even from the younger generation. I will never forget that! Both the Danish and German minorities have contributed to the development of the border region over the years, creating a remarkably harmonious and peaceful environment. It has become a model for the world. I send my warmest New Year wishes to everyone in the border region.
model_reasons: environment: refers to the social and political atmosphere of the border region
paragraph_summary: The speaker commemorates the 100th anniversary of Southern Jutland's reunification with Denmark and highlights the peaceful, cooperative relationship between Denmark and Germany.
reason: The text discusses a national anniversary while highlighting the positive cross-border relationship with Germany.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on a historical anniversary and the celebration of a peaceful, cooperative relationship, which are affirmative and ceremonial themes rather than threats.

paragraph_text: Our Danish soldiers, serving in distant lands, contribute to freedom and stability. We must remember that this evening, when they are particularly missed at home. I send my warmest greetings and best wishes for the New Year to all those serving abroad, and to their families. It’s not just abroad that people are on duty. My New Year greetings go to all those in the armed forces, emergency services, police, healthcare system, and all others who are working tonight and throughout the night. Men and women who are all doing their duty, while others can take time off and celebrate the New Year safely and securely.
paragraph_summary: The speaker expresses gratitude and New Year greetings to Danish military personnel serving abroad and essential workers on duty during the holiday.
reason: It addresses both Danish soldiers serving abroad and domestic emergency/healthcare workers, bridging international and national service.
crisis_reason: The paragraph is framed as a ceremonial expression of gratitude and holiday greetings, which is explicitly excluded from the definition of a crisis.

paragraph_text: Wherever you turn, you find Danes. Last spring, I visited Argentina, where many people live who have roots in Denmark and who maintain that heritage. To them, and to all others with Danish roots, wherever they may be, I send my New Year wishes, with gratitude for their loyalty to the old country.
paragraph_summary: The speaker extends New Year greetings and expresses gratitude to Danish expatriates and individuals of Danish descent living abroad.
reason: It discusses the Danish diaspora in Argentina, connecting international citizens to their national heritage.
crisis_reason: the paragraph is a ceremonial holiday greeting and expression of gratitude, which does not contain any language framing a threat or urgent problem.

paragraph_text: Tonight, my thoughts also turn to the Danish soldiers, police officers, and specialists who are deployed abroad. Although the numbers are not as high this year, each and every one of them is important, wherever they are – in countries such as Iraq, Afghanistan, Kosovo, and the Baltic states. They are missed at home. We are thinking of them and wish them a happy New Year. I also wish a happy New Year to all Danes who are living abroad. Whether they are away for a short time or have settled there, perhaps permanently, they contribute to shaping Denmark.
paragraph_summary: The speaker expresses gratitude and well wishes to Danish personnel deployed abroad and citizens living in other countries.
reason: It addresses both international military deployments and Danish citizens living abroad.
crisis_reason: the paragraph is purely ceremonial, focusing on gratitude and well wishes for citizens and personnel abroad without invoking any threat or urgent danger.

paragraph_text: A special New Year's greeting goes to the Danish minority in South Schleswig. Here, Danish culture and language coexist peacefully with the majority German population. In a world where so many border regions are characterized by conflict and entrenched positions, this region is a beautiful example that both Danish and German institutions and organizations can be proud of.
paragraph_summary: The speaker commends the peaceful coexistence of the Danish minority and German majority in South Schleswig as a positive example of regional harmony.
reason: It discusses a border region and the peaceful coexistence of a minority group, touching on international relations.
crisis_reason: The paragraph describes a state of peaceful coexistence and regional harmony, which is framed as a positive achievement rather than a threat or urgent problem.

paragraph_text: A year is drawing to a close; tomorrow, a new one begins. We cannot leave 2015 without reflecting on what the year has brought. In January, we witnessed the shocking attack on the French satirical magazine in Paris, and just a month later, we experienced the double attack at the Krudttønden and at the synagogue in Krystalgade in Copenhagen. Two Danes lost their lives, and we were all left reeling with shock and horror – terror had reached us. My thoughts are with the families of both victims. We all felt deeply affected, as demonstrated by the immense outpouring of support in the days and weeks following those tragic events. But that was not to be the end of it. Just a few weeks ago, terror struck Paris again, resulting in many deaths and injuries. Not only Europe, but the entire world has shown its solidarity with France. Terror is not a European phenomenon; it affects innocent people everywhere, people simply going about their daily lives. We too feel affected, and we must understand and recognize that no one is an island; we are all part of a whole. Our reaction must not become paralyzing fear. We must adapt to the reality we face, and we must be vigilant. But we must live our lives with hope and optimism – yes, with good cheer, because fear weakens us.
paragraph_summary: The speech reflects on the 2015 terrorist attacks in Copenhagen and Paris, urging citizens to remain vigilant while rejecting fear in favor of optimism.
reason: It discusses the impact of international terrorism on domestic security and national sentiment.
crisis_reason: The paragraph invokes the threat of terrorist attacks and explicitly calls for vigilance, framing the situation as an ongoing security danger that requires a specific response from the citizenry.

paragraph_text: The world has always known war, hardship, and terror; we have been able to witness it every day in the newspapers and on television: harrowing images of the misfortune of others. Yet, until this year, it has only affected us in a somewhat detached way, until we saw desperate people and entire families seeking refuge in Europe, and coming to our country in greater numbers than ever before. Many refugees have sought protection in Denmark, and it is our responsibility to help. It is a significant task, but also a difficult dilemma: how many can we help, and how can we best provide that help? I hope that the new year will be a good year for all those who are now finding their place in Denmark. Tonight, I would also like to extend my greetings to all those – both authorities, organizations, and individuals – who are making a significant and valuable effort to care for the people who are coming here, and who are taking on the responsibilities that entails.
paragraph_summary: The speaker addresses the arrival of refugees in Denmark, emphasizing the responsibility to provide aid while acknowledging the challenges of managing the influx.
reason: It addresses the international refugee crisis and the domestic responsibility to provide aid.
crisis_reason: The paragraph acknowledges the challenges of managing the influx of refugees, but frames the situation as a responsibility to provide aid rather than an urgent threat to national security, social cohesion, or the integrity of the state.

paragraph_text: The year that has passed has presented significant challenges, particularly for the Police. I would like to thank them for their efforts; it has not been without cost, both for the individual and for the entire force. I wish them a happy new year, with a warm thank you for all that they have done. I would also like to extend my new year greetings to all Danish personnel serving abroad, who throughout the year have tackled dangerous assignments around the world. They have all performed a distinguished service on behalf of Denmark, and I would like to send them a special thank you. Specialists from the Emergency Services assisted in Nepal after the devastating earthquakes. In West Africa, where the Ebola epidemic has claimed so many lives, Danish doctors and nurses have provided courageous assistance, enabling the affected countries to now manage the terrible disease. Denmark is aware of its international responsibility, and therefore, the Defence Forces have also been actively involved in several places around the world where urgent action is needed. Everywhere, we hear words of praise for the efforts of the Danish forces. This applies to both the Army, Navy, and Air Force. We can be proud of that. I would like to thank everyone who has been deployed. For everyone, both those at home and those currently abroad, I wish a happy new year. Tonight, I would also like to extend my greetings and thoughts to all Danes, wherever they may be in the world. Thank you for their efforts, and I wish them and their work a happy new year. I would also like to extend my greetings to the Danish South Schleswigers. Just as the German minority north of the border, they contribute to making our border region a model for other border regions.
paragraph_summary: The speaker expresses gratitude to Danish police, military personnel, and emergency responders for their domestic and international service throughout the past year.
reason: It discusses domestic police and personnel alongside international deployments and humanitarian aid, blending national and global responsibilities.
crisis_reason: The paragraph is framed as an expression of gratitude for service rendered, which is explicitly categorized as a non-crisis activity in the instructions.

paragraph_text: In times of crisis, difficulties can seem overwhelming, and obstacles can feel like insurmountable barriers. Therefore, it can be difficult to look forward to tomorrow, especially if one feels excluded, while the wheels keep turning, and one is without work or fears losing their job. Our society is making a great effort to address these problems. However, we must be careful not to simply leave it to society to solve everything. We must always start with ourselves, with our loved ones, and with those we meet along the way. Each individual can make an immeasurable difference with an encouraging word, a helping hand, or a considerate respect for others. It has always been our strength here in Denmark that we know each other so well, and that even the geographical distances are small. The crisis that affects the global community in these years, and which we also feel here, should call upon all our ingenuity and drive, for the benefit of each individual and for our entire society: for our country's future.
paragraph_summary: The speaker encourages individual responsibility and community support to overcome economic challenges and societal crises in Denmark.
reason: It connects the impact of the global economic crisis to the domestic social response and individual responsibility.
crisis_reason: The speaker explicitly frames the situation as 'societal crises' and 'economic challenges' that require urgent individual and community action, indicating an active threat to social stability.

paragraph_text: To everyone who celebrates New Year far from Denmark, I send my greetings and best wishes. In particular, I would like to send my greetings to our soldiers and other personnel serving in dangerous locations. They are making a great and courageous effort, bringing honor to Denmark. I wish them and their families a happy New Year. May we all bring them safely home again. Also, my thoughts are with our veterans and their families this evening. For some of them, their service is not a closed chapter, as both they and their families must cope with the consequences of what they have been through. May the new year bring them renewed strength, and may we all work to ensure their future as well. A society like ours could not function if there weren't people, even on New Year's Eve, who remain on duty to ensure our safety. I wish each of them a happy New Year and thank them for their service.
paragraph_summary: The speaker expresses gratitude and well wishes to Danish soldiers, personnel serving abroad, and veterans for their service and sacrifices.
reason: It addresses both international military deployments and domestic safety/veteran support.
crisis_reason: The paragraph is framed as an expression of gratitude and well wishes, which falls under the category of ceremonial or routine acknowledgments rather than the framing of an active threat.

paragraph_text: Although we all may find much to be grateful for in the past year, it has nonetheless been a year marked by the economic crisis that has affected the entire world, and consequently, Danish society as well. It is concerning, as many have lost their jobs, businesses are earning less as customers have less to spend, and the global scope of the crisis means that companies are experiencing reduced revenue both domestically and internationally. Nevertheless, Denmark is among the nations that have been affected less severely by the crisis. Throughout the crisis, it has helped us that we, here in Denmark, have had wealth and savings to draw upon.
paragraph_summary: The speech addresses the impact of the global economic crisis on Danish businesses and employment, noting Denmark's relative resilience due to national savings.
reason: It links the global economic crisis to its specific consequences for the Danish economy.
crisis_reason: The paragraph explicitly identifies the situation as a 'global economic crisis' and frames it as a threat to businesses and employment, which constitutes a crisis framing.

paragraph_text: On this last evening of the year, when so many gather with family and friends, let us not forget those who must celebrate New Year's far from home. I am particularly thinking of our soldiers in Afghanistan. They are undertaking a task that is extremely difficult and dangerous, but they are doing so with purpose and responsibility, and with great courage. Both they and their families here at home should be in our thoughts this evening. It can be difficult to sit at home while your loved one is far away in dangerous circumstances. From the Crown Prince, who visited our soldiers shortly before Christmas, I know that they approach their tasks with both seriousness and confidence, and that they are aware that they can handle them. Nevertheless, our efforts in Afghanistan come at a high price: several young Danes have lost their lives this year as well. Let us remember them this evening and think of their families, their friends, and their comrades, who are now left with a sense of loss. Some of our soldiers have returned home seriously wounded, and others are psychologically marked by their experiences. For all of them, it is essential that they receive all our care and recognition. Last autumn, I had the opportunity to meet some of our wounded soldiers during their rehabilitation at the Rigshospitalet (National Hospital). They all demonstrate a remarkable will to overcome their disabilities, which made a profound impression on me and can only inspire admiration. I extend my New Year's greetings to all Danish men and women who work to ensure our safety and security within the Defence Forces, Emergency Services, and Police.
paragraph_summary: The speaker honors Danish soldiers serving in Afghanistan, acknowledging their sacrifices, the loss of life, and the rehabilitation of wounded personnel.
reason: Discusses military deployment abroad and its impact on domestic families and national security.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on honoring past sacrifices and the ongoing rehabilitation of personnel, which frames the content as a reflection on past events and recovery rather than an active, urgent threat requiring immediate response.

paragraph_text: In the debate about the government's reform proposals, businesses have called for it to be easier to recruit workers from abroad. We are willing to discuss that. However, we must do so wisely. Many years ago, we decided that we would not have free immigration to Denmark. We want to uphold that decision. Therefore, we should only recruit workers where there is a shortage. It should be for a limited period, and of course, it must be done properly. We must also strengthen the rights of Danish workers and make a joint effort to get those still outside the labor market into work. That is the Danish social contract. Denmark must be built on fairness and responsibility. Many of you who have come to Denmark over the years have embraced those values. You often take on some of the hardest jobs. You work tirelessly. Now, we need to bring more people along. Therefore, the government has proposed that we replace passive welfare assistance with a clear focus on employment for citizens who need integration support. The best thing is to get a job. If that is not possible, you should contribute in other ways in return for your benefits. And to those – especially women from non-Western backgrounds – who have not found work, even though they have been in Denmark for many years: We need you. We will target our proposals for a 37-hour work requirement to areas where there is a real shortage of employees, particularly in elderly care. I know that it is not possible to simply start working alongside colleagues with education and experience. But you can do other things. Make sandwiches. Wash clothes. Do extra cleaning. You start by contributing to the care home in return for your benefits. As soon as you are ready for education or a job, you should earn your own money. That is good for equality and integration. And when parents are working, that is the best protection against child poverty.
paragraph_summary: The government proposes addressing labor shortages through targeted foreign recruitment and a mandatory 37-hour work requirement for welfare recipients to promote integration and employment.
reason: Addresses immigration policy and labor market integration, which involves international movement and domestic social contracts.
crisis_reason: The paragraph presents labor shortages and welfare integration as routine policy proposals for economic management rather than framing them as an urgent threat to the state or social order.

paragraph_text: It is the last evening of the year. Following Christmas, with its sparkling lights and warm tones, its red hearts and deep green fir branches, a time when we traditionally gather with family and those closest to us, comes New Year's Eve. It bursts upon us with great fanfare, although some also prefer to celebrate it quietly this evening. For tonight, we bid farewell to the old year and welcome the new. It prompts reflection. The new and unknown can feel both alluring and daunting. We experienced it that way as children, but, hand on heart, it can still feel that way. Particularly this year, many may be concerned about what the new year will bring. Everywhere, serious discussions and writings address the global economy, which is showing signs of weakness, and we may fear that we will feel the effects in our own daily lives. Many will naturally be worried about their jobs and whether it will become more difficult to manage their families' finances. For a long time, we have grown accustomed to things getting better and better, and the future seemed increasingly bright. Now, the picture is painted with darker colors; but let us nevertheless look up and outward, and put our concerns into perspective.
paragraph_summary: The speaker addresses public anxiety regarding the weakening global economy and potential impacts on personal financial stability and employment in the coming year.
reason: Connects global economic concerns to their potential impact on domestic daily life.
crisis_reason: The speaker frames the weakening global economy as a source of public anxiety and a direct threat to personal financial stability and employment, treating it as an urgent concern for the coming year.

paragraph_text: This year, too, there are many families who, on this evening, must miss being together with a loved one, because he or she is far from home and from Denmark. I am thinking particularly of our soldiers, many of whom are stationed around the world, but especially those deployed to Afghanistan. There, they perform their duties with skill and courage, but consistently, despite all the dangers, with a strong sense of responsibility and a good sense of humor. They all deserve our appreciation and our thanks for what they do. It was a pleasure for the Crown Prince to visit the Danish soldiers in Helmand province and personally gain insight into their lives there. When we think of them this evening, let us also remember those who have made sacrifices and think of those left behind, who are dealing with grief and longing. This evening, my New Year's greetings also extend to the entire Armed Forces, the Home Guard, and the Emergency Services, thanking them for their daily efforts to ensure our safety and security here in Denmark. It takes a price to uphold Denmark's commitment to expressing viewpoints and defending ideals rooted in our culture and background. Those working for Denmark abroad feel that. It became clear this year when the Danish embassy and a hotel in Islamabad, Pakistan, were hit by a terrorist bombing. My thoughts and condolences go to those left behind and to those who were injured.
paragraph_summary: The speech honors Danish soldiers deployed abroad, acknowledges their sacrifices, and expresses condolences for victims of the terrorist attack in Islamabad.
reason: Discusses military deployment abroad and the domestic impact of international terrorism.
crisis_reason: The paragraph acknowledges a terrorist attack, which is an acute threat to safety and security, and frames the soldiers' deployments in the context of responding to such threats.

paragraph_text: It is always a pleasure for me to extend my greetings to the Danes in South Schleswig, and particularly this year, following my recent and wonderful experience of attending the inauguration of the new Danish gymnasium in Schleswig. It was a confirmation of the beautiful way in which the people of South Schleswig continue to uphold their Danish heritage, while also being respected citizens of their country. This year, too, my thoughts are with the Faroe Islands, and I remember the many good meetings my family and I have had with the Faroese people over the years. I wish everyone a happy New Year. This autumn, Greenland made a major decision with the referendum on November 25th. I look forward to being in Greenland on National Day, June 21st, when the new framework for relations between Denmark and Greenland will take effect. I look forward to a continued close and trusting partnership between Denmark and Greenland. I wish the Greenlandic community and every household a happy New Year.
paragraph_summary: The monarch extends New Year greetings to the Danish minority in South Schleswig, the Faroe Islands, and Greenland, while noting upcoming changes in Greenlandic relations.
reason: Discusses relations with territories and neighboring regions, blending domestic and international political ties.
crisis_reason: The paragraph is ceremonial in nature, focusing on holiday greetings and routine administrative updates regarding relations, without framing these as threats or urgent dangers.

paragraph_text: Every nation, every people, is shaped by its culture and its history. That imprint accompanies every individual, whether they remain at home or venture out into the world, perhaps to settle in a completely new place. There, in that new location, perhaps in a completely different part of the world, the newcomer will encounter a new country and a different society, shaped by that country's location, its history, its culture, and its customs. The country and the people who receive the newcomers will also have entirely new experiences to process. We have come to realize that here in Denmark as well. While we have always known that the world is vast, it is when the world comes to us that we truly understand its diversity and how different customs and ways of life can be.
paragraph_summary: The speaker reflects on the cultural and societal impacts of immigration and the encounter with global diversity within Denmark.
reason: It discusses the integration of newcomers and the interaction between different cultures within a domestic context.
crisis_reason: The summary describes a reflection on cultural and societal impacts without using language that frames these developments as an urgent threat, danger, or instability requiring immediate intervention.

paragraph_text: Settling in a new place is challenging; it requires considerable effort to learn about the new environment, to master the language, and to adapt to the rhythm of the year and the pace of daily life. New boundaries are established for one's existence, and it will also become apparent that there are habits and customs that must be changed, or even completely abandoned. No one should expect those who come to a new place, a foreign land, to immediately discard everything they have inherited, as if it were superfluous. That would easily lead to a sense of rootlessness. If a tree is transplanted, it needs a good root system to begin with, so that it can draw nourishment in its new location and grow and thrive.
model_reasons: environment: refers to the social and cultural setting of a new place
paragraph_summary: The speech addresses the challenges of integration for immigrants and the importance of balancing cultural adaptation with the preservation of one's personal heritage.
reason: It addresses the challenges of immigrants adapting to a new society, which is a transnational issue with local implications.
crisis_reason: The summary describes integration as a challenge to be balanced rather than an urgent threat or danger to social cohesion, framing it as a standard policy or social topic.

paragraph_text: Every year, on New Year's Eve, our thoughts turn to those who may be away from home on this evening. Are they completely alone, or are they perhaps surrounded by friends who would like to hear how things are done in Denmark? Or are they together with fellow countrymen, celebrating in a completely foreign land? Tonight, we send a warm greeting to all Danes, wherever they may be: to those south of our borders, as well as to those who have ventured out to settle in foreign lands, for shorter or longer periods. A special greeting goes to those who may be celebrating New Year at sea. Our thoughts are particularly with those aboard the "Vædderen" on the Galathea expedition; they are helping to carry a fresh greeting from Denmark around the world.
paragraph_summary: The speaker extends New Year greetings to Danish citizens living abroad, traveling, or serving on the Galathea expedition.
reason: It addresses Danish citizens living abroad and international expeditions, bridging domestic and foreign contexts.
crisis_reason: The paragraph is a ceremonial greeting to citizens, which is explicitly excluded from the definition of a crisis.

paragraph_text: Tonight, we must also think of those who are working far from Denmark, in public service or with humanitarian organizations. They have undertaken difficult and often dangerous tasks, whether of a humanitarian or military nature. We deeply respect their efforts, which they perform with courage and skill, fully aware of the responsibility they carry. We send them each and every one our best wishes for a happy New Year. We know that it may involve risks to their lives and health, and it deeply affects us when we realize that someone has paid the ultimate price. Tonight, our thoughts are with each and every one who has lost a husband, a father, a son or brother, or a close friend.
paragraph_summary: The speaker honors Danish citizens serving abroad in humanitarian or military roles and expresses condolences to those who have lost loved ones in service.
reason: It discusses Danish personnel serving in international humanitarian or military missions.
crisis_reason: The paragraph is ceremonial and focused on expressing gratitude and condolences, which are explicitly excluded from the definition of a crisis.

paragraph_text: Last time we celebrated New Year's, it was under the shadow of the devastating tsunami in Southeast Asia. It deeply affected us, as we were still, in those days, fearing that the number of Danish casualties would be high. After many anxious weeks, it turned out that the losses were, thankfully, not as severe as we initially feared; but for every family that was affected, the loss remains immense, and in the countries ravaged by the tsunami, it will take a long time before life returns to normal. This autumn, we once again witnessed natural disasters of a scale that is difficult for us to comprehend, as northeastern Pakistan was struck by an earthquake in October, which destroyed lives and property and left thousands more homeless. But even a technologically advanced society cannot feel safe from the forces of nature. We had to acknowledge that when a series of tropical storms swept across Central America and the southern United States, turning even a major city like New Orleans into a disaster zone. Every time, the Danish response has been spontaneous and generous. While we were quick and generous in assisting those where our own countrymen were among the victims, it is a joy to see how the aid to Pakistan is now truly gaining momentum. But tonight, we will first and foremost gather to thank everyone from Denmark who has personally participated in the relief efforts. Under very difficult conditions, they have repeatedly stepped forward to contribute in every way they can. This includes the Rescue Services, the Armed Forces, and the Police, the Red Cross, and other humanitarian organizations, both large and small. Along with my New Year's wishes, I would like to express a heartfelt thank you for their efforts. They bring honor to Denmark.
model_actions: Dropped count of 'tsunami' | Dropped count of 'natural disasters' | Dropped count of 'disasters' | Dropped count of 'earthquake' | Dropped count of 'tropical storms' | Dropped count of 'storms'
model_reasons: tsunami: geological event, not framed as climate change or environmental policy | natural disasters: refers to geological and weather events without explicit climate change framing | disasters: general reference to humanitarian crises and property destruction | earthquake: geological event unrelated to climate change or environmental protection | tropical storms: weather events described as natural forces, not linked to climate change | storms: weather events described as natural forces, not linked to climate change
paragraph_summary: The speaker acknowledges international natural disasters from the past year and expresses gratitude to Danish relief workers and organizations for their humanitarian efforts.
reason: It discusses the Danish response to international natural disasters and the role of Danish relief workers abroad.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on gratitude for humanitarian efforts and past events rather than framing the disasters as an active, ongoing threat requiring urgent response or vigilance.

paragraph_text: However, it is not only the forces of nature that have ravaged in 2005. Blind terror has also contributed to casting dark shadows over societies throughout the year. Sometimes, it felt so close, as it did when London was struck in the middle of a busy day that resembled our own. Acts of violence and suicide bombings like these are far removed from our understanding here in Denmark. Nevertheless, we must acknowledge that extreme views and actions can seem tempting to young people, and that sacrificing oneself for a cause can appear heroic. But sacrificing others out of one's own anger or despair is cruel and cynical; it does not make anyone a hero. We do not wish that any young person growing up here in Denmark would be tempted by such a fate. Danish society is open and democratic; it is based on respect for each other as human beings and on respect for each other's opinions. It is typical of us Danes that we do not like conflict; we prefer that a discussion should end with everyone agreeing. We find sharply defined viewpoints and categorical demands, and dramatic statements difficult to take seriously, and we often react by either laughing or becoming offended. But it is important that we both listen to each other and can clearly express our viewpoints, while also stating when we agree and when we disagree. This is how we have built the society that we are proud of, and one that is admired and, perhaps, even a little envied in other parts of the world. It is precisely this society that we want all future generations to feel a part of, so that it feels natural for every young man or woman to hold onto it and defend it in their daily lives.
paragraph_summary: The speaker addresses the threat of global terrorism and emphasizes the importance of preserving Danish democratic values and open dialogue for future generations.
reason: It links international terrorism to domestic social values and the preservation of Danish democracy.
crisis_reason: The speaker explicitly identifies global terrorism as a threat and frames the preservation of democratic values as an urgent necessity for future generations, indicating a focus on an active security threat.

paragraph_text: These years, the Danish Armed Forces are also undergoing major changes, affecting individuals both in their service and in their private lives. At the same time, the work of the tasks assigned to the Armed Forces continues. The year has been marked by the significant efforts being made internationally, and it is clear that there is great respect for the Danish Armed Forces and its personnel. We have all seen that it can take its toll, and my thoughts are with those who have been affected so suddenly and severely, and with the families who must now cope with grief and loss. I send my New Year's greetings to everyone within the Armed Forces, to their families, and to their loved ones. May the new year be a good year for the Danish Armed Forces as well.
paragraph_summary: The speaker acknowledges the ongoing transformation of the Danish Armed Forces, honors the sacrifices of personnel, and offers condolences to families affected by loss.
reason: It addresses the international deployment of the Danish Armed Forces and its impact on domestic families.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on honoring personnel and offering condolences for past losses, which frames the content as a reflection on service and sacrifice rather than an active, urgent threat requiring immediate response.

paragraph_text: My thoughts and wishes go to all Danes who may be celebrating New Year's far from home, and to all those who may be missing a family member on this very evening. My greetings also go to the many who have moved abroad to settle down, either for a shorter or longer period, and to the Danes living south of the border, who so faithfully maintain their Danish heritage, while also being respected citizens in the country where they live. I wish them all a happy New Year.
paragraph_summary: The speaker extends New Year's greetings to Danes living abroad, including those serving away from home and those maintaining their heritage in other countries.
reason: It sends greetings to Danish citizens living abroad, maintaining ties between the nation and its diaspora.
crisis_reason: The paragraph is a ceremonial greeting directed at citizens living abroad, which falls under the category of holiday greetings and does not invoke any threat or urgent situation.

paragraph_text: Much has happened since the last New Year's Eve, both in the world at large, in Denmark, and for each of us individually. More than ever, we feel that events happening around the world affect us all, and that we don't live in isolation here in this country. In the past year, many events have stirred strong emotions. The war in Iraq, in which Denmark also participated, questions on the national agenda, and events from our more distant or recent past have sparked discussions, some of which have been quite heated.
paragraph_summary: The speaker reflects on how global events, including the Iraq War and domestic debates, have impacted Denmark and stirred public emotion over the past year.
reason: It discusses how international events like the Iraq war impact domestic national discourse.
crisis_reason: The paragraph reflects on past events and public sentiment rather than framing them as an active, ongoing threat requiring urgent response or vigilance.

paragraph_text: Denmark's international engagement means that many Danes are serving in conflict zones or where disasters have struck. This includes the military and police, as well as those involved in humanitarian aid. I send them all a warm New Year's greeting, with thanks for their efforts and their willingness to sacrifice. They are highly respected everywhere, and we here at home can be proud of them.
model_reasons: disasters: refers to humanitarian or conflict-related crises, not environmental or climate disasters
paragraph_summary: The speaker expresses gratitude and pride toward Danish military, police, and humanitarian personnel serving in international conflict zones and disaster areas.
reason: It discusses the domestic pride and support for Danish personnel serving in international conflict zones.
crisis_reason: The paragraph is focused on expressing gratitude and pride for personnel, which is a ceremonial and affirmative framing rather than a focus on an active threat or crisis.

paragraph_text: Throughout nearly every corner of the world, there are Danes or people of Danish descent. In their daily lives, they are busy with what surrounds them, but at Christmas and New Year's time, their thoughts may turn to Denmark, the country they left behind, or the country with which they feel a deep connection. They should know that we here at home are thinking of them, and I wish them a happy New Year. The same applies to those Danes who live just south of the border. Deeply connected to Denmark and to the region they inhabit, they are held in high regard, and I send them my warmest greetings.
paragraph_summary: The speaker extends New Year's greetings to Danes living abroad and those residing in the border region south of Denmark.
reason: It addresses the connection between the Danish state and its citizens living abroad.
crisis_reason: The paragraph is a ceremonial greeting to citizens living abroad and in border regions, which falls under the category of holiday greetings and routine expressions of connection rather than a threat-based framing.

paragraph_text: It’s New Year’s Eve – again. It all seems to pass by so quickly, especially as we get older. Time certainly flies, particularly for those who are busy, and there are many people who are busy right now. This applies to both younger and older generations. Information, suggestions, and data can be accessed with the press of a few buttons. There’s an impressive display of dexterity as people navigate the possibilities of the information age, seemingly comfortable with it; yet, it still creates a stressful daily life for many. We haven’t quite fully adjusted. News from all over the world pours in, demanding that we respond to everything that happens. Today’s major challenges are pressing: international terrorism, the situation in the Middle East, chaotic conditions in Africa and elsewhere, looming famine, and man-made environmental problems. It can quickly become overwhelming, and one is tempted to shut out the constant stream of impressions. But the major questions of our time don’t simply disappear by pulling the covers over your head and ignoring them. After all, these are issues that concern us all, both as a nation and as individuals. What is being negotiated on the international stage today will impact our daily lives tomorrow. We are all, each of us, a part of a larger whole, just as Denmark is a part not only of Europe, but of the entire global community. An active Danish effort in international cooperation requires commitment from all of us. We must each individually keep abreast of what is happening in the world around us. This also applies to the year that is now beginning, with its new challenges and new opportunities. A new beginning, allowing us to move forward with renewed hope: It’s New Year’s Eve – once again.
paragraph_summary: The speaker discusses the challenges of the information age and the necessity for Danes to remain engaged with pressing global issues and international cooperation.
reason: It links global challenges like terrorism and environmental issues to their impact on the Danish nation and individuals.
crisis_reason: The paragraph frames the information age and global engagement as a matter of civic duty and routine international cooperation rather than an urgent threat or danger requiring emergency action.

paragraph_text: As chair of the European Union, Denmark had the responsibility to organize and oversee the final negotiations on the EU’s expansion. This has placed significant demands on our abilities, both diplomatic and organizational. Many individuals have been involved in this work, both in prominent and less visible roles. Denmark’s recognition is largely due to their efforts. I would like to express my sincere gratitude to them all. This year, borders are truly opening up across Europe, and more people are travelling than ever before. Even destinations far from our continent are now accessible, not just for the adventurous few. People say the world has become smaller; this also means that a greater diversity of personal connections are being created, especially among young people. This inevitably influences our perspective on what is happening around the world. What was once considered foreign now feels relevant in a different way when we have the opportunity to learn about the conditions firsthand. However, it also means that the wider world, with its threats and dangers, is drawing closer, so close that we ourselves are affected. This was a painful reality for some Danish families when three young Danish women were among the victims of the terrorist attack in Bali.
paragraph_summary: The speaker discusses Denmark's role in EU expansion, increased global connectivity, and the impact of international terrorism on Danish citizens.
reason: It connects Denmark's diplomatic role in EU expansion to the personal impact of global terrorism on Danish families.
crisis_reason: The mention of international terrorism impacting Danish citizens frames the situation as a direct threat to the safety and security of the population, which constitutes a crisis.
