Therefore, we must participate in the EU's cooperation on the single currency. This will create the best and most secure foundation for the continued development of Danish society. It will be best for employment in Denmark. It means that we ourselves are helping to shape our future.
paragraph_summary: The speaker advocates for Denmark to adopt the euro to ensure economic stability, protect employment, and secure the country's future within the European Union.
reason: It discusses the domestic impact of joining the European single currency and international economic cooperation.
crisis_reason: The paragraph frames the adoption of the euro as a proactive measure for economic stability and future security rather than responding to an urgent, active threat or danger.

paragraph_text: We are now strengthening our preparedness and defense. We are reinforcing cooperation within NATO and with our Nordic neighbors. All parties in Parliament have come together to support the Nansen program, our support for Ukraine. Now, we have invited the parties to participate in the work on a new long-term plan for defense. At the same time, it is important to remember this: For a thousand years, Norway has lived in peace with our neighbor, Russia. We take care of our security, and Norway threatens no one.
paragraph_summary: The Prime Minister discusses strengthening Norway's national defense, international security cooperation, and support for Ukraine while maintaining a peaceful relationship with Russia.
reason: It links national defense and NATO cooperation to the broader geopolitical situation.
crisis_reason: The paragraph frames national defense and international security in the context of the ongoing war in Ukraine and the need for vigilance, which constitutes an active threat-based framing.

paragraph_text: Wars, instability in the global economy, the aftermath of the pandemic, rising prices and interest rates: all of this affects both government budgets and household budgets. Many people experienced a decline in their financial situation last year, and they are worried about their own finances after many years of stability and steady progress. Just go to the store or check your online banking. Everything, especially what we use most, has become more expensive. All the interest rate increases are taking their toll.
paragraph_summary: The speech addresses how global instability, inflation, and rising interest rates have negatively impacted household finances and economic security for many citizens.
reason: It connects global economic instability and international events to domestic household financial pressures.
crisis_reason: The paragraph frames global instability, inflation, and rising interest rates as direct threats to household finances and economic security, characterizing these issues as urgent problems requiring attention.

paragraph_text: We have a strong community in Norway. Many Ukrainian refugees have experienced that firsthand in recent months. We have welcomed 35, 000 refugees from Ukraine so far, and there may be as many again this year. People have opened their homes, and municipalities have made a tremendous effort to settle refugees throughout the country. In fact, every single municipality in Norway – from the small island of Utsira to the major city of Oslo – is settling refugees. Millions of Ukrainians are fleeing the horrors of war. Others are now facing a cold winter in Ukraine without water or electricity. On behalf of Norway, I have told President Zelenskyy that the Ukrainians’ fight is also our fight. They are defending the right to freedom, democracy, and independence. Therefore, we will continue to support the Ukrainians in the year ahead, with humanitarian aid and assistance in defending themselves. And by helping refugees find work and integrate into our community here in Norway.
paragraph_summary: The speech highlights Norway's commitment to supporting Ukrainian refugees and providing humanitarian and military aid to Ukraine during the ongoing war.
reason: It discusses the domestic integration of international refugees fleeing a foreign war.
crisis_reason: The paragraph frames the ongoing war in Ukraine as a situation requiring active, urgent support and intervention, which constitutes a threat-focused response to an acute international crisis.

paragraph_text: One of them is Helga Botten. Normally, Helga is a nurse at Rena camp in Østerdalen. But this summer, she was one of a few who remained at the Norwegian field hospital in Kabul, Afghanistan, after the Taliban took control of the city. Following a terrorist attack nearby, 65 critically injured people arrived at the hospital – all at the same time. Helga remembers the horrific injuries, the fear in the eyes of the people who poured in. But equally strong are her memories of the solidarity, the sense of community, the camaraderie among those working at the hospital. The profound meaning of helping, a feeling that I know many in the healthcare sector can relate to.
paragraph_summary: The speaker highlights the experiences of a Norwegian nurse providing emergency medical care during the Taliban takeover of Kabul.
reason: It describes the experience of a Norwegian nurse working in an international conflict zone.
crisis_reason: The paragraph describes a situation of emergency medical care during a violent political takeover, which constitutes an acute threat to safety and stability.

paragraph_text: And to put it somewhat jokingly, it will arrive by truck and air freight – I am, of course, referring to the vaccines. The fact that completely new vaccines have been developed so quickly is a triumph for science and for international cooperation. It is something worth reflecting on in a time when conspiracy theories are spreading rapidly, when more people seem to question science and knowledge, and when some advocate that each nation should be left to its own devices. On the contrary, the pandemic has underscored that no country can solve these challenges alone. For Norway, the EEA agreement has been crucial in ensuring access to vaccines. We have particularly received valuable assistance from our neighboring country, Sweden, for which we are grateful. Norway has also taken responsibility. We were instrumental in establishing CEPI in 2016, an international mechanism that contributes to the rapid development of vaccines for rare and emerging viruses. We should be proud of that.
paragraph_summary: The speaker highlights the importance of international cooperation and scientific advancement in securing COVID-19 vaccines for Norway.
reason: It discusses international vaccine cooperation and the importance of global scientific collaboration for Norway.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on the successful procurement of vaccines through cooperation and scientific progress, which frames the situation as a positive achievement and recovery rather than an active, ongoing threat.

paragraph_text: We have now learned that the world achieves much more when countries work together. If you get sick, you might receive breathing assistance from a ventilator from Switzerland. The medicines you need may be manufactured in Italy or India. One of the vaccines we will be using was developed in the United States and Germany, and it has been tested by over 40, 000 volunteers from six countries across five different continents to ensure its safety. We have also made our own contributions. Researchers at NTNU have developed a method that allows us to test thousands of people each day. This method has been exported to countries such as Denmark and India. We can achieve a great deal in Norway. But we are only a population of just over 5 million people. We will never be able to meet all our own needs for security alone. We will always be better and safer in this country if we can also benefit from the progress made by nearly eight billion people outside of Norway.
paragraph_summary: The speaker emphasizes the necessity of international cooperation and global interdependence for Norway's security and access to essential medical resources.
reason: Discusses how Norway relies on international cooperation and global supply chains for domestic security and health.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on the necessity of cooperation for security and resource access as a standard diplomatic and governance strategy rather than framing these as an urgent, active threat or crisis.

paragraph_text: Norway’s wealth allows us to help other countries achieve their own Sustainable Development Goals. The government has particularly prioritized ensuring that girls around the world have access to education. Fortunately, progress is being made. Around the world, there are now nearly as many girls as boys attending primary school. However, more girls drop out at the secondary level, often because they are expected to take on household responsibilities. This is both unfair and harmful. Education for girls boosts a country’s economy and helps prevent child marriage. Therefore, girls’ education will be a Norwegian priority in the years to come. One of the most devastating impacts on children’s education is war and conflict. In Syria, nearly an entire generation of children has lost the opportunity to attend school. Norway will do more to support children’s education in conflict zones. Last fall, I met Norwegian soldiers in Iraq. A few weeks later, they had to take cover in the camp due to rocket attacks. This is a reminder of the risks that Norwegian soldiers and aid workers face abroad. To create a better world, so that all children can go to school. To all those serving Norway abroad, who must spend the holidays away from their loved ones, I extend my warmest greetings and thank you for your efforts on behalf of us all.
paragraph_summary: The speaker highlights Norway's commitment to global girls' education and support for children in conflict zones, while thanking Norwegian personnel serving abroad.
reason: Discusses Norway's international development aid and the risks faced by Norwegian soldiers serving abroad.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on international commitments and expressions of gratitude for personnel, which are framed as positive contributions and ceremonial acknowledgments rather than urgent threats requiring immediate defensive action.

paragraph_text: On Christmas Day, we received the sad news that Ari Behn had taken his own life. Norway has lost a creative artist and writer. His family and loved ones have lost a beloved family member and a good friend. I send my warmest thoughts to all those who have lost a loved one in the past year. In September, I hosted an open house to mark World Suicide Prevention Day. In 2018, 674 people took their own lives. That is 674 too many. They each had 674 different reasons. Regardless, we must work to reduce that number. Openness, and being brave enough to talk about our feelings, is important. Two people who attended the open house were Andrea Bratt Mæhlum and Anna Uvizjeva. They hadn't known each other before, but have stayed in touch since. Their message is to give each other time to talk, time to care. It’s a wonderful message, and one I want to share with everyone here tonight. In many countries, more and more young people are facing mental health challenges, particularly among girls. We don't yet know why. Therefore, the government will be launching an initiative to investigate the underlying causes. This will help us move closer to effective solutions. But one thing we know for sure: caring for one another is always important.
paragraph_summary: The speaker addresses the issue of suicide, mental health challenges among youth, and the importance of openness and caring for one another.
reason: Discusses a domestic tragedy and mental health policy while noting that mental health challenges are a global trend.
crisis_reason: The speaker frames youth mental health and suicide as a serious, urgent threat to the well-being of the population that requires active intervention and collective care.

paragraph_text: Unfortunately, there is still a need for armed intervention in the fight against terrorism. We were recently reminded of this when we received the news that Islamist terrorists had attacked two young women in a cowardly and reckless manner in Morocco. During this Christmas season, friends and family in Denmark and Norway deeply miss two wonderful, young people with their lives ahead of them. We mourn with you and are thinking of you. After July 22nd, we know all too well the pain that terrorism can inflict. At the same time, we learned how important it is to stand together and defend the values that terrorists attack: freedom, openness, democracy, and justice. We are doing so again this time. Terrorists will not prevail. They must be combated. This happens through development and through a significant international military effort. Norwegian soldiers are also contributing. They are doing an important job for us all. For our democracy. For our freedom. Far from Norway. Many of them are also spending Christmas away from their loved ones. I would like to extend a special greeting to you, to aid workers and to all Norwegians working abroad, and thank you for your efforts. You are a role model for your children and for all of us.
paragraph_summary: The speaker addresses the fight against terrorism, mourns victims of a recent attack in Morocco, and honors Norwegian soldiers and workers serving abroad.
reason: Discusses the international fight against terrorism and the role of Norwegian soldiers serving abroad.
crisis_reason: The paragraph frames terrorism as an active, serious threat to safety and security, and explicitly mourns victims of a recent attack, which constitutes a crisis framing.

paragraph_text: Change, in various ways, has shaped our society throughout history. Some changes have left a deeper mark than others. Last year, we commemorated the 500th anniversary of Martin Luther’s challenge to the church of that time – the Reformation. The Reformation promoted the idea of the individual's importance and fostered the development of freedom of speech, education, and science. Without these values, our society would be a very different place today. In our daily lives, it is easy to take these values for granted. We should not do so. Our democratic society is not threatened today, but it is challenged in various ways. Our freedom and our liberal values are the most important things we have. Therefore, we must always be vigilant. We must combat radicalization and recruitment to violent Islamism, and to far-right extremism. Norway is a peaceful country. Nevertheless, we see that conflicts abroad can be reflected here at home. Earlier this autumn, I visited Haugeåsen secondary school in Fredrikstad. They have a special program to educate young people about radicalization. They discuss, among other things, how the words we use in public debate affect us. I met thoughtful young people who want to counter radicalization with knowledge and open debate. Many can learn from this. We need to build resilience in each young person against those who lure them with simplistic solutions to complex problems.
paragraph_summary: The speaker emphasizes the importance of democratic values and the need to build resilience against radicalization and extremism among young people in Norway.
reason: Addresses domestic radicalization and extremism influenced by international conflicts.
crisis_reason: The speaker frames radicalization and extremism as urgent threats to democratic values and social stability, necessitating active measures to build resilience.

paragraph_text: The oldest written records of the name "Norway" date back to the end of the 9th century. Interestingly, these records aren't from Norway itself, but from England. A Viking chieftain from northern Norway, engaged in trade both domestically and internationally, explained where he came from. Since then, Norwegians have ventured out into the world, reaching mountain peaks, the North Pole, and the South Pole. We have hunted whales and sold fish. During the era of sailing ships, we were the world's third-largest maritime nation. Today, we primarily export our oil and gas to foreign markets, as well as the majority of our cod from the sea and salmon from the farms. This also applies to aluminum and fertilizers.
paragraph_summary: The speech outlines Norway's historical and contemporary role as an internationally oriented nation through trade, exploration, and the export of natural resources.
reason: Discusses Norway's historical and current economic reliance on international trade.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on historical and contemporary achievements and the nation's role in trade and exploration, which are presented as positive developments rather than threats.

paragraph_text: Our engagement with the world ensures jobs, good salaries, and finances our welfare system. That's why I am pleased that Norway has once again established normal cooperation with China. History has also taught us what it's like to be a small piece in the games played by major powers, and that we are safest when we stand together with other countries that share our democratic values. The story of Norway shows that we best create both our prosperity and our security when we do so together with others. Forces skeptical of trade cooperation and collective security are now gaining momentum, challenging the foundations of Norway's success over the centuries.
paragraph_summary: The speaker emphasizes the importance of international trade and collective security for Norway's prosperity and safety amidst rising skepticism toward global cooperation.
reason: Discusses Norway's foreign relations and the impact of international trade on domestic prosperity.
crisis_reason: The speaker frames the rising skepticism toward global cooperation as a threat to the nation's prosperity and safety, requiring a defensive stance on international engagement.

paragraph_text: I understand that this creates uncertainty. However, it is in this world that we must prevent unemployment from taking hold after the fall in oil prices, and where we must manage the transition of our economy after oil activity peaks. We must provide good care for an aging population, integrate newly arrived refugees through work and education, and transition to a low-emission society. We face significant challenges. But even though we live in a time of uncertainty, I feel optimistic. I have faith in Norway, and I have faith in the Norwegian people.
paragraph_summary: The speaker addresses Norway's economic transition, an aging population, refugee integration, and climate goals while expressing optimism about the nation's future.
reason: Addresses domestic challenges like refugee integration and economic transition in a globalized context.
crisis_reason: The paragraph frames these topics as part of a transition and expresses optimism about the future, focusing on long-term national development rather than presenting these issues as urgent, immediate threats requiring emergency action.

paragraph_text: Dear everyone, This year, more people are welcoming the new year in Norway than last year. This is due to the birth of 60, 000 children, the arrival of many people from Europe to work, and also because 30, 000 people have arrived as asylum seekers. Crises and conflicts are driving millions to flee from hardship and brutality. We are experiencing the ripple effects here at home. We have seen Norwegians leaving, becoming foreign fighters, and participating in brutal conflicts. We have also taken in asylum seekers from the same conflicts. We have witnessed terror in Paris, Beirut, Copenhagen, and other cities. Norwegian citizens abroad have lost their lives. Suffering and cruelty are being transmitted through the internet, newspapers, and television. I understand that many people are feeling anxious. Therefore, it is important for us to strengthen our preparedness and combat terrorism. We must be confident that our way of life, our democracy, is not threatened. When conflicts around the world have consequences here at home, it is also true that our security begins with what is done abroad. In Afghanistan and Iraq, Norwegian soldiers are training those who will fight terrorists in their own countries. In this way, they also contribute to our security. Aid workers and volunteers are also helping to strengthen peace. I extend my greetings to Norwegian soldiers and other Norwegians who contribute to peace and security, and I thank you for the important work you do on behalf of us all.
paragraph_summary: The speaker addresses the impact of global conflicts on Norway, emphasizing the need for national security, counter-terrorism efforts, and international peacekeeping contributions.
reason: It connects international conflicts and terrorism to domestic security and preparedness.
crisis_reason: The paragraph frames global conflicts as a direct threat to national security and emphasizes the urgent need for counter-terrorism and peacekeeping, which constitutes an active threat-focused framing.

paragraph_text: Well-functioning democracies, built on human rights and security, prevent conflict and prevent people from being forced to flee. Many who are now fleeing have had to leave loved ones behind. Some have seen loved ones lost at sea in the Mediterranean. Others have travelled through Europe with young children on their shoulders. The year 2015 that we are leaving behind is quite different from the year 2015 that you and I are leaving behind. However, we all share the hopes for the new year, regardless of where we come from. We all share the desire to be able to support ourselves and our families, and to ensure that our families live in safety and good health.
paragraph_summary: The speaker discusses the plight of refugees fleeing conflict and emphasizes the shared human desire for safety, health, and family security.
reason: It discusses the global refugee crisis and its impact on the shared human experience within Norway.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on the humanitarian plight of refugees and the fundamental need for safety and security, framing the situation as a serious threat to human well-being that demands attention.

paragraph_text: Dr. Silje Lehne Michalsen travelled to Sierra Leone to share her knowledge and care with people desperately fighting against the Ebola outbreak. She saved lives, but was close to sacrificing her own. She survived and gave us a powerful example of what one person can do for others. Over the course of a year, I meet many people who are making a difference. There's Heidi, who struggled with addiction from the age of 14 and became a patient at the Gate Hospital. She is now sober and shares her experiences to help others. Then there's Knut Granheim, who during last autumn's floods led the evacuation in Odda, even as his own house was swept away by the floodwaters. And Fredrik Dyb Alnes, a 14-year-old from Godøya, who told me what we can do to help him and other students with dyslexia. And during the Advent season, we had the privilege of meeting Malala Yousafzai, the Nobel Peace Prize winner, who has become a voice for girls' right to education. What unites them all is that they have made a difference in the lives of others. We can all make a difference.
model_actions: Dropped count of 'floods' | Dropped count of 'floodwaters'
model_reasons: floods: refers to a specific natural disaster event, not climate change or environmental policy | floodwaters: refers to the physical water from a specific event, not climate change
paragraph_summary: The speaker highlights several individuals who have positively impacted their communities to illustrate that every person has the capacity to make a difference.
reason: It highlights individuals who have made a difference in both local and international contexts.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on positive individual contributions and community impact, which is an affirmative and celebratory framing rather than one identifying a threat or urgent danger.

paragraph_text: We have a strong sense of national identity. But it is a positive force that does not compromise our international solidarity or our connection to the wider world. Our world is becoming increasingly interconnected. When terrorists attacked a gas plant in the desert in Algeria, one of the operators at the plant was Norwegian company Statoil. Many people were killed in the brutal attack, including five Norwegians. When a typhoon struck the Philippines, aid had to be delivered quickly. The training ship for Filipino sailors, operated by a Norwegian shipping company, made an important contribution. Filipinos in Norway collected money. A Norwegian ship and the frigate KNM Helge Ingstad are assisting in the destruction of Syria’s chemical weapons. Another of our frigates, KNM Fridtjof Nansen, led NATO operations to protect international shipping and trade from pirates in the Gulf of Aden. Our strong international engagement means that many Norwegians are working abroad. Norwegian soldiers in Afghanistan and on our frigates, Norwegians working in humanitarian aid and conflict zones, are taking personal risks on all our behalf. To create a better world. For the values that we hold dear and that we will celebrate during the 200th anniversary of the Constitution. We are deeply grateful for their efforts. We are thinking of you, especially during this holiday season. I wish you all, both at home and abroad, a very happy New Year.
paragraph_summary: The speaker highlights Norway's international engagement and the contributions of its citizens working abroad in humanitarian, security, and peacekeeping efforts.
reason: Discusses Norwegian international engagement and the personal risks taken by citizens abroad to support global values.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on positive contributions and international engagement rather than framing these activities as a response to an urgent threat or danger.

paragraph_text: This year, many Norwegians are celebrating New Year while working to create peace around the world – soldiers, aid workers, healthcare professionals. We extend a warm thank you to all of you. And we remember those we have lost. During the NATO exercise Cold Response in March, five of our officers died in the tragic helicopter crash near Kebnekaise: Lieutenant Colonel Truls Audun Ørpen, Captain Bjørn Yngvar Haug, Captain Ståle Garberg, Captain Siw Robertsen, and Captain Steinar Utne. We received the news of their passing with deep sorrow. # On this first day of the year, our thoughts are with our beloved royal family. Last year, we celebrated the King and Queen's dual 75th birthdays. Today, we would like to express our gratitude for another year of outstanding service to Norway. I would also like to thank everyone who is on duty this New Year's weekend: nurses and firefighters, snowplow operators and harbor pilots, and so many others. Thank you for keeping us safe while we celebrate. A special greeting to each and every police officer who is out on patrol tonight. You carry the responsibility for our safety with wisdom and courage, and for that, we thank you. Happy New Year!
paragraph_summary: The speaker honors Norwegians serving abroad, mourns soldiers lost in a helicopter crash, celebrates the royal family, and thanks essential workers on duty.
reason: Combines recognition of Norwegians working in international peace efforts with domestic gratitude for emergency services.
crisis_reason: The paragraph is primarily ceremonial and commemorative, focusing on honoring service members, mourning a specific past tragedy, and expressing gratitude, rather than framing current events as an active, ongoing threat.

paragraph_text: On July 22nd, children were also born in Norway. One of them was Thomas. Today, he is a happy and content little boy in Lillehammer. Thomas will have to live with the fact that his birthday is associated with sorrow. We will ensure that it is not just about that. We will show that the crisis also brought forth something warm and valuable in our country. Thomas and Danica will grow up on opposite sides of the world. Our mission is to show them how much more unites them than divides them. When we show a willingness to learn about each other, we grow as individuals. When we are able to respect our differences, we become stronger. When we dare to trust each other, we achieve the most. Perhaps one day, Thomas and Danica will become friends on the future’s version of Facebook. Thomas will learn about Manila, and Danica will see pictures of something she has never seen before: the ski jumps in Lillehammer. This is the internet at its best. But the internet can also be a dark place, where extremist voices can speak unchallenged. We must confront this with firmness. We must drive them back with the light of knowledge. Challenging extremism is taking responsibility for the future. I encourage everyone to become good digital neighbors. Not to censor opinions or stifle debate. We must tolerate the uncomfortable, what irritates, provokes, and even shocks us. But we must respond. It is demonstrating responsible freedom of expression to say, "No, you are wrong. " We do that around the lunch table at work. Now, it’s time to do it online as well.
paragraph_summary: The speaker discusses the legacy of the July 22nd attacks, the importance of global connection, and the need to challenge online extremism through responsible discourse.
reason: It connects domestic social values and internet safety to global digital interactions and the influence of international extremist content.
crisis_reason: The paragraph frames online extremism as an active, ongoing threat to society that requires a deliberate response and responsible discourse to counter.

paragraph_text: Another challenge in the new year is the European debt crisis. It appears to be about money, but it’s really about people. Once again, we see armies of unemployed people in Europe. Many young people are losing faith in the future. Europe cannot allow that. For us in Norway, it’s about securing our jobs and keeping housing costs manageable. We don’t know how severely we will be affected, but I promise that we will act when necessary, as we have done before.
paragraph_summary: The speaker addresses the impact of the European debt crisis on unemployment and economic stability, pledging to protect Norwegian jobs and housing affordability.
reason: It discusses the impact of the European debt crisis on the domestic Norwegian economy and employment.
crisis_reason: The speaker frames the debt crisis as an urgent threat to economic stability, employment, and housing, necessitating active protection and intervention.

paragraph_text: Over 1200 of today’s Norwegians were born when Roald Amundsen reached the South Pole. In the century that has passed since then, they have participated in a remarkable transformation of Norway. At the same time that the polar explorers traveled south with the *Fram*, thousands of Norwegian women and farmworkers traveled west with the ships to America. In the new country, there were better opportunities for work and land. One of the Norwegian emigrants wrote home enthusiastically that in America, “. . . we eat Christmas food every day. ” In 1911, we were a poor, agricultural society that many people were leaving. In 2011, we are a rich, modern society that many people want to move to. Many Swedes are essential to keeping Norway running, and they say that Norway is incredibly attractive with its high salaries and good job opportunities. What a journey our elders have witnessed! They were born into a young nation that was among the poorest in Europe. In their youth, many of them were unemployed during the “difficult thirties, ” the last major economic crisis in the world. They became adults during the fight against Nazism. For the rest of their lives, they worked to build a welfare state, a system that no other nation, anywhere in the world, had ever seen before. Therefore, it is fitting that they are rightly called “the pioneering generation. ”
paragraph_summary: The speech reflects on Norway's historical transformation from a poor, agricultural nation to a prosperous welfare state over the past century.
reason: It contrasts Norway's historical emigration and current immigration trends with its domestic economic evolution.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on historical reflection and national achievement, framing the country's development as a positive transformation rather than an urgent threat or danger.

paragraph_text: Dear everyone. Granaries are among the first public buildings we know from the earliest civilizations. They contained emergency supplies for years when harvests failed. We remember the story of Joseph, who advised Pharaoh in Egypt to store grain because, after seven years of plenty, seven years of famine would follow. It's an age-old lesson that we should save during good times to prepare for leaner times. The past year has seen the world experience the most severe economic downturn since the 1930s. This international storm has also affected Norway. We have a small, open economy where half of what we produce is sold to foreign markets. When those markets disappear, we feel the impact here at home. People who produce car parts, melt aluminum, or build boats have lost their jobs because foreigners are no longer buying what they make. Losing a job is particularly difficult for the individual. But unemployment also harms the community. When fewer people are creating, there is less to share. During this crisis, we have spent a great deal of extra money to keep things moving. We were able to spend generously during difficult times because we had saved during good times. In this way, we can say that we are following the advice Joseph gave to Pharaoh, albeit in a slightly different way than in ancient Egypt. The Egyptians built granaries. We built a pension fund.
model_reasons: storm: metaphorical reference to an economic downturn, not a climate event
paragraph_summary: The speaker explains how Norway utilized its national pension fund to mitigate the economic impact of the global financial crisis.
reason: The paragraph discusses the impact of the global economic downturn on the domestic Norwegian economy and labor market.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on the past utilization of a fund to mitigate a previous economic impact, framing the situation as a recovery or management achievement rather than an active, ongoing threat.

paragraph_text: I must admit that I have been thinking a great deal about this New Year's address. I have been searching for the right balance to describe the effects of the international financial crisis on Norway. On one hand, it is important to portray the seriousness of the situation. There is no doubt that we are experiencing a dramatic downturn in the global economy. For the first time since 1945, the total production in industrialized countries will decline. We sell almost half of what we produce here in Norway to other countries. When foreigners buy less of what we have to sell, Norwegian businesses must temporarily lay off and dismiss employees. People in other countries are now buying fewer cars and furniture, and building fewer houses and factories than before. This is already being felt in places like Raufoss and Karmøy, in Mosjøen and Sykkylven. Therefore, we must prepare for the fact that unemployment will rise throughout 2009. This is a serious matter. Our task is to do everything we can to minimize the number of people who become unemployed and to ensure that those who lose their jobs find new ones as quickly as possible. Jobs for everyone is our top priority.
paragraph_summary: The Prime Minister addresses the impact of the global financial crisis on Norway, emphasizing rising unemployment and the government's commitment to job preservation.
reason: The text explains how the global financial crisis is specifically affecting Norwegian businesses and unemployment.
crisis_reason: The paragraph frames the financial crisis as an urgent threat to livelihoods and employment, explicitly highlighting the government's commitment to intervention and job preservation.

paragraph_text: In the years leading up to 2030, today's newborns will be entering adulthood. Learning is something we do every day, throughout our lives. But it is in school that we lay the foundation. We must take this seriously, and also because others are doing so. During a visit to the United Nations, I happened to be standing behind a small and agile young Chinese woman. She was wearing a T-shirt with text printed on the back. It said, "You will be working for me one day. " That T-shirt is an expression of confidence and a desire to conquer the world, a determination not to rest until they achieve the prosperity that we enjoy. In the past, the United States educated the most people with university degrees. Today, China is educating twice as many people at the university and college level than the United States. This is an educational revolution that is lifting hundreds of millions of people out of poverty. These young people are thirsting for knowledge and learning, seeing it as the key to a better life. This shows that we, who are currently in positions of responsibility, must understand the times and fulfill our obligations to our own children, namely to provide them with the best possible education. Not just as a basis for work and income. No, knowledge also brings joy in itself. And there is enough knowledge for everyone. Sharing it does not diminish it; rather, it increases when more people share knowledge.
paragraph_summary: The speaker emphasizes the necessity of prioritizing high-quality education to remain competitive in a global landscape increasingly dominated by China's educational advancements.
reason: The paragraph discusses global educational competition and its implications for domestic policy and responsibility.
crisis_reason: The paragraph frames the global educational landscape as a competitive threat to the nation's standing, treating the need for educational reform as an urgent response to an external challenge.

paragraph_text: Our newborns are among the luckiest children in the world. They are born into a country that enjoys peace and prosperity. But when the sun rose over the mountains near Kabul in Afghanistan today, it did not shine on a country with peace and prosperity. In Khaled Hosseini's book, "The Kite Runner, " by the Afghan-American author, we gain insight into what it was like in the country under the Taliban – what it was like to live in daily fear and under terror. We read about the beatings inflicted on defenseless girls and women. The murders. Even music was forbidden. We read about how football matches had to be cancelled, and how the stadium was instead used as an arena for the execution of people who had done nothing wrong. And this in our time! In November, I had a meeting with Afghanistan’s Minister of Education at the Deichman Library in Oslo. The library has books in Afghan languages, which are frequently borrowed. The Minister comes from a country where no girls were allowed to attend school. Today, there are nearly 5 million children in school, and nearly 2 million of them are girls. Almost half a million girls started school just this year. The Minister stated that Norwegian soldiers contribute to ensuring that these Afghan girls and boys can attend school. Now, we will further increase our civilian assistance – focusing on healthcare, good governance, and education. This is what will shape Afghanistan’s future.
paragraph_summary: The speaker discusses the contrast between Norwegian prosperity and Afghan instability, highlighting Norway's commitment to supporting education and civilian development in Afghanistan.
reason: It discusses international humanitarian issues in Afghanistan and links them to Norwegian foreign aid and military involvement.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on the contrast between prosperity and instability and the commitment to development aid, which frames the situation as a routine diplomatic and humanitarian policy rather than an urgent threat requiring immediate response from the speaker's nation.

paragraph_text: Therefore, at this turn of the year, I would like to send a special greeting to Norwegian soldiers and aid workers in Afghanistan, to those few who give of their time and energy, on behalf of us all. We deeply respect the efforts made by our military and civilian personnel in this hard-tested country. Today is also the first day of the year in Kabul, Meymaneh, and Mazar-i-Sharif, where many Norwegians are marking the new year, far from home, but serving Norway. I would also like to extend greetings to all other fellow citizens who are currently serving abroad, working for peace and reconciliation. We are proud of the efforts you make.
paragraph_summary: The speaker expresses gratitude and pride toward Norwegian military and civilian personnel serving abroad in Afghanistan and other regions for their contributions to peace.
reason: It addresses Norwegian personnel serving abroad in international conflicts, bridging domestic support with foreign policy.
crisis_reason: The paragraph is framed as an expression of gratitude and pride for past and ongoing contributions to peace, rather than focusing on an active threat or a situation requiring urgent intervention.

paragraph_text: Just before Christmas, I visited Lebanon, Israel, and Palestine. Such a trip provides many powerful and lasting impressions. It was a special moment to light a candle in the Church of the Nativity in Bethlehem, in an area filled with conflict. In Beirut, I met Muhammed, an 11-year-old boy. He and his father had been riding a scooter when a cluster bomb exploded. The boy lost both of his legs. We, who have so much, cannot give him his legs back. But we can provide him with help with surgery and prosthetics. That will give him new hope for a better life. Meeting him is something I will never forget. In February, we experienced an attack on Norwegian soldiers in Afghanistan. Your courage, skill, and unity were strong enough that we did not experience any loss of life among our soldiers. What happened demonstrates the demanding mission they have been sent on – and how important it is that they are where they are. We are proud of the efforts Norwegians make in international service – as soldiers in Lebanon and Afghanistan, and through the many observers and aid workers we have deployed. They go out with the most important asset we can send: extensive knowledge, respect for the mission, and a commitment to working for peace and human dignity. They are helping to provide hope for a better future.
paragraph_summary: The speaker reflects on Norwegian international contributions, including humanitarian aid in the Middle East and the service of soldiers in Afghanistan.
reason: It discusses international conflict zones and the specific role of Norwegian soldiers and aid workers within them.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on past contributions and service, framing them as achievements or routine diplomatic and military activities rather than an active, ongoing threat requiring urgent response.

paragraph_text: During a meeting in India in December, we presented Norway’s commitment to ensuring that every child in the world is vaccinated. It’s about saving millions of young children from death. Norway is already a leader in this area. By 2015, we will have saved one child for every Norwegian, thanks to our contributions. Norwegian men and women in uniform are making a heroic effort every day to ensure people’s safety, such as our soldiers in Afghanistan. Particularly women and children are safer on the streets of Kabul because of the efforts of our forces. One of the things that fills me with joy, and makes me proud, is when leaders in other countries praise our soldiers. And it happens often. Tonight, I would like to extend greetings to all Norwegians living abroad. And I would like to express a special thank you to those many individuals who work in the service of peace and solidarity.
paragraph_summary: The speech highlights Norway's global contributions to childhood vaccination programs and the peacekeeping efforts of Norwegian soldiers in Afghanistan.
reason: It details Norway's international health commitments and military contributions abroad.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on positive contributions and international achievements, which are framed as accomplishments rather than urgent threats or active crises.

paragraph_text: Norway has great opportunities to contribute to peace, greater justice, and to alleviate suffering. During a recent trip to Asia, I saw several aspects of our international engagement that we have reason to be proud of – and in which we are succeeding. In Pakistan, we visited earthquake victims in the mountainous regions. Over 70, 000 people died when the ground shook one morning in October. Millions became homeless. I met a mother there, high in the mountains. When the earthquake struck, she had to make the impossible choice about which child to save, as the house collapsed around her family. She managed to rescue her infant. Life can be that brutal. In a refugee camp, I heard the excited voices of children from a tent. There was a school. Amidst the hardship, the children were learning English. There was a sense of vitality radiating from these young children. They were on their way to a future. Norway has strong, experienced aid organizations and a skilled diplomatic corps that are working to help these people. Many of you have contributed significantly this year, enabling aid organizations to provide assistance to people in need. Last year, Norway also experienced a tragedy, which brought a sense of closeness to the obligation to help.
model_reasons: earthquake: geological event, not framed as climate change or environmental policy
paragraph_summary: The speaker highlights Norway's international humanitarian efforts and aid contributions in response to global tragedies and the needs of vulnerable populations.
reason: Discusses Norway's international aid efforts and humanitarian engagement in response to global crises.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on humanitarian aid and international contributions as a positive achievement and a reflection of national values, rather than framing the tragedies themselves as an urgent threat requiring immediate defensive action or sacrifice from the nation.

paragraph_text: Much of our growth is due to hard work, and we are grateful to those who built the country. However, we did not achieve prosperity in isolation. It was also fostered through trade with other nations, which contributed to industrial development in Norway. After the war, we received the Marshall Plan from the United States. Previously, others helped us. Now, it is our turn to help others. And I am not just talking about the urgent humanitarian aid needed in South Asia. I am also talking about Norway’s long-term, steadfast assistance to developing countries. And here, we face another vision: that Norway should be a nation characterized by compassion – a nation of solidarity. And that our gaze should constantly be directed towards a world that suffers. There is disagreement about this aid, but I believe we have done the right thing by increasing it to a record level, and by providing trade advantages to the poorest countries, and by offering debt relief. It is producing results. Widespread diseases are being overcome. Education is being expanded. Life expectancy is increasing. Much has been achieved, but we have more to do!
paragraph_summary: The speaker advocates for Norway's continued commitment to international development aid, humanitarian assistance, and global solidarity as a core national responsibility.
reason: Connects Norway's domestic prosperity to international trade and its ongoing commitment to foreign aid.
crisis_reason: The paragraph frames international aid and solidarity as a core national responsibility and a positive, ongoing commitment rather than a response to an urgent threat or danger.

paragraph_text: On December 26th, the earth’s crust shifted violently, causing immense tremors. Gigantic waves, like rings spreading across the water, surged outwards from the earthquake’s epicenter off the coast of Indonesia. They built up into a devastating tsunami that struck numerous countries around the Indian Ocean. In a matter of minutes, the lives of millions were turned upside down. More than we can comprehend have lost their lives. Asia has experienced major, tragic natural disasters before. This time, we too have been directly and profoundly affected. Norwegians who were looking forward to sunny holiday days have perished. Many more have returned home with physical and emotional scars from the ordeal. Here at home, many are now grieving and missing loved ones, or living with a crushing uncertainty about the fate of those they hold dear. Some may never know for sure. This is especially true for many of our new countrymen and women. We feel deeply for all those affected by the ripple effects of this flood disaster.
model_actions: Dropped count of 'tsunami' | Dropped count of 'natural disasters' | Dropped count of 'disasters' | Dropped count of 'flood'
model_reasons: tsunami: geological event, not related to climate change or environmental policy | natural disasters: refers to geological earthquake/tsunami, not climate-linked environmental phenomena | disasters: refers to the earthquake and tsunami event | flood: refers to the tsunami wave, not climate-related flooding
paragraph_summary: The speaker addresses the tragic loss of Norwegian lives and the widespread suffering caused by the 2004 Indian Ocean tsunami.
reason: Discusses the impact of an international natural disaster on Norwegian citizens and the nation.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on a catastrophic natural disaster involving the loss of life and widespread suffering, framing it as a major tragedy that demands national attention and mourning.

paragraph_text: A sense of helplessness is close at hand when a catastrophe is so immense. As a nation, we are shaken, but it also gives us an opportunity for recognition and reflection. It reminds us that we need each other. Because there are other ripple effects beyond just the destructive ones. There is a surge of action, a will, and a kindness that is being expressed throughout our people. As a counterpoint to the destructive tsunami, a new wave is emerging. It too is spreading like rings on the water. Now, it’s not about natural forces – but about moral strength and compassion. We hear stories of people taking care of each other amidst the chaos, and of local communities sharing what they have with traumatized tourists. A massive relief effort is pouring in to the disaster-stricken coastal areas.
model_reasons: tsunami: refers to a specific disaster event, not climate change or environmental policy
paragraph_summary: The speaker highlights the national response of compassion and solidarity following the 2004 Indian Ocean tsunami.
reason: Describes the domestic response and moral reaction to an international catastrophe.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on the national response and solidarity following a past event, framing it as a moment of unity rather than an active, ongoing threat.

paragraph_text: There has been criticism directed at the government for being too slow to provide assistance. I understand that many feel this way, and our entire crisis management response will be evaluated in retrospect. But let us not forget all those who, under immense personal strain, have worked day and night to find and assist Norwegians affected by the disaster. Public servants and volunteers are making a tremendous effort. Our healthcare system is on high alert. Aircraft are transporting people home. A reception center has been established. Thank you to everyone who is now stepping up to provide professional assistance and practical support! And thank you to families, neighbors, and friends who are showing care for those affected and for the grieving and missing loved ones. At the same time, we must remember that many cannot be evacuated but must continue to live in damaged homes and villages in South Asia. It brings me great joy to see the immense willingness of the Norwegian people to help them as well. In this way, each of us can show compassion, and in this way, Norway – as a small nation – can demonstrate its strength.
paragraph_summary: The speaker acknowledges criticism of the government's disaster response while praising the efforts of public servants, volunteers, and citizens in assisting those affected.
reason: Addresses the domestic crisis management response to an international disaster and the aid provided to foreign victims.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on the aftermath and recovery efforts following a disaster, emphasizing gratitude and praise rather than framing the situation as an ongoing or active threat requiring urgent intervention.

paragraph_text: Good evening, and a happy new year! Amidst the joy of the Christmas season, we were deeply affected by the earthquake disaster in Iran, with tens of thousands of people killed and injured. In keeping with our humanitarian tradition, Norway is providing rapid and extensive emergency assistance. A picture remains etched in my mind that I cannot erase: A devastated father emerges from a ruined house. In his arms, he carries his four-year-old daughter, who has passed away. They are on their way to a mass grave. Another image comes to mind: A joyful father holding his three-year-old son – who, by a miracle, has been rescued from the rubble, alive and unharmed. The contrast between the two fathers' faces is stark. One weeps with grief, the other radiates with joy. Yet, both faces speak to the same truth: Every individual is unique. We are all equally valuable. This applies to everyone, regardless of age, those who thrive in life, and those who need help. Such a perspective on humanity obligates us. That is why Norway stands at the forefront of international humanitarian efforts. That is why we are steadily increasing our investment in healthcare and social care within Norway. For me, this is becoming increasingly important in my personal choices, and as Prime Minister, in shaping our shared future. We are often confronted with fundamental questions: About the value of human life, our responsibilities, and our opportunities. I would like to express it this way: Every individual has the right to feel valued and unique. It’s a simple sentence, but difficult to fully realize. We must never abandon our vision of a humane society and a humane world. The term "environmental protection" has rightfully earned a place in our collective consciousness. Now, I want to talk about "human dignity, " protecting each individual, in their immediate surroundings and within the larger context.
model_reasons: earthquake: geological event, not framed as climate change or environmental policy
paragraph_summary: The Prime Minister emphasizes the importance of human dignity and the value of every individual in both domestic social policy and international humanitarian efforts.
reason: Links international humanitarian aid to domestic social policy and the universal value of human dignity.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on universal values and humanitarian principles as a reflection of national identity and policy, rather than framing a specific threat or urgent danger that requires immediate defensive action.

paragraph_text: This year, the European Union is expanding to include ten new countries. At the same time, the internal market, of which Norway is a part through the European Economic Area (EEA), is expanding. This presents us with challenges and new opportunities for collaboration. Norway is among the countries with the highest employment rate and lowest unemployment rate. However, even we have been affected by international economic downturns in recent years. We currently have approximately 100, 000 unemployed people. Many say that the biggest problem is losing the feeling of being a valuable individual. Therefore, creating and securing jobs is a priority for the government. Over the past two years, we have pursued a strict economic policy to reduce the high cost levels for Norwegian businesses. The results are now beginning to show: In the past year, the exchange rate has fallen, and interest rates have more than halved. If you have a mortgage of 1. 5 million kroner, the reduction in interest rates means a reduction of approximately 51, 000 kroner in your annual expenses after tax. For municipalities, this translates to approximately 3. 5 billion kroner in savings annually. Businesses have also benefited from significant tax and duty reductions. Business leaders are now reporting increased optimism. The willingness to invest is growing. Unemployment has already begun to decline. The improvement in the Norwegian economy is noticeable. We expect this trend to continue in the new year. Optimism is returning. If we want to strengthen this positive trend, we must all contribute.
paragraph_summary: The speaker discusses Norway's economic recovery, focusing on job creation, reduced interest rates, and the impact of European integration on the national economy.
reason: It discusses the impact of international economic trends and EU expansion on the domestic Norwegian economy.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on economic recovery, job creation, and positive indicators, which are framed as achievements rather than an active or urgent threat.

paragraph_text: Human dignity knows no borders. We must always fight for the interests of the poor and the persecuted, even in other countries. One of the major threats to human dignity today is terrorism. It spreads fear and harms innocent people. Terrorism is a contempt for human dignity. To make the fight against terrorism more effective, we must also ask: why do some people sacrifice their own lives and the lives of others to achieve their goals? In collaboration with Nobel laureate Elie Wiesel, I had the honor of hosting a conference in New York in September. The theme was the roots of terrorism. Hatred, fanaticism, humiliation, and oppression can all be contributing factors. It is a complex picture. Poverty and regional conflicts can also play a role. We also see that religious differences can be part of conflicts. However, the shared values of religions regarding human dignity and reconciliation should be part of the solution. Therefore, we need more forums for dialogue between religions, where we can learn to understand and respect one another. I have personally enjoyed participating in such gatherings, and I will continue this work. Knowledge of the beliefs and thoughts of others should build bridges – not create conflict.
paragraph_summary: The speaker discusses the roots of terrorism and advocates for interreligious dialogue to promote human dignity and mutual understanding.
reason: It connects the global issue of terrorism and religious dialogue to the universal value of human dignity.
crisis_reason: The paragraph frames terrorism as a fundamental threat to human dignity and social order, necessitating urgent action through interreligious dialogue.

paragraph_text: Norway will be a nation of peace. To achieve this, we must also build a culture of peace in our everyday lives, showing respect and consideration for each other. We must fight against violence and bullying, creating peace between people. At the same time, as much as a small nation can, we must build a culture of peace in the broader world, between nations, all built on a foundation of human dignity and human rights. We celebrate the New Year with different moods and in different circumstances. But we are united in the sense that we all need human connection. We need the message of Christmas – peace and love – in our everyday lives in the new year. The goal of love is not primarily that we find ourselves, but that we find each other. If we could show each other a little more kindness and show more gratitude, we have much to look forward to. On behalf of us all, I would like to extend greetings to the King, the Queen, and the rest of the royal family, and thank them for their efforts for Norway in the year that has passed. Last year, we rejoiced in a princess’s wedding, which became a national celebration. Now, we are looking forward to the arrival of a new member of the royal family. I would also like to extend greetings to all Norwegians serving abroad – in the foreign service and in peacekeeping forces, in missionary, humanitarian, and human rights work, in shipping, and in other contexts. I wish you all a happy and peaceful New Year!
paragraph_summary: The speech emphasizes fostering a culture of peace, kindness, and human connection while extending New Year greetings to the royal family and Norwegians abroad.
reason: It balances domestic social goals with Norway's international role in promoting peace and human rights.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on positive values like peace and kindness alongside ceremonial greetings, which are explicitly excluded from the definition of a crisis.

paragraph_text: Good evening, and a happy New Year! The past year has seen our nation witness the most fundamental themes of life unfold before our eyes: there has been loss, and there has been love. On August 25th, many were captivated by love as our Crown Prince and Crown Princess were married in Oslo Cathedral. Not many days later, on September 11th, tragedy took center stage. We witnessed passenger planes being deliberately crashed into the World Trade Center in New York. Human wickedness reached unprecedented levels. Many, including myself, were unable to complete their workday as usual. It was a massacre of thousands of people. They had done nothing to deserve such a fate, other than being on their way to work or in the office.
paragraph_summary: The speaker reflects on the contrast between the royal wedding in Oslo and the September 11 terrorist attacks in New York.
reason: It contrasts a local royal wedding with the global impact of the 9/11 terrorist attacks.
crisis_reason: The paragraph invokes the September 11 terrorist attacks, which are framed as a major security threat and a defining moment of danger, rather than focusing on recovery or routine events.

paragraph_text: The manufacturer Dale was forced to implement temporary layoffs following the terrorist attacks in the United States. They were not the only ones in our country to experience the consequences. Globalization has become a part of our everyday lives. Norway is part of the world, and the world is a part of Norway. This is particularly evident among young people through the internet, mobile phones, fashion, and music. They must also be prepared to compare themselves internationally.
paragraph_summary: The speaker discusses the economic impact of globalization on Norway and the necessity for young people to compete in an international context.
reason: Discusses the domestic economic impact of international terrorism and the influence of globalization on Norwegian youth.
crisis_reason: The paragraph describes globalization as a context for competition and opportunity rather than framing it as an urgent threat or a danger to national stability.

paragraph_text: But we were not paralyzed for long. Politics also means setting boundaries for evil. As wickedness reached new extremes, it was met with new boundaries on the international stage. Rarely has the world seen such a united front emerge after September 11th. Here we see states that we have been accustomed to seeing as adversaries: the United States and Russia, India and Pakistan. Here we see Christians and Muslims, all united under the United Nations umbrella. The United Nations, which just a month later would be awarded the Nobel Peace Prize. Here in our country, we have been in no doubt: we want to stand alongside this alliance against terrorism. Because there are limits to what we can accept. Because we want peace, democracy, and respect for human dignity.
paragraph_summary: The speaker discusses the international coalition against terrorism following the September 11 attacks and Norway's commitment to supporting this global alliance.
reason: Connects the rise of right-wing extremism globally to its specific manifestation and threat within Sweden.
crisis_reason: The paragraph frames the September 11 attacks as a major security threat and the subsequent international coalition as an urgent, necessary response to that threat.

paragraph_text: After the Second World War, we have based our peace and security on the contributions of others, should it prove necessary. All nations, but especially small nations, are dependent on international cooperation and binding agreements. The fight against terrorism is a meeting point for our international commitments and our obligations to our allies. Now, Norway too is called upon to play its part. It is a heavy responsibility, and it is dangerous. The people of Afghanistan have suffered under war, mismanagement, and natural disasters for the past decades. Now that the fighting is ending, a new chapter opens for rebuilding Afghanistan. Here, we will contribute.
model_actions: Dropped count of 'natural disasters' | Dropped count of 'disasters'
model_reasons: natural disasters: Refers to humanitarian context of war-torn Afghanistan, not climate change. | disasters: Refers to general humanitarian suffering, not environmental or climate events.
paragraph_summary: Norway commits to participating in the international rebuilding efforts and security operations in post-war Afghanistan.
reason: Uses historical international examples of extremism to warn against domestic political threats in Sweden.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on post-war rebuilding and security operations, which frames the situation as a recovery effort rather than an active, ongoing threat.

paragraph_text: His Majesty the King spoke yesterday about the need for hope in a turbulent world. We share his view. And we also see the need for greater security. During the 100th anniversary of the Nobel Prizes in December, I had the opportunity to meet many former Nobel Peace Prize laureates. These meetings gave me a sense of hope in a difficult time. These are individuals who have lived by the motto: "We knew that it was impossible, therefore we did it. " The world is a better place thanks to men and women like Desmond Tutu, Nelson Mandela, Kofi Annan, Eli Wiesel, Aung San Suu Kyi, and Rigoberta Menchú, who have dedicated their lives to the common good of their people and of us all. Some of them were unable to visit Oslo in December. Aung San Suu Kyi could not leave her homeland, Burma. But in the fight for democracy, for freedom of expression, and for human dignity, she is a shining example, far beyond her own country.
paragraph_summary: The speaker reflects on the importance of hope, security, and the contributions of Nobel Peace Prize laureates to democracy and human dignity.
reason: Critiques European political trends regarding collaboration with extremist parties and their domestic consequences.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on positive values like hope, security, and the contributions of laureates, which are presented as achievements or ideals rather than urgent threats requiring a crisis response.

paragraph_text: We should not be ashamed that we live in one of the world’s wealthiest countries. But that brings with it obligations. Gro Harlem Brundtland used to put it this way: More than 99 out of every 100 kroner that our nation earns, we spend on ourselves. Is it not then reasonable that we share the last kroner with some of the others? I believe the answer must be yes.
paragraph_summary: The speaker argues that Norway's significant national wealth creates a moral obligation to share resources with other nations.
reason: It connects national wealth to the moral obligation of international aid and sharing resources.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on a moral obligation and the distribution of national wealth rather than framing a situation as an urgent threat or danger to the state.

paragraph_text: As we face the new century, we must set new goals. I would particularly like to highlight one challenge: the meeting between the broader community and the traditional minorities and new immigrants. Being a "community" means finding common ground. If we are to create and preserve a national community, we must address past injustices and create reconciliatory communities. Something positive has happened in that regard: by addressing the Jewish community’s historical grievances, we have rectified some of the injustices that Norwegian Jews suffered during the war. The Sami people have been granted a new status, and I can announce today that the government will propose the establishment of a dedicated fund, as a collective compensation for the damages caused by the assimilation policies inflicted upon the Sami people. We cannot let the turn of the century pass without acknowledging the injustices suffered by many war children in the post-war period. On behalf of the Norwegian state, I apologize for the discrimination and injustice that war children have experienced. Addressing the past is important. But we must also ask ourselves: Are we doing anything today that we might one day need to apologize to our children for? In other words, it is just as important to prevent as it is to rectify. We must ensure that our new countrymen, immigrants from other cultures, do not become new marginalized groups within the broader community. Therefore, they must learn Norwegian, understand our culture and our laws, so that they can participate on an equal footing with others. Therefore, they must have the same rights – the same obligations, the same opportunities. Racism and xenophobia must be un-Norwegian. Equality and respect must be binding national values. Difference must never be a source of hostility, but a source of enriching diversity. To achieve this, we need reconciliatory communities.
paragraph_summary: The speech addresses national reconciliation, historical injustices toward minorities, and the integration of immigrants into Norwegian society through equality and mutual respect.
reason: The paragraph addresses the integration of immigrants and historical reconciliation with minorities within the national community.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on the positive themes of reconciliation, equality, and mutual respect as ongoing societal goals rather than framing these issues as an urgent threat or an active danger to the state.

paragraph_text: Here at home, His Majesty the King has often emphasized the importance of community – also in relation to our new countrymen. It is a worthy continuation of the symbol of unity and community that His Majesty King Olav V was throughout his life – and before him, King Haakon VII. We thank the Royal Family for what they have meant to our nation – in times of war and peace – throughout the century that has passed. We wish them all the best in the coming decades! I extend greetings to all Norwegians serving abroad, in foreign service, peacekeeping forces, missionary work, humanitarian and human rights work, shipping, and other contexts. My wish for the new century is that we all strive for a commitment to humanity – and reconciliatory communities. If we find common ground in this vision, if we find common ground as human beings, as a nation, and as a global community, we can move closer to what Alfred Hauge wrote in his poem, "A Prayer for the Year 2000": "May those in the year 2000 seek wisdom rather than knowledge, love rather than cunning, valuing others more than themselves. Lord, I pray for all people who may live on earth in the year 2000: May they use the sea and our good earth so that there is enough food for everyone. May no child have to reach out for bread without receiving it. But may also humanity’s hunger for the eternal endure. " Alfred Hauge’s thirty-year-old poem challenges us – both as individuals and as a society. In the new century, we must make his vision a reality, so that all children have a future filled with hope. Impossible, you say? No, I say. We must invest heavily for the next century! Happy New Year!
paragraph_summary: The speaker reflects on the Royal Family's legacy, acknowledges Norwegians serving abroad, and advocates for global humanitarianism and sustainable development in the new century.
reason: The paragraph blends national greetings with a vision for a global community and shared humanity.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on reflection, tradition, and aspirational goals for the future rather than identifying a specific threat or urgent danger requiring immediate response.

paragraph_text: In five years, we will celebrate the hundredth anniversary of our national independence. It is certainly worth celebrating. As it did in 1945, when we regained our freedom. But we have gained more. The political record of the century shows a clear positive balance: we have gained independence, freedom, and peace. We have developed a democratic system with voting rights for all. We have achieved a level of prosperity and welfare unprecedented in history. We have gained control of vast oil and gas resources. We have managed to keep unemployment low, far below the average in Western Europe. We have many reasons for self-respect, but no reason for complacency. What we have gained is important, but greatness is measured by what we do for others. The question is: what will we use our national freedom for? Here, we have a legacy to live up to: Norway must be a nation of compassion and solidarity. Let us follow in the footsteps of Fridtjof Nansen by steadfastly supporting refugees and those in need in other countries. Our nation is now taking the lead and cancelling the debt of the poorest developing countries. Norway is among the world's foremost nations in terms of aid. Our vision is that one day, no child should have to reach out for bread without receiving it. If we are remembered as a solidary Norway, Norwegians have reason to be proud.
paragraph_summary: The speaker reflects on Norway's historical achievements and prosperity while emphasizing the nation's moral obligation to practice global solidarity and humanitarian aid.
reason: It balances domestic achievements with Norway's international role in foreign aid and debt relief.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on national achievements, prosperity, and moral values rather than identifying an urgent threat or danger that requires a crisis response.

paragraph_text: Norway must be a technologically pioneering nation. The Vikings' voyages, Roald Amundsen's polar expeditions, and today's giant offshore platforms in the North Sea – these are highlights of Norwegian pioneering spirit within the maritime sector. A growing number of small and medium-sized businesses possess specialized expertise that is in demand in the global market, as is the case with Norwegian internet search engines. We must be at the forefront as a knowledge society, developing our schools, investing in research and development. If we are an innovative nation, we also have something to offer others.
paragraph_summary: The speaker emphasizes the importance of fostering innovation, research, and technological expertise to maintain Norway's status as a pioneering knowledge society.
reason: It links domestic technological innovation and research to Norway's competitiveness in the global market.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on the positive goal of fostering innovation and maintaining status as a knowledge society, which is framed as an achievement or developmental ambition rather than a response to an urgent threat.

paragraph_text: Despite the fact that we continue to suffer from its aggression, this year we have taken significant steps in the fight against terrorism. Violence degrades those who practice it and those who support it, as demonstrated once again by the heinous attacks against public servants and other citizens throughout the year. Following the inhumane kidnapping of José Antonio Ortega Lara, thankfully resolved, and the horrific murder of Miguel Ángel Blanco, Basque society, along with the rest of Spain, rose up to declare, "Enough! " This massive display of public rejection was recently repeated in response to the tragic murder of José Luis Caso. The Spanish people are not alone in this fight against terrorism. We are receiving growing expressions of understanding, support, and cooperation from the international community. On this special night, I pay tribute to all the victims of terrorist violence and stand in solidarity with their families. The mobilization of citizens and the unity of democratic forces offer a glimmer of hope, which I pray that we all may see. Life and freedom, which may seem fragile, ultimately triumph over those who deny them. I would like to express my gratitude to all those who defend them with courage and determination, and in particular, to the men and women of the security forces, whose dedication and sacrifice have saved countless lives and have effectively advanced the pursuit and prevention of terrorist activities.
paragraph_summary: The speaker condemns terrorist violence in Spain, honors its victims, and praises the unity of citizens and security forces in defending democracy and freedom.
reason: It addresses domestic terrorism while acknowledging support from the international community.
crisis_reason: The paragraph frames terrorist violence as a direct threat to democracy and freedom, requiring a defensive response and vigilance, which constitutes an acute crisis.

paragraph_text: Throughout this year, in the daily activities of the Crown, I have consistently observed that the Spanish people continue to uphold the values of hard work, enthusiasm, and generosity in striving to improve our collective life, with a focus on both the present and the future of our country. Spain has successfully achieved the economic objectives we had widely agreed upon, which have made our participation in the establishment of the single European currency, the euro, possible. For many years, it has been a rare opportunity for Spain to be a participant and a driving force, from its very beginning, in a process of such profound importance for our continent. Ultimately, it was the collective effort of Spanish society that made it possible, and it is right that they should receive recognition for this success. We have reached where we are today because we believe in ourselves, we embrace the desire to grow and improve, and we have confidence in our collective aspirations as a nation. The process that led us to converge with our partners in the Economic and Monetary Union required discipline, rigor, and solidarity. Today, and in the future, it is essential that we continue to practice these qualities to ensure the stability of the foundations upon which we have placed the best of ourselves. However, this effort would be meaningless if achieving the objectives of the single European currency did not provide Spain with new opportunities for social and economic progress.
