reason: The paragraph acknowledges various domestic groups contributing to the Spanish economy.
crisis_reason: The paragraph is a ceremonial expression of gratitude and recognition of various societal groups' contributions to national productivity, lacking any framing of threat or urgency.

paragraph_text: And I am thinking of you, our senior citizens and pensioners, who are a vital support to so many families. Thank you for your help. The strength of the family in Spain is extraordinary, and its role is fundamental in this serious crisis. I also thank civil society, which has demonstrated truly exemplary solidarity in assisting millions of people facing severe difficulties. Ultimately, I thank all citizens for their example of responsibility and civic engagement in what are certainly difficult times.
paragraph_summary: The speaker expresses gratitude to senior citizens, families, and civil society for their solidarity and civic responsibility during a period of economic crisis.
reason: It focuses on the role of the family and civil society within Spain.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on gratitude for past solidarity during an economic crisis rather than presenting the crisis as an active, ongoing threat requiring immediate action.

paragraph_text: Please allow me to dedicate a very special and heartfelt memory to the victims of terrorism, with whom society continues to have a lasting debt of gratitude. These are people and families who, for decades, have suffered cruelly from the violence and terror of totalitarian criminals. I know you are going through particularly difficult times. Today, as before and as always, I want to share your pain with renewed solidarity and to express my full support.
paragraph_summary: The speaker expresses solidarity and support for the victims of terrorism and their families who have suffered from long-term violence.
reason: It addresses the domestic issue of victims of terrorism within Spain.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on expressing solidarity and support for victims of past violence rather than framing the situation as an active, ongoing threat requiring urgent response or vigilance.

paragraph_text: It is undeniable that the economic crisis facing Spain has caused discouragement among citizens, and that the difficulty in finding quick solutions, as well as instances of a lack of integrity in public life, have damaged the prestige of politics and institutions. I know that Spanish society is calling today for a profound change in attitude and a commitment to ethical conduct in all areas of political, economic, and social life that will meet the essential requirements of a democracy. It is true that there are voices in our society that want an update of the agreements on coexistence. I am convinced that all these issues can be resolved with realism, with effort, with the proper functioning of the rule of law, and with the generosity of the representative political and social forces.
paragraph_summary: The speaker addresses public disillusionment with the economic crisis and political integrity, advocating for ethical reform and renewed social consensus through the rule of law.
reason: The text discusses the internal crisis of confidence in Spanish institutions and politics.
crisis_reason: The speaker frames public disillusionment and the economic situation as a threat to political integrity and social consensus, explicitly calling for urgent reform to address these dangers.

paragraph_text: Realism, to recognize that the moral health of a society is defined by the ethical behavior of each of its citizens, starting with its leaders, since we are all jointly responsible for the collective future. Effort, so that the economy confirms the signs of recovery that are beginning to be seen, and that must be even stronger, because we cannot accept as normal the anguish of the millions of Spaniards who cannot work. For me, the crisis will begin to resolve when unemployed people have the opportunity to work.
paragraph_summary: The speaker emphasizes the importance of ethical leadership and individual responsibility while calling for economic recovery to address high unemployment rates in Spain.
reason: It emphasizes the need for ethical behavior and economic recovery within the Spanish nation.
crisis_reason: The paragraph frames high unemployment as a problem requiring economic recovery, which constitutes a chronic issue framed as an urgent threat to livelihoods and social stability.

paragraph_text: Proper functioning of the rule of law, so that integrity guides institutions, so that the Constitution and laws are upheld and enforced, and so that differences and controversies are resolved according to the democratic rules approved by all. Respect for those rules is the guarantee of our coexistence and the strength of our democracy. This is an undeniable truth that we must keep in mind. And, as always, generosity, to know when to yield, to understand the reasons of others, and to make dialogue the priority and most effective method for resolving collective problems.
paragraph_summary: The speaker emphasizes the importance of upholding the rule of law, respecting democratic institutions, and prioritizing dialogue to resolve collective societal challenges.
reason: The paragraph focuses on the rule of law and democratic processes within the Spanish state.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on the importance of democratic values and dialogue as a standard for governance rather than framing these as being under an urgent or active threat.

paragraph_text: My position has allowed me to witness the many challenges that Spain has faced throughout its history, a country to which I have dedicated my life. I have seen both difficult and good times, and we Spaniards have always managed to overcome the difficult times together and build the good times together. With that experience, I can say that the political system that was born with the Constitution of 1978 has provided us with the longest period of freedom, coexistence, and prosperity in all of our history, and with effective recognition of the diversity that makes up our reality. It is important that we keep this in mind, as there are often those who seek to have us ignore or forget it when they proclaim a supposed decline in our society and our institutions.
paragraph_summary: The King defends the 1978 Constitution as the foundation for Spain's historical period of freedom, prosperity, and coexistence despite claims of societal decline.
reason: The paragraph discusses the internal political history and constitutional framework of Spain.
crisis_reason: The paragraph frames the constitution as a stable foundation for national success and explicitly rejects the narrative of societal decline, focusing on achievement and tradition rather than an urgent threat.

paragraph_text: Good evening. First and foremost, I want to thank you for opening your homes to me this Christmas Eve. This is a moment, above all, of closeness and reunion; a time to connect, to look at each other with a willingness and desire to understand, to convey to those around us our best feelings of affection, peace, and joy. Tonight, I want to stand by your side to share—in my first Christmas message to you—some reflections on our future, with a hopeful outlook for the year 2015. We are living through complex and difficult times for many citizens and for Spain as a whole. The severity and duration of the economic crisis are causing uncertainty about the future for many families; the importance of some of our political problems is causing concern; and the behavior of some public servants, which falls short of what is expected, is rightly causing indignation and disappointment. These problems have led to serious social concern. However, we must not allow ourselves to be defeated by pessimism, social unrest, or discouragement; instead, we must confront the causes of these problems with firmness and effectiveness, resolve them, and restore the tranquility and serenity that a democratic society like ours requires and deserves.
paragraph_summary: The King addresses Spain's economic crisis, political concerns, and public corruption while urging citizens to confront these challenges with hope and resolve.
reason: The text addresses domestic economic and political challenges facing Spanish citizens.
crisis_reason: The speaker explicitly identifies economic instability, political concerns, and corruption as urgent challenges that threaten the nation, framing them as serious issues requiring collective resolve and action.

paragraph_text: Last October, in Asturias, I stated that we needed role models to admire, ethical principles to recognize, and civic values to preserve. I said that we needed a great collective moral boost. And I want to add now that we need a profound regeneration of our collective life. And in that effort, the fight against corruption is an indispensable goal. It is true that those responsible for these irregular practices are being held accountable; that is a testament to the functioning of our rule of law. It is also true that the vast majority of public servants perform their duties with integrity and a desire to serve the general interest. But it is also necessary—and above all—to prevent these practices from taking root in our society and from being reproduced in the future. Citizens need to be assured that public funds are administered for the legally defined purposes; that there are no favors granted due to holding a public position; that holding a public office is not a means to take advantage or enrich oneself; and that our reputation and good image in the world are not tarnished. Few issues elicit such unanimous opinions. We must eradicate corruption completely and without exception. The integrity of public servants is a fundamental pillar of our coexistence in a Spain that we all want to be healthy and clean.
paragraph_summary: The speaker calls for the eradication of corruption and the promotion of integrity among public servants to ensure ethical governance and national trust.
reason: The focus is on domestic political corruption and the integrity of Spanish public institutions.
crisis_reason: The speaker frames corruption as a fundamental threat to national trust and ethical governance, necessitating urgent action to eradicate it, which constitutes a chronic issue framed as a crisis.

paragraph_text: I also want to talk to you about the economic situation, because it continues to be a major concern for everyone. Unemployment rates are still unacceptable and frustrate the expectations of our young people and of many other men and women who have been out of work for a long time. It is true that our companies are leading in many sectors around the world. But it is also true that our economy has not yet been able to definitively resolve this fundamental imbalance. Nevertheless, it is a very positive fact that the main economic indicators are improving and that we have recovered economic growth and job creation. These figures provide a new basis for hope that, in the future, many more jobs can be generated sustainably, and especially, quality jobs. Therefore, it is evident that the fight against unemployment must continue to be our top priority. The sacrifices and efforts of citizens throughout the economic crisis demand that political, economic, and social agents work together permanently in this direction, prioritizing the interests of citizens above all else. Because the economy must always be at the service of people. Therefore, we must especially protect those who are most vulnerable and disadvantaged. And to that end, we must continue to guarantee our welfare state, which has been a source of social cohesion during these years of crisis, alongside families and solidarity associations and movements. Something we should truly be proud of.
paragraph_summary: The speaker addresses the economic situation, emphasizing the need to prioritize job creation, reduce unemployment, and protect the welfare state for vulnerable citizens.
reason: The paragraph discusses domestic unemployment and the Spanish welfare state.
crisis_reason: The paragraph frames unemployment and the protection of the welfare state as urgent priorities requiring intervention, which constitutes a chronic threat to livelihoods and social stability.

paragraph_text: I now want to address the situation currently unfolding in Catalonia. The Spanish people, exercising their national sovereignty, ratified the Constitution of 1978 through a referendum, which proclaimed our historical and political unity and recognized the right of everyone to feel and be respected in their own identity, culture, traditions, languages, and institutions. “I do not want to end my words without conveying a message of hope to you. Regenerating our political life, regaining the citizens' trust in their institutions, guaranteeing our welfare state, and preserving our unity through diversity are our great challenges. These are not easy tasks. They are not easy challenges. But we will overcome them, I am convinced. We have more than enough capacity and courage. We also have the desire and the will. And we must add to that, confidence in ourselves. ” Under that constitutional spirit, we have lived together these years. Every region, every town and territory in Spain, every citizen, has contributed the best of themselves for the benefit of everyone. And, without a doubt, Catalonia has contributed to the political stability of all of Spain and to its economic progress. It is evident that we all need each other. We are part of a common trunk from which we are complementary to one another, but essential for the progress of each individually and of all together. But it is not only about the economy or about interests; it is also and above all, about feelings. Millions of Spaniards carry Catalonia in their hearts. And, likewise, for millions of Catalans, other Spaniards are part of their own being. That is why I am pained and concerned by the possibility of emotional fractures, estrangement, or rejection between families, friends, or citizens. No one in Spain today is an adversary of anyone. And what makes Spain a nation with a unique strength is the sum of our differences, which we must understand and respect, and which should always bring us closer together and never drive us apart. Because everything we have achieved together stems from the strength of unity. And the strength of that unity is what will allow us to go further and better in a world that does not accept either weakness or division among societies, and that is moving towards greater integration. Disagreements are not resolved with emotional or sentimental ruptures. Let us all make a sincere and honest effort, and let us reconnect with what we should never lose: mutual affection and the feelings we share. Let us respect the Constitution, which is the guarantee of a democratic, orderly, peaceful, and free coexistence. Let us continue building, together, a project that respects our diversity and generates hope and confidence in the future. Because we need, also, hope and confidence.
paragraph_summary: The speaker advocates for national unity and constitutional adherence to address political tensions and potential emotional fractures regarding the situation in Catalonia.
reason: The text addresses the internal political situation regarding Catalonia and national unity.
crisis_reason: The speaker frames political tensions and potential fractures in Catalonia as a threat to national unity and constitutional order, requiring urgent attention and adherence to the law.

paragraph_text: Last June, Spain demonstrated to itself and to the world an example of seriousness and dignity in the process of my father, King Juan Carlos’s, abdication and my own proclamation as King; all in accordance with our Constitution. And throughout these past months, you have surrounded me with your respect, affection, and warmth. Honestly, I have felt valued and appreciated, and I thank you from the bottom of my heart. And I must tell you that I have also seen hope in many of you, in your eyes, in your words, at the beginning of a new era in our history. It is true that we are living through complex and difficult times. Without a doubt. But they are also times that we must face with responsibility, with hope, and with a spirit of renewal. New times that are projected in all areas of our collective and individual lives. And now, it is up to the Spaniards of today to continue the task of building our best future; a future that begins now, that has already begun. Fortunately, we are not starting from scratch, not at all, and therefore, we must not forget what we have achieved together, through great effort and sacrifice, generation after generation; and that is a great deal, and we must value it with pride. Although we also have the responsibility to correct our shortcomings and to improve and enhance the strengths of Spain today, with our sights set on a future that belongs to all Spaniards. We are a consolidated democracy. We enjoy a political stability as never before in our history. Our constitutional framework has allowed for political alternation based on free and democratic elections. We are, furthermore, a nation respected and valued around the world, with a deep universal vocation, essential for promoting our culture and defending our interests in a globalized world. Today, more than ever, we are a fundamental part of a European project that makes us stronger, more competitive, and more prominent in a future of integration. As I said in my proclamation speech, every political era has its own challenges. We must continue to advance in our political coexistence, step by step, adapting it to the needs of our time. We must modernize and update the functioning of our democratic society and ensure that citizens regain their trust in our institutions. Institutions that are vigorous and dynamic, that people can feel are their own.
paragraph_summary: King Felipe VI reflects on his recent accession, the strength of Spain's democracy, and the need for institutional renewal and national progress.
reason: The paragraph reflects on domestic political stability and the transition of the Spanish monarchy.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on institutional renewal and national progress as positive, forward-looking goals rather than framing the state of democracy as an urgent threat or danger.

paragraph_text: I do not want to end my words without sending you a message of hope. Regenerating our political life, regaining the citizens’ trust in their institutions, guaranteeing our welfare state, and preserving our unity through diversity are our great challenges. They are not easy tasks. They are not easy challenges. But we will overcome them, without a doubt; I am convinced of that. We have more than enough capacity and courage. We also have the desire and the will. And we must add to that, confidence in ourselves. That is the key to our hope for the future. The key to regaining pride in our national identity: that of a modern Spain, with deep democratic convictions, diverse, open to the world, supportive, powerful, and dynamic. With that same dynamism and with the example with which you face your daily lives, fighting against adversity, striving to progress, honestly seeking to improve your lives and the lives of your families. And I will always be by your side as the first servant of the Spanish people. Thank you again for listening to me tonight, and many congratulations on behalf of the Queen, the Princess of Asturias, and the Infanta Sofía. Merry Christmas, Eguberri on, Bon Nadal, Boas Festas.
paragraph_summary: The King expresses confidence in Spain's ability to overcome political, institutional, and social challenges while fostering national unity and pride.
reason: The text focuses on domestic challenges like political trust and national identity.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on confidence, national unity, and pride, framing the situation as an achievement or a positive outlook rather than an urgent threat requiring immediate defensive action.

paragraph_text: On the other hand, improving the economy is a priority for everyone. I believe that all institutions have a duty to citizens, families, and especially young people, so they can regain something that should never be lost: the peace of mind and stability needed to face the future and the hope for a fulfilling life. We all desire sustained economic growth, a growth that allows us to continue creating jobs – and decent jobs – that strengthens essential public services like healthcare and education, and that helps reduce inequalities exacerbated by the harshness of the economic crisis.
paragraph_summary: The speaker emphasizes the need for sustained economic growth to create jobs, strengthen public services, and reduce inequalities for the benefit of all citizens.
reason: The paragraph discusses domestic economic priorities and public services like healthcare and education.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on the positive goals of economic growth, job creation, and service improvement as standard governance objectives rather than framing these issues as an urgent threat or a state of emergency.

paragraph_text: Finally, I do not want to say goodbye tonight without telling you, with complete conviction, that it is up to the Spaniards of today to continue writing the story of our time, and that we will do so as we have already demonstrated that we can: by including everyone – men and women, young and old, born here or coming from elsewhere – by encouraging everyone to move forward together, without leaving anyone behind. We must look to the future, because in today’s world, no one waits for those who only look back. We must banish divisions and resentments, and replace selfishness with generosity, pessimism with hope, and despair with solidarity. Let us have faith and believe in our country. Spain has a remarkable resilience, a capacity for overcoming adversity, and an inner strength far greater than we sometimes realize. The strength of Spain lies within us – in our courage, our character, and our talent. It also lies, why not say so, in our way of life and our understanding of life. Spaniards have never given up in the face of difficulties, which have been great, and we have always overcome them. And we know, furthermore, that we must continue to move forward with a spirit of understanding and a sense of unity, as I mentioned at the beginning. Through dialogue and commitment, with a sense of duty and responsibility, feeling and living, each and every one of us, that ethical commitment that makes a people great. Let us unite our hearts, because decades ago, the Spanish people decided, once and for all and forever, to extend a hand to one another, not a back. Let us do so with all the strength and confidence of those who are rightly proud of what we have achieved together, and above all, of what we will achieve together. With that emotion, with that confidence in our future – in that future for Spain that I believe in – I wish you all a very Merry Christmas, Eguberri on, Bon Nadal, Boas Festas, and a prosperous 2016.
paragraph_summary: The speaker calls for national unity, social solidarity, and collective progress among Spaniards to overcome challenges and build a prosperous future.
reason: The text focuses on national unity, domestic social cohesion, and the future of Spain.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on national unity and collective progress as a positive, aspirational goal for the future rather than framing specific challenges as urgent, existential threats.

paragraph_text: Tonight, I address you from the Royal Palace, where the Crown is celebrating state events, in which we want to express, with the utmost dignity and solemnity, the greatness of Spain. This Palace belongs to all Spaniards and is a symbol of our history, open to all citizens who wish to better understand and learn about our past. Within its ceilings, its walls, its paintings and tapestries, in short, throughout its entire heritage, centuries of our shared history are reflected. And that history, without a doubt, we must know and remember, because it helps us understand our present and guide our future, and also allows us to better appreciate our successes and our failures; because history, moreover, defines and explains our identity over time.
paragraph_summary: The King emphasizes the importance of Spain's historical heritage and national identity as reflected in the Royal Palace.
reason: The paragraph discusses the historical heritage and identity of Spain through the Royal Palace.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on national identity and historical heritage as a source of pride and tradition, which does not involve any framing of threat, urgency, or danger.

paragraph_text: I sincerely believe that we are living in times when it is more necessary than ever to recognize what unites us. It is necessary to value what we have built together over the years, with so much sacrifice, but also with generosity and immense dedication. It is necessary to celebrate everything that we are, what makes us, and makes us feel Spanish.
paragraph_summary: The speaker emphasizes the importance of national unity and collective pride in Spanish identity and shared achievements.
reason: The text emphasizes national unity and the shared identity of the Spanish people.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on national unity, pride, and shared achievements, which are affirmative and ceremonial themes rather than descriptions of a threat or urgent danger.

paragraph_text: In my inaugural address, I stated that within the constitutional Spain, all feelings and sensitivities are welcome, that different ways of feeling Spanish are accepted; of being and feeling part of the same political and social community, of the same historical, current, and future reality, as is our nation. A great nation defined by a culture that has transcended time and borders, by the arts and by a universal literature, enriched by our common language, alongside the other languages of Spain, which also explain our identity. A country that, over the centuries, has been shaped by thinkers, scientists, creators, and so many men and women, many of whom have given their lives for Spain. And it is also a great state, whose strength today is based on the same constitutional values that we share and on the common rules of coexistence that we have established and that unite us; a state that recognizes our diversity in the self-governance of our regions and nationalities, and that places the democratic will of all Spaniards, expressed through the law, at the foundation of our freedom.
paragraph_summary: The speaker emphasizes Spanish national unity, cultural diversity, and the importance of constitutional values and democratic coexistence within the state.
reason: The paragraph discusses the constitutional values and cultural identity of the Spanish state.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on national identity, constitutional values, and democratic coexistence as positive, foundational elements of the state rather than framing them as under immediate or urgent threat.

paragraph_text: For all these reasons, we – I – have many reasons to affirm tonight that being and feeling Spanish, wanting, admiring, and respecting Spain, is a deep feeling, a sincere emotion, and a very legitimate source of pride. With these reasons, and sharing these feelings, we will honor our history, of which we are today the protagonists, and whose great legacy we have a responsibility to manage; and we will strengthen our national unity, which is essential to drive our political, civic, and moral progress, to drive our common project of coexistence. Because now, more than anything, what should matter to all of us is Spain and the general interest of the Spanish people.
paragraph_summary: The speaker emphasizes the importance of national pride, historical responsibility, and unity to ensure Spain's political, civic, and moral progress.
reason: The text focuses on national pride and the importance of the general interest of Spain.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on national values, historical responsibility, and progress, which are presented as positive, aspirational goals rather than urgent threats or dangers.

paragraph_text: Following the general elections held on the 20th of this month, and as always after each renewal of the Congress of Deputies and the Senate, the procedure established in our Constitution for the governance of our country is initiated. In a constitutional and democratic parliamentary monarchy like ours, the General Cortes, as the repository of national sovereignty, hold the power to make decisions on matters that concern and affect all Spaniards: they are the venue where, after debate and dialogue between political forces, essential national issues must be addressed and decided. ". . . With these reasons, and sharing these feelings, we will honor our history, of which we are today the protagonists, and whose great legacy we have a responsibility to manage; and we will strengthen our national unity, which is essential to drive our political, civic and moral progress, to drive our common project of coexistence. Because now, more than anything, what should matter to all of us is Spain and the general interest of the Spanish people. . . . "
paragraph_summary: The speaker discusses the constitutional process following general elections and emphasizes the importance of national unity and the general interest of the Spanish people.
reason: The paragraph explains the domestic constitutional procedure for forming a government after elections.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on the routine constitutional process and the abstract values of unity and national interest, lacking any language describing a threat, danger, or urgent problem.

paragraph_text: The political pluralism expressed in the ballot box undoubtedly brings different sensitivities, perspectives, and viewpoints; and it entails a form of politics based on dialogue, consensus, and commitment, with the aim of making the best decisions that resolve the problems of citizens. Spain is beginning a new legislative term that requires all the efforts, all the energy, and all the commitment of our democratic institutions, to ensure and consolidate what has been achieved over the last few decades and to adapt our political progress to the reality of Spanish society today. Dynamic institutions that always move in step with the Spanish people they serve and represent; and that are sensitive to the demands for rigor, integrity, and honesty that citizens expect from public life.
paragraph_summary: The speech emphasizes the need for political dialogue, consensus, and institutional integrity to address the evolving needs of Spanish society in the new legislative term.
reason: The text discusses the need for domestic political dialogue and institutional reform.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on routine governance, political dialogue, and institutional processes rather than framing the situation as an urgent threat or danger to the state.

paragraph_text: Today's Spain is very different from the Spain of centuries past, thanks to a genuine and generous willingness to understand among all Spaniards, a sincere spirit of reconciliation and overcoming our historical differences, and a commitment by political and social forces to serve an entire people, to the general interests of the nation, which must always come first. This is the great lesson of our most recent history that we must never forget. Nor should we forget that breaking the law, imposing an idea or a project from some onto the will of other Spaniards, has only led us in our history to decline, impoverishment, and isolation. That is a mistake from our past that we must not repeat. Our path is now, irrevocably, the path of understanding, coexistence, and harmony in democracy and freedom. Therefore, respecting our constitutional order is defending the democratic coexistence approved by all the Spanish people; it is defending the rights and freedoms of all citizens, and it is also defending our cultural and territorial diversity. That is why, tonight, I want to reiterate a message of serenity, of reassurance, and of confidence in the unity and continuity of Spain; a message of security in the primacy and defense of our Constitution.
paragraph_summary: The speaker emphasizes the importance of national unity, democratic coexistence, and adherence to the Spanish Constitution to ensure the country's stability and future.
reason: The paragraph emphasizes the importance of the Spanish Constitution for domestic coexistence.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on the importance of national unity and constitutional adherence as a positive, foundational principle for stability rather than framing these as responses to an urgent or active threat.

paragraph_text: I would also like to convey a message of hope, believing that thoughtful reflection, sincere and loyal discussion, and respect for both the reality of our history and the close-knit community of affections and interests among all Spaniards, will nurture the enduring spirit of our constitution.
paragraph_summary: The King calls for reflection, dialogue, and respect for history and community to strengthen the enduring spirit of the Spanish constitution.
reason: The text calls for internal reflection and respect for the Spanish constitutional order.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on reflection, dialogue, and the enduring spirit of the constitution as a positive, foundational value rather than framing these as responses to an urgent threat or danger.

paragraph_text: Good evening. At this hour, during the Christmas season, I want to extend, together with the Queen and our daughters Leonor and Sofía, my warmest wishes for a joyful holiday season and my hope that 2017 will be a better year for everyone. And on a night like tonight, I want to especially acknowledge the families who have suffered from the recent floods in our country. We are deeply aware of your struggles. Christmas is a celebration of birth, and celebrating that birth with joy means having faith in the future. It is in moments like these, when our personal and collective feelings of affection, friendship, and brotherhood, forged through our shared experiences, remind us of the great common heritage we share. A heritage that deserves our care and that we must all help to protect, as it represents the best of who we are and what unites us. As is tradition, allow me this evening to share with you some reflections on our present and our future, drawing from all that we have experienced, especially during this year, 2016, to help us move forward.

reason: The paragraph addresses domestic issues such as recent floods and national heritage.
crisis_reason: The paragraph combines ceremonial holiday greetings with a reflection on national unity; while it mentions flood victims, it does not frame the floods as an ongoing, urgent threat requiring action, but rather as a point of sympathy within a broader message of national cohesion.

paragraph_text: I would not like to take up any more of your time this evening, a night that should be a time for family celebration; but I would like to conclude by saying that I genuinely believe in a Spain that is aware, supportive, firm in its values, and far from pessimism, disillusionment, or cynicism. I believe in a Spain that is determined to overcome the challenges that, although significant, are also surmountable. And I have no doubt that we will be able to overcome them if we understand that these are no longer times to close in on ourselves, but to open up to the world; if we are clear that they are not times for fractures or internal divisions, but for focusing on what unites us, building on our diversity. These are times to deepen our commitment to an open and welcoming Spain, with outstretched hands, where no one stirs up old resentments or reopens old wounds. Ultimately, these are times when we have more than sufficient reasons and motivations to unite, to work together, from every corner of our great country, with enthusiasm, with ideals, and with projects for a better Spain. That is how I feel, and that is what I believe. And with that deep conviction, I wish you all, and each and every one of you and your families, a very happy Christmas. Happy Christmas / Merry Christmas / Happy Holidays. Good night. And a very happy and prosperous 2017.
paragraph_summary: The King calls for national unity, social cohesion, and optimism to overcome Spain's challenges while fostering an open and welcoming society.
reason: The text focuses on national unity and overcoming internal divisions within Spain.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on national unity and optimism as a general aspiration for the future rather than framing specific challenges as urgent, active threats to the state or society.

paragraph_text: It has always been said that the most difficult times in life are the best opportunities to discover our inner strength, to test our character, and to understand our true potential. Throughout this year, I have visited many places across our country. And I must tell you that, during my travels through our towns and cities, I have seen difficulties and challenges for many of our fellow citizens. But I have also witnessed hard work, honesty, dedication, great talent, and, above all, determination and a desire to succeed. I have seen, once again, the value that family holds in our society, as its support has helped so many to overcome the most difficult moments.
paragraph_summary: The speaker reflects on the resilience, hard work, and importance of family support demonstrated by citizens while facing challenges throughout the past year.
reason: The paragraph focuses on the internal social challenges and resilience of the Spanish people.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on resilience and the importance of family support in the past tense, framing the year as a period of overcoming challenges rather than presenting an active, ongoing threat that requires urgent intervention.

paragraph_text: I have met workers and professionals, men and women who, with their quiet dedication, have persevered through these long and difficult years, without giving up or resigning, supporting their families, their lives, and their livelihoods with great dignity and courage. I have also seen in many of our citizens the resolve to take risks to create or defend jobs, and the strength to get back on their feet and continue their work after seeing projects built with passion and great sacrifice destroyed. I could provide countless examples of solidarity. Many of you generously share your knowledge, your time, and your efforts, and above all, your hearts, to help others. You are able to react to any emergency, proving time and time again that wherever needed, wherever a word of encouragement or a helping hand is required, there is a Spaniard who demonstrates, through their actions, the greatness and the deepest soul of our land.
paragraph_summary: The speaker praises the resilience, dedication, and solidarity of Spanish citizens in overcoming personal and professional hardships.
reason: It highlights the domestic efforts of Spanish workers and citizens in supporting their communities.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on the successful overcoming of hardships and praises the resilience of the citizens, which frames the situation as a past achievement or recovery rather than an active, ongoing threat.

paragraph_text: I have also, and continue to witness, the work of so many public servants who, with an extraordinary commitment to serving the community, guarantee our freedoms, care for our hospitals, and educate our children. Many of our citizens, both within and outside of Spain, work to ensure our safety, defend our values, and contribute to the advancement of science and the enrichment of our culture. They are the image of our country and also make it possible for our state to function and for us to celebrate a day like today. All of this, for me and for all of us, is a reason to feel genuinely proud, and it is also a reason for hope, because a society that maintains these attitudes, convictions, and values cannot be afraid of the future. I am confident that our collective memory will reserve a place of honor in history for these times of sacrifice and dedication, but also of generosity and perseverance. But we must continue to look forward, building our country and also building Europe. We must strive, step by step, day by day, with a positive spirit, so that prosperity and well-being are the foundation of a fulfilling and hopeful life together. And that is why I would like to address several specific matters tonight:
paragraph_summary: The speaker commends the dedication of public servants and citizens while emphasizing the importance of national progress, European integration, and collective prosperity.
reason: The text discusses domestic public services and the national goal of building the country.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on commendation, national progress, and prosperity, which are affirmative and celebratory themes rather than threats requiring urgent response.

paragraph_text: It is true that the crisis has imposed great sacrifices. Today, however, we live with the hope of recovery, which we have already begun. We all wish for that recovery to be consolidated, allowing us to create even more jobs, and of a higher quality, and to correct the inequalities resulting from a crisis as profound as the one we have experienced, as well as to strengthen, in general, our social cohesion, which is a guarantee of ensuring the stability and balance of our society. In that regard, it is very important for all of us that many families can regain their standard of living, and that our young people have opportunities for a future filled with hope and confidence. Above all, those who are most vulnerable or disadvantaged must have the certainty that they will not be left behind as Spain moves forward in the 21st century.
paragraph_summary: The speaker discusses economic recovery, job creation, social cohesion, and support for vulnerable populations to ensure future stability and equality in Spain.
reason: It addresses domestic economic recovery, social cohesion, and the welfare of Spanish citizens.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on economic recovery, job creation, and social stability as positive outcomes and future goals, rather than framing current conditions as an urgent, ongoing threat.

paragraph_text: Furthermore, we have overcome a complex political situation that you are all familiar with. It is now important that our society has regained a sense of calm, and that citizens can have the peace of mind necessary to pursue their life goals. Equally essential for the future is that dialogue and understanding between political groups can preserve and promote the basic consensus necessary for the proper functioning of our society.
paragraph_summary: The speaker emphasizes the importance of political stability, social calm, and consensus-building among political groups to ensure the effective functioning of society.
reason: The paragraph discusses internal political stability and dialogue between domestic political groups.
crisis_reason: the paragraph focuses on the importance of stability and consensus as a general principle of governance rather than framing a specific threat or urgent danger to the state.

paragraph_text: And in that endeavor, education – and it will undoubtedly remain – the essential key. An education that ensures and constantly updates our knowledge, but also fosters language and culture, civic responsibility, and values; that prepares our young people to be citizens of this new world, more free and more capable, and able to draw on the experience of our elders. An education that encourages research, drives innovation, promotes creativity, and fosters an entrepreneurial spirit, as essential qualities and demands of the society of the future – a society that is already ours today.
paragraph_summary: The speaker emphasizes the role of education in fostering civic values, innovation, and entrepreneurial skills to prepare youth for the demands of modern society.
reason: It emphasizes the importance of education as a domestic tool for preparing citizens for the future.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on the positive role of education in fostering values and skills for the future, which is framed as a routine developmental goal rather than an urgent threat or crisis.

paragraph_text: We have many other concerns, of course, but tonight I don't want to forget the women who, often silenced by fear, suffer from gender-based violence. An unacceptable scourge that wounds our deepest feelings and shames and outrages us. Let us maintain our resolve and political support to help and defend the victims, and let us raise awareness throughout society against that violence, which is criminal and cowardly, and which degrades our shared existence.
paragraph_summary: The speaker calls for societal awareness and political support to combat the issue of gender-based violence and protect its victims.
reason: It addresses the domestic issue of gender-based violence within Spanish society.
crisis_reason: The speaker frames gender-based violence as an urgent issue requiring societal awareness and political intervention, treating it as a serious threat to safety and social integrity.

paragraph_text: 2018 awaits us in a few days, and we must continue building our country, because history does not stand still. And we have not come this far to fear the future, but to create it. And I am certain that no one desires a paralyzed or complacent Spain, but a modern and appealing Spain, one that inspires hope; a Spain that is calm, but in motion, and ready to evolve and adapt to the changing times. Let us, without reservation, feel proud of everything we have achieved, because it is a testament to us all; let us trust in what has always united us, in who we are, just as we are, and above all, in what we can achieve together with a firm belief in our convictions and our abilities. If we continue on that path, if we do things that way, and with all our energy, I am convinced that the coming year – and the years that follow – will be much better. Without a doubt. That is my wish for everyone this special night. Thank you very much. Merry Christmas, Happy Holidays, and a Happy and prosperous New Year 2018. Good night.
paragraph_summary: The King of Spain calls for national unity, progress, and confidence in the country's future as it prepares to enter 2018.
reason: The paragraph focuses on national progress and the future development of Spain.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on national unity, progress, and confidence, which are affirmative and ceremonial themes rather than descriptions of an urgent threat or danger.

paragraph_text: 2017 has, without a doubt, been a difficult year for Spain, a year marked, above all, by the situation in Catalonia, which I will address shortly. But it has also been a year in which we have witnessed the deeply felt, firm, and sincere commitment of the Spanish people to the democratic Spain that we have built together.
paragraph_summary: The King reflects on the challenges posed by the 2017 Catalan political crisis while commending the Spanish people's commitment to democratic values.
reason: It discusses the internal political situation in Catalonia and national democratic commitment.
crisis_reason: The paragraph explicitly identifies the situation as a 'political crisis' and frames it as a challenge to democratic values, which constitutes a threat to the integrity of the state.

paragraph_text: Over the past 40 years, we have managed to create a new and modern country, one among the most advanced in the world. We have firmly established democracy, even overcoming, decades ago, an attempt to revert to a less free system of rights and liberties. We are an integral part of a European Union with which we share objectives and a common vision of the world. Against terrorism, we have managed to uphold life, dignity, and freedom, with the strength of our democratic convictions. And, ultimately, we have carried out the most profound transformation in our history across many areas of our lives: in education and culture, in healthcare and social services, in infrastructure and communications, or in defense and citizen security. In short, over all these years of democratic coexistence, the rights and freedoms, the progress and modernization of Spain, and also its international projection and relevance, have gone hand in hand. And all that great change, that unprecedented leap in our history, has been possible thanks to an open and supportive Spain, not one that is closed in on itself; a Spain that recognizes and respects our differences, our plurality, and our diversity, with an inclusive spirit; a Spain inspired by an unwavering commitment to harmony.
paragraph_summary: The speaker reflects on Spain's democratic development, modernization, and social progress over the past four decades, emphasizing the importance of unity and diversity.
reason: The text reviews the domestic development and modernization of Spain over the last 40 years.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on historical achievements, modernization, and national values, which are explicitly excluded from the definition of a crisis.

paragraph_text: Along the path we have traveled, it is certainly necessary to acknowledge that not everything has been successful; that difficult and complex situations persist that need to be corrected, and that require a commitment from all of society to overcome them. Despite all of this, the overwhelmingly positive record of these years is undeniable. We must appreciate and value it. It is worthwhile, and we deserve it as a country and as a society. Because the history of the Spain that we have built together is the story of a great triumph for all Spaniards. A Spain to which we must not renounce, a Spain that should inspire and motivate us, and a Spain that we must continue to build, improve, and modernize, based on the solid foundation of democratic principles and civic values of respect and dialogue that underpin our coexistence.
paragraph_summary: The speaker reflects on Spain's democratic progress and collective achievements while acknowledging persistent challenges that require continued national commitment and civic cooperation.
reason: It reflects on the domestic progress and the need for continued national improvement.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on national progress and collective achievements, framing challenges as routine matters requiring ongoing cooperation rather than as urgent threats to the state or society.

paragraph_text: These principles and values, as we have seen even in this year 2017, are deeply rooted in our society, in the daily lives of our citizens, and have very deep roots in the consciences and feelings of the Spanish people. Much more than we could have imagined. Spain is today a mature democracy, where any citizen can think, defend, and freely and democratically contrast their opinions and ideas; but not impose their own ideas against the rights of others. Respecting and preserving the principles and values of our democratic state of law is essential to guarantee coexistence that ensures "freedom, equality, justice, and political pluralism, " as stated in our Constitution. Because when these basic principles are undermined, coexistence first deteriorates and then becomes unsustainable.
paragraph_summary: The King emphasizes the importance of upholding democratic principles, constitutional values, and mutual respect to ensure sustainable coexistence within Spanish society.
reason: The paragraph discusses the importance of the Spanish Constitution and domestic democratic values.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on the affirmation of values and the promotion of social cohesion as a positive, routine goal rather than framing the current state of democracy as an urgent threat or a crisis.

paragraph_text: A few days ago, the citizens of Catalonia voted to elect their representatives to the Parliament, who must now address the problems that affect all Catalans, respecting plurality and thinking responsibly about the common good of all. The path cannot lead back to confrontation or exclusion, which – as we now know – only generates discord, uncertainty, discouragement, and moral, civic, and, of course, economic impoverishment for an entire society. Instead, the path must lead to restoring serenity, stability, and mutual respect within Catalan society – a society that is as diverse and plural as it is – so that ideas do not distance or separate families and friends. The path must also lead to restoring the confidence, prestige, and best image of Catalonia, and to reaffirming the values that have always characterized its identity and have given it its best moments: its capacity for leadership and effort, its creative spirit and openness, its commitment, and its sense of responsibility.
paragraph_summary: The speaker urges newly elected Catalan representatives to prioritize social stability, mutual respect, and economic recovery over political confrontation and division.
reason: It addresses the internal political situation and social cohesion in Catalonia.
crisis_reason: The speaker frames political confrontation and division as a threat to social stability and economic recovery, demanding a shift in priorities to mitigate these dangers.

paragraph_text: But overcoming the coexistence problems generated by this situation must not lead us to forget, of course, other serious concerns and challenges facing Spanish society, which also condition our future, and to which I will briefly refer: Our economy and employment have improved significantly, but creating stable jobs must always be an essential and top priority goal. Equally, we cannot overlook the obligation and responsibility of addressing social inequality and differences, especially after the consequences of the recent economic crisis, which has caused so much damage to so many families and has affected the future of so many young people.
paragraph_summary: The speaker emphasizes the need to prioritize stable job creation and address social inequality to support families and youth following the economic crisis.
reason: It focuses on domestic economic priorities and social inequality within Spain.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on the recovery phase and the subsequent policy goals of job creation and social support, rather than framing the economic situation as an active, ongoing threat.

paragraph_text: Corruption remains a major concern for society, demanding that measures continue to be taken to eradicate it completely and that citizens can fully trust the proper administration of public funds.
paragraph_summary: The speaker emphasizes the need for continued anti-corruption measures to ensure public trust in the administration of state funds.
reason: It addresses the domestic issue of corruption and the administration of public funds.
crisis_reason: The speaker frames corruption as a threat to public trust and the integrity of state administration, necessitating urgent and continued intervention.

paragraph_text: Over the past few years, on Christmas Eve, I have shared with you my concerns and reflections on our democracy, on social and territorial cohesion, on the economy and unemployment, or on education; also on corruption, terrorism, or violence against women—a violence that is sadly prevalent and deserves our strongest condemnation and the commitment of all of society to eradicate it from our lives. These issues continue to concern us deeply, of course. Allow me to speak to you tonight about something else that I believe is very important. I want to talk about our coexistence, about the principles that inspire it, thinking about the present and also about the future, about our younger generations, to whom I will address myself especially today.
paragraph_summary: The speaker transitions from addressing ongoing societal challenges to discussing the principles of coexistence and the future for younger generations.
reason: The speaker discusses domestic social issues and national coexistence.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on principles of coexistence and future prospects for younger generations, which are presented as aspirational or reflective topics rather than urgent threats requiring immediate crisis response.

paragraph_text: On December 6th, on the occasion of the 40th anniversary of our Constitution, I once again recalled the ideals that inspired and united the Spanish people during the political transition, and that have been the foundation, the basis of our freedom and our progress over the past 40 years: reconciliation and harmony, dialogue and understanding, integration and solidarity. Those who lived through those years know well that what defined them was the value of working together, united, thinking about our country and its future; it was the willingness of the Spanish people to understand each other, and of political, economic, and social leaders to reach agreements, despite being very different in their ideas and feelings. They were all united by a very clear goal: democracy and freedom in Spain; to define common rules that would guarantee our coexistence. And they achieved it.
paragraph_summary: The speaker reflects on the 40th anniversary of the Spanish Constitution, emphasizing the importance of national unity, reconciliation, and democratic consensus during the political transition.
reason: The paragraph focuses on the history and constitutional foundations of Spain.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on historical reflection, national unity, and the celebration of a democratic milestone, which are framed as achievements and traditions rather than urgent threats.

paragraph_text: We have all benefited from that success in subsequent generations, and what we must do today is everything in our power to ensure that those principles are not lost or forgotten, that the rules that apply to everyone are respected by everyone. And in that way, we can ensure that young people have greater guarantees of new decades of progress and advancement, as we have fortunately experienced. Those of you who were protagonists or witnesses of the transition, I am sure you will understand very well what I have just said. For those who did not live through that time, and especially for the younger generations, we have a duty to involve you in that period of our history, in that path that allows you to understand why and how Spain achieved the most radical change in its history; why and how our society has advanced and prospered so much since then.
paragraph_summary: The speaker emphasizes the importance of preserving the principles of Spain's democratic transition to ensure future progress and prosperity for younger generations.
reason: The text emphasizes the importance of national history and constitutional principles for future generations in Spain.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on the preservation of democratic principles as a positive, foundational goal for future prosperity rather than framing the current state of democracy as an urgent threat or a crisis.

paragraph_text: Today, as we live in a established democracy and share the same principles and values with other democracies in our region, it is essential that we constantly ensure our coexistence. A coexistence that is based on consideration and respect for people, ideas, and the rights of others; that requires us to nurture and strengthen the deep bonds that unite us and that should always unite all Spanish people; a coexistence that is incompatible with resentment and bitterness, because these attitudes are part of our worst history, and we must not allow them to resurface; a coexistence in which overcoming major problems and injustices can never come from division, let alone confrontation, but from agreement and unity in the face of challenges and difficulties.
paragraph_summary: The speaker emphasizes the importance of national unity, mutual respect, and democratic coexistence among Spanish citizens to overcome challenges and avoid historical division.
reason: The speaker addresses the importance of national unity and internal social cohesion.
crisis_reason: The paragraph frames national division as a threat to democratic coexistence and stability, positioning the need for unity as an urgent response to avoid historical damage.

paragraph_text: Ultimately, a coexistence that demands respect for our Constitution; a Constitution that is not a static reality, but a living reality that protects, safeguards, and guarantees our rights and freedoms. All projects need solid foundations, and today’s Spain has them, because they are built on a firm commitment to harmony, peace, and understanding. And that is the reflection I wanted to share with you tonight: that coexistence—which is always fragile, let us not forget—is the greatest asset we Spanish people have. It is the most valuable work of our democracy and the best legacy that we can entrust to the younger generations; and therefore, we must avoid it deteriorating or eroding; we must defend it, care for it, protect it; and do so with responsibility and conviction.
paragraph_summary: The speaker emphasizes the importance of protecting and maintaining constitutional coexistence as the foundation of Spanish democracy and a legacy for future generations.
reason: The focus is on the protection and maintenance of the Spanish Constitution as a national asset.
crisis_reason: The paragraph frames constitutional coexistence as a foundational value and a legacy to be maintained, which is an affirmative and ceremonial reflection on national identity rather than a response to an urgent threat.

paragraph_text: We have demonstrated that over the past forty years of freedom because we have believed in ourselves and in our own strength. Now, we must value with pride who we are, what we have achieved, and trust in what we can accomplish together, setting aside disillusionment or pessimism. We must be aware of the new reality imposed upon us by the 21st century, and be capable of reaching civic and social agreements that secure Spain’s great modernization project. With that hope and confidence in the future, let us move forward. With courage and without rest. Because we can all do so much for the common good, and strive to improve every day; each of us doing what is our responsibility and supporting those who fulfill their obligations; encouraging those who need it—ensuring that no one is left behind—and uniting all our strengths in the desire for a better Spain, because the Spanish people deserve it.
paragraph_summary: The speaker calls for national unity, civic cooperation, and collective effort to modernize Spain and ensure the country's future prosperity.
reason: The paragraph calls for national unity and internal social agreements to improve Spain.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on modernization and future prosperity, which are framed as positive goals and collective achievements rather than responses to an urgent threat or danger.

paragraph_text: Everything we have achieved – as I have mentioned on other occasions – has not been generated spontaneously. It is, ultimately, the result of millions of Spaniards, thanks to our Constitution, who, over the years, have shared the same values upon which to base our coexistence. " Among those values, I would like to highlight first and foremost the desire for harmony, which, thanks to responsibility, affection, generosity, dialogue, and respect among people with very different ideologies, has torn down walls of intolerance, resentment, and misunderstanding that have marked so many episodes in our history. Secondly, the will to understand and integrate our differences within the framework of our Constitution, which recognizes the territorial diversity that defines us and preserves the unity that gives us strength. And thirdly, and finally, the defense and promotion of solidarity, equality, and freedom as fundamental principles of our society, making tolerance and respect expressions of the best civic spirit of our shared life.
paragraph_summary: The speaker attributes Spain's achievements to constitutional values of harmony, territorial integration, and the promotion of solidarity, equality, and freedom.
reason: The text highlights the values and constitutional framework that define Spanish society.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on celebrating national values and achievements rather than identifying a threat or an urgent problem requiring action.

paragraph_text: These values have been present among us for many years and constitute a defining characteristic of Spain today; but we cannot take them for granted, nor can we forget their fragility. Therefore, we must do everything possible to strengthen them and prevent them from deteriorating. Time does not stand still, and Spain cannot remain static, nor can it lag behind events. It must continue on its path, without isolating itself as it has done in other periods of the past, and raising its gaze so as not to lose pace with the major social, scientific, and educational changes that point to the future. The challenges ahead of us are not easy, but as in so many occasions in our recent history, I am convinced that we will overcome them. Let us trust in ourselves, in our society; let us trust in Spain and remain united in the democratic values that we share to resolve our problems, without divisions or confrontations that only erode our coexistence and impoverish our future. We have great potential as a country. Let us think big. Let us move forward with ambition. Together. We know how to do it, and we know the way…
paragraph_summary: The speaker calls for national unity and commitment to democratic values to overcome future challenges and advance Spain's social and scientific progress.
reason: The speaker discusses the need for Spain to adapt to future challenges while maintaining national unity.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on future progress and national unity as a positive, aspirational goal rather than framing current conditions as an urgent threat or danger.

paragraph_text: Now, following the elections held on November 10th, we are immersed in the constitutional process by which the Congress of Deputies will decide whether to grant or deny its confidence to the candidate proposed for the Presidency of the Government. Therefore, it is up to the Congress, in accordance with our Constitution, to make the decision that it considers to be in the best interests of all Spaniards.
paragraph_summary: The speaker addresses the ongoing constitutional process in the Congress of Deputies to determine the formation of a new government following the November elections.
reason: The paragraph discusses the internal parliamentary process for forming a government in Spain.
crisis_reason: The paragraph describes a standard constitutional process for government formation following an election, which is presented as a routine matter of governance rather than an urgent threat to the state or democratic order.

paragraph_text: I said earlier that we do not live in easy times; but I also believe that, precisely because of that, we must have more than ever a firm belief in ourselves and in Spain, which has always known how to find its way when we have faced the future with responsibility, generosity, and rigor; with determination, but also with reflection and serenity. And we have ample reasons to have that confidence. The progress of a country depends, to a large extent, on the character of its citizens, the strength of its society, and the proper functioning of its state.
paragraph_summary: The King emphasizes the importance of national confidence, civic character, and institutional stability in overcoming current challenges facing Spain.
reason: The speaker emphasizes the importance of national character and state functioning for domestic progress.
crisis_reason: The paragraph frames the current situation as a set of 'challenges' that threaten national stability and civic character, requiring a specific response of confidence and institutional strength.

paragraph_text: . . . thanks to our Constitution, millions of Spaniards have, over the years, shared the same values upon which to base our coexistence, our great common projects, our feelings, and our ideas. . . " A society that has faced – and overcome – very difficult situations with admirable serenity and resilience, demonstrating great strength and maturity. A society, in short, that is entrepreneurial and generous, that develops great creativity and an undeniable leadership in many fields such as engineering, medicine, science, culture, sport, and business.
paragraph_summary: The speaker praises Spanish society for its resilience, shared constitutional values, and achievements across various professional and cultural fields.
reason: The paragraph focuses on the resilience and achievements of Spanish society.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on praise, resilience, and national achievements, which are explicitly excluded from the definition of a crisis.

paragraph_text: It is a great deal that we have built together, that we have advanced together. And we can feel very proud of the values that inspire our citizens, the energy, vitality, and dynamism of our society, and the strength of our state. And I believe it is important to say this, not out of a misguided sense of self-esteem, but because it is a proven reality that we must value. Faced with this reality, we must not fall into extremes, neither into complacency that silences our shortcomings or errors, nor into a destructive self-criticism that denies the great civic, social, and political heritage that we have accumulated. Without a doubt, there is still much to do, to improve, and to renew. To that end, it is necessary to have a clear and objective understanding of our strengths and weaknesses; and also, to have the most realistic and comprehensive view possible of how and where the world is heading.
paragraph_summary: The speaker reflects on the nation's achievements and values while advocating for a balanced, objective approach to addressing future challenges and national progress.
reason: The speaker reflects on the need for a realistic assessment of Spain's internal strengths and weaknesses.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on national achievements, values, and a general approach to progress rather than identifying a specific, urgent threat or danger requiring immediate action.

paragraph_text: Good evening. I address you on this Christmas Eve, at a time when we are living through truly exceptional circumstances due to the pandemic. Many families have been unable to gather tonight as they had planned, due to the health measures; and in countless homes, there is an unfillable void caused by the passing of loved ones, whom I want to remember now with emotion and with the utmost respect. A memory that fills our hearts with profound feelings. And also, at this moment, so many citizens are battling the illness or its aftereffects, in their homes, in hospitals, or in care facilities. To all of you, I send my strongest encouragement and affection today. 2020 has been a very difficult and challenging year. The virus has disrupted our lives, bringing suffering, sadness, or fear; it has altered the way we live and work, and it has seriously affected our economy, even paralyzing or destroying many businesses. So many citizens and families are experiencing the anguish of unemployment or precarious employment; the anguish of barely managing to cover basic needs; or the sadness of having to abandon a business to which they have dedicated their lives. For all of this, it is logical and understandable that discouragement or distrust are very present in so many homes. And yet, the response to a crisis as serious as the one we are experiencing cannot come from more discouragement or more distrust. The situation is serious. But, even so, we must face the future with determination and confidence in ourselves, in what we are capable of achieving together, with enthusiasm and hope; with confidence in our country and in our democratic model of coexistence. We have reasons to be optimistic; throughout the past decades, even in the face of serious difficulties, we have always been able to overcome them. And this situation we are experiencing will be no different; because neither the virus nor the economic crisis will break us.
paragraph_summary: The speaker addresses the social and economic hardships caused by the COVID-19 pandemic while urging national resilience and confidence in overcoming the crisis.
reason: The paragraph addresses the domestic impact of the pandemic on Spanish families and the economy.
crisis_reason: The paragraph explicitly identifies the pandemic as a crisis and frames the social and economic hardships as ongoing threats that require national resilience and urgent collective action.

paragraph_text: Regarding the health situation, it is clear that overcoming this illness will be thanks to science and research. The new treatments against the virus and the development of vaccines currently underway offer us great hope. But, in the meantime, we have much to do. Individual responsibility remains essential and is an effective tool in the fight against the virus. Therefore, it is so important that we remain vigilant and do not let our guard down. To the healthcare professionals, we once again express our gratitude for their immense effort, their extraordinary professionalism, and their great humanity towards the sick. They faced the initial impact of the virus in extreme and, in some cases, overwhelming situations in our hospitals. Today, they continue to confront this battle with a great emotional and physical burden. We ask them to maintain their spirit and strength, and to continue taking care of our health.
paragraph_summary: The speaker emphasizes the role of science in combating the pandemic while urging continued individual vigilance and expressing gratitude for healthcare workers' ongoing efforts.
reason: The text focuses on the domestic health response and gratitude toward national healthcare professionals.
crisis_reason: The speaker frames the pandemic as an ongoing threat by explicitly urging continued individual vigilance, which indicates that the situation is still treated as an active danger rather than a past event.

paragraph_text: The other major problem and challenge is the economic crisis and avoiding, above all, that it leads to a social crisis. Every person matters greatly. Therefore, people and families must be our primary concern. Especially our young people; their unemployment rate is extremely high, and they cannot be the losers in this situation. Our youth deserve to have the best possible education, to grow personally and professionally, and to be able to carry out their projects. Spain cannot afford a lost generation. Protecting the most vulnerable and fighting against the inequalities that the pandemic has created or exacerbated is a matter of dignity among those of us who form the same political community. But it will also be fundamental to recover our economy. And for that, it is essential to strengthen the business and productive fabric, the industrial and service sectors. Recognizing and supporting our businesses, protecting our self-employed workers and retailers, who have been so affected in recent months, will be essential to create employment, that employment that our country so desperately needs. We need, therefore, to consolidate the foundations that will give us a clear horizon of growth, stability, and economic confidence, which will encourage investment and job creation.
paragraph_summary: The speech addresses the economic crisis, emphasizing the need to support youth employment, protect vulnerable citizens, and strengthen businesses to foster national growth and stability.
reason: The speaker discusses domestic economic recovery and the need to protect Spanish youth and businesses.
crisis_reason: The speaker explicitly identifies an 'economic crisis' and frames it as a threat to national stability, necessitating urgent support for vulnerable groups and businesses.

paragraph_text: At a time when the pandemic and its economic and social consequences are causing so much uncertainty, our Constitution guarantees our way of understanding life, our vision of society and of the human being; of their dignity, of their rights and freedoms. A Constitution that we all have a duty to respect; and that, in our day, is the foundation of our social and political coexistence; and that represents, in our history, a success of and for democracy and freedom. . . The health, economic, and social challenges we face are therefore great… enormous, but not insurmountable. Overcoming them constitutes a great national goal that should unite us all; that, as citizens, it commits us and obliges us all, to ourselves, to others, and to our country. And that requires a great collective effort, a great effort in which each of us continues to give their best, according to their responsibilities and to the extent of their abilities. For that great national effort, we first and foremost have the most important thing: the people; the example of thousands of citizens who have dedicated their work to the service of others, who have lived these past months with selflessness, commitment, and great generosity. People who inspire our spirit of overcoming and of whom we should rightly feel proud. Both the Queen and I have personally witnessed this during this time. In the countryside and at sea; in villages and cities; in markets and factories, we have seen the courage and resilience of this country. We have felt the pulse of our society, which, despite everything, has kept Spain standing. We both remember the image of those thousands of citizens who represent a society that has felt more united than ever in its fight and resistance against such an adverse situation; a society that has effective and supportive organizations so that no one feels alone or abandoned; a society that has overcome these difficult months with fortitude, responsibility, and composure. We therefore have a strong society and a solid state. Throughout this time, both public and essential services, and businesses in essential sectors, have functioned well, striving to provide all available resources. The pandemic has revealed aspects that need to be improved and strengthened, but it also shows us our strengths as an advanced state.
paragraph_summary: The speaker emphasizes national unity, constitutional values, and the resilience of Spanish society and public services in overcoming the challenges of the COVID-19 pandemic.
reason: The paragraph focuses on national unity, the Constitution, and the resilience of Spanish society during the pandemic.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on the resilience of society and the successful overcoming of past challenges, framing the pandemic as a historical event rather than an active, ongoing threat.
