paragraph_text: And above all, we rely on our democratic system of coexistence. In a time when the pandemic and its economic and social consequences are causing so much uncertainty, our Constitution guarantees our way of understanding life, our vision of society and of the individual; of their dignity, their rights, and their freedoms. A Constitution that we all have a duty to respect, and which, in our day, is the foundation of our social and political coexistence, and which represents, in our history, a success of and for democracy and freedom. Let us not forget that the advances and progress achieved through democracy are the result of reconciliation and agreement among Spaniards, after a long period of confrontation and division. They are the result of a desire to look together towards the future, united in democratic values, united in a spirit of integration, in respect for diversity and differences, and in the ability to dialogue and reach agreements. These are principles that never lose their relevance with the passage of time. And alongside our democratic principles and the rule of law, we also need to preserve the ethical values that lie at the heart of our society. As far back as 2014, in my address to the General Courts, I spoke about the moral and ethical principles that citizens demand of our actions. Principles that obligate us all, without exception, and that are above any consideration, whatever its nature, even personal or family matters. I have always understood it this way, in accordance with my convictions, with my understanding of my responsibilities as Head of State, and with the spirit of renewal that has inspired my reign from the very first day.
paragraph_summary: The King emphasizes the importance of the Spanish Constitution, democratic coexistence, and ethical integrity in public life during times of uncertainty.
reason: The text emphasizes the Spanish democratic system, the Constitution, and the ethical responsibilities of the Head of State.
crisis_reason: The paragraph frames the current situation as one of 'uncertainty' that poses a threat to democratic coexistence and ethical integrity, requiring a defensive emphasis on constitutional values.

paragraph_text: I have always believed that Spain is an extraordinary country, with immense cultural richness and diversity, built over centuries thanks to the efforts of many generations of Spaniards, and with a great history that, for a time, was the history of our world. We are not a people who give up or resign themselves in difficult times. It will not be easy to overcome this situation, and you all know that in your own homes. But I am sure that we will move forward. With effort, unity, and solidarity, Spain will move forward. With everyone, and for everyone. And as King, I will be with everyone, and for everyone, not only because it is my duty and my conviction, but also because it is my commitment to all of you, to Spain. It will not be difficult for the year 2021 to be better than 2020. We will strive to restore normalcy in workplaces, classrooms, squares, and neighborhoods; in shops, markets, and bars; in cinemas, theaters…; in the everyday life that shapes the character of a society like ours. That is what we all want. And with the certainty that this will be so, the Queen, the Princess of Asturias, the Infanta Sofía, and I, sincerely thank you for all the expressions of affection and support that you have sent us this year, and we wish you a very Happy Christmas and all the best for a 2021 that is especially full of hope. Happy holidays. Merry Christmas and Happy Holidays.
paragraph_summary: The King expresses confidence in Spain's recovery from the challenges of 2020 through national unity, solidarity, and a collective effort to restore societal normalcy.
reason: The King expresses commitment to the Spanish people and offers holiday wishes to the nation.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on recovery, national unity, and the restoration of normalcy following past challenges, rather than framing the current situation as an active or ongoing threat.

paragraph_text: Buenas noches, My first words this Christmas Eve, I want to dedicate them to those of you living on the island of La Palma, who are enduring such a painful and difficult situation for the past three months. The volcano has left so many of you without a home, without a livelihood, and it has filled us all with sadness. Today, our hearts and our thoughts are with you. We feel very close, and you know that you have our solidarity, and that you can count on the support and commitment of all government agencies to help you rebuild your lives, your economy, and to pursue your projects with renewed hope, as soon as possible.
paragraph_summary: The King expresses solidarity and promises government support for the residents of La Palma following the devastating volcanic eruption.
reason: The paragraph addresses a specific domestic disaster on the island of La Palma and promises government support.
crisis_reason: The paragraph describes a devastating volcanic eruption and the resulting need for government support, framing the event as an acute threat to the safety and livelihoods of the residents.

paragraph_text: The times are certainly difficult, but stopping today would mean falling behind; it would mean taking a step back. We must continue forward, because history teaches us that the Spanish people have always known how to react and overcome adversity. A future now lies before us, one that demands – from all of us – responsibility, a willingness to collaborate and understand one another; and that future requires that we place greater faith in our own strength as a nation. I have no doubt about our commitment, our ability, and that with determination, drive, and character, we will succeed.
paragraph_summary: The speaker encourages national unity, resilience, and collective responsibility to overcome current challenges and ensure Spain's future success.
reason: The text focuses on national resilience and the internal strength of the Spanish people to overcome adversity.
crisis_reason: The paragraph frames the current situation as a set of challenges that require collective action and resilience to overcome, indicating an active threat to the nation's future.

paragraph_text: I will not elaborate further, but let us remember that tonight, many people are working to safeguard our health, our security, and our peace of mind, and to guarantee public services… All of them deserve our recognition and should be especially present in our thoughts during this time. Thank you for your attention; and with the peace and joy that this night represents, alongside the Queen and our daughters, Princess Leonor and Infanta Sofía, I wish you all a very happy Christmas and New Year. Happy Holidays.
paragraph_summary: The King expresses gratitude to public service workers and extends Christmas and New Year greetings to the nation on behalf of the royal family.
reason: The King thanks domestic public servants and offers holiday greetings to the Spanish population.
crisis_reason: The paragraph is purely ceremonial, focusing on expressions of gratitude and holiday greetings, which are explicitly excluded from the definition of a crisis.

paragraph_text: Also, this past year, many citizens continue to experience the social, economic, and emotional consequences of the pandemic. On the one hand, our economy has grown again, and the vast majority of jobs that were temporarily suspended have been recovered; and the number of employed people is evolving at a truly positive rate. On the other hand, the number of people in vulnerable situations has increased, and many households are concerned about rising prices, the cost of energy, or the difficulties in finding stable employment, especially for young people.
paragraph_summary: The speech addresses the country's economic recovery alongside persistent challenges regarding social vulnerability, rising costs of living, and youth unemployment.
reason: The paragraph addresses domestic economic concerns such as employment, inflation, and household vulnerability.
crisis_reason: The paragraph frames social vulnerability, rising costs of living, and youth unemployment as persistent challenges that require ongoing attention, which constitutes a chronic threat framing.

paragraph_text: I sincerely believe that the challenges we face represent a crossroads for Spain, as they do for many other nations; but they are undoubtedly a historic opportunity, and even an obligation to catch up, to modernize our country, while remaining steadfast in our democratic principles and the values that inspire our coexistence. Because what we do or decide in the years to come will determine whether we can continue to progress alongside the most advanced nations, or whether we will fall behind on our path. And in that great task, institutions have the greatest responsibility. We must always keep the general interest in mind and think about citizens, their concerns, and their worries. We must be permanently at their service and address their problems. We must be in the place that the constitution assigns to us; assume, each one of us, the obligations entrusted to us; respect and comply with the laws, and be an example of public integrity and morality. To achieve all of this, understanding and collaboration are essential attitudes that dignify institutions; more importantly, they strengthen them, because they generate the trust of citizens. And differences of opinion should not prevent consensus that guarantees greater stability, greater well-being in households, and provides the necessary peace of mind for families regarding their future.
paragraph_summary: The speaker calls for institutional responsibility, integrity, and political consensus to modernize Spain and ensure national progress while upholding democratic values.
reason: The text focuses on the modernization of Spain, institutional responsibility, and domestic political consensus.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on modernization, progress, and the upholding of democratic values as a positive, routine governance objective rather than framing the current state as an urgent threat or danger.

paragraph_text: But without a doubt, society must also play an essential role in these times of change. The circumstances, many of which have been far from easy, that we have experienced, especially over the past 40 years, have forged us into a strong, responsible, and enormously supportive society, as we have seen from the beginning of this pandemic. It is our civic values, along with the talent, vitality, and energy that we have in industry, services, and agriculture, that must be at the forefront of building our future.
paragraph_summary: The speaker highlights the resilience and civic values of Spanish society as essential drivers for building the country's future.
reason: The paragraph highlights the strength and civic values of Spanish society in the context of national development.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on positive national attributes like resilience and civic values as foundations for the future, rather than framing any specific situation as an urgent threat or danger.

paragraph_text: If we know where we want to go, we must also be aware of where we come from: The profound transformation of Spain over these past four decades of democracy and freedom has been extraordinary, and it has not been the result of chance. It has been based on the effort and sacrifice of so many people, of millions of Spaniards. It is due to many reasons: a sense of history, significant agreements, generosity, responsibility, and a vision for the future. Our Constitution, a great project of transformation, symbolizes and represents this. It fully integrates us into modern Western democracies, and its spirit calls us to unity over division, to dialogue rather than confrontation, to respect over resentment, and to integration over exclusion. It constantly calls us to a civic, calm, and free coexistence. The Constitution has been and remains the cornerstone that has fostered our progress, supporting our democratic coexistence through serious and significant crises of various kinds that we have experienced. It deserves our respect, recognition, and loyalty.
paragraph_summary: The speaker highlights the Spanish Constitution as the foundational pillar of the nation's democratic progress, unity, and peaceful coexistence over the past four decades.
reason: It reflects on the historical transformation of Spain and the role of the Constitution in domestic stability.
crisis_reason: The paragraph frames the constitution as a foundational pillar of progress and unity, focusing on historical achievement and stability rather than an urgent threat or danger.

paragraph_text: The distribution of refugees must be shared fairly between countries, so that each country can live up to its societal commitments. I am a social democrat. I believe that a nation is more than just a collection of people in a geographical area. It is a community where we feel responsible for each other, where we support each other through life's challenges, and lay the foundation for each other's success. That means that as a country, we must be able to uphold our commitments, both to those who already live here and to those who come to us. It means that everyone who is able to work should work, that all children should receive a good education, that healthcare, social care, and social services should be available when you need them. Otherwise, we will create a society that is torn apart, a society that is more fearful, more insecure, where selfishness triumphs over solidarity. That is not Sweden. And I believe in a society where we defend the equal value and rights of all people in our daily lives. The wave of hatred directed at the most vulnerable members of society that we have seen this year, the vandalism, the threats, the arson attacks on our asylum accommodations, it is disgusting, pitiful, abhorrent. And it is up to you, and me, and all of us to stand against it. The police are being strengthened. Security is being increased. But you and I must do everything in our power to confront and combat hatred and racism. Sweden's strength lies in being an egalitarian and equal society; a society that is not defiant, but rather, thanks to its solidarity and tolerance, is prosperous, modern, and a shining leader in the world's development. That is the Sweden we strive for. That is the Sweden we believe in.
paragraph_summary: The speaker advocates for fair refugee distribution, social solidarity, and collective responsibility in combating hatred, racism, and threats against vulnerable groups in Sweden.
reason: The text focuses on Swedish societal values, internal security, and the fight against domestic racism.
crisis_reason: The speaker frames hatred, racism, and threats against vulnerable groups as urgent dangers that undermine social solidarity and require collective action, characterizing these issues as a threat to the social fabric.

paragraph_text: Friends, we shape our own future. Society is our creation, and we decide its development together. And now, we stand before two crucial choices regarding our shared future. The first choice is between cuts or investments. Now we know what years of underinvestment does to a society. We can see it in the housing shortage in more than 180 of Sweden’s municipalities. We can hear it in the reports of lost productivity and competitiveness, in the warnings from businesses that four out of every ten recruitment attempts fail because they cannot find the right skills, despite the fact that 350, 000 people are unemployed. We can feel it in the disappointment, the hopelessness of every student who does not receive the support they need in school. We see it, we hear it, we feel it. That is why the government is now creating over 50, 000 new educational opportunities. We are launching the largest housing investment in 20 years. We are increasing maintenance of the road and rail networks, launching Sweden’s new export strategy, and increasing collaboration between businesses, research institutions, and elected officials. And we are allocating resources to schools – to higher salaries for teachers and smaller class sizes in primary school. Sweden chooses a path: societal investments over tax cuts and austerity. When you want to develop something, you invest in it. Therefore, we are investing in Sweden.
paragraph_summary: The Swedish government prioritizes public investments in education, housing, and infrastructure over tax cuts to address societal challenges and improve national competitiveness.
reason: The paragraph outlines domestic policy choices regarding investments in housing, education, and infrastructure.
crisis_reason: The paragraph describes routine policy priorities and investments aimed at national improvement rather than framing these issues as urgent threats or existential dangers.

paragraph_text: The second choice is between shadow societies or cohesion. In the wake of the refugee crisis, voices are rising to create shadow societies, where people live with lower levels of welfare, weaker labor rights, and without reasonable wages. They say it is a solution to the refugee crisis, but in reality, it is just the same recipe for inequality that has been heard throughout history. It is impossible to lower anyone’s wages without lowering everyone’s wages in the long run. It is impossible to cut anyone’s welfare without eroding the entire general welfare system. It is impossible to attack the Swedish model without damaging what makes Sweden strong. We have higher ambitions than that. People should not just be offered a roof over their heads; they should have a home. They should be able to study, work, support themselves, and realize their dreams, not as second-class citizens – but as equals. Everyone should be able to demand their rights, but also fulfill their responsibilities. That is what we owe to those who come to Sweden, and what they owe to Swedish society. Therefore, we choose the path that leads to a society that we create together, where no one is left behind, but no one is held back. We choose a society built on trust, equality, and community, because only in that way can everyone in that society feel hope for the future. A community must be for everyone, otherwise it is no community at all. Therefore, we will not create shadow societies, but a Sweden that holds together!
paragraph_summary: The speaker rejects the creation of shadow societies for refugees, advocating instead for national cohesion, equality, and the preservation of the Swedish welfare model.
reason: The text discusses domestic labor rights, welfare systems, and the goal of maintaining social cohesion within Sweden.
crisis_reason: The speaker frames the emergence of 'shadow societies' as a direct threat to national cohesion, equality, and the integrity of the welfare model, which constitutes a chronic threat framed as an urgent danger.

paragraph_text: Friends, one thing I love about Christmas is that it focuses our attention on others – on what makes another person happy. Christmas is also a time for gratitude. I would like to conclude by extending a special thank you to all of you who are working during Christmas and New Year's so that the rest of us can feel safe. Thank you to all the police officers and firefighters, to all of you working in healthcare, social care, and public transportation, who take care of and ensure that both young and old can enjoy the joy of Christmas. Thank you to all the churches and non-profit organizations that set up Christmas dinners and light a candle for those, as the song goes, "who have frozen and ventured out into the world. " During this dark autumn, one of the bright spots has been all the people who have volunteered their time, welcomed others, and offered warmth. The newspaper Fokus named a volunteer as the Swedish Person of the Year, and rarely has that recognition been more well-deserved! Thank you! The value to society when people come together and collaborate, and the value to each of us when we get to know new people and can contribute to the common good – that value cannot be measured in currency. It happens every day in community organizations, on sports fields and in gymnasiums, among board members and parent volunteers, in the interactions between newly arrived immigrants and those of us who have lived here for a long time. That's where trust is built, and that's where the foundation is laid for the Sweden that we are so proud of. And next year, if you have the opportunity, get a little more involved in volunteer work. That will be my New Year's resolution. I hope you're inspired to make it yours as well. Because society is our shared responsibility, and we build it together. Wishing you all a very Merry Christmas and a very Happy New Year!
paragraph_summary: The speaker expresses gratitude to essential workers and volunteers while encouraging citizens to engage in community service to strengthen social trust and unity.
reason: The paragraph expresses gratitude to domestic workers and volunteers while offering holiday wishes to the Swedish people.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on gratitude and the promotion of social cohesion as a positive, routine civic value rather than framing the state of society as an urgent threat or danger.

paragraph_text: In 1997, a new majority was elected. I have therefore appointed a Prime Minister from that majority, and the government is implementing its policies. And I will tell you how things stand. In accordance with the letter and spirit of our institutions, I fully assume the powers and responsibilities of my office. Guardian of the continuity of the state, I am also the protector of the values of the Republic, foremost among which are freedom and solidarity. Responsible for the future of the nation, I will intervene whenever the nation’s interests are at stake, to tell you what I believe is good for the French people, or, conversely, what is dangerous for France.
paragraph_summary: President Jacques Chirac outlines his constitutional role, responsibilities, and commitment to protecting national interests and republican values following the 1997 legislative elections.
reason: The paragraph focuses on the internal political structure, the role of the President, and the domestic interests of France.
crisis_reason: The paragraph describes the president's routine constitutional duties and commitment to national values following an election, which is presented as a standard governance function rather than a response to an urgent threat.

paragraph_text: I wish for a united France, a France at one with itself, where citizens live in good harmony. This means, first and foremost, a reassured France. There is too much violence and insecurity within our country, in our schools, on our transport systems, and in our streets. Every day, lines are crossed, beyond which society begins to unravel. Today, I know, unemployment is your primary concern. The state must play its role. It must strive to better understand, to prevent, and also to punish when necessary. I call on each of you to take responsibility. Citizens must respect their duties. Public authorities must restore order and security, which is the foundation of all freedoms.
paragraph_summary: The President calls for national unity, addressing concerns regarding domestic violence, insecurity, and unemployment while emphasizing the need for public order and civic responsibility.
reason: The text addresses domestic issues such as unemployment, public security, and internal social order.
crisis_reason: The speaker frames domestic violence, insecurity, and unemployment as urgent threats to public order and social stability, necessitating a call for civic responsibility and national unity.

paragraph_text: A united France is a France proud of its values. The dignity of men and women, of course, the protection of children, but also secularism and integration. The ideal of tolerance, to which we are all deeply committed, must not lead us to accept anything that jeopardizes the unity of the nation. Our country is not, and never will be, simply the sum of disparate communities. The common good is not, and never will be, the sum of individual interests.
paragraph_summary: The president emphasizes national unity, secularism, and shared values over individual or community interests to maintain the integrity of the French nation.
reason: The discussion centers on national values, secularism, and the internal unity of the French state.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on the preservation of national integrity and shared values as a foundational principle rather than framing these as an urgent, active threat or a crisis requiring emergency intervention.

paragraph_text: Finally, a united France is a France that talks to itself, a France that knows how to listen, that knows how to engage in exchange and dialogue, that knows how to anticipate necessary changes and guide them through consultation. Modernity is not a battlefield, with winners and losers. Everyone can and should find advantages in it. To that end, let us discuss, overcome our contradictions and disagreements, and invent new solutions. I invite you all, with great enthusiasm, to better live our democracy.
paragraph_summary: The President calls for national unity, constructive dialogue, and collaborative democratic engagement to address societal changes and resolve disagreements.
reason: The focus is on domestic political dialogue, democratic engagement, and internal social cohesion.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on the need for unity and dialogue as a standard democratic process rather than framing societal changes as an urgent, existential threat or an acute danger.

paragraph_text: We can be, if we change our minds. There is no exception for France that would allow us to escape the rules that apply to everyone else. But there must be an ambition for France: to unleash the forces and energies of our people, to create the conditions for wealth and activity so that all French people can benefit. So that we can create the jobs that we are lacking and reduce unemployment, which is so harshly affecting so many of us. We can clearly see today the gravity and intensity of this drama. Let us fight more effectively against exclusion. Let no one be left behind. There is our dream of dignity and greatness, more alive than ever. We will make it a reality.
paragraph_summary: The President outlines a national ambition to foster economic growth, create jobs, and reduce unemployment while addressing social exclusion.
reason: The paragraph addresses domestic economic policy, job creation, and the fight against internal social exclusion.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on positive national ambitions and routine economic goals like growth and job creation rather than framing these issues as urgent threats or dangers.

paragraph_text: Together, we will usher in a new era. Preparing for the future is the first duty of any leader. It means listening to you, being attentive to your aspirations and concerns, and offering a clear path that will allow you to reach your full potential, to develop your talents, and to realize your projects. To those in public office, you demand courage – the courage to speak, the courage to act, the courage to change, and the courage to take responsibility. We have so much to gain by frankly addressing the problems we face. Everyone knows what works well and what doesn't in France. Despite significant progress, too many ingrained habits and constraints still hold us back. Too often, particular interests prevail, and we do not prioritize the collective enough. Let us resolutely choose to move forward. This is how we will open up our political life and strengthen our democracy. This is how we will create a new form of solidarity, a responsible solidarity that will help those excluded from employment, sometimes after many years. This is how we will provide our children with a good education that prepares them for the job market. This is how we will guarantee the future of our pensions.
paragraph_summary: The President outlines a vision for national reform, focusing on political renewal, economic solidarity, education, and the long-term sustainability of the pension system.
reason: The text outlines domestic governance, political responsibility, and internal social reforms like education and pensions.
crisis_reason: The paragraph describes routine governance and long-term policy planning rather than framing these issues as urgent threats or immediate dangers to the state.

paragraph_text: But to be among the best, we must overcome the obstacles that hold us back. We must unlock our capacity for innovation, lower our taxes and charges, which are among the highest in Europe, and value those who create. We must make the most of new technologies. This is how we must build our future. It is an ambitious goal, a reasonable ambition, and it is my ambition.
paragraph_summary: The speaker outlines a strategy for national economic growth through innovation, tax reduction, and the adoption of new technologies.
reason: The focus is on domestic economic competitiveness, tax policy, and national innovation.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on economic growth and routine policy measures like tax reduction and innovation, which are presented as positive developments rather than urgent threats.

paragraph_text: To those in public office, my dear fellow citizens, you also expect them to enforce the law. You want authority, a responsible and intelligent authority, confident in its purpose, which is simply to uphold our republican values. These values, and in particular integration and equal opportunities, are sometimes threatened. The safety of property and people is not guaranteed everywhere. Education and prevention are essential, but you also know that sanctions are equally important. I would like to reiterate that security is the first of our freedoms.
paragraph_summary: The President emphasizes the necessity of enforcing law and order to protect republican values, ensure public safety, and guarantee equal opportunities for all citizens.
reason: The paragraph addresses internal law enforcement, security, and the protection of republican values.
crisis_reason: The paragraph frames the enforcement of law and order as a necessary measure to protect fundamental values and public safety, implying an active threat to the social order that requires urgent attention.

paragraph_text: Finally, I know that you aspire to greater unity. You appreciate genuine debate, but you are tired of pointless arguments. I believe, as you do, that we must avoid unnecessary divisions and avoid hurting people with our convictions. There are so many other priorities, so many other challenges today. Everywhere, I see a tremendous desire to act and create, a thirst for knowledge, and a need to succeed. I see new energies at work, gradually transforming our country. If we can encourage and unleash these energies, then yes, France will be well-positioned for the century to come! Everywhere, I meet women and men who come together to make progress. On so many issues, it is possible. We have seen what France can achieve when it is united, enthusiastic, and focused on a common goal, a shared ambition.
paragraph_summary: The President calls for national unity and collective action to overcome division and foster progress for France's future.
reason: The text calls for internal unity and a focus on domestic challenges to ensure national progress.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on fostering progress and unity as a positive goal rather than framing division as an urgent, existential threat or a crisis requiring emergency response.

paragraph_text: My dear fellow citizens, I would first like to express my sympathy to all those who are experiencing hardship these past few days of 1999. I think of the many victims of the storm and of all the grieving families with whom we share this sorrow. I think of our fellow citizens who have been so cruelly affected in their daily lives, those whose property has been destroyed, those who fear for their livelihoods and their jobs, and those who are heartbroken to see our heritage, our coastline, our forests, and our monuments so disfigured. I want to reiterate my emotion, but also my pride, in the extraordinary outpouring of solidarity shown by so many volunteers and organizations working alongside the civil and military services and elected officials.
model_reasons: storm: refers to a specific weather event, not climate change or environmental policy
paragraph_summary: The President expresses sympathy for victims of the 1999 storms and commends the national solidarity shown in response to the destruction.
reason: The paragraph addresses a specific domestic disaster and the national response to it.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on expressing sympathy for past victims and commending the subsequent national solidarity, which frames the event as a recovery and reflection rather than an active, ongoing threat.

paragraph_text: In these difficult times, we deeply feel the fragility of things, the precariousness of what we thought was assured. We see how much can be challenged, sometimes due to human carelessness or the fury of nature. We also recognize the vital role of the state in our society – a state with essential responsibilities: public service, security, and solidarity. A state that must anticipate, respond, and coordinate the resources of the nation. Above all, we recognize the value of fraternal support, spontaneous kindness, and a helping hand – the very foundations of our nation. As we stand on the threshold of the year 2000, there is truly nothing more modern, more necessary, or more enduring than the feeling of belonging to a shared community and being responsible for one another. France, wounded, wants to come together, united and fraternal. Because our fellow citizens have always known, in times of adversity, how to let their hearts speak, I want to thank all the French people.
paragraph_summary: The President emphasizes the importance of state responsibility, national unity, and mutual solidarity among French citizens as they face challenges entering the year 2000.
reason: The text discusses the role of the state in domestic crisis management and the importance of national solidarity.
crisis_reason: The paragraph frames the upcoming year as a period of challenges requiring national unity and solidarity, which implies a threat-based framing of the state's situation rather than a routine or celebratory one.

paragraph_text: My dear fellow citizens, we share certain values: the desire to give everyone a chance so that our society can be more dynamic, more adaptable, and more optimistic. The demand for solidarity – a more responsible solidarity, where everyone strives to contribute their share. An attachment to family, because it represents warmth, mutual support, and security. The desire to be useful, to find our place in society, to give back, and to realize our potential. Tolerance, which is not a renunciation of our convictions, but a respect for others. The republican spirit and a sense of the common good, which require the state to maintain its role in upholding the law, enforcing it with authority and justice. Let us preserve these values. By upholding them, we will be stronger as we face the times ahead.
paragraph_summary: The president calls for national unity by upholding core republican values such as solidarity, tolerance, family, and the rule of law.
reason: The focus is on internal societal values and the role of the state in maintaining order.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on national values and social cohesion as a positive, aspirational goal rather than framing a specific threat or danger to the state.

paragraph_text: France is changing. It must evolve in step with the world. By remaining true to its unique spirit, it will be able to combine change and social cohesion, initiative and security, modernity and a good quality of life.
paragraph_summary: The President emphasizes the necessity of national evolution while maintaining social cohesion, security, and quality of life.
reason: It addresses the internal evolution and social cohesion of France.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on the maintenance of stability and quality of life as a general goal of governance rather than framing these as being under an urgent or active threat.

paragraph_text: And then, there are the difficulties faced by the French people, your difficulties, the fears that are experienced daily. Many of our fellow citizens are deprived of a fundamental freedom and right: the right to live in safety. The rise in violence and incivility, even in schools, the increasingly serious assaults, sometimes committed by young people who are still children, the repetition of senseless murders – all of this calls for a national mobilization, a realistic assessment of the problems, and above all, real solutions. It is not acceptable that a portion of the population should sometimes feel abandoned, and especially by the state. I hope that on these issues, which are so important to our citizens, the year 2001 will be a year of action and progress.
paragraph_summary: The President addresses rising violence and insecurity in France, calling for national mobilization and state action to ensure citizen safety in 2001.
reason: It addresses domestic concerns regarding safety, violence, and the role of the state.
crisis_reason: The speaker frames rising violence and insecurity as a direct threat to citizen safety, explicitly calling for national mobilization and state action to address the danger.

paragraph_text: A truly useful year, for our democracy. Day after day, the democratic debate must inform and enlighten the French people about the choices that lie ahead. It must allow convictions to be expressed with dignity, with serenity, and above all, with respect for others. Let us never allow the republican dialogue to be diminished. Let us always defend the highest ideal of the common good. Our political life now rests on firmer foundations than it did in the past. Its modernization must continue. Our society can no longer be managed as it was just twenty years ago. The participation of each individual in civic life is currently too limited. We must open it up to everyone. In this way, our democracy will be more vibrant and stronger.
paragraph_summary: The President advocates for modernizing French democracy by increasing civic participation and fostering respectful, constructive political dialogue among citizens.
reason: The focus is on the modernization of French democratic debate and civic participation.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on the positive goal of modernizing democracy and fostering dialogue, which is framed as a constructive improvement rather than an urgent response to a threat or democratic collapse.

paragraph_text: Faced with all these changes, which inspire so much hope but also anxieties, there are new opportunities to seize, new horizons to open, and new frontiers to conquer. Of course, nothing will happen on its own. The future is entirely ours to build. And I know that you are increasingly eager to take ownership of it, to take action, to move forward, and to pursue projects and achievements that are today the great strengths of France. But we also need a national will, a collective enthusiasm, and we must move forward without waiting for tomorrow. That is why, my dear fellow citizens, 2001 must be a productive year. Every year counts; no year can be lost.
paragraph_summary: The President calls for collective national action and productivity to seize future opportunities and ensure 2001 is a successful year for France.
reason: It calls for national will and collective action to make the coming year productive for France.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on future opportunities and achieving success rather than framing the current situation as a threat or an urgent danger.

paragraph_text: It must be a productive year for France. The work and efforts of the French people, the actions of successive governments, and global growth have created a real momentum for our economy. Because we have bold and imaginative entrepreneurs, and because the skills and quality of French workers are recognized, France is innovating, producing, and exporting. Our companies are creating jobs. Unemployment is declining. However, for many families, it remains a harsh reality or a constant threat. To ensure that every French person has a job tomorrow, we must implement the reforms that will prepare for the future – the reforms that many of our neighbors have already implemented. These reforms concern education, pensions, taxation, the role of the state, public spending, and local freedoms. The current economic situation gives us the means to act. This is the moment to do so. To remove barriers and obstacles, to unleash energy, and to amplify activity where it has already resumed. To open wide the doors that are only slightly ajar today.
paragraph_summary: The president outlines economic progress and advocates for structural reforms in education, pensions, and taxation to reduce unemployment and boost national productivity.
reason: It outlines domestic economic reforms and the need to address unemployment within France.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on economic progress and routine structural reforms to improve productivity, which are presented as standard governance rather than an urgent threat or emergency.

paragraph_text: France is dynamic, courageous, and ambitious. First and foremost, it needs to rediscover itself, to affirm its values – the values of the Republic, which underpin our freedoms. It needs a strong state, an authority that commands respect, and a republican order that is clearly defined. It needs unity and cohesion. National cohesion is essential. We see this time and again when a tragedy or disaster strikes, triggering an extraordinary outpouring of solidarity within us. We see it again, with joy, whenever France wins. This sense of cohesion, of belonging to a common community, to a common nation, should not be an exceptional moment, linked to a particular emotion, but the very foundation of our French citizenship. It is the source of all national will. It must be lived every day. It is our strength. It must be our ambition. French society, over the decades, has gained momentum, dynamism, and diversity. But within this vibrant French landscape, there are also doubts, fears, and divisions. The cohesion of our nation requires the affirmation of a great collective endeavor, an ideal, dreams, and common projects. But it also requires the fair recognition of each person's place, their work, their role, and their dignity. This requires a society that values merit, energy, talent, and generosity. While we all have a role to play in national cohesion, the state must, of course, guarantee this and serve this great ambition.
paragraph_summary: The speaker emphasizes the necessity of national cohesion, republican values, and a strong state to overcome societal divisions and foster a shared collective ambition.
reason: The focus is on national cohesion, republican values, and the role of the state.
crisis_reason: The paragraph frames societal divisions as a problem to be overcome through national cohesion and a strong state, treating the lack of unity as a threat to the collective ambition and stability of the nation.

paragraph_text: My dear fellow citizens, for France to thrive, to flourish, and for every French person to prosper in the world of tomorrow, we must choose Europe, embrace progress, and remain faithful to the ideals of the Republic. We must choose a French ambition, a commitment to action. Whatever trials we have recently faced, and whatever uncertainties the future may hold, I know that you want to uphold the values that are at the heart of our democracy, of our Republic. I know that you want to strengthen our national unity. I know that you want to move forward, to succeed, and to ensure the success of France. To all the women and men of France, from mainland France, from overseas territories, and from abroad, I wish you a happy and prosperous New Year. Long live the Republic, and long live France.
paragraph_summary: The President calls for national unity, commitment to European integration, and adherence to republican values to ensure France's future prosperity.
reason: It concludes with a call for national unity and commitment to French democratic ideals.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on positive aspirations for prosperity and adherence to values rather than framing the current state as an urgent threat or danger.

paragraph_text: My dear fellow citizens, I am delighted to be with you on this New Year's Day. Many of you are happily gathered with family or friends to welcome the year 2003, and I extend my warmest greetings to you all. Others, I know, find this festive season difficult, as their worries and, at times, their distress, weigh heavily upon them. To all those who feel isolated, who are experiencing hardship, or facing challenges, I want to express my sympathy and solidarity. The year 2002 was a year of debate, and for some, an opportunity to express their dissatisfaction and concerns. It was also, and will remain, the year of a renewed sense of national purpose, a year of unity, and a year of change, for security and justice, for the economy and employment, and for national defense. The government, led by Jean-Pierre Raffarin, has taken a first step, and I want to commend his dedication and his determination to bring about change, working with and for the French people. Reasserting the authority of the state was a necessary first step, and we can now move forward with the next stages, with clarity, of course, but also with confidence and determination.
paragraph_summary: President Chirac reflects on the challenges of 2002 while expressing solidarity with citizens and outlining the government's commitment to national reform and state authority.
reason: The paragraph focuses on domestic national unity, government leadership, and internal state authority.
crisis_reason: The summary describes the reflection on challenges and the commitment to reform and state authority as a routine governance agenda rather than framing specific threats as urgent, active dangers.

paragraph_text: But we must also have confidence, because France has everything it needs to be a strong nation, thanks to its dynamism, its ability to unite, and its spirit of solidarity. Our demographic vitality sets us apart in Europe. The work, creativity, and skills of the French people are recognized throughout the world. Our businesses are present across the globe. We are also rediscovering essential values such as personal responsibility, a sense of collective interest, authority, and local solidarity. Personal responsibility begins with civic engagement, respect for others, respect for differences, and respect for those who are most vulnerable. It is, of course, the opposite of contempt, indifference, and recklessness, which cause so much suffering. And the campaign against road violence should serve as an example.
paragraph_summary: The speaker highlights France's national strengths, including its demographic vitality, economic dynamism, and the importance of civic responsibility and social solidarity.
reason: The text emphasizes domestic values, demographic vitality, and internal civic engagement.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on national strengths, positive attributes, and social values rather than presenting a threat or an urgent problem requiring intervention.

paragraph_text: Finally, we must be determined, because to prepare for and shape the future, we must act, change, and challenge ourselves. We all know that reforms are essential, reforms that our European partners have already implemented, and which we have too long delayed. France has changed significantly. It was once comprised of opposing blocs, often irreconcilable, and ready to tear each other apart over every issue. Today, I am convinced that uniting the majority around ambitious collective goals, and moving beyond outdated divisions, is now possible, allowing us to implement the reforms that our country needs. The reform of the pension system, which concerns everyone and is the only way to guarantee a decent retirement for every French citizen, is a crucial opportunity, and it would be a grave mistake not to seize it. Decentralization, already underway, is also essential. We need to renew our education system, safeguard our healthcare system, modernize the state, and protect our environment. We must also revitalize and make our economy more attractive, by reducing excessive regulations and taxes, which have led to a loss of investment and jobs.
model_reasons: environment: General political/social context; lacks specific reference to natural or ecological environment.
paragraph_summary: The President advocates for national unity to implement essential reforms in pensions, education, healthcare, and the economy to ensure France's future prosperity.
reason: It outlines domestic reforms regarding pensions, education, healthcare, and the national economy.
crisis_reason: The paragraph frames the reforms as essential for future prosperity rather than as a response to an urgent threat or danger, characterizing them as routine governance and forward-looking development.

paragraph_text: I will ensure that these reforms are carried out effectively, with the involvement of everyone, and in the best interests of all. The Prime Minister and the government will lead this effort with transparency, dialogue, and fairness, as they have done since taking office. Every French citizen, fully and clearly informed, will be able to play their part in the necessary changes. France will only progress if we all move forward together. Then, nothing that is necessary will be beyond our reach. The effort required from the nation will be all the easier if it is shared fairly. There is a fundamental principle of justice among the French people, and I intend to be its guarantor. Collective interest, responsibility, and fairness: that is how we will succeed! That is the French pact: the demand for freedom and solidarity, the constant search for a balance between the ambition of a nation and the place to be reserved for each citizen and their expectations.
paragraph_summary: The President outlines his commitment to implementing national reforms through collective effort, transparency, and a focus on social justice and fairness for all citizens.
reason: The focus is on the implementation of domestic reforms and the internal social pact between the government and citizens.
crisis_reason: The paragraph describes routine governance and policy goals such as reform, transparency, and social justice without framing them as urgent threats or existential dangers.

paragraph_text: The key challenge for 2004 is to make the most of this growth, with a priority: employment. Our goal is to bring French men and women, and especially young people, back into the workforce, those who have, until now, been left behind. Our goal is to give workers new tools to find employment. Our goal is to give France the economic strength it needs, because that is the very foundation of social progress. These objectives can, and must, be achieved within a modern economy. The government has already taken significant steps. But to move forward, we need new tools. I have asked the government, led by Jean-Pierre Raffarin, to begin consultations with social partners at the start of the year, in order to pass a major law to promote employment.
paragraph_summary: President Chirac outlines his 2004 priority of increasing employment through new government initiatives and consultations with social partners to strengthen the French economy.
reason: The primary focus is on domestic economic growth and national employment policy.
crisis_reason: The paragraph describes routine government policy and economic initiatives rather than framing unemployment as an urgent, existential threat or a crisis requiring emergency action.

paragraph_text: Mobilizing for employment means allowing businesses to reach their full potential, because let us never forget that businesses are the ones who create jobs. Therefore, new decisions will be made to ease unnecessary procedures and excessive burdens that hinder their dynamism and that lead too many of them to be reluctant to hire. Mobilizing for employment also means reinvigorating our efforts in research, as well as in equipment and infrastructure, which are essential for a sustainable economic power. It also means continuing our policy of creating businesses and defending our industry.
paragraph_summary: The president outlines strategies to boost employment by reducing business regulations, increasing investment in research and infrastructure, and supporting industrial growth.
reason: It discusses domestic business regulation, infrastructure, and industrial policy.
crisis_reason: The paragraph describes routine economic policy measures aimed at growth and development rather than framing the current state of employment as an urgent threat or crisis.

paragraph_text: Mobilizing for employment means giving French men and women new opportunities. To that end, let us open up access to training and a second chance for workers who have left the education system prematurely. Let us strengthen prevention against layoffs and social plans. Let us establish new re-employment rights for workers in small and medium-sized enterprises. Let us modernize our public employment service. Mobilizing for employment means addressing the difficulties faced by young people, aged 16 to 24, entering the workforce by creating a genuine right to activity, training, or employment for them. No young person should be left without a solution or without support. Finally, prioritizing employment means challenging outdated attitudes to achieve equal opportunities for women and men, because we are still, unfortunately, far from that goal today.
paragraph_summary: The speaker outlines strategies to reduce unemployment, including vocational training, support for young workers, and the promotion of gender equality in the workplace.
reason: The text details domestic labor market reforms, education, and social equality initiatives.
crisis_reason: The paragraph describes routine policy measures and economic development strategies rather than framing unemployment as an urgent, existential threat or a crisis requiring immediate emergency action.

paragraph_text: The principles of action and justice must guide us in pursuing two other priorities for the nation: social cohesion and education. In six months, by appealing to the responsibility of all, our healthcare system, which is currently at risk, will be strengthened. We will continue to modernize our healthcare system, particularly our hospitals. The government will also contribute to our social framework by creating a national solidarity fund for autonomy, because we have a duty to better support people with disabilities and elderly dependents. We must also recognize everyone's place, break down barriers, and reach out to all those who feel marginalized because of their social situation or their background. We must restore the full meaning of a secularism that is open, generous, and promotes harmony among all French people. Finally, we must reaffirm the fundamental value of equal opportunity, which begins with education. A national debate on education has been launched. This is your opportunity to say what kind of education you want for the future of your children. In one year, the education system will have its orientation law.
paragraph_summary: The president outlines national priorities including healthcare reform, social cohesion, support for vulnerable populations, secularism, and the modernization of the education system.
reason: It addresses domestic social cohesion, healthcare, disability support, and national education policy.
crisis_reason: The paragraph lists routine policy priorities and governance goals without framing them as urgent threats or existential dangers to the state.

paragraph_text: If we can unite around these priorities, nothing will be impossible for us. My dear fellow citizens, from mainland France, overseas territories, and abroad, thanks to you, thanks to your commitment, and thanks to your sense of responsibility, the horizon is brighter, and our country is now on the right path. United by the values that define France, and which we are so proud of, let us together make our nation stronger, more united, and more just. From the bottom of my heart, I wish you all a happy and prosperous New Year 2004. Long live the Republic, Long live France.
paragraph_summary: The President calls for national unity and shared responsibility to strengthen France while offering New Year greetings for 2004.
reason: The paragraph is a domestic closing statement focused on national unity and values.
crisis_reason: The paragraph is framed as a ceremonial New Year greeting and a general call for unity, lacking any specific language identifying a threat or urgent danger to the state.

paragraph_text: Finally, we must do everything to be at the forefront of the technologies that will create the jobs of tomorrow. Industrial successes are prepared long in advance. Programs from previous decades, such as the TGV, nuclear power, Airbus, and Ariane, continue to underpin the strength of our industry. I have asked the government to launch, in support of our businesses and with our European partners, the industrial projects that will drive future growth and enable us to increase our technological lead. We will resolutely move towards a society of innovation and high qualifications. These will be the primary sources of dynamism and wealth in the 21st century.
paragraph_summary: The president outlines a strategy to foster future economic growth and industrial strength through technological innovation and European partnerships.
reason: The focus is on domestic industrial strategy, technological innovation, and national economic growth.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on future growth, industrial strength, and innovation, which are framed as positive developments and routine policy goals rather than urgent threats or dangers.

paragraph_text: Our place in the world is determined by our competition with the most advanced countries. That is why I have also sought to ensure that a reform will help our education system adapt to the challenges and demands of our time. The orientation law will begin to be implemented from the start of the next school year. It is essential for the success of every child and for the dynamism of the entire nation. At the same time, let us provide our research community with the means to mobilize in support of our future: that is the ambition of the reform that the government is preparing with the scientific community. And let us enable our higher education system to compete, in all fields, with the world’s highest-performing universities.
paragraph_summary: The president outlines reforms to the education, research, and higher education systems to improve national competitiveness and global standing.
reason: It discusses domestic reforms to the education and research systems to improve national competitiveness.
crisis_reason: The paragraph describes routine policy reforms aimed at improving competitiveness and standing, which are presented as positive developments rather than responses to an urgent threat or danger.

paragraph_text: My dear compatriots, from mainland France, from overseas territories, and from abroad, I wish you all a very happy New Year. To those who are entering this new year in solitude, distress, or illness, I want to express my warmest thoughts. I also want to pay tribute, on your behalf and on behalf of us all, to our soldiers serving on every continent in the service of peace. They carry out their mission with exemplary courage and professionalism. Our nation is united, ambitious, determined, and proud of its values. It is on all of you that its future rests. And it is to you that I want to say tonight my belief in you, expressing my warmest wishes for 2005. Long live the Republic, Long live France!
paragraph_summary: The President offers New Year greetings to French citizens, honors soldiers serving abroad, and expresses confidence in the nation's future.
reason: The paragraph is a domestic New Year's greeting and a tribute to national soldiers.
crisis_reason: The paragraph consists of ceremonial greetings, expressions of gratitude, and optimistic reflections on the future, which are explicitly excluded from the definition of a crisis.

paragraph_text: My dear compatriots, tonight, I also want to speak to you about our country and its future. After two and a half years of action to preserve and strengthen our solidarity, to restore sustained growth, to restore the authority of the state, and to restore the values of the Republic, the time has come to launch our project for the future: a project for a society open to Europe. A society of justice, growth, and employment. A society of intelligence, creativity, and innovation.
paragraph_summary: The President outlines a future national project focused on European integration, social justice, economic growth, and innovation.
reason: It outlines a domestic project for the future of French society, growth, and employment.
crisis_reason: The paragraph describes a forward-looking national project focused on growth and integration, which is framed as a positive development and routine governance rather than an urgent threat or danger.

paragraph_text: Let us also build a society that gives everyone a chance, a society of growth, focused on activity and employment. Since 2002, the government of Jean-Pierre Raffarin has taken the necessary steps to revitalize our economy, prioritizing employment, supporting consumer spending, and fostering the dynamism of our businesses. 2004 was the best year of growth in the past four years. For the first time since 2001, unemployment has been stabilized. This is not enough, but we are on the right track at the halfway point, and I urge the government to continue and amplify its efforts.
paragraph_summary: The president highlights economic growth and the stabilization of unemployment rates achieved through government policies while urging continued efforts to support employment.
reason: The text discusses domestic economic growth, employment, and government policy within France.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on economic achievements and the stabilization of indicators, framing the situation as a positive development rather than an urgent threat.

paragraph_text: We must do everything to promote growth. We must continue to raise the minimum wage and support purchasing power. We must continue to reduce income tax and reduce charges. We must continue to control our spending. We must encourage investment by implementing the reform of the professional tax. We must enable those who want to earn more to work more. We must give consumers greater rights. We must promote competition. We must encourage exports and strengthen our presence in emerging markets.
paragraph_summary: The speaker outlines a comprehensive economic strategy focused on promoting growth, increasing purchasing power, reducing taxes, and enhancing national competitiveness.
reason: The paragraph outlines domestic fiscal and economic policies such as income tax, minimum wage, and national business reforms.
crisis_reason: The paragraph describes routine economic policy goals such as growth and competitiveness rather than framing the economic situation as an urgent threat or danger.

paragraph_text: We must do everything to ensure that this growth leads to employment. I have sought to mobilize all branches of government to serve our social cohesion. The law has just been passed. We now have new tools to develop employment for young people, to bring those receiving minimum income support back into the workforce, and to combat discrimination in hiring. We must modernize the public employment service and actively seek out all unfilled positions, particularly in crafts and personal services, where needs are immense.
paragraph_summary: The president outlines government initiatives to boost employment, support social cohesion, and modernize public services to address labor market needs.
reason: The focus is on domestic social cohesion, employment services, and internal labor market policies.
crisis_reason: The paragraph describes routine government initiatives and policy goals for modernization and support, rather than framing these issues as urgent threats or dangers to the state.

paragraph_text: My dear compatriots, from mainland France, from overseas, and from abroad, let us be true to ourselves. Let us harness globalization to build a better future. France has always overcome the challenges it has faced. Every time, it has emerged stronger. The answer is ambition, fraternity, unity, and the Republic. Long live the Republic! Long live France!
paragraph_summary: The President calls for national unity, fraternity, and ambition to overcome challenges and leverage globalization for France's future.
reason: The paragraph is a patriotic closing statement focused on national unity and the French Republic.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on national unity and ambition as a means to leverage future opportunities rather than framing specific challenges as urgent, existential, or damaging threats.

paragraph_text: Economic growth has returned. And, most importantly, we have made significant progress in tackling unemployment. This is a major challenge, a source of so many difficulties, so much misfortune, and so many obstacles. For eight months, month after month, unemployment has been decreasing – already nearly 160, 000 fewer unemployed people. This is proof that your efforts, the deep reforms implemented since 2002, and the resolute action of the government are beginning to bear fruit. It is an extraordinary source of encouragement to continue striving to win the battle for employment.
paragraph_summary: The president highlights recent economic growth and a consistent decline in unemployment as evidence that government reforms and public efforts are succeeding.
reason: The text reports on domestic economic progress and the reduction of unemployment in France.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on economic growth and declining unemployment as evidence of success and achievement, rather than framing the economic situation as a threat or urgent danger.

paragraph_text: My dear compatriots, we must believe in France. We must rediscover the driving force and the profoundly modern meaning of the word "patriotism": to love your country, to be proud of it, and to work for it. Together, we will accelerate our actions and commit to a collective project. This project is not something to be invented; it is something to be brought to life, in our principles and in our actions: that project is the Republic.
paragraph_summary: The President calls for renewed patriotism and collective commitment to the principles of the French Republic.
reason: The paragraph emphasizes national identity, patriotism, and the internal project of the Republic.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on national values and collective commitment as a positive, aspirational goal rather than framing the state of the republic as an urgent threat or danger.

paragraph_text: At the very heart of the Republic lies respect for the law and for rules. We will intensify our fight against violence and crime. We will take stronger measures against illegal immigration. We will reform our justice system, so that a tragedy like the one in Ostraux can never happen again.
paragraph_summary: The President outlines plans to strengthen law enforcement, address illegal immigration, and reform the justice system to improve public safety.
reason: The focus is on domestic law enforcement, crime, and national immigration policy.
crisis_reason: The paragraph describes routine policy goals such as strengthening law enforcement and reforming the justice system without using language that frames these issues as an urgent, existential, or acute threat to the state.

paragraph_text: And at the core of the Republic, there is merit: the ability to succeed through your work, your determination, and your talent, regardless of where you live or your background. The ability to progress, to succeed in society, without being hindered by social barriers or privileges. Education is the key to everything. We will provide better support to schools that need it, we will make vocational training another path to success, and we will open up more widely the doors of universities and prestigious schools to children from modest backgrounds. And, importantly, honoring the Republic means intensifying our efforts to support residents of disadvantaged neighborhoods, because there can be no Republic without equal opportunities.
paragraph_summary: The President outlines initiatives to promote social mobility and equal opportunity through education reform, vocational training, and support for disadvantaged neighborhoods.
reason: The paragraph addresses domestic education, social mobility, and equal opportunity within French neighborhoods.
crisis_reason: The paragraph describes routine policy initiatives aimed at social improvement and opportunity, rather than framing these issues as urgent threats or dangers to the state.

paragraph_text: My dear compatriots, let us be uncompromising in upholding the ideals of the Republic. We must reject communautarism. We must show respect for everyone. We must uphold the principle of secularism. We must fight against discrimination. We must combat racism and antisemitism. Because we are truly ourselves only when we are freed from everything that degrades us, everything that sullies us, and everything that sows discord. Because diversity is part of our history: it is a source of richness, an asset for our future.
paragraph_summary: The President calls for upholding republican ideals, secularism, and diversity while rejecting discrimination, racism, antisemitism, and social fragmentation.
reason: The text discusses internal social values, secularism, and the fight against discrimination within French society.
crisis_reason: The paragraph frames social fragmentation, racism, and antisemitism as active threats to the nation's core values and social cohesion, demanding a defensive response.

paragraph_text: Of course, I would like to see things move faster, and to move forward in a way that benefits everyone. But thanks to your talent and your hard work, France is asserting itself. With the resolute action of the Prime Minister and his government, unemployment is falling sharply – already 360, 000 fewer unemployed people. Economic growth is here and it is solid. Our pensions and our social security system are being reformed, guaranteeing their future. The number of new social housing units built each year has doubled since 2002. Fortunately, our attitudes and behaviors are evolving regarding people with disabilities, cancer victims, and those affected by road accidents. Thanks to your commitment, nearly 9, 000 lives have been saved on French roads.
paragraph_summary: The President highlights improvements in unemployment rates, economic growth, social reforms, housing development, and increased road safety in France.
reason: The text highlights domestic economic achievements, social housing, and public health improvements in France.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on positive developments, achievements, and routine policy progress rather than presenting these issues as urgent threats or dangers.

paragraph_text: Before the upcoming elections, I will have two priorities: that the government remains hard at work, at your service, for security, for employment, and for purchasing power. And I want these months to be a time for open, democratic, and responsible debate, and I will fully commit to that.
paragraph_summary: The president outlines his priorities of maintaining government focus on security, employment, and purchasing power while fostering responsible democratic debate before the upcoming elections.
reason: The paragraph outlines domestic political priorities and the upcoming national election process.
crisis_reason: The paragraph describes routine governance priorities and the maintenance of democratic processes rather than framing these issues as urgent threats or existential dangers.

paragraph_text: Tonight, I want to tell you what I believe are the major challenges facing us. First, it is the unity and cohesion around the values that define France: freedom, humanism, respect, and in particular, respect for diversity and differences, secularism, and the fight against racism, antisemitism, and communautarism. Do not listen to the amateur sorcerers of extremism. France is strong, and France is beautiful when it knows how to both enforce its rules and extend a helping hand.
paragraph_summary: The President calls for national unity and cohesion based on French values while rejecting extremism and discrimination.
reason: The focus is on internal national values, social cohesion, and domestic political discourse.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on the promotion of national values and social cohesion as a positive, normative goal rather than framing extremism or discrimination as an urgent, active threat requiring immediate crisis response.

paragraph_text: Second, it is obviously economic and social progress. I know the hardships and difficulties that some of you are facing. I understand your expectations regarding salaries and purchasing power – that is, the fair reward for your work. But let us be wary of ideologies, illusions, and a return to outdated solutions that simply do not work. It is work, training, and research that drive modern economies. By fostering social dialogue, we have important reforms ahead of us: to further reduce unemployment, to make participation a true social project, to empower our businesses, and to implement a genuine, enforceable right to housing – that is, to make the right to housing a reality. And I am asking the government to make progress on this issue in the coming weeks.
paragraph_summary: The president outlines economic and social priorities, including improving purchasing power, reducing unemployment, and implementing housing reforms through work and social dialogue.
reason: The text addresses domestic economic and social reforms, including housing and labor market policies.
crisis_reason: The paragraph describes routine governance and policy priorities such as economic improvement and housing reform without framing them as urgent threats or existential dangers.

paragraph_text: My dear compatriots, yes, we can be proud to be French! Let us continue our efforts to modernize. Let us not seek to imitate others. Let us be ourselves. Next spring, you will have to make important choices. Live your convictions intensely. You are the sovereign people. France needs each and every one of you. It counts on you. Long live the Republic! Long live France!
paragraph_summary: The President encourages citizens to embrace national identity and participate actively in the upcoming spring elections.
reason: Focuses on national pride, domestic modernization, and upcoming elections.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on civic participation and national identity, which are presented as routine democratic processes rather than urgent threats or dangers.

paragraph_text: This is the task you entrusted to me when you elected me President of the Republic last May. It is a monumental task, given how far behind France has fallen in keeping pace with the world. I know how great your expectations are for profound change, after years of effort and sacrifice, many of whom feel that those sacrifices have been in vain. I understand the anxieties that so many of you feel about the future of your children. I am aware of the anxieties that grip you when you fear losing your jobs, or when you worry that the rising cost of living will no longer allow you, even with hard work, to provide a decent living for your families. I understand your frustration when you want to start a business or work harder, but feel that everything is designed to prevent you from doing so. Of course, not everything can be resolved in a single day! But believe me, my determination is unwavering. Despite the obstacles, despite the difficulties, I promise you, I will deliver. I will do so simply because it is in the best interests of France.
paragraph_summary: The President acknowledges public anxieties regarding economic hardship and employment while reaffirming his commitment to delivering promised reforms for the benefit of France.
reason: Addresses domestic economic concerns and the President's national mandate.
crisis_reason: The paragraph acknowledges public anxiety regarding economic hardship, but frames the situation through the lens of the president's commitment to delivering reforms, which shifts the focus from the threat itself to the government's ongoing policy response.

paragraph_text: Since you chose me to lead our country, I have strived to fulfill the promise I made to you: to restore your pride in being French, to give you the feeling that anything is possible in our great nation. Together with François Fillon and the entire government, we have implemented significant changes over the past eight months. To those who feel that progress has been too slow, I want to say that I have done everything I believed was possible, while respecting the need for social dialogue and negotiation. I do not believe in imposing change through force; I believe my role is to persuade and unite, not to shock and divide. This is what I have strived to do, with respect for all. To those who feel that change has been too rapid, I want to remind you that our country has waited too long, and time is of the essence if we are to remain masters of our own destiny. I have sought to hold everyone accountable. I have taken responsibility for my own actions. I may have made mistakes. But for the past eight months, I have acted solely in the interests of France, and not a single day has passed without me reaffirming my commitment to each and every one of you: "I will not deceive you, I will not betray you. " I owe you the truth, and I will always tell it. I will not allow any hypocrisy. I have poured my heart and my energy into being the President of all French people, not just those who have always shared my views. That is why I have championed openness, and why I have surrounded myself with capable men and women. I have not asked them to renounce their beliefs; I have simply asked them to serve their country. They have accepted, and I am grateful for their service.
paragraph_summary: The President reflects on his first eight months in office, emphasizing his commitment to national unity, transparent governance, and balanced political reform.
reason: Reviews domestic governance, political strategy, and internal reforms.
