crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on the president's commitment to governance, unity, and reform as a reflection of his tenure, rather than framing these as responses to an urgent threat or danger.

paragraph_text: With 2008, a second stage begins: a policy that addresses even more fundamental issues, our way of being in society and in the world, our culture, our identity, our values, our relationship with others – in short, everything that constitutes a civilization. For too long, politics has been reduced to mere administration, remaining apart from the underlying causes of our problems, which are often much deeper. I am convinced that in this era, we need a policy of civilization. We will resolve nothing if we do not build the schools and cities of the 21st century, if we do not place at the heart of our policies the concerns of integration, diversity, justice, human rights, and the environment, if we do not rediscover a taste for adventure and risk, a sense of responsibility alongside respect and solidarity, or if we do not undertake to reform financial capitalism. It is not about giving speeches – we have given so many – it is about taking action to achieve results.
model_reasons: environment: refers to a broad social or political context, not the natural environment
paragraph_summary: The President advocates for a policy of civilization addressing societal values, integration, environmental concerns, and financial reform to move beyond mere administrative governance.
reason: Outlines a domestic policy agenda focused on social, educational, and economic reforms.
crisis_reason: The paragraph describes a shift in governance strategy and policy priorities rather than framing these issues as urgent threats or existential dangers to the state.

paragraph_text: My dear fellow citizens, the year 2008 is drawing to a close. It has been a difficult year. That is why I want to first pay tribute to those who have been most severely tested by life, to those who have lost their jobs through no fault of their own, to those who have suffered injustice, and to those who are mourning the loss of a loved one. For all French people, this year has been challenging. The global economic and financial crisis has added its share of hardship and suffering. Each and every one of you is feeling the consequences. Faced with this crisis, I am acutely aware of the responsibility that rests with me. I will assume that responsibility, ensuring that those who need it most are protected by the state, and that our country emerges stronger from this ordeal. Since these difficulties began, I have always told you the truth and acted accordingly. That was my duty.
paragraph_summary: President Sarkozy addresses the hardships caused by the 2008 global financial crisis and pledges state protection for vulnerable citizens.
reason: Addresses the domestic impact of the global economic crisis on French citizens.
crisis_reason: The paragraph explicitly frames the 2008 financial situation as a source of hardship and identifies the need for state protection, treating the economic instability as an urgent threat to citizens' livelihoods.

paragraph_text: The challenges that lie ahead in 2009 will be significant. I am fully aware of that. I am more determined than ever to face them, with a commitment to justice and a relentless focus on achieving results. After preserving the economy through the bank rescue plan, we must now focus on saving jobs. The massive investment stimulus plan of 26 billion euros that has been decided will contribute to that goal. This is a considerable effort. Measures have been approved to support our automotive industry, in exchange for commitments from manufacturers not to relocate production. Further initiatives will be taken with the sovereign fund that we have established, to safeguard our industrial base. We will be pragmatic, attentive, and responsive, and if more action is needed, we will take it, while maintaining our composure.
paragraph_summary: President Sarkozy outlines economic stimulus measures and industrial support policies intended to protect jobs and the French industrial base during the 2009 financial crisis.
reason: Details domestic economic stimulus plans and industrial support measures.
crisis_reason: The paragraph frames the 2009 financial crisis as an active threat to jobs and the industrial base, necessitating urgent government intervention and stimulus measures.

paragraph_text: My dear fellow citizens, we have the means to overcome these challenges, provided we stand together in solidarity. I will not allow the most vulnerable to struggle alone in their most difficult circumstances. In times of adversity, solidarity must prevail, and work must be encouraged. That is why I wanted the RSA (Revenu de Solidarité Active) to be created, and it will be implemented for the first time in 2009. From now on, every French person who finds employment will be encouraged, valued, and rewarded.
paragraph_summary: The President announces the 2009 implementation of the RSA to support vulnerable citizens and incentivize employment through solidarity.
reason: Discusses domestic social welfare policy and employment incentives.
crisis_reason: The paragraph describes the implementation of a social policy as a routine governance measure to support citizens and incentivize employment, rather than framing the situation as an urgent threat or crisis.

paragraph_text: We all must make an effort to overcome this crisis, because from this crisis will emerge a new world to which we must prepare by working harder, investing more, and continuing the reforms that we cannot afford to abandon, as they are vital for our future. During 2009, we will reform the healthcare system, whose staff demonstrate such admirable dedication and expertise; vocational training, essential for ensuring that everyone has the opportunity to find employment; our regional organization, which has been made so complex by so much conservatism; and research, which is essential for our competitiveness. I also want to address the reform of the high school system, which is necessary to prevent so many of our children from failing in higher education and to address the injustice that prevents so many children and young people from modest backgrounds from having the same opportunities as others. I have asked that we take the time for consultation, because taking the time to reflect together is not a waste of time for reform; it is an investment. Finally, I want to address the reform of our criminal procedure, which is so important for better protecting our individual freedoms, a need that has become strikingly clear during the past year. My dear fellow citizens, I, along with Prime Minister François Fillon and the government, will carry out these reforms, not out of a desire to follow a particular agenda, but because they are essential for France to secure its place in this new world that is being built. In doing so, we will become more competitive and more innovative. At the same time, we will preserve the values that define us: work, effort, merit, secularism, and solidarity, without which no effort is acceptable.
paragraph_summary: The President outlines a series of structural reforms in healthcare, education, vocational training, and criminal procedure to improve France's competitiveness and future prospects.
reason: Outlines a comprehensive list of domestic institutional and social reforms.
crisis_reason: The paragraph describes routine policy reforms aimed at improvement and future prospects rather than framing these areas as urgent threats or existential dangers.

paragraph_text: My dear fellow citizens, this crisis requires us to change more quickly and more profoundly. The crisis is a test, but it is also a challenge. I want to meet that challenge with you. You can count on me. We have considerable strengths. There is within the French people, when united, enough energy, intelligence, and courage for us to confidently look to the future. We will emerge from this crisis stronger. From the bottom of my heart, I extend to each and every one of you my best wishes for 2009. Long live the Republic, and long live France.
paragraph_summary: The President addresses the 2009 crisis by calling for national unity and expressing confidence in France's ability to emerge stronger.
reason: Offers domestic encouragement and New Year wishes to the French people.
crisis_reason: The paragraph explicitly identifies the 2009 situation as a 'crisis' and frames it as a threat requiring national unity, which constitutes an active threat-focused framing.

paragraph_text: At the end of this year, it has a more favorable tax system for work and investment, thanks to the reform of the professional tax; a high school system that better prepares students for higher education; universities that are finally autonomous; a public transportation system with a minimum service that functions; a RSA (social assistance) program that encourages those most in need to return to work; vocational training that is more focused on young people and those seeking employment; a healthcare system, a judicial system, and a defense organization that are better adapted to the needs of our time; and a French sovereign fund that now stands alongside our businesses to help them grow and protect them.
paragraph_summary: The president highlights various structural reforms implemented across the tax, education, transportation, social assistance, healthcare, judicial, and defense sectors.
reason: Reviews specific domestic policy reforms implemented by the government.
crisis_reason: The paragraph describes a series of routine policy implementations and structural reforms as achievements of governance rather than as responses to an urgent threat or danger.

paragraph_text: Many reforms have been accomplished. I know that they have disrupted established habits and that, before they take effect, they may have caused concerns. But who can believe that in a world that is constantly changing, standing still is a viable alternative? We still have much work to do. I will lead it, together with the Prime Minister and the Government, through dialogue and with a sense of fairness. In 2010, we will need to: reduce unemployment and social exclusion, reduce our current spending to enable us to increase investment in the future, simplify our territorial organization, which is currently too cumbersome, too complex, and too expensive, consolidate our pension system, the financial sustainability of which I have a duty to ensure, and address the challenge of dependency, which will be one of the most painful problems facing our families in the decades to come. In 2010, we will reform our justice system to better protect freedoms and to be more attentive to victims.
paragraph_summary: The President outlines his 2010 agenda, focusing on economic reforms, unemployment reduction, pension sustainability, territorial simplification, and justice system improvements.
reason: Outlines the domestic reform agenda for the upcoming year.
crisis_reason: The paragraph describes a standard legislative agenda and routine policy goals for the upcoming year, lacking any language framing these issues as urgent threats or existential dangers.

paragraph_text: To each and every one of you, my dear compatriots, I extend my best wishes for happiness in the year ahead, with a special thought for our soldiers, separated from their families, who risk their lives to defend our values and guarantee our security. To our compatriots overseas, I want to reaffirm my determination that the Republic must keep its promise of equality and dignity towards them, a promise that has not always been fully kept in the past. And to the most vulnerable among us, to those weakened by age, to those who have been severely affected by the hardships of life, I want to say tonight that they will not be abandoned. Faced with isolation and loneliness, which are so prevalent in our modern societies, I hope that 2010 will be the year in which we restore meaning to the beautiful word "fraternity, " which is enshrined in our republican motto. My dear compatriots, long live the Republic, and long live France.
paragraph_summary: The President extends New Year wishes while emphasizing support for soldiers, overseas citizens, vulnerable populations, and the importance of national fraternity.
reason: The paragraph focuses on domestic social issues, national values, and addressing the needs of French citizens.
crisis_reason: The paragraph is framed as a ceremonial expression of New Year wishes and national solidarity, lacking any language that identifies a specific threat or urgent danger requiring response.

paragraph_text: My dear fellow citizens, the year 2010 is drawing to a close. I know that it has been a difficult year for many of you. The economic and financial crisis, which began three years ago, has continued to take its toll, and many have lost their jobs, which has only exacerbated the sense of injustice felt by employees who bear no responsibility for the crisis. Nevertheless, thanks to the hard work of the French people, their courage, their ability to adapt, the strength of our economy, and the advantages of our social model, the recession was less severe and shorter than that experienced by many of our partners. And the year 2011 promises to be a year of hope. Growth is returning.
paragraph_summary: The President reflects on the impact of the economic crisis on French citizens while highlighting signs of recovery and economic growth for 2011.
reason: The text discusses the domestic economic situation and the impact of the recession on French citizens.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on the recovery and economic growth for the upcoming year rather than the threat itself, framing the situation as an achievement rather than an active crisis.

paragraph_text: The major reforms we have undertaken are beginning to bear fruit. Our universities, now finally autonomous, are opening up and modernizing as never before. Our researchers have been provided with substantial financial resources thanks to the major investment program. Our businesses are fully utilizing the research tax credit to innovate. More than 5 million employees have worked overtime, with all the earnings fully tax-free, benefiting both themselves and the companies that employ them, which has helped to support purchasing power despite the crisis. Our pension system has been safeguarded from the inevitable collapse that loomed if we had done nothing. We have protected the pensions of our seniors, and for the first time, France has been able to undertake a major reform without violence or disruption, thanks to the minimum service provision, which worked well, and the sense of responsibility demonstrated by the French people, who understood that this, however painful, was unavoidable. I want to pay tribute to their maturity and their collective intelligence.
paragraph_summary: The president highlights the success of recent reforms in higher education, research, business innovation, tax-free overtime, and the stabilization of the national pension system.
reason: The paragraph details specific domestic policy reforms regarding education, research, taxation, and the pension system.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on the success of reforms and the stabilization of systems, which frames the content as an achievement and positive development rather than an urgent threat.

paragraph_text: We are in 2011, and 2012 will be a major electoral year. However, we cannot afford a year of pre-election inertia, when the world is advancing at a staggering pace. 2011 must therefore be a productive year for the French people. Difficulties are secondary when the interests of the nation and the well-being of the French people are at stake. My duty is to prioritize the general interest in all circumstances. Until the very last minute of my term, I will have no other rule than that. We will therefore continue to reform, because that is the only way to preserve our model and our identity, the only way to protect France and the French people.
paragraph_summary: President Sarkozy emphasizes the necessity of continuing national reforms and prioritizing the general interest despite the upcoming 2012 election cycle.
reason: The focus is on domestic political productivity and the government's commitment to internal reform despite the upcoming election.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on routine governance and the prioritization of national interest during an election cycle, lacking any language framing these reforms as a response to an urgent threat or danger.

paragraph_text: We must protect them from dependence, because everyone has the right to dignity in the face of the hardships of old age. We must protect them from relocations by harmonizing our taxation with our German neighbors. We must protect them from the increasingly brutal violence perpetrated by repeat offenders by opening our correctional courts to popular juries. Thus, it is the people who will be able to express their opinion on the severity of the response to behaviors that provoke the exasperation of the country.
paragraph_summary: The speaker proposes protecting the elderly from dependence, harmonizing taxation with Germany, and involving popular juries in sentencing repeat offenders to address public frustration.
reason: The paragraph addresses domestic social issues including elderly care, taxation, and judicial reforms.
crisis_reason: The speaker frames public frustration and the need for specific interventions like popular juries as a response to a problem, but these are presented as routine policy proposals rather than an urgent threat to the state or society.

paragraph_text: With Prime Minister François Fillon, whom I have complete confidence in, and with the government, we must work tirelessly throughout this year to achieve a renewed French prosperity that will allow us to create the jobs we need. I will do my duty by listening, by engaging in dialogue, but when the time comes, by making the decisions that are necessary with a spirit of truth and justice. I will do so by scrupulously respecting our most cherished republican principles: secularism and the rejection of communautarism. The law prohibiting the burqa will be applied in both letter and spirit. We must remind everyone that rights must always be accompanied by responsibilities. Education is compulsory. Absenteeism is unacceptable, as it condemns those who abandon themselves to it to failure. Respect for the law is non-negotiable, and it cannot be flouted. Similarly, respect for France is a requirement for those we welcome. Equal opportunities and justice are neither egalitarianism nor welfare, and they must lead us to consider the revaluation of work as an absolutely non-negotiable priority. Finally, freedom must go hand in hand with respect for others.
paragraph_summary: The President outlines priorities including economic prosperity, job creation, the enforcement of secularism, and the necessity of upholding republican values and the rule of law.
reason: The text outlines domestic policy priorities such as secularism, education, and the enforcement of national laws.
crisis_reason: The paragraph describes routine governance priorities and the affirmation of national values rather than framing these topics as urgent threats or dangers.

paragraph_text: And yet, there are reasons for hope. We must, we can maintain confidence in the future. For while so many countries have faced insurmountable difficulties, France has persevered. It has resisted. And if it has persevered, if it has resisted, if it has succeeded so far in averting the doubt that triggers crises of confidence, it is thanks to the courage and composure you have shown for the past three years, thanks to the strength of our institutions, and thanks to our social safety net, which guarantees solidarity in times of hardship, and thanks to the reforms we have implemented in recent years. I am particularly thinking of the pension reform and all the measures aimed at reducing our public spending, which have enabled France to maintain the confidence of those who lend their savings to finance our economy. It is not about denying the difficulties we are facing. But within these challenges, France has managed to preserve what is essential. I would like to pay particular tribute to all of you who, through your work, have contributed to the development of our economy.
paragraph_summary: The President attributes France's economic resilience and stability to public reforms, institutional strength, and the collective efforts of the workforce.
reason: The paragraph highlights domestic resilience, institutional strength, and the success of internal reforms.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on economic resilience, stability, and the success of reforms, which frames the situation as an achievement rather than an active threat or urgent danger.

paragraph_text: In five months, we have a presidential election. It is an important milestone. In due time, you will make your choice. But until then, I must continue to act, because the history of the decades to come is being written now. Together with the Prime Minister, we will meet on January 18th with representatives from the economic and social forces of our country. I will listen to the proposals of everyone, and before the end of January, we will make and assume important decisions, because the stakes are crucial. The crisis is serious, the circumstances are exceptional, and the decisions must be commensurate with this gravity. It is a duty that I will not shirk.
paragraph_summary: The President announces upcoming consultations with economic and social leaders to address the country's serious crisis through decisive government action before the presidential election.
reason: The focus is on the upcoming domestic presidential election and the government's immediate internal decision-making process.
crisis_reason: The speaker explicitly labels the situation as a 'serious crisis' and frames it as an urgent threat requiring 'decisive government action' and immediate consultations.

paragraph_text: I do not underestimate the consequences that the rating agencies and the volatility of the financial markets may have on our economy, nor do I underestimate our past mistakes, but I say this so that everyone understands: neither the markets nor the rating agencies will dictate the policy of France. Ultimately, the only way to preserve our sovereignty, to control our destiny, is to choose, as we have done so far, the path of structural reforms rather than reacting impulsively, which only adds to the confusion and disorder without restoring confidence. The problem is not a new round of spending cuts for the coming year. The government has already done what needed to be done. Now, we must prioritize growth, competitiveness, and industrial revitalization, which alone will allow us to create jobs and increase purchasing power.
paragraph_summary: The President asserts that France will prioritize structural reforms, growth, and industrial revitalization over market pressures to ensure national sovereignty and economic stability.
reason: The paragraph emphasizes maintaining national sovereignty and prioritizing domestic structural reforms over market pressures.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on proactive economic policy, growth, and industrial revitalization as a means of ensuring stability, rather than framing the current situation as an urgent threat or crisis.

paragraph_text: Three issues seem to dominate all others. We will not overcome this crisis by abandoning those who are already suffering the painful consequences of a crisis they did not cause. We will not build our competitiveness on exclusion, but on our ability to give everyone a place in our nation. That is why those who have lost their jobs must be the focus of our attention. We must change our perspective on unemployment. We must ensure that training for the unemployed becomes our top priority, so that everyone can rebuild their future. Training, not just providing assistance – that must be our goal. No one should be excluded from this opportunity. The second issue is the funding of our social safety net, which can no longer primarily rely on employment, which is so easily relocated. We must ease the burden on employment and ensure that imports, which compete with our products using low-wage labor, contribute financially. This issue has been at the heart of all debates for years. I will listen to the proposals of the social partners, and then we will make a decision. The third issue is the instability in the financial sector, which shocks you so deeply, largely because it is largely responsible for the current difficulties. We must ensure that the financial sector contributes to repairing the damage it has caused. It is a matter of efficiency, a matter of justice, and a matter of morality. The tax on financial transactions must be implemented.
paragraph_summary: The president outlines priorities for addressing unemployment through training, reforming social safety net funding, and implementing a tax on the financial sector.
reason: The text proposes domestic policy solutions for unemployment, social safety net funding, and financial regulation.
crisis_reason: The paragraph describes routine policy measures such as training programs, funding reforms, and tax implementation, which are presented as standard governance rather than an urgent threat to the state or society.

paragraph_text: My dear fellow citizens, my duty is to face these challenges and to protect you. You can be sure that I will fully assume the heavy responsibilities that you have entrusted to me, and that I will tirelessly work in the best interests of the nation. Everyone must take responsibility. That is why I call on all business and economic leaders to do everything possible to preserve jobs. I call on all those working in the public sector to redouble their efforts to support those who need our solidarity.
paragraph_summary: The President calls on business leaders to protect jobs and public sector workers to increase efforts in supporting vulnerable citizens.
reason: The paragraph calls for domestic cooperation from business leaders and public sector workers to support the national economy.
crisis_reason: The paragraph frames the economic situation as a threat to livelihoods and social stability by explicitly calling for urgent protective action and increased support for vulnerable citizens.

paragraph_text: My dear fellow citizens, faithful to a cherished tradition, I extend to each and every one of you my warmest wishes for the new year. Last May, you entrusted me with the task of leading our country through a particularly difficult period. We face a historic crisis, with unemployment steadily rising for nearly two years, and a record level of debt. I am not ignoring your concerns; they are legitimate. And I do not intend to conceal the difficulties that lie ahead. They are serious. But tonight, I want to express my confidence in our future: the Eurozone has been safeguarded, and Europe has finally established the stability and growth instruments it was lacking. This result, just six months ago, seemed out of reach. It has been achieved. My confidence, above all, rests in France. I know the talent of our entrepreneurs, as well as our employees. My duty, my primary duty, my sole duty, is to ensure that our country progresses and that our young people regain hope.
paragraph_summary: The President addresses rising unemployment and national debt while expressing confidence in France's economic future and European stability.
reason: The paragraph addresses domestic concerns regarding unemployment and debt while expressing confidence in national recovery.
crisis_reason: The paragraph frames rising unemployment and national debt as significant economic challenges, which constitutes a chronic threat to the nation's stability.

paragraph_text: That is why, since my election, together with the government of Jean-Marc Ayrault, I have taken three major decisions. The first is the restoration of our public finances. I want to reduce France’s debt. An effort has been requested. I know what that represents after so many years of sacrifices. I assure you that every euro collected will be accompanied by a rigorous effort to reduce unnecessary public spending. The money of the French people is precious. Each of you earns it through hard work. It must therefore serve an exemplary and efficient state. The second decision is the competitiveness pact. I want to give businesses greater flexibility, starting in 2013, through a €20 billion tax credit to enable them to hire, invest, and export. The third is the control of finance: the public investment bank is being created, the upcoming banking law will protect us from speculation, and a financial transaction tax will be introduced at the European level next year.
paragraph_summary: The president outlines three economic strategies: reducing public debt, implementing a competitiveness pact for businesses, and increasing financial regulation.
reason: The text details specific domestic economic policies including public finance restoration, tax credits for businesses, and banking regulation.
crisis_reason: The paragraph describes routine economic governance and policy strategies rather than framing the economic situation as an urgent threat or danger.

paragraph_text: This progress has not been without its challenges and setbacks. I acknowledge that. But the timetable I have set is to implement reforms now, to overcome the crisis more quickly and more strongly. These decisions were essential; they had been delayed for too long. I have implemented them with a sense of fairness: fiscal fairness, first of all. Income from capital is now taxed the same as income from labor. And those with the most will always be asked to contribute more. This is the purpose of the exceptional contribution on the highest incomes, which will be revised, following the Constitutional Council’s decision, without changing its objective. Social fairness. With the increase in the minimum wage (SMIC), the RSA (a basic income benefit), and the back-to-school allowance. And the right to retire at 60 for those who began working at a young age. Fairness between generations, with priority given to the national education system, with more and better-trained teachers.
paragraph_summary: The president defends his reform agenda, emphasizing fiscal, social, and intergenerational fairness through tax adjustments, wage increases, and educational investments.
reason: Discusses domestic fiscal reforms, social benefits, and national education policy.
crisis_reason: The paragraph describes a routine policy agenda focused on fiscal and social reforms, framing them as positive developments or governance rather than as a response to an urgent threat or danger.

paragraph_text: 2012 was the year when, together, we began the recovery. 2013 will be the year of collective effort to achieve that recovery. All our energies will be focused on a single goal: to reverse the unemployment trend within a year. We must achieve this, whatever it takes. With 150, 000 “future employment” positions for young people most distant from the job market. With “generation contracts” that will link the experience of senior workers with the aspirations of young people. They will take effect tomorrow. With vocational training, which will be reformed to prioritize job seekers.
paragraph_summary: President Hollande outlines government initiatives to combat unemployment and stimulate job growth for young and senior workers throughout 2013.
reason: Focuses on domestic unemployment reduction and national job market initiatives.
crisis_reason: The paragraph describes routine government initiatives and policy goals for job growth rather than framing unemployment as an urgent, existential threat or a crisis requiring immediate emergency action.

paragraph_text: But the state is not the only actor. That is why the government has opened negotiations on job security. The objective? To provide greater stability for employees and greater flexibility for businesses. In short, to address a dual fear. The fear of losing one’s job for workers, and the fear of hiring for employers. If this negotiation is successful, it will be an opportunity for France. I trust our social partners to take responsibility. If not, I will. That is the path we have set: everything for employment, competitiveness, and growth. This path will be maintained, come what may. I will not deviate, not out of stubbornness, but out of conviction. It is in the best interests of France.
paragraph_summary: The president outlines government negotiations aimed at balancing job security for employees with increased flexibility for businesses to foster economic growth and competitiveness.
reason: Addresses domestic labor negotiations and national economic competitiveness.
crisis_reason: the paragraph describes routine government negotiations and policy adjustments aimed at economic growth and competitiveness, rather than framing the situation as an urgent threat or danger.

paragraph_text: To prepare for the future, our country needs to invest in all areas: in our industrial and agricultural sectors, in housing, in the environment, in healthcare, in research, in new technologies. I have therefore asked the government to propose a strategy for public and private investment to modernize France by 2020.
model_reasons: environment: refers to the general socio-economic or investment environment
paragraph_summary: The President outlines a comprehensive national investment strategy across multiple sectors to modernize France by 2020.
reason: Outlines a national investment strategy for modernizing French infrastructure and sectors.
crisis_reason: The paragraph describes a routine national investment strategy and modernization plan, which is framed as a positive development and ordinary governance rather than an urgent threat.

paragraph_text: My fellow citizens, from mainland France, from overseas territories, and from abroad, we have all the resources to succeed, provided we focus on what truly matters. And if we can sometimes debate important social issues – and we will in 2013 – France is France when it moves forward on equality of rights – including marriage for all, on democracy – including the prohibition of holding multiple political offices, and on respect for human dignity – including at the end of life.
paragraph_summary: President Hollande outlines his 2013 legislative agenda, focusing on marriage equality, political office reform, and end-of-life dignity.
reason: Discusses domestic social issues, equality rights, and political reform within France.
crisis_reason: The paragraph describes a routine legislative agenda and policy goals, which are presented as standard governance rather than urgent threats or dangers requiring immediate crisis response.

paragraph_text: One of our strengths is solidarity. We owe it to oppressed peoples. We owe it, close to home, to the most vulnerable, the sick, the isolated, those with disabilities, or those experiencing precariousness or loneliness. They are not dependents. They are citizens, sometimes wounded by life. It is fitting for a great nation like ours to be able to combine competitiveness and solidarity, performance and protection, success and sharing. This is the ambition of a reconciled and confident France that I champion for the year ahead. It is this ambition that gives meaning to everyone’s efforts. Long live the Republic! Long live France!
paragraph_summary: The President emphasizes the importance of national solidarity and social protection for vulnerable citizens as a core ambition for France's future.
reason: Emphasizes national solidarity and domestic social welfare for vulnerable citizens.
crisis_reason: the paragraph frames social protection and solidarity as a positive, forward-looking ambition for the nation's future rather than as a response to an urgent threat or emergency.

paragraph_text: I repeat to you tonight: I have only one priority, one goal, one commitment: jobs! Because every job created is a small victory, a renewed sense of strength. Every unemployed person who finds work is a family that can breathe again, a source of hope returning, a boost to purchasing power, a return to social justice. In 2014, we will need everyone to mobilize to win this battle. That is why I am proposing a responsibility pact with businesses. It is based on a simple principle: fewer costs on labor, fewer constraints on their activities, and in return, more hiring and more social dialogue. I would also like to acknowledge the social partners who, at the beginning of the year, already managed to reach an agreement on securing employment, and in the last months of this year, reached an agreement on vocational training. This will help young people to better integrate into the workforce, help the unemployed to be better supported in their job search, and give those with fewer qualifications a new opportunity within businesses. Therefore, a law will be passed at the beginning of 2014, because I want to translate this reform into reality as quickly as possible.
paragraph_summary: The President outlines a strategy to reduce unemployment through a responsibility pact with businesses, labor cost reductions, and new vocational training legislation.
reason: Details domestic employment strategies and national labor agreements.
crisis_reason: The paragraph describes routine policy measures such as vocational training and labor cost adjustments, which are presented as standard governance rather than an urgent threat to the state or society.

paragraph_text: My dear fellow citizens, now more than ever, we must love France. Nothing is worse than self-deprecation. Being realistic has never prevented us from being proud. France is not just a great history, and we will be celebrating that history in 2014, marking the centenary of the Great War. France is a promise, a future, an opportunity. Yes, it is a privilege to be French in today’s world. And it is with this conviction that I send you my warmest wishes for this new year. My wishes are for the success of each and every one of you, and for all of us. At this moment, I think of those who are suffering, those who live in isolation, those who lack adequate housing, and even those who are homeless. We have a duty of solidarity towards them. I am also thinking of our fellow citizens who are being held hostage. One of them, Father Vandenbeusch, has just been released. I am delighted for him and his family. But six others remain detained. In the name of the fraternity that unites us, I will do everything possible to secure their release. That is my wish for the new year: a commitment to employment that will lead to the success of France. Long live the Republic! Long live France!
paragraph_summary: The President calls for national pride and solidarity while addressing the release of hostages and the importance of employment for France's future success.
reason: Offers New Year wishes and addresses domestic social solidarity and national history.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on national pride, solidarity, and the positive goal of employment for future success, which frames the situation as an achievement or a vision for the future rather than an active threat.

paragraph_text: The year 2014 was a difficult year, marked by various challenges. I remained steadfast and firmly followed the course I had set. The responsibility pact, which I announced at the beginning of the year, will take effect tomorrow morning. Businesses and self-employed workers will see a reduction in their charges; there will no longer be any employer contributions for employees earning the minimum wage. Now it is up to businesses to hire and invest. This is the meaning of the word "responsibility, " because our obligation, I would even say our common obligation, is to fight unemployment. Significant reforms have also been implemented throughout the year that is drawing to a close. As of January 1st, tomorrow, the most demanding working conditions will finally be taken into account when calculating retirement benefits. The system must be as simple as possible for businesses, and I will personally ensure that, in consultation with social partners, who have also committed to an essential negotiation aimed at modernizing social dialogue in our country.
paragraph_summary: The President outlines economic reforms, including the responsibility pact to reduce business costs and measures to address unemployment and retirement benefits.
reason: Focuses on domestic economic pacts, unemployment, and national retirement reforms.
crisis_reason: The paragraph describes routine economic policy and governance measures rather than framing these issues as an urgent threat or an existential danger to the state.

paragraph_text: Similarly, the territorial reform, the one that had been announced and abandoned so many times, has been adopted in less than six months. Next year, you will elect representatives for these new local authorities. Whatever your choice, it will mean greater efficiency and reduced spending. France is capable of transformation, and I know that you are ready for it. And that is what we will continue to do in 2015, starting with the law that will be presented by the Minister of Economy, Emmanuel Macron, as early as January. It will unleash initiatives, dismantle vested interests, free up energy and activity, develop employment, and simplify life for businesses, while protecting employees. This will be a boost for our society, as this law is primarily aimed at young people.
paragraph_summary: The President discusses the implementation of territorial reform and upcoming economic legislation aimed at fostering growth, simplifying business, and supporting youth employment.
reason: Discusses domestic territorial reform and national economic legislation.
crisis_reason: The paragraph describes routine governance, legislative planning, and positive economic development goals rather than framing these issues as urgent threats or dangers.

paragraph_text: Young people will always be my priority, with additional resources to combat educational inequality, with better-trained young teachers, and with the launch of a major digital plan for schools, because I want France to be the leading European country in the use of new technologies. This will be a powerful tool for knowledge and also for social justice. Because France is only strong when it is fair. In 2015, we will also abolish the first tier of income tax, as I promised. Family allowances will now be adjusted according to income. Support for the elderly will be improved, and access to healthcare will be facilitated without compromising the freedom of healthcare professionals. But we must also be able to come together on the most difficult, I would even say the most intimate, social issues: I am thinking of end-of-life care and the right to die with dignity. I hope that in 2015, Parliament will adopt a consensus law that contributes to easing suffering and takes into account the wishes of those who are ill.
paragraph_summary: The President outlines priorities for 2015, including educational reform, tax adjustments, healthcare improvements, and legislative consensus on end-of-life care.
reason: Outlines domestic policies on education, healthcare, and social welfare.
crisis_reason: The paragraph describes routine policy priorities and legislative goals for the upcoming year without framing them as urgent threats or dangers to the state.

paragraph_text: And when faced with the threats that are rising and causing concern – threats such as terrorism, communautarism, and fundamentalism – we will not protect ourselves by dividing ourselves, stigmatizing a religion, or giving in to fear. Instead, we must firmly defend our common values: secularism, the republican order, the safety of people, and the dignity of women. It is when France forgets its principles that it loses its way, that it drowns. That is the only decline that threatens us: the decline of abandonment. It has happened before, in history, both in France and in Europe. Let us never forget that. That is why I am making the fight against racism and antisemitism a major national cause. Similarly, we must come together through engagement, which is a virtue for the nation, and which unites us in a common homeland. Civic service will therefore be extended to all young people, in all their diversity, to all young people who apply.
paragraph_summary: The President advocates for national unity and secular values while announcing initiatives to combat discrimination and expand civic service for young people.
reason: Focuses on domestic social cohesion, secularism, and national anti-racism efforts.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on proactive policy initiatives and the promotion of national values rather than framing discrimination or social issues as an urgent, existential threat requiring emergency response.

paragraph_text: My dear fellow citizens, The wishes I offer you tonight are unlike any that have come before, because we have just lived through a terrible year. Beginning with the appalling attacks against Charlie Hebdo and the Hypercacher, it was marked by assaults in Montrouge, Villejuif, Saint-Quentin Fallavier, and on the Thalys train, and ended in horror with the acts of war perpetrated in Saint-Denis and Paris. My first thoughts are with the victims of fanaticism, with their families plunged into grief, and with the wounded, suffering physical pain. Tonight, on your behalf, I express our compassion and our affection to them. These tragedies will remain etched in each of our memories. They will never be forgotten.
paragraph_summary: President Hollande reflects on the victims and national trauma caused by the series of terrorist attacks in France throughout 2015.
reason: Addresses domestic terrorist attacks and their impact on the French nation.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on the national trauma and the victims of terrorist attacks, framing the events as a severe threat to the nation's security and social fabric.

paragraph_text: By fading away, the year 2015 does not free us from the causes of the tragedies we have endured. It compels us to confront them, to address them with resolve. But what has happened has changed us, transformed us even. And we must harness this vitality, this energy that has surged from within us, this resurgence that has been applauded around the world – to successfully implement all reforms, to become stronger economically, fairer socially, and more exemplary in our democracy. It is in this way that France will emerge stronger, with this beautiful vision of us all succeeding together.
paragraph_summary: President Hollande calls for national unity and reform to strengthen France's economy, social fairness, and democracy following the tragedies of 2015.
reason: Focuses on domestic economic and social reforms to strengthen France.
crisis_reason: The paragraph frames the situation as a response to the 'tragedies of 2015' and identifies a need for reform to address threats to the economy, social fairness, and democracy, indicating an urgent, threat-focused posture.

paragraph_text: But despite the tragedy, France has not yielded. Despite the tears, it has stood firm. Faced with hatred, it has demonstrated the strength of its values – the values of the Republic. French people, I am proud of you. You have shown, in these circumstances, determination, solidarity, and composure. At this moment, I salute the bravery of our soldiers, our police officers, and our gendarmes, who take so many risks for our security. I extend my gratitude to the healthcare workers, the firefighters, the civil protection services, and to all the volunteers who provided assistance to our fellow citizens in distress.
paragraph_summary: The President praises the resilience, solidarity, and bravery of French citizens and public service personnel in response to national tragedies.
reason: Praises domestic security forces and emergency responders for their service to the nation.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on the resilience and solidarity shown in response to past tragedies, framing the situation as an achievement of national character rather than an active, ongoing threat.

paragraph_text: Protecting you also means taking action here, on our own soil. Following the attacks, at the suggestion of Manuel Valls, I declared a state of emergency. Then, before a Parliament convened in extraordinary session at Versailles, I made a conscious decision, a decision commensurate with what we had experienced. First, I decided to strengthen the numbers and resources of the police, the justice system, intelligence services, and the armed forces. Next, I launched a reform of criminal procedure to more effectively combat organized crime, its funding, and the trafficking that fuels it. Finally, I announced a revision of the Constitution to provide an unassailable basis for the use of the state of emergency when we face an imminent threat, and to strip French citizenship from individuals definitively convicted of terrorist crimes.
paragraph_summary: President Hollande outlines legislative and security measures taken to combat terrorism and organized crime following national attacks.
reason: Details domestic legislative and security measures taken within France to combat terrorism.
crisis_reason: The paragraph frames terrorism and organized crime as active, serious threats to national security that necessitate urgent legislative and security responses.

paragraph_text: It now falls to Parliament to take its responsibilities. The debate is legitimate. I respect it. It must take place. And when it comes to your protection, France must not be divided. It must make the right decisions, transcending partisan divisions and in accordance with our fundamental principles. I will ensure that this happens, because I am its guarantor. I will never accept that the French people be pitted against each other. Division is what extremists seek. I will not tolerate attacks – within our secular Republic – against any of our citizens for practicing their religion, nor will I tolerate the desecration of places of worship, as happened recently in Corsica with a prayer room. Such acts will never go unpunished, whether they target a mosque, a synagogue, a temple, or a church. It is a matter of France’s honor.
paragraph_summary: The President calls for national unity and religious tolerance while condemning attacks on places of worship and partisan division within the French Republic.
reason: Addresses internal political unity and the protection of religious freedom within the French Republic.
crisis_reason: The speaker frames partisan division and attacks on places of worship as active threats to the integrity of the republic and social cohesion, demanding a response of unity and tolerance.

paragraph_text: My dear fellow citizens, in 2016, we will fight against terrorism. Certainly, and intensely. But we will also fight against everything that fractures our society and that fuels both isolation and exclusion. If there is a state of emergency for security, there is also a state of emergency for the economy and for social issues. The fight against unemployment remains my top priority. This will be the focus of the measures prepared by the government to simplify the labor code, to establish a new system of social security, and to seize the economic opportunities offered by the digital revolution.
paragraph_summary: President Hollande outlines his 2016 priorities, focusing on counter-terrorism, social cohesion, and economic reforms to combat unemployment.
reason: Focuses on domestic economic policy, unemployment, and labor code reforms.
crisis_reason: The paragraph frames counter-terrorism and unemployment as urgent threats to national security and social stability that require specific, prioritized government action.

paragraph_text: Alongside this, a massive training program for job seekers will be launched: more than 500, 000 people will be accompanied towards the jobs of tomorrow. But everyone knows that it is in small and medium-sized enterprises that jobs are created. Therefore, we will introduce new incentives for hiring, starting at the beginning of the new year. Finally, apprenticeship programs will be significantly expanded. I set the goal that no apprentice should be without an employer, and that no employer should be without an apprentice. Every young person must be in training or employment, in whatever form.
paragraph_summary: The president outlines initiatives to boost employment through job training programs, hiring incentives for small businesses, and the expansion of apprenticeship opportunities for youth.
reason: Outlines domestic job training and apprenticeship programs for French citizens.
crisis_reason: The paragraph describes routine policy initiatives for economic development and employment, lacking any language framing these issues as an urgent threat or crisis.

paragraph_text: This effort in favor of the younger generation is a sacred duty. It calls for a mobilization of all: the state, of course, the regions that have just been established, and businesses. This is also what national unity means. France needs dynamism. It needs action. It also needs commitment. Civic service is a catalyst for interaction, integration, and inclusion. It has demonstrated its usefulness for young people and for our society. Therefore, I ask the government to gradually generalize it. The missions will be diverse: from providing support to the most vulnerable to preserving our planet.
paragraph_summary: The president calls for the generalization of civic service to foster national unity, youth integration, and societal dynamism.
reason: Discusses domestic civic service and national mobilization for youth integration.
crisis_reason: The paragraph frames the proposal as a positive initiative for national unity and youth integration rather than a response to an urgent threat or danger.

paragraph_text: The debates that will open will shed light on them, but in these circumstances, the role of political forces and leaders is immense. They must rise to the occasion, demonstrate clarity of thought, and avoid alienating society. They must also prevent fragmentation within our political forces, a fragmentation that would ultimately lead to their demise. But ultimately, it will be you who have the final say. That is why your responsibility is so great, and France is counting on you. For my part, until the very last day of my term, I will fully dedicate myself to serving our country; acting for France, fighting for justice and progress – that is a commitment that has defined my entire life. I will never abandon that commitment. I have shared with you trials and hardships, but also joys and happiness. I have felt immense pride in leading a people who stand tall, true to themselves and to their universal purpose. It is an unbreakable bond that unites us, and nothing will alter it. It is with this conviction that I offer you, from the bottom of my heart, my warmest wishes for this new year. Long live the Republic! Long live France!
paragraph_summary: The President calls for political responsibility and unity while reaffirming his commitment to serving France and expressing his dedication to the nation.
reason: Discusses domestic political responsibility and the President's commitment to the nation.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on political responsibility, unity, and a commitment to service, which are standard ceremonial and affirmative themes rather than descriptions of an urgent threat or danger.

paragraph_text: In the face of these attacks, you have stood firm. The terrorists sought to divide you, to separate you, to frighten you, but you have shown that you are stronger, united, supportive, and resolute. You have refused to succumb to prejudice, stigmatization, or pointless arguments. You have continued to live, to work, to go out, to move freely, and to cherish freedom. You have every right to be proud of yourselves.
paragraph_summary: The president commends the French public for maintaining national unity and resilience in the face of terrorist attacks.
reason: Commends the resilience and unity of the French people in the face of domestic challenges.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on the resilience and unity shown in response to past terrorist attacks rather than the threat itself, framing the situation as a past achievement of national character.

paragraph_text: Throughout my term, my priority has been to revitalize our economy and reduce unemployment. I stand by the choices I have made – the results are arriving, albeit later than I initially anticipated, I admit, but they are tangible. Public finances have been stabilized, the social security system is balanced, the competitiveness of our businesses has been restored, housing construction has reached a record level, investment is increasing, and, most importantly, the number of unemployed people is finally decreasing after a year. At the same time, and as I intended, social progress has not stopped. New rights have been established for employees, for lifelong learning, for the integration of young people, and for access to healthcare for all. There is still work to be done, but the foundation is solid.
paragraph_summary: The president reviews his economic record, highlighting improvements in employment, business competitiveness, and public finances alongside advancements in social rights and healthcare access.
reason: Reviews domestic economic performance, unemployment statistics, and social reforms.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on achievements, economic improvements, and positive developments in social and healthcare sectors, which are explicitly excluded from the definition of a crisis.

paragraph_text: These successes are yours. You must embrace them, not by ignoring the difficulties – they remain – or by overlooking the suffering – it is real – or by postponing necessary choices – there will be more to make. But you must recognize your strengths, your talents, your abilities, and your achievements. Our main adversary is doubt. You must have confidence in yourselves, especially in the face of the challenges that lie ahead.
paragraph_summary: The President encourages citizens to overcome self-doubt and recognize their personal and national achievements while acknowledging ongoing societal challenges.
reason: Encourages national confidence and recognition of domestic achievements.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on personal and national achievements and encourages overcoming self-doubt, which frames the societal challenges as background conditions rather than an urgent, active threat requiring immediate crisis response.

paragraph_text: The year 2017 is drawing to a close, and I do not wish to dwell excessively on the past. It was a year of choices: the choice of the French people, a choice by which you entrusted me with your confidence, and with that, your expectations, your demands, your aspirations. I am fully aware of that. Since my election last May, I have strived to simply deliver on the commitments I made during the presidential campaign. The Prime Minister and his government have been working tirelessly since last May, alongside a deeply renewed Parliament, to implement those commitments. Through your choices during 2017, you profoundly renewed our political landscape, and you enabled a deep transformation of our country: in education for our children, in the workplace for all our citizens, for the environment, and for the everyday lives of each and every one of you. These profound changes have begun, and they will continue with the same strength, the same pace, and the same intensity throughout 2018.
model_reasons: environment: refers to the general political or social landscape, not natural environment
paragraph_summary: President Macron reflects on his 2017 election mandate and the ongoing implementation of his government's political and social reform agenda.
reason: The paragraph focuses on domestic political transformation, education, and workplace reforms within France.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on the implementation of a political agenda and the reflection on a mandate, which are presented as routine governance and achievements rather than urgent threats or dangers.

paragraph_text: I will continue to do what you elected me to do: to make France stronger and fairer, to enable our country not to simply adapt to the changes in the world, but to be what it is: a strong nation, with a universal ideal, a nation that, because it is stronger, produces more, and can justly ensure solidarity within our country, and uphold a humanistic approach on the international stage. I know that some of you do not agree with the policies being pursued by the government today. I respect that, and I will always listen to you. I will ensure that all debates are conducted fairly, and that all voices, even those that dissent, are heard. Nevertheless, I will not stop acting. I will continue to listen, to explain our situation, and to present the realities. I will respect those perspectives, and ultimately, I will act in the best interests of our country, and in accordance with what I believe you expect from me.
paragraph_summary: The President reaffirms his commitment to national economic and social policy while pledging to respect dissenting voices and maintain open dialogue with the public.
reason: The text centers on domestic governance, national strength, and the president's relationship with French citizens.
crisis_reason: The paragraph describes routine governance, the maintenance of democratic dialogue, and a commitment to existing policy, which are presented as standard political practice rather than an urgent threat or emergency.

paragraph_text: Above all, for me, 2018 will be the year of national unity. We have been divided for too long, too often. Debate is necessary, disagreement is legitimate, but irreconcilable divisions undermine our country. I want greater harmony for France in 2018. To that end, I want to prioritize French ingenuity, because we have that within us. Our schools must be a crucible for this national unity, and we will continue to strengthen them. Lifelong learning is an essential safeguard that will enable everyone to face major changes, to understand them better, and to be trained for new professions. Science is an indispensable tool for preparing our future, and that is why our research is crucial. Our culture is a common foundation for our collective imagination, an imagination we need, an imagination of the future where everyone can find their place. I also want to prioritize work. Work is at the heart of our society, first because it allows everyone to find their place, to progress in life, and to overcome their background, if that is their wish. But it is also through work that our nation will be stronger, because it will produce and create wealth. We need work, and I will defend it relentlessly, by enabling each worker to earn more, by training those who are unemployed so they can find work and acquire the necessary skills, and by training our young people through apprenticeships. Work is the core of our shared project.
paragraph_summary: President Macron calls for national unity in 2018, emphasizing the importance of education, scientific research, cultural development, and employment to strengthen France.
reason: The paragraph discusses domestic priorities such as national unity, education, research, and labor policies.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on positive national development, education, and employment as pillars of strength rather than framing them as urgent threats or dangers requiring emergency response.

paragraph_text: Finally, our national unity also depends on your commitment. Yes, national unity is not simply the work of the President, the Prime Minister, or the government; it is the work of each and every one of you. Ask yourselves every morning what you can do for the country, and beyond your daily lives, your struggles, always remember that you belong to a larger, stronger collective: the French nation. This collective has educated you, cared for you, and when you fall, it helps you get back on your feet. It will help you in your old age. And always remember that you have something to contribute to the nation. I need that commitment. Since I was elected President of the Republic, I have witnessed these miracles of solidarity, engagement, and enthusiasm in our country. That is what I need, and that is why I need you. The French people are a great people who sometimes underestimate their own inner strengths. We are capable of extraordinary things. So, my fellow citizens, tonight, I am addressing you as a new year begins. There will be challenges, and there will undoubtedly be things we have not anticipated. You may have personal moments of doubt or hardship, but never forget that we are the French nation. And tonight, I want to tell you that we are moving forward with a spirit of ambition, with unwavering determination, with a sincere desire for our country and for each and every one of you, and with a desire to revive our French Renaissance. I wish you all the best for the year 2018. Long live the Republic, and long live France.
paragraph_summary: The President calls for individual commitment and national unity to foster a collective spirit of ambition and renewal for the French nation in 2018.
reason: The text is a domestic appeal for national unity and civic engagement among French citizens.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on national unity, ambition, and renewal as positive, aspirational goals rather than framing the state of the nation as an urgent threat or danger.

paragraph_text: But speaking the truth means facing reality. The desire for truth is also the desire that must lead us, in order to remain a robust democracy, to better protect ourselves from false information, manipulation, and disinformation. We can debate anything, but debating falsehoods can lead us astray, especially when driven by particular interests. In this age of social media, the cult of immediacy and images, and constant commentary, it is essential to rebuild a democratic trust in the truth of information, based on principles of transparency and ethics. Ultimately, this desire for truth is a desire for all of us: a desire for listening, dialogue, and humility. There is not one single truth, and I believe that each of us begins to stray into error when we assert things without dialogue, without confronting them with reality or with the arguments of others. So let us debate, because from that can emerge useful action that unites us.
paragraph_summary: The speaker emphasizes the importance of truth, transparency, and ethical dialogue to combat disinformation and strengthen democratic trust in the digital age.
reason: The paragraph discusses the domestic democratic challenge of disinformation and the need for internal trust.
crisis_reason: The speaker frames disinformation as a threat to democratic trust, requiring active intervention and vigilance to protect the integrity of the democratic order.

paragraph_text: My second wish for 2019 is a wish for dignity. I am deeply convinced that every citizen is essential to the project of the Nation. Many of our fellow citizens do not feel respected or valued. They feel that their lives are somehow restricted. I think of single mothers raising their children and struggling to make ends meet, of our farmers who simply want to live a dignified life from their work, or of our modest retirees who still help their children and care for their parents. We have begun to provide them with answers, and I know of their legitimate impatience, but we must go further. This means enabling everyone, regardless of their neighborhood or background, to access better education, and through that, to access employment to build their lives and the lives of their families. It also means ensuring that everyone has the rights they deserve in society, and that we expect them to fulfill their responsibilities. We have begun to restore this, but it is our collective responsibility, and it requires respect, a commitment to effort and work. It also means fighting against deep-seated interests that sometimes block our society and our state, interests that do not sufficiently recognize merit, or that confine too many of our fellow citizens into narrow categories. Our dignity as citizens demands that each of us feels fully engaged in the life of the Nation, in its major decisions, through their representatives or directly. We must, thanks to the debates that have begun, revitalize our democracy. I will be making decisions on this subject, because it is clear that our institutions must continue to evolve.
paragraph_summary: The President addresses the need for social dignity, economic opportunity, and democratic reform to ensure all citizens feel valued and engaged in the nation.
reason: It focuses on domestic social issues, including education, employment, and the revitalization of French democratic institutions.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on aspirational goals and general governance objectives like dignity and reform rather than framing these issues as urgent, existential threats or active dangers to the state.

paragraph_text: But dignity, my dear compatriots, is also about respecting everyone. And I must say, I have seen and heard some truly unacceptable things recently. We live free in our country only because generations before us fought to avoid both despotism and tyranny. And this freedom requires a republican order; it demands respect for everyone and for all opinions. Some claim to speak on behalf of the people – but whose people, from where, and how? In reality, they are often merely the voices of a hateful mob, attacking elected officials, law enforcement, journalists, Jews, foreigners, and homosexuals. This is simply a rejection of France! The people are sovereign. They express themselves through elections, where they choose representatives who make laws precisely because we are a state of law. The republican order will be ensured without complacency, because I expect everyone to show the respect that is essential for a functioning society. I want to acknowledge all those who, every day, enable our Republic to strive for the greatest dignity for everyone: our military, who tonight, and for many of them, are thousands of miles from their families; our firefighters, gendarmes, police officers, healthcare workers, elected officials of the Republic, volunteers in associations – all those who weave the fabric of the Nation, who work towards daily solidarity, and who, I know, tonight, are standing alongside the most vulnerable and the most fragile members of our society, whom I particularly think of.
paragraph_summary: The President calls for national respect and republican order while condemning hateful rhetoric and honoring those who serve the French Republic.
reason: The text addresses domestic social order, respect for institutions, and the role of various French public service sectors.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on upholding national values and honoring public service rather than framing a specific threat or danger that requires urgent intervention.

paragraph_text: I grew up in the provinces, and I know these regions that have been disrupted in recent decades and that sometimes doubt. And I know that our future will only be achieved through a renewed sense of unity and a commitment from everyone. It is through this that each citizen will rediscover a little more meaning and a little more control over their lives. And I know that our future depends on our ability to love each other and to love our country; from all backgrounds, from all generations – that is the energy of France. I am working, proud of our country, proud of all French women and men; determined to fight all the present and future battles, because I believe in us; I believe in this French and European hope that we can embody. So, my dear compatriots, I wish you a happy year 2019. Long live the Republic, and long live France!
paragraph_summary: The President calls for national unity and collective commitment to overcome regional challenges and foster a shared sense of hope for the future.
reason: The paragraph is a domestic call for national unity and pride in the French Republic.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on fostering hope and unity as a positive, forward-looking aspiration rather than framing regional challenges as an urgent, existential threat requiring immediate defensive action.

paragraph_text: At the same time, we have begun to see, in concrete terms, the first results of the transformation effort we launched two and a half years ago. More than 500, 000 jobs have been created since May 2017, often benefiting people who have been distanced from the world of work for a long time; we have seen a growing number of business creations; international investments in our economy are higher than those recorded by our neighbors; factories are reopening, allowing struggling regions to regain hope: France has not seen such momentum in years. We have achieved all of this together. These positive figures, which make France stronger every day, are ultimately yours – the result of your hard work, the risks taken by our entrepreneurs, artisans, shopkeepers, farmers, the creative genius of our inventors and artists, and the commitment of our workers, employees, and engineers. These figures are also an encouragement to continue the progress we have made.
paragraph_summary: The President highlights positive economic indicators, including job creation and increased investment, as evidence of the success of his administration's transformation efforts.
reason: The text highlights domestic economic performance, job creation, and business growth within France.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on positive economic indicators and the success of government policies, which frames the situation as an achievement rather than a threat.

paragraph_text: As I said, this is the third time I address you with these end-of-year wishes. Usually, this is the time of year when we tend to restrain our actions, lest we displease anyone, especially as we approach upcoming elections – municipal, senatorial, departmental, regional, and then presidential. We cannot afford to give in to that fatalism. Quite the opposite must happen. Tonight, I make a commitment before you: I will dedicate all my energy to transforming our country, to make it stronger, fairer, and more humane. I am aware that change can often be disruptive. But we cannot let anxieties lead to inaction. There is simply too much to do. I also recognize that decisions can sometimes cause friction, generate fears, and provoke opposition. Should we therefore abandon our efforts to change our country and our daily lives? No. To do so would be to abandon those whom the system has already left behind; it would be to betray our children, and their children after them, who would then have to pay the price for our reluctance. That is why, the pension reform that I committed to before you, and which is being championed by the government, will be completed.
paragraph_summary: The President commits to continuing his reform agenda, specifically the pension reform, despite potential political opposition and the upcoming election cycle.
reason: It discusses domestic political commitment and the implementation of national pension reforms.
crisis_reason: The paragraph describes a routine policy agenda and political maneuvering rather than framing the pension system as an urgent threat or a danger to the state.

paragraph_text: It is a project of justice and social progress, a project of justice and social progress because it ensures universality: it aims to ensure that every euro contributed opens up the same rights for everyone, from the very first hour of work. That is not the case today. This project of justice and social progress also translates into greater fairness: we will take into account demanding jobs, so that those who perform them can retire earlier, without that being linked to a particular status or company. We want everyone to be able to benefit from a decent pension, especially those who have been overlooked by the current system – women whose pensions are often almost twice as low as those of men, those with interrupted careers, shopkeepers, artisans, and farmers who feed us but who, after a lifetime of work, receive only a few hundred euros a month in pension, or, finally, the millions of French people who today are forced to work beyond the age of 64 in order to receive their full pension. Finally, this project of justice and social progress is based on a principle of responsibility: it aims to ensure the stability of the system, which is based on the principle of redistribution, as established by the National Resistance Council, and therefore to ensure its resilience over time. This is the foundation of intergenerational solidarity. It means ensuring that those who are working are able to pay for the fair pensions of our elders, in a world – and this is a privilege – where we are increasingly studying for longer and living longer.
paragraph_summary: The speaker outlines a pension reform project aimed at ensuring universality, fairness, and long-term financial stability for the French social security system.
reason: The paragraph details the specifics of a domestic pension reform project and its impact on French citizens.
crisis_reason: The paragraph describes a routine policy reform aimed at long-term stability and fairness rather than framing the pension system as an urgent, immediate threat or a state of emergency.

paragraph_text: So, do not be mistaken. On this subject, which is so central to French identity, I hear the fears and anxieties that are emerging. I also hear a great deal of falsehood and manipulation. Above all, we must prioritize appeasement over confrontation. Appeasement does not mean giving up, but rather respecting our disagreements. My sole compass, and it will always be so, is the best interests of our country, our ability to provide the best possible pensions for our elders, and the defense of those who do not always have the means to express themselves – that is, our children. Therefore, with the organizations representing workers and employers who wish to do so, I expect the government, under the leadership of Edouard Philippe, to find a way to reach a rapid compromise, while respecting the principles I have just outlined.
paragraph_summary: The President calls for compromise and dialogue between the government and social partners to reform the national pension system.
reason: It addresses domestic political tensions and the need for compromise regarding national pension policies.
crisis_reason: The paragraph describes a routine policy reform process and calls for dialogue, lacking any language framing the pension system as an urgent threat or a danger to the state.

paragraph_text: My dear fellow citizens, in a few hours, a new decade will begin.
