I see the expectations, the impatience, and I, too, am eager to see the situation improve more quickly for all of us, and especially for those who are most vulnerable. We are changing things, but sometimes we need to catch up on a great deal of ground. That does not happen overnight. As we stand on the threshold of this new decade, I want to assure you that I will not yield to pessimism or inertia. I am the guarantor of what makes our country, France: our institutions, our driving forces, our social security system, our culture, our secularism, equality between men and women, and our solidarity. Like you, I cherish what binds us together, what unites us, and what we are. We do not need to adapt to changing circumstances – that is not the French way! Instead, we must remain true to who we are, building a new society that responds, according to our choices, to the changes taking place.
paragraph_summary: The President addresses national progress, social values, and the commitment to maintaining French identity while navigating societal changes at the start of a new decade.
reason: The text reaffirms the president's commitment to French institutions, secularism, and national social security.
crisis_reason: the paragraph focuses on national progress, values, and identity in a reflective, ceremonial manner without framing these societal changes as an urgent threat or danger.

paragraph_text: More than ever, it will be essential to place work and merit at the heart of our actions. The government has already taken significant steps to ensure more French people are employed and that work is better rewarded. We must continue on this path. Tax cuts on income and the complete elimination of the housing tax for a large majority of you will become realities in 2020. I want us to continue to encourage initiative, simplify processes, enable greater innovation, and create an environment where people can work better, share the wealth created within businesses, and support our farmers and fishermen so they can live dignified lives from their work, as should all entrepreneurs and employees. This is the key to a strong and independent nation. If we want to effectively combat injustice and address the fact that, within our Republic, too many children see their future determined from the day they are born, we must continue to invest more in education and healthcare. Educating, instructing, training, and providing care are essential missions. We will continue to make schools, vocational training, and lifelong learning the foundation of our society. We will launch a program to modernize and transform the careers of teachers, professors, and healthcare workers. We will implement an ambitious policy for hospitals, a cause that is very close to my heart, and for a more patient-centered healthcare system. This year, we will also need to make crucial decisions for our citizens with disabilities and for our elderly citizens who require care.
model_reasons: environment: refers to the professional and economic working environment
paragraph_summary: The president outlines national priorities focused on employment, economic incentives, education reform, and improvements to the healthcare system.
reason: The focus is on domestic policy priorities including tax reform, education, and healthcare improvements.
crisis_reason: The paragraph describes routine governance and policy priorities rather than framing these areas as urgent threats or existential dangers.

paragraph_text: Finally, 2020 should mark the beginning of a decade of renewed national unity. I see too many divisions based on origins, religions, and interests. I will resolutely fight against the forces that undermine national unity, and in the coming weeks, I will make new decisions on this issue. The state and public services have a vital role to play in strengthening this French unity. I know that I can also count on all elected officials. Tonight, I would like to pay tribute to the mayors of France. In a few weeks, many of them will be stepping down after one or more terms, while others will be seeking your trust. Whether they are leaving or seeking re-election, I want to thank them all. They are the pillars of the Republic, the cornerstones of our communities and regions. We need them so much. And I know that I can also count on you, my dear fellow citizens. Yes, to ensure the unity of the nation, let us always remember that we owe more to France than she owes to us. Our commitment, our sense of duty, and our respect for other citizens are essential values for our unity and harmony. These values are at the heart of the universal national service program, which will be implemented over the coming year and to which I attach the utmost importance.
paragraph_summary: The President calls for renewed national unity to overcome social divisions and emphasizes the vital roles of mayors and citizens in strengthening the Republic.
reason: The paragraph emphasizes national unity, the role of local mayors, and domestic civic programs.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on the need for unity to overcome social divisions, which frames the fragmentation of society as an urgent threat to the Republic's stability.

paragraph_text: Fellow French women and men, my dear compatriots from mainland France, overseas territories, and abroad, tonight, we are not experiencing a New Year’s Eve like any other. In our towns and villages, the usual festive gatherings are, this year, forbidden by the epidemic; our squares are dark, our homes less joyful than usual. The year 2020 is drawing to a close as it has unfolded: through effort and restrictions. And I am acutely aware, given the decisions I have had to make, of the sacrifices I have asked of you. I am sincerely convinced that we have made the right choices at the right moments, and I want to thank you for your civic responsibility. For this spirit of collective responsibility, through which we have saved so many lives, and which allows us today to weather the epidemic better than many others. On behalf of you all, my thoughts tonight are with the 64, 000 victims of this virus, their families, and loved ones. Parents, friends, so many cherished figures of French life have left us in recent months. Yes, this year, 2020, has been difficult. It has reminded us of our vulnerabilities. It has been even more unfair to the most vulnerable among us. But together, we are emerging from it stronger, and having learned so much.
paragraph_summary: President Macron reflects on the hardships, sacrifices, and collective responsibility of the French people during the 2020 COVID-19 pandemic.
reason: The president addresses the domestic impact of the pandemic on French citizens and society.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on reflecting on past hardships and collective responsibility during the pandemic, which frames the situation as a period of recovery and historical acknowledgment rather than an active, ongoing threat.

paragraph_text: By fighting the virus, by tackling its economic and social consequences, and by building a stronger, more equitable, and sustainable society, we are building the France of 2030. That is our goal. So, together, in unity, let us look forward, let us look to our future, let us prepare for this spring of 2021, which will mark the beginning of a new dawn for France, a renaissance for Europe. Let us remain a united, supportive, proud people, proud of our history, our values, our culture, confident in the future and progress, and ambitious for ourselves. Whatever may happen. Let us be proud. Proud to be us, the French, France. That is what I say to you, my fellow citizens. Happy New Year to all. May 2021 be a happy year for each and every one of you, and a productive year for our country. Long live the Republic. Long live France.
paragraph_summary: The President outlines a vision for France's post-pandemic recovery and future national development while offering New Year wishes to the French people.
reason: The paragraph focuses on national unity, future domestic goals, and patriotic sentiments for France.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on post-pandemic recovery and future development, which frames the situation as a transition toward growth and normalcy rather than an active, ongoing threat.

paragraph_text: I also want to remember all our compatriots living in precarious, sometimes impoverished, circumstances, for whom the crisis we are experiencing makes daily life even more difficult. I want to express our gratitude to those who have mobilized to provide care, nourishment, education, and protection; to all those whose work and commitment have allowed us to stand strong and united during these difficult months, and who continue to do so tonight, for the nation. To our fellow citizens working in the cultural, sports, hospitality, tourism, and event sectors; to the students who have suffered, and continue to suffer, more than most, I want to say that we are here for you. We will, of course, strive to overcome this situation as quickly as possible, and to support them through this exceptionally difficult and unfair period, during which we are asking them to adapt their work, and sometimes even to give it up.
paragraph_summary: The President expresses support for vulnerable citizens and gratitude to those providing essential services during the ongoing economic and social crisis.
reason: The text discusses domestic support for vulnerable French citizens and specific national economic sectors.
crisis_reason: The paragraph explicitly identifies the situation as an 'ongoing economic and social crisis' and frames the support for vulnerable citizens as a necessary response to this threat.

paragraph_text: This historic ordeal has also revealed the resilience of our nation. Despite the pandemic, we have never abandoned our ambition to progress, by cutting taxes, opening up new rights such as caregiver leave and extending paternity leave, by supporting the conversion of our car fleet through incentives, by opening France Services centers throughout the country, by investing historically in our sovereignty and research, and by working to produce more in France and in Europe. Our healthcare workers have not only persevered, but we have also launched a profound transformation and historic investments in our healthcare system, the relevance of which will become clear in the years to come. Our country is one of the most proactive in protecting and supporting young people, workers, and entrepreneurs. I stand by the commitment, "whatever it costs, " because it has preserved lives and protected jobs. We must work together to build solutions that will not burden future generations.
paragraph_summary: The president highlights national resilience and government initiatives in economic reform, healthcare investment, and social support programs implemented during the pandemic.
reason: The paragraph highlights domestic policy achievements such as tax cuts, healthcare investments, and national labor support.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on government initiatives, economic reform, and social support programs as achievements and recovery efforts following the pandemic, rather than framing the pandemic itself as an active, ongoing threat.

paragraph_text: Yes, this year, 2020, is not solely defined by the epidemic, even though it has changed our lives. It has also, in my view, revealed exemplary stories that serve as guiding lights for the years to come. Indeed, our greatest source of pride are the French women and men – you. Marie-Corentine is a 24-year-old. Last spring, this newly qualified nurse from Limoges, without hesitation, left everything to support the teams at Créteil hospital, overwhelmed by the influx of patients with the virus. Jean-Luc is a garbage collector in French Guiana. Together with his colleagues, Anthony and Maxime, they have been working without interruption since the beginning of the pandemic. During the strictest lockdown, they formed this "second line" that allowed the country to continue functioning, and life to continue. Gérald is an entrepreneur near Angers. To meet the demand for masks, he and his employees managed to open their factories seven days a week, 24 hours a day, and more recently, to set up another one in record time.
paragraph_summary: The President highlights the dedication of French citizens who served as essential workers and volunteers during the 2020 COVID-19 pandemic.
reason: The paragraph highlights individual French citizens as examples of national resilience during the pandemic.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on expressing gratitude for past service during the pandemic rather than framing the situation as an ongoing or active threat requiring urgent response.

paragraph_text: Lucas is 11 years old and lives in Béthune. When he learned about the isolation of elderly people affected by COVID-19 in his town's hospital, he didn't hesitate and donated his tablet to allow them to communicate with their loved ones and regain a smile. He was followed by many others. Rosalie is a bookseller in Bagnolet, in the Seine-Saint-Denis department. Like all her colleagues, she greatly suffered from the closure of businesses, despite the assistance provided. She managed to innovate, setting up a website to allow for pick-up sales during the most difficult weeks. And at Christmas, customers responded enthusiastically.
paragraph_summary: The president highlights individual acts of solidarity and business innovation by citizens during the COVID-19 pandemic.
reason: The text provides examples of local French citizens and businesses adapting to the pandemic.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on individual acts of solidarity and innovation as a retrospective reflection on the pandemic, framing the content as a celebration of resilience rather than an active, ongoing threat.

paragraph_text: Medhi is a professor of economics and social sciences in the northern districts of Marseille. Like thousands of teachers, he had, just days after the assassination of Samuel Paty, to organize a lesson on secularism. To that end, he took the time to train with other teachers and with school inspectors. With courage, he then returned to his students to discuss our values, their history. Embracing this vital mission of our schools, of our teachers: to shape citizens of the Republic. Wendie is a soccer player. Like all amateur and professional athletes, she experienced a difficult season, with impossible training sessions, postponed competitions, and matches without an audience. Yet, she found the strength within herself to help her club, which she’s always supported, win a 7th Champions League title – a record – and to inspire hope during this difficult period.
paragraph_summary: The president highlights the resilience and dedication of teachers and athletes in upholding republican values and inspiring hope during challenging times.
reason: The text focuses on domestic education, secularism, and national sports achievements.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on resilience, inspiration, and the upholding of values, which frames the situation as an achievement or a positive reflection rather than an active threat requiring urgent response.

paragraph_text: Mauricette is 78 years old. She lives in a retirement home in Sevran, and just a few days ago, as I did, you all saw her become the first French person to receive a COVID-19 vaccine, sending a powerful message of hope against obscurantism and conspiracy theories, for the year ahead. All these names, these faces, represent your sister, your neighbor, your friends, those thousands of ordinary people who, engaged and united, held our country together during this ordeal. All these names, these faces, represent hope. They represent France.
paragraph_summary: The President highlights the first COVID-19 vaccination in France as a symbol of national unity and hope during the pandemic.
reason: The paragraph emphasizes national unity and the domestic vaccination campaign as a symbol of French hope.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on the vaccination as a symbol of hope and unity, framing the event as a positive development and a milestone in the recovery process rather than an active, ongoing threat.

paragraph_text: Fellow French women and men, My dear compatriots, from mainland France, from overseas, and from abroad, Tonight, we are not experiencing a New Year’s Eve like any other. In our towns and villages, it is usually a time for large gatherings, but this year, those are forbidden by the epidemic. Our town squares are dark, our homes less joyful than usual. The year 2020 is drawing to a close as it has unfolded: through effort and restrictions. And I am fully aware, given the decisions I have had to make, of the sacrifices I have asked of you. I am sincerely convinced that we have made the right choices at the right moments, and I want to thank you for your civic responsibility. For this spirit of collective responsibility that has allowed us to save so many lives and that allows us today to weather the epidemic better than many others. On your behalf, I am thinking tonight of the 64, 000 victims of this virus, their families, and their loved ones. Parents, friends, so many figures that shaped the French identity have left us in recent months. Yes, this year 2020 has been difficult. It has reminded us of our vulnerabilities. It has been even more unfair to those who are most fragile. But together, we are emerging from it stronger, and having learned so much.
paragraph_summary: The President addresses the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic on France, acknowledging the sacrifices made by citizens and mourning the lives lost during 2020.
reason: The president addresses the domestic impact of the pandemic on French citizens and society.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on mourning the lives lost and acknowledging past sacrifices, which frames the situation as a reflection on a concluded period of hardship rather than an active, ongoing threat requiring urgent response.

paragraph_text: I also want to remember all our compatriots who are living in precarious circumstances, sometimes poverty, for whom the crisis we are experiencing is making daily life even more difficult. I want to express our gratitude to those who have mobilized to provide care, nourishment, education, and protection; to all those whose work and commitment have allowed us to stand strong and united during these difficult months, and who are doing so again tonight, for the nation. To our fellow citizens working in the cultural, sports, hospitality, tourism, and event sectors, and to the students who have suffered and are still suffering more than others, I want to tell them that we are here for them. We will, of course, strive to overcome this situation as quickly as possible, and to support them during this exceptionally difficult and unfair period, when we are asking them to work differently, and sometimes even to give up their activities.
paragraph_summary: The President expresses support for citizens facing poverty and those in sectors severely impacted by the ongoing crisis, while thanking essential workers for their service.
reason: The text discusses domestic support for vulnerable French citizens and specific national economic sectors.
crisis_reason: The paragraph explicitly identifies an 'ongoing crisis' and frames the situation as a threat to the livelihoods of citizens, requiring the president's attention and support.

paragraph_text: This historic ordeal has also revealed the resilience of our nation. Despite the pandemic, we have never abandoned our ambition to progress for everyone, by cutting taxes, opening up new rights such as caregiver leave and extending paternity leave, by supporting the conversion of our car fleet through incentives, by opening France Services centers throughout the country, by investing historically in our sovereignty and research, and by working to produce more in France and in Europe. Our healthcare workers have not only held the line, but we have also launched a profound transformation and historic investments in our healthcare system, the relevance of which will be revealed in the years to come. Our country is one of the most proactive in protecting and supporting young people, workers, and entrepreneurs. I stand by the commitment, "whatever it costs, " because it has allowed us to preserve lives and protect jobs. We will need to build together the solutions that will prevent it from becoming a burden for future generations.
paragraph_summary: The president highlights national resilience and government initiatives in healthcare, economic support, and industrial investment during the COVID-19 pandemic.
reason: The paragraph highlights domestic policy achievements such as tax cuts, healthcare investments, and national labor support.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on government initiatives, resilience, and support measures, which frames the situation as a recovery and management effort rather than an active, ongoing threat.

paragraph_summary: The speaker highlights the resilience and contributions of various French citizens who supported the country during the COVID-19 pandemic.

crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on gratitude and the resilience of citizens in the aftermath of the pandemic, which frames the situation as a recovery and an achievement rather than an active, ongoing threat.

paragraph_text: By fighting the virus, by tackling its economic and social consequences, and by rebuilding a stronger, more united, and more sustainable society, we are building the France of 2030. That is our goal. So, together, in unity, let us look forward, let us look to our future, let us prepare for the spring of 2021, which will mark the beginning of a new dawn for France, a renaissance for Europe. Let us remain a united, supportive, proud people, proud of our history, our values, our culture, confident in the future and progress, and ambitious for ourselves. Whatever may come. Let us be proud. Proud to be us, the French, France. That is what I say to you, my fellow citizens. Happy New Year to all. May 2021 be a happy year for each and every one of you, and a productive year for our country. Long live the Republic. Long live France.
paragraph_summary: The President calls for national unity and resilience to overcome the pandemic and build a stronger, more prosperous France by 2030.
reason: The paragraph focuses on national unity, future domestic goals, and patriotic sentiments for France.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on the future goal of building a stronger and more prosperous nation, framing the pandemic as a past event to be overcome through resilience rather than an active, ongoing threat.

paragraph_text: Fellow French men and women, My dear compatriots, from mainland France, overseas territories, and abroad, Once again, this end of the year is marked by the epidemic and the strengthened restrictions that weigh on our daily lives. So, at this moment, my thoughts are first and foremost with our 123, 000 fellow citizens who have lost their lives to the virus. My thoughts are with all those who are grieving, suffering, or feeling lonely. I also remember those among you who are dealing with long COVID, as well as those experiencing the psychological consequences of the health crisis. Tonight, once again, on your behalf, I want to express our gratitude to our healthcare workers, our armed forces, our police force, our firefighters, our home care assistants, and so many other professions, all working on December 31st, as they do every day, to protect us and take care of us. The weeks ahead will be difficult, we all know that. The virus is circulating, and will continue to circulate more and more. The government has taken measures to address this, and I ask you all to adhere to them. Sectors such as culture, sports, hospitality, tourism, and event planning will once again feel the economic consequences of this situation. We will support them as we have done since the beginning of this pandemic. There will also be disruptions to many of our activities due to this new, highly contagious variant. In this context, we will ensure the continuity of public services and the life of the nation.
paragraph_summary: The President addresses the ongoing COVID-19 pandemic, expressing condolences for the deceased, gratitude to essential workers, and commitment to supporting affected economic sectors.
reason: The president addresses the domestic health situation, national mourning, and support for French public services.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on expressing condolences, gratitude, and economic support, which frames the situation as a period of recovery and reflection rather than an active, ongoing threat requiring urgent intervention.

paragraph_text: However, compared to the same time last year, when restrictions were much stricter, we have the advantage of the vaccine, and the lessons we have learned from our collective experience. And therefore, we have real reasons for hope. Today, we have more than 53 million fully vaccinated people, placing our country among the world's leaders. Twenty-four million have received a booster dose, and our goal is to ensure that everyone is vaccinated and receives their booster. In this way, we can overcome this wave while limiting restrictions as much as possible. Continuing as we have done since the beginning, we will do everything to preserve activity and what we hold most precious: our schools and the education of our children. So, tonight, I want to reiterate, with great conviction: vaccination is our most effective tool. It significantly reduces transmission and reduces the number of serious cases by a factor of ten. That is why, once again, I am calling on the five million unvaccinated people. Take this simple step. For yourselves, for your fellow citizens, and for our country. France is counting on you.
paragraph_summary: The President emphasizes the importance of vaccination and booster doses to manage the COVID-19 pandemic while maintaining national activity and keeping schools open.
reason: The text focuses on the domestic vaccination campaign and national health policy.
crisis_reason: The paragraph frames the pandemic as an ongoing threat that requires active management through vaccination and boosters to prevent disruption to national life and education.

paragraph_text: Is vaccination alone sufficient? No. That is why respecting preventative measures against the virus remains essential, particularly wearing a mask. Together, we will navigate this new challenge, following the same principles we have followed from the very beginning. First, we must protect ourselves. Protect the most vulnerable, our hospitals, and our healthcare workers, who are under immense pressure, even as they are treating other illnesses. We must also protect our economy and our jobs, as we have done with the "whatever it costs" initiative. Second, based on facts and science, we must take proportionate measures. This is exactly what the Prime Minister and the ministers have done in recent days. We must do everything to avoid measures that restrict our freedoms and ensure that we uphold all our democratic principles. Finally, we must rely on the responsibility of each individual, primarily by getting vaccinated, for themselves and for others. To be a free citizen and always a responsible citizen, for oneself and for others; duties come before rights.
paragraph_summary: The President outlines public health strategies, including vaccination and preventative measures, to manage the COVID-19 pandemic while protecting the economy and individual freedoms.
reason: The paragraph discusses domestic health measures, economic protection, and the responsibilities of French citizens.
crisis_reason: The paragraph frames the pandemic as an ongoing threat that requires active management and strategic intervention to protect public health and the economy, indicating a crisis posture.

paragraph_text: My dear fellow citizens, For nearly five years, we have worked tirelessly to ensure that France is listened to and respected in Europe and on the world stage. And it is. We have also worked to unite our country. This has been a difficult path, as it is so easy to divide people by generation, social category, origin, or region. But it is a necessary path, because when united, the French people can overcome anything. Wishing you all a year filled with happiness and personal accomplishments, I offer this wish for us all: let us continue to respect our differences, to have confidence in who we are, and to look with courage, boldness, and clarity towards our future, so that we can act. Let us decide, for ourselves, to be both rooted in our language, our culture, and our secular values, and to embrace freedom, universality, and creativity. Let us remain united, compassionate, and supportive. Let us stand on the side of life. That is what we owe to ourselves. Therefore, 2022 will be a year of endless possibilities. Long live Europe. Long live the Republic. Long live France.
paragraph_summary: The President reflects on France's international standing and national unity while encouraging citizens to embrace shared values and optimism for the upcoming year.
reason: The focus is on national unity, French identity, and domestic social cohesion.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on national unity, shared values, and optimism for the future, which are affirmative and ceremonial themes rather than descriptions of an urgent threat.

paragraph_text: My dear fellow citizens, For the sixth time, I have the privilege of sharing this moment of New Year’s greetings with you. With you, officers and soldiers in our armed forces, who protect us; with you, police officers, gendarmes, firefighters, security forces, coast guard, who watch over us this evening; with you, doctors, caregivers, patients; with you, civil servants who ensure the continuity of our services; with you, engaged citizens, whether professionally or in our associations; with you, our fellow citizens from overseas; with all of you who are with your families, here in France or abroad, and particularly with those of you who may feel alone tonight. Those of you who are facing difficult times, and to whom our thoughts are directed. New Year’s greetings have a unique quality: they require us to speak of a future that we, in truth, do not yet know, a future that we nonetheless know we must face, with our strengths and our weaknesses, but as a united nation. In the responsibilities that are mine, I never lose sight of this imperative of national unity, which we all build together. If we were to yield to the spirit of division that presses on us from every side, we would have little chance of succeeding in this harsh world, in these difficult times. So, I wish us all a 2023, as much as possible, marked by unity and solidarity, recognizing the place of each and every one of us and respecting all.
paragraph_summary: The President addresses the nation, emphasizing the importance of national unity and solidarity among all citizens to overcome the challenges of the coming year.
reason: The speaker addresses domestic citizens and public servants regarding national unity.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on the need for unity and solidarity to overcome future challenges, but it lacks specific language identifying a concrete threat or urgent danger, making it a standard call for national cohesion rather than a crisis framing.

paragraph_text: It is through our work and commitment that we will also restore a nation built on trust. By ensuring that our secular principles are effective every day, as they are a cornerstone of freedom. This does not diminish the place of religion in the hearts of those who believe, and I would like to pay tribute to the memory of His Holiness Pope Benedict XVI, whose passing deeply affects so many Catholics in France and around the world. This nation of trust will also be strengthened by a constant fight against all forms of discrimination, in a society based on respect and recognition. It will also be reinforced by our ongoing commitment to those living with disabilities.
paragraph_summary: The President discusses building a nation of trust through secularism, respect, anti-discrimination efforts, support for the disabled, and tribute to Pope Benedict XVI.
reason: The paragraph focuses on domestic secular principles, discrimination, and support for citizens with disabilities.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on national values, social cohesion, and ceremonial tributes rather than framing these topics as urgent threats or active dangers.

paragraph_text: It is through our work and commitment that we must rebuild our essential public services, so that they fully embody our ideal of equality. Through the National Council for Reconstruction, we have begun to renew the social contract between generations and to better support our elderly citizens in situations of dependency. We have also begun to restore confidence in our education system and healthcare system, thanks to the energy and dedication of our teachers and healthcare professionals. We will pursue this with vigor during the coming year, through clear, strong choices and close collaboration with those on the ground.
paragraph_summary: The president outlines plans to strengthen public services, including education, healthcare, and support for the elderly, through national reconstruction efforts.
reason: The text discusses domestic public services, education, and healthcare reform.
crisis_reason: The paragraph describes routine governance and national development plans rather than framing the state of public services as an urgent threat or an active danger.

paragraph_text: It is through our work and commitment that we will build a fairer society. A fairer society is first and foremost a society where women and men have equal opportunities, and we will continue to prioritize this key objective of my two terms in office, driving government action every day. But a fairer society is also one that is socially just. Not through higher taxes, nor by passing on more debt to future generations. Instead, we will improve support for our children and adolescents, reform our vocational high schools, and improve guidance for young people, helping them find the right training and careers. We must also accelerate and strengthen the industrial revitalization of our country, to create new jobs and career opportunities. The greatest injustice in our country remains the influence of family background and the limited social mobility. The solution lies in education, guidance, higher education, our innovation policy, and our industrialization efforts.
paragraph_summary: The President outlines plans to promote social mobility and fairness through education reform, vocational training, and industrial revitalization rather than tax or debt increases.
reason: The focus is on domestic social policy, education, and industrial revitalization.
crisis_reason: The paragraph describes routine policy initiatives for social mobility and economic development rather than framing these issues as urgent threats or dangers to the state.

paragraph_text: For all of this, I offer all of us wishes for unity, wishes for boldness, wishes for collective ambition, and wishes for kindness. We have overcome so many crises together, my dear fellow citizens. I know we are capable of meeting the challenges before us, and of being the generation with the responsibility to rebuild France and Europe, to pass them on to our children, stronger, more beautiful, and more just. Together, we will succeed. And with this confidence, I offer you all my best wishes for the New Year. Long live the Republic. Long live France.
paragraph_summary: The President expresses confidence in national resilience and calls for collective unity to rebuild France and Europe in the coming year.
reason: The speaker offers New Year wishes for national unity and the future of France.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on resilience, unity, and the process of rebuilding, which frames the situation as a recovery effort rather than an active, ongoing threat.

paragraph_text: And despite all these crises, all these challenges, 2022 was also, for all of us, a year of intense democracy, in which you renewed our National Assembly, and in which, during the presidential election this spring, you chose to entrust me with a new five-year mandate to lead our nation, a great honor and a great responsibility. 2022 was also, once again, a year of artistic, cultural, and sporting radiance for our country, with two Nobel Prizes, and so many great moments of creation and sport. Let us be proud of all of this, of everything we have accomplished together during the year that is ending.
paragraph_summary: The President reflects on the 2022 democratic elections, his renewed mandate, and France's cultural and sporting achievements throughout the year.
reason: The paragraph reflects on domestic political events and cultural achievements from the past year.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on democratic processes, a renewed mandate, and cultural and sporting achievements, which are presented as positive developments and reflections rather than urgent threats.

paragraph_text: Will we have to work longer in 2023? Here too, as I promised you, this year will indeed be the year of a pension reform that aims to ensure the balance of our system for the years and decades to come. We need to work harder; that is the very essence of the unemployment insurance reform, championed by the government and passed by parliament. It is also the focus of the ongoing work between social partners and the government, who will be finalizing the new rules that will come into effect at the end of summer 2023. The goal is to strengthen our pay-as-you-go pension system, which, without this, would be threatened, as we continue to finance it through borrowing. To achieve this, we will gradually extend working careers, in stages over a period of nearly ten years. This will be fair, taking into account long careers, interrupted careers, and the challenges of certain jobs and professions. This is what will allow us to balance the funding of our pension system in our country, and it is an opportunity to improve the minimum pension for those who have worked to earn their qualifying years, and to pass on a just and solid social model to our children, a model that will be credible and sustainable.
paragraph_summary: The president outlines plans for pension and unemployment insurance reforms to ensure the long-term financial sustainability of the French social security system.
reason: The text outlines domestic pension and unemployment insurance reforms.
crisis_reason: The paragraph describes routine policy adjustments for long-term sustainability rather than framing the situation as an urgent threat or an immediate danger to the state.

paragraph_text: Throughout our nation’s long history, there have been generations who resisted, others who rebuilt, and still others who expanded prosperity. For us, we must face this new chapter of a difficult era, and beyond the immediate challenges of this year, we have a responsibility to rebuild many of the pillars of our nation – our education system, our healthcare system, our transportation network, our regional development, our industries, and so on. To achieve this, we must remain united and maintain a collective ambition, an ambition to continue transforming our country, overcoming entrenched interests and resisting the temptation to continue as before, or to succumb to a sense of defeat. This is how we will be useful to our young people and future generations. For the past five years, we have made significant progress in transforming and simplifying, and fighting against inertia. But sometimes, it’s not enough, and often, it’s too slow. And like you, I am impatient, but I will never give in to complacency or fatalism. So, for 2023, through our work and commitment, let us strive to rebuild a stronger, fairer France, one that we can pass on to our children.
paragraph_summary: The President calls for national unity and collective effort to rebuild key public sectors and transform France for the benefit of future generations.
reason: The focus is on rebuilding domestic infrastructure and national institutions.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on rebuilding and transformation for future generations, which frames the current state as a project of improvement and progress rather than an urgent threat or active crisis.

paragraph_text: To all French people, from mainland France, our overseas territories, and from abroad, As I extend my best wishes to you, I want to begin by expressing our gratitude to everyone for the dedication of our fellow citizens who, even tonight, are working to protect, care for, assist, and ensure the continuity of our nation's life. Thanks to them, we are able to spend these moments with our families and loved ones. And on this occasion, I also want to express my affection to those who are ill or who may be alone.
paragraph_summary: The President expresses gratitude to essential workers and offers support to those who are ill or isolated during the holiday season.
reason: The speaker expresses gratitude to domestic public servants and citizens.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on gratitude and support during the holiday season rather than framing the illness or isolation as an active, urgent threat requiring a response.

paragraph_text: 2024 will also be a year of French pride. We celebrate the thousands of dedicated workers, artisans, and entrepreneurs who participated in the magnificent project to rebuild Notre-Dame de Paris. Its spire now soars once again towards the sky, crowning a cathedral that will reopen on December 8th. We are a nation of builders, capable of resisting and recovering when we unite. Yes, as promised, within five years, we have rebuilt the cathedral and accomplished the impossible. We are a nation that, when proud of itself, always carries a universal hope.
paragraph_summary: The President highlights the successful reconstruction of Notre-Dame Cathedral as a symbol of French national pride, resilience, and collective achievement.
reason: The text celebrates the domestic achievement of rebuilding Notre-Dame de Paris.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on the successful reconstruction and national pride, framing the event as an achievement and a symbol of resilience rather than an active or ongoing threat.

paragraph_text: In 2024, we will also celebrate our history. Eighty years after 1944, we will be proud of our past and our heroes, whose destinies are intertwined. Soldiers of the Allied forces, but also French citizens by birth or by choice, French and foreign members of the Resistance. All fighting to liberate a France faithful to the spirit of the Enlightenment and to the spirit of our Revolution. 2024 will be a year of pride in the French language. After restoring the Château de Villers-Cotterêts and creating the International City of the French Language there, we will welcome the Francophonie community there in a few months. 2024 will also be a year of French sporting pride, as the Olympic and Paralympic Games will be held here, in France. And so, as at home, in mainland France and in our overseas territories. We will be proud of our athletes, our artists, our landscapes, and this popular celebration, made possible by thousands of volunteers, who are also building a lasting sporting legacy for our nation. Through our collective commitment, beginning tomorrow.
paragraph_summary: The President highlights upcoming 2024 events, including the 80th anniversary of the Liberation, the promotion of the French language, and the Paris Olympic Games.
reason: The focus is on French history, language, and hosting the Olympic Games in France.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on celebratory national events, anniversaries, and cultural promotion, which are presented as positive milestones rather than threats requiring urgent action or vigilance.

paragraph_text: Yes, 2024, as you have understood, is a vintage year for France. It is once a decade that we commemorate our liberation with such grandeur. It is once a century that we host the Olympic and Paralympic Games. And it is once a millennium that we rebuild a cathedral. It is once a generation that the destiny of the next is determined, as it undoubtedly is now. 2024, a year of determination, of choices, of regeneration, of pride. Ultimately, a year of hope. Indeed, so much of our future will be determined this year. Let us work together. Let us choose rather than be subjected, let us forge our own path rather than follow one. And our entire history teaches that the will of a few can overcome any fate. Let us act, yes, let us act together, let us continue to overcome divisions and special interests, because within our people lie the deep-rooted forces to meet these challenges. My fellow citizens, I wish you a very happy and a very prosperous New Year 2024, so that France can unite, act, and shine. May it be worthy of "that indefinable splendor that belongs to those destined for great endeavors, " because that is who we are. Long live the Republic. Long live France.
paragraph_summary: President Macron outlines his vision for 2024 as a pivotal year of national pride, unity, and collective action for France.
reason: The speaker concludes with a call for national unity and domestic action.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on national pride, unity, and collective action as a positive vision for the future, lacking any language describing a threat or urgent danger.

paragraph_text: Nevertheless, I am convinced, and this is not false optimism, that in times of crisis, the best can emerge. That is why I have consistently followed the same approach: to act with determination and constancy, for today and for tomorrow. For seven years, in the position you have placed me, I have strived to make France stronger and fairer, to free, protect, and unite. That is our goal, and we are staying true to it. And that was the case in 2023. In 2023, France was undoubtedly one of the Western countries that, despite the circumstances, took the most decisions and implemented the most significant transformations. Some, such as the pension reform, were, I know, unpopular. I take responsibility for them, as I had committed to them, and they were necessary for the country. Therefore, these months have been far from the sense of paralysis that was predicted for us, and that is a good thing. And I want to particularly thank the Prime Minister and her government for their efforts.
paragraph_summary: The President defends his administration's record in 2023, specifically highlighting the necessity of unpopular reforms like the pension system to strengthen France.
reason: The paragraph discusses domestic political achievements, pension reform, and government performance in France.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on defending the administration's record and framing the pension reform as a necessary achievement for national strength, rather than presenting the situation as an urgent, ongoing threat.

paragraph_text: In 2023, we also passed historic laws for the years to come, to protect ourselves. A historic law for our military: within a decade, the budget for our armed forces will have doubled. A historic law for our police officers, gendarmes, judges, and court clerks, to ensure the order and unity of the Republic, by creating thousands of new positions to better protect you. We will continue to restore authority wherever it is lacking, to address petty offenses and crime. Regarding immigration, the law passed in December, as well as the agreement reached at the European level, provide us with the tools necessary to better uphold the principles of the Republic, that is, to combat human traffickers and illegal immigration, while also better integrating those who are expected to remain in our country: refugees, students, researchers, and workers.
paragraph_summary: The president outlines legislative achievements in 2023 concerning military funding, law enforcement expansion, judicial authority, and new immigration and integration policies.
reason: It focuses on national security, police, and domestic immigration policy within France.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on legislative achievements and routine policy implementation rather than framing these topics as urgent threats or dangers.

paragraph_text: I know that there is impatience, and I dare to say that I share it, even if the first changes are already visible. I know that you want us to do more and faster. And that is why 2024 must above all be a year of determination. Let us act, and continue to act, in the best interests of the nation. We will be determined to act for education, childhood, and education, to restore the level of our students, the authority of our teachers, and the strength of our secular and republican education system. Following economic strengthening, we must now strengthen the state and our public services. We will also launch a broader effort to strengthen our civic values. France is a culture, a history, a language, and universal values that are learned from a young age, in every generation.
paragraph_summary: The President outlines a 2024 agenda focused on strengthening education, public services, and civic values to address public impatience for faster national progress.
reason: The text outlines domestic priorities for education, public services, and national civic values.
crisis_reason: The paragraph describes a routine policy agenda focused on national progress and civic values rather than framing the situation as an urgent threat or danger to the state.

paragraph_text: We will also be determined to strengthen our Republic by fighting crime, more firmly addressing discrimination, and making concrete progress towards equality between men and women, for young children, and for our fellow citizens with disabilities. We will be determined to act to amplify our industrial, technological, and scientific revitalization. We must do everything we can to achieve our goal of full employment, to continue innovating, but also to attract talent and companies, and to produce more in France. Therefore, next year, we will launch major projects in areas such as nuclear power, artificial intelligence, and transportation. By 2027, we want to be ten years ahead of where we were ten years behind in 2017. In 2024, we must accelerate our efforts and drastically simplify the lives of our entrepreneurs, farmers, businesses, artisans, elected officials, and especially our mayors, and for many sectors, including housing.
paragraph_summary: The president outlines national priorities including crime reduction, social equality, industrial revitalization, full employment, and the simplification of administrative processes for various sectors.
reason: It details domestic economic goals, industrial revitalization, and national infrastructure projects.
crisis_reason: The paragraph presents a list of standard policy priorities and governance goals rather than framing these issues as urgent threats or existential dangers.

paragraph_text: 2024, a year of determination, of effective results. In the weeks ahead, I will have the opportunity to tell you how our nation will meet these challenges. My fellow citizens, action is not an option; it is our duty to future generations. That is why I will steadfastly stand alongside those who take action in service of our country, and never alongside those who, refusing to tell you the truth, seek to justify doing nothing, ultimately weakening us. Never alongside those who prioritize electoral calculations, petty compromises, or personal interests. We must therefore continue to strengthen our nation in the face of a world in turmoil. For strength of character is the virtue of difficult times.
paragraph_summary: President Macron calls for national determination and decisive action to strengthen France against global instability while criticizing political inaction and self-interest.
reason: The paragraph emphasizes national unity and domestic political resolve in the face of challenges.
crisis_reason: The speaker frames the situation as a serious threat to the nation, explicitly calling for 'decisive action' to counter 'global instability' and criticizing political inaction, which characterizes the state of affairs as an urgent danger requiring a response.

paragraph_text: I have also traveled throughout Italy, many times. I won’t bore you with the details. I have been to the north, the center, and the south. I have found enthusiasm everywhere, and occasionally, some controversy. That is democracy; it is not entirely accurate to say ". . . that is democracy. . . ", but rather, it reflects the various ways of interpreting democracy. Nevertheless, democracy exists. Let us occasionally reflect on those who paid the price for it, those who died for it, and let us strive to use it well, out of respect for those who gave it their all. I still have in mind, as an example from the south, Secondigliano. This area, so deeply scarred, with that explosion, with eleven dead, and still three, a young woman, missing, with the suffering of their families. There is so much unemployment, with some priests on the front lines, who are leaders, who are fathers. They think that Tizio needs to be hospitalized, that Caio doesn’t have anything to eat today, that Sempronio needs shoes for his child. They deal with every problem, because they bring the peace of Christ, they bring it by meeting people’s needs. And with these needs, thousands are cheering for Italy, applauding… Thousands. How generous you are! Be careful not to exploit these generous acts, not to think that it can last for millennia… Be careful. Let me jump to Timau, in the Carnic Alps: there, we remembered the ‘Carnic women carriers. ’ They were there, those who remained, over ninety years old. I presented each of them with the Knight’s Cross. " My hand trembled as I tried to place it on the chest of these women; some looked at it with wonder, some caressed it, some kissed it. One of them was struck by a sniper, and… there is a profound memory. I do not forget – you, citizens of those beautiful mountains – how many times your leaders have said, "Do not forget us. Rome must not forget us…" But I also do not forget the times you spoke with such moving enthusiasm about the unity of Italy: "President, a united Italy, President. " This cry, from north to south: "President, a united Italy. " What is important is that the people are united. Best wishes, then. Best wishes to everyone. To all citizens, I say this with all my heart; I hope you feel it. I try to express the best I can. Best wishes to those who are here, even if just passing through, even for tourism, those who spend the year here; but best wishes to those who, with different skin colors, different languages, are here working and helping our well-being. Best wishes, thank you for being here with us. Best wishes to everyone.
paragraph_summary: The President reflects on Italian democracy, regional challenges, and the importance of national unity while extending New Year wishes to all residents.
reason: It reflects on domestic travel, national unity, and local social issues within Italy.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on reflection, national unity, and ceremonial New Year wishes rather than framing the state of democracy or regional challenges as an urgent, active threat.

paragraph_text: And now, I have finished. I am a little moved – you can see that – and for a reason. Here, it is just past nine. In three hours, not three hours exactly, at midnight, fifty years will have passed since the Constitution, in which even I, as a young man, had the courage to speak, I too voted for it: January 1st, 1948. Those who died, those who sacrificed themselves, those who gave doctrine, those who gave so much doctrine and knowledge. That first part that no one wants to touch, and that is right, it protects the human person, with their rights, with their dignity. I would like to say to those who feel abandoned, to those who feel crushed by the selfishness of others, to those who feel alone: that is where solidarity is affirmed. To those who feel exploited or feel injustice: that is where equality is affirmed, proclaimed. To those who fear violence or are afraid of oppression: that is where justice and peace are announced. Justice, the condition of peace: it is written there. But in these fifty years, it has also been experienced, it could have been more, certainly, but it has been experienced. Well, citizens, who with me have lived on this platform of law, which has been the great pact between us, if we love this Constitution, if we live it, if each of us commits to making it a living reality, then, certainly, there will be more justice, there will be more ability to think of others, more ability to brotherhood, more ability to walk together, more ability to love. Happy New Year. Happy New Year!
paragraph_summary: The speaker reflects on the fiftieth anniversary of the Italian Constitution, emphasizing its role in promoting solidarity, equality, justice, and national unity.
reason: The paragraph focuses on the Italian Constitution and national solidarity.
crisis_reason: The paragraph is a reflection on national values and historical milestones, which is explicitly excluded from the definition of a crisis.

paragraph_text: The Bicameral Commission, that too served a great purpose. I will not go into the details. There are many discussions. I will simply say that, at the end, at the end of January, the discussion will begin in the Parliament. Best wishes to each and every member of parliament. Sit down to work as reformers, as constitution-makers. Constitution-makers. Review that second great chapter, review it for the Italian people. Best wishes to the Presidents of the Assemblies. Best wishes for that task, the Italian people are waiting. But what happened with the Bicameral Commission? I have said this before: there was that ability to find a consensus, and I hope that this consensus continues. And, if you will allow me, I would like to congratulate those who made it possible, those who wanted it, those who brought it forward in the face of serious difficulties. There is no doubt that it is important. But it is important, above all, because when an agreement is reached, when an agreement is accepted, it is a sign that the goal is much closer, in our minds, in our hearts, in our intelligence. It is a sign that we are willing to relinquish a vision of our own, perhaps legitimate, perhaps excellent, but partial. Well, at this point, unity is achieved. We are talking about unity. Here is the English translation of the provided Italian text: "This is the moment when each individual sets aside a partial viewpoint to embrace what we call the common good. Yes, the common good! The common good for everyone. It’s very positive. So, I will say: very good, the majority has moved forward, certainly, but let me say: very good to the opposition. I cannot forget, as Head of State, when the majority in Parliament fractured over the issue of sending people to Albania, and the opposition stepped up, not exploiting the situation, but serving the interests of Italy. A valuable lesson for everyone, and one I take to heart as well. These agreements are excellent, and they give the feeling – I repeat – that these achievements are achievements of democracy, achievements for all of Italy. It’s important.
paragraph_summary: The President encourages parliamentary consensus and bipartisan cooperation on constitutional reforms for the common good of Italy.
reason: The focus is on domestic parliamentary reform and the consensus-building process within the Bicameral Commission.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on parliamentary cooperation and constitutional reform as a positive, routine governance objective rather than framing the situation as an urgent threat or danger.

paragraph_text: Looking back for a moment, allow me to pause on a fact that I want to bring up at this point in my conversation, because it’s a fact that I’m finding everywhere in the world – a true source of prestige for Italy: volunteer work, heroic in nature, with people who have given their lives for peace. Young and not-so-young people who discover their abilities, their potential to serve others, to help them. It’s incredible. It’s true that volunteer work comes from within, it’s a vocation, a calling from within. Thank you. A truly splendid chapter. In Italy, without a doubt, but throughout the world. How many times have Heads of State told me, "Your people, in various sectors, are carrying out initiatives of their own accord, without asking for anything. " On this matter, I would say that the State has a responsibility, a duty to protect volunteer work. Let me be clear. There are initiatives that are also excellent, that are useful, that achieve results. But they cannot be called volunteer work, because there is a salary involved, or because an entire family has been placed, or because a series of people, friends or acquaintances – even capable ones – have been placed. That is not volunteer work. The essence, the hallmark of volunteer work, is selflessness. And the State, by engaging with volunteer work, must defend that, which is a very high value. When I say defend, I mean that I know that from time to time, proposals of different kinds are put forward, under various pretexts, but their content undermines that selflessness. That is not volunteer work.
paragraph_summary: The President praises the selfless nature of volunteer work in Italy and emphasizes the state's duty to protect its integrity from non-voluntary initiatives.
reason: It discusses the domestic value and regulation of volunteer work within Italy.
crisis_reason: The paragraph frames non-voluntary initiatives as a threat to the state's integrity, which the speaker presents as a danger requiring the state's protective intervention.

paragraph_text: We have living problems, yes, we have others as well. There are many. I will mention a few. One living problem is indifference. Democracy needs support, it needs participation. I say this to you young people. Many enter politics as something new, they enter politics without proper preparation, and sometimes they achieve excellent results. But you young people, prepare yourselves with education, study, prepare yourselves! There is a need to feel that participation. Democracies are measured by the number of citizens who are interested. And think about employment. You have heard me talk about that number of times. It pleased me that the majority came together and made a commitment that this year will be the year against unemployment, the year for employment. It is a very serious problem. It is a problem that. . . I will say this: I don’t feel that I can absolve myself of responsibility for this problem. I don’t feel it. We are behind on this issue, and we need to move forward on this issue. Young people face enormous dangers. Think about crime, where and how it infiltrates. A brief aside: and thank you to our law enforcement agencies, for what you do, every day. How many have fallen, how many have been injured. Thank you. Young people are exposed to these dangers. We must do everything possible to ensure that we have done our duty. I wish the government, the majority, the opposition, and the Parliament well. There is education. There is education, with the reform; it is important to have the reform. " It is being discussed, and there are differing opinions. Young people have demonstrated that they have different perspectives. The importance of dialogue. Dialogue with teachers, especially those who are deeply involved in the education system; dialogue also with students. Dialogue, it’s easy to say, is difficult to achieve: dialogue with people, with families. But, overall, dialogue is essential. The rest is of little value, because there is an urgent need for reform; there is an absolute need. We have such a rich heritage, which also addresses the gaps that often exist and lead to harmful consequences. Our heritage of humanism, our wealth. . . Sometimes, when traveling the world, we find people who know more about our culture than we do ourselves.
paragraph_summary: The speaker addresses youth participation in democracy, the urgent need to combat unemployment, the dangers of crime, and the importance of educational reform through dialogue.
reason: It addresses domestic challenges like unemployment, education reform, and youth engagement.
crisis_reason: The speaker frames unemployment, crime, and the state of democracy as urgent dangers that require immediate attention and reform, characterizing them as threats to the social order.

paragraph_text: And there is a delicate issue. Many have asked me to address it: justice and politics. Here, the relationship has, at times, been a difficult, contentious one. There have been invitations for a more peaceful dialogue from many sides. I would like to take a significant invitation: that of the Vice President of the Council of the Judiciary, Professor Grosso. He said words that I completely share, about the ability to lower one’s voice, about the ability to be silent, of silence, because these controversies are unnecessary. Especially when they occur between individuals. They are pointless; they ignite conflict. The Judiciary has an association. Over 90 percent of judges belong to this association. This association has engaged in extensive dialogue with the Bicameral Parliament. I hope that it can continue to engage in dialogue during this very important phase of the reforms. This association has leadership, all the leadership, let’s be clear, and I say this with gratitude, of great wisdom, great balance, and great ability to engage in dialogue, to understand the perspective of the other side. Best wishes for this dialogue. But I have a duty, a duty to clarify. There have been controversies because, when greeting state officials to offer New Year’s greetings, and when speaking about the Judiciary, I used the term "normalization" and spoke of "returning to the right track. " This led to the comment: "Does the President believe that there are no more crimes, that the era of serious illegal acts is over?" But the President never dreamed of such a thing. The President never dreamed of such a thing. Sadly, no one can dream of that. There are crimes, and there are trials that must – must – be held. They must. But when I said those things, I recalled that four years ago, when speaking at the Chamber of Deputies, following an initiative by the current President of the Chamber, Mr. Violante, who was then President of the Anti-Mafia Commission – if I recall correctly – there was a large gathering of highly respected judges. The President Violante asked me to speak. I was the first to speak about this. I say this only because it was my duty to speak. And I spoke, denouncing the excessive use of preventive detention. This has existed. I believe that everyone knows my deep commitment to the Judiciary. It has been my primary vocation, and it still is. I have said on occasion: "The judicial robe is attached to the soul. " I live it that way. My respect is always total. And the gratitude for what the Judiciary has done to remove so many injustices from the political world must remain. However, we must have the courage to say that preventive detention, in particular, sometimes, I don’t know if it was the judge or a colleague, let me say, rather bluntly, who said, "Talk or stay in, " that has no place in any legal system. It belongs under the heading of torture. Not that! The sound of handcuffs being rattled in the face of someone being questioned by a colleague is an abhorrent system, because it is an offense. Even the defendant facing serious charges is entitled to respect. But there was another issue, which I denounced, not to this Council of the Judiciary, but to the previous Council – we need to go back three or four years – and it concerned warnings of legal action being sent to the press before they reached the person concerned. No, no. There is an article in the penal code that specifically addresses the violation of investigative confidentiality. It’s simply not possible. When I speak of returning to the right track, I sense that we have already begun to do so, thank God, but I need to say it because these things must not be repeated. In that sense, my words. I have another clarification to make, because I have signed pardons. I know, it may require some courage, some assumption of responsibility. It has been said: "The Head of State signed pardons for people who hadn't even asked for them. " All six had requested pardons more than once, because for several of them, three years ago, if I remember correctly, their requests had been denied. Now, the situation was very different, because the Judiciary’s opinion was one that I would summarize as the possibility, the ability to reintegrate. When these individuals are authorized to leave their cells for the entire day and return to prison only at night to sleep, it shows that the judge responsible for granting that permission has great faith in these people's ability to reintegrate. In these cases, the Head of State consulted with the Ministry, as is required, but the responsibility for the pardon rests with the Head of State. I have assumed that responsibility, and I stand by that decision. It is not an act of heroism; it is a normal act. I assumed that responsibility because I disapprove of those who, in an attempt to please everyone, fail to do their duty. I was convinced that these matters had matured, but since it was said that I had changed my opinion, because I had spoken and written in one way, and then acted differently, please allow me to make a clarification. Feel free to think that I was wrong. But I spoke earlier, privately, on several occasions, with politicians; then, on June 2nd of last year, I spoke before the Chamber and the Senate, and I spoke about not extinguishing hope, especially for young people who have been convicted and who have served or are serving their sentences. I also spoke, respecting the competence of Parliament, about a rather limited number of cases. This is the letter I wrote last October, regarding the case of those convicted of the murder of Commissioner Calabresi: ". . . Here is the English translation of the provided Italian text: "Only a very limited number of purely individual situations, each with its own unique and specific characteristics, could allow for the exercise of the power of pardon, " which is a power vested in the Head of State. "For these few cases, however, I have asked the Minister of Justice to ensure that the necessary investigative procedures are carried out with diligence. " In certain cases, there have been requests to confirm this possibility of reintegration, "so that those benefits provided by the penal system can be made available to individuals currently excluded from them, for the independent assessment of the judicial authority. " This means that when a sentence is reduced to three years, the judicial authority does not – no, it does not automatically trigger – but if they deem it appropriate, and if the conditions are met, they can grant certain benefits provided by law. And I added: "I repeat what I have said on many occasions: a crime does not change its nature with the passage of time. " I would add: murder after a hundred years is still murder. "The suffering of innocent victims must never be forgotten, never. But, of course, it is not contrary to the fundamental values of justice to seek a path that, without disturbing these fundamental principles, allows for a gradual return to society for those deserving it. " And I have acted in accordance with this letter, with these statements. I believed that the conditions were met for me to take on this responsibility. I know that you are waiting for my words on another topic: amnesty and pardon. What do I think? As a private citizen, I have a thought; as Head of State, I also have a thought, one of absolute clarity: amnesty and pardon are the responsibility of Parliament. Parliament itself, in its reform efforts, will see whether it needs to correct anything, but this is the exclusive competence of Parliament, and on this issue, the Head of State has no right to have opinions.
paragraph_summary: The President discusses the relationship between politics and the judiciary, clarifies his stance on preventive detention, and explains his exercise of presidential pardon powers.
reason: The text deals with the domestic relationship between the judiciary and politics, and the power of pardon.
crisis_reason: The paragraph describes routine governance, legal clarifications, and the exercise of executive powers without framing these topics as urgent threats or dangers to the state.

paragraph_text: Good evening, and Happy New Year. It’s the seventh time I have the honor of wishing the Italian people a Happy New Year, a people of which I am so proud to be a part. Happy New Year. Happy New Year to everyone, to each and every one of you, said with such intensity, said with a certain familiarity, as we began last year, because I hope it will feel like a conversation that enters your homes with serenity, a chance to exchange a few words. First of all, I should tell you that the preparation for this evening has been quite involved, because not only does the Head of State have to think about what to say – and there’s no doubt that he has been thinking about it for some time, following events, episodes – but also, the interest of the public, the countless letters, suggesting to the Head of State topics to address, one after another… Just this morning, the Secretary General presented me with a package of proposals, of letters, on matters of great importance. I want to express my gratitude, because it seems to me that this is a shared event: people feel – and I am grateful to them – that the voice of the Head of State is their voice. I hope I am worthy of that. I would like to say one thing: of course, it is impossible for me to address all of these topics, even those that are truly of great interest and importance; I can say that none of those letters will be ignored. My task is to follow up on them in some way, even if I cannot discuss them tonight. Happy New Year! And, in wishing you well, we also take a moment for reflection. I was watching on television just a few moments ago an interview with one of the two people who managed to survive after twelve, thirteen hours, I would say, trapped under the building that collapsed in Rome. And when tragedies like these occur – meanwhile, best wishes to you and your wife – when they occur during festive days, they carry a terrible, poignant sense of suffering, and we already fear that, in the years to come, amidst the celebrations, there will be this reminder, this memory… I cannot forget the two white coffins of those children, there, under the grand dome of the Basilica of Saint Paul… And the question returns: Why, Lord…? And the three police officers who were lost, which adds to the suffering that has occurred this year, from various parts of the country, within the police forces, and among the military serving abroad… These moments of reflection… I would like to add one thing. It seems to me that there is a crucial point that needs to be highlighted: the murder of Father Graziano Muntoni. This man, a professor, had dedicated his life to teaching; he had been a priest for eight years; he had left teaching, which was for young people, to make a more complete donation, to be completely at the service of the people. And, while he was going to celebrate Mass, his sacrifice, his altar on the street! A synthesis of suffering: in hospitals, in prisons, from all parts, in moments of solitude… I hope that a spirit of solidarity reaches everyone. This is my final wish, marking the end of my seventh year.
paragraph_summary: The President reflects on national tragedies, the suffering of citizens, and the importance of solidarity while acknowledging public input for his annual address.
reason: The paragraph offers New Year greetings and reflects on domestic tragedies and national solidarity.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on reflecting on past tragedies and suffering, which frames these events as historical occurrences rather than active, ongoing threats requiring urgent response or vigilance.

paragraph_text: And among the visits to the Quirinale, there was one this year that deeply moved us. Last October, here at the Quirinale, Pope John Paul II came to visit. Thank you, Holiness! Thank you for this visit, which came at a time of great political significance and was of immense importance. Thank you. Thank you for the discussions, thank you for your kindness. Above all, thank you for something that everyone felt: it wasn’t just a great gesture of attention – and that was already a great thing! – but it was, above all, a gesture of love for Italy and for the Italian people. And it is the Italian people who, through my voice, Holiness, who thank you for your unwavering, thank God, testimony of truth, of peace, of justice, which overcomes every resistance that life sometimes presents. Courage and onward, Holiness! Just a moment, and then I’ll continue…
paragraph_summary: The President expresses gratitude to Pope John Paul II for his significant visit to the Quirinale and his enduring message of peace and justice.
reason: The paragraph focuses on a domestic visit by the Pope and its significance to the Italian people.
crisis_reason: The paragraph is framed as a ceremonial expression of gratitude and reflection on a past visit, lacking any language regarding threats, urgency, or danger.

paragraph_text: Because I would like to briefly address two points that I’m calling “reconciliations. ” I strongly believe in these reconciliations. There may be others: I’m mentioning two. The first is the reconciliation of trust between citizens and politics. Political parties have resumed their activities, and I believe that’s important. The Constitution speaks of political parties and assigns them a role. When the parties were first formed, after the uprising, they represented thought, culture, education, especially for young people. They were a source of rich programs, a forum for dialectical exchange of ideas. Then, there was a period of decline… If they exist, political parties must return to that original approach, helping to elevate the political landscape. If they return with the failings of the past, if they return with those same failings, it’s a tragedy, because it alienates citizens. We ask ourselves why people don’t vote. Yes, of course, if you vote almost every week, people get tired… All the parties have made that comment, and it will be easy to correct that. But how can people connect when they encounter people who claim to be politicians, but who, in the complete absence of any political thought, offer instead slogans, insults of every kind, where education seems to have disappeared, with insinuations, if not outright falsehoods? But can that be politics? And with that system, do we honestly believe that decent people will want to engage in politics?
