crisis_reason: The paragraph describes routine economic management and policy goals rather than framing the economic situation as an urgent threat or a state of emergency.

paragraph_text: The path to a secure economy is through work for everyone who can work. And never before have so many people been employed in Norway. Thousands who previously were outside of education and employment are now experiencing the security of having a job. That's good for self-esteem, for health, for community – and of course, for the economy.
paragraph_summary: The speaker highlights record-high employment levels in Norway and the positive impact of work on individual well-being and the national economy.
reason: It focuses on domestic employment rates and the national labor market.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on positive economic achievements and the benefits of employment, which are explicitly excluded from the definition of a crisis.

paragraph_text: In August, I visited employees and residents at a nursing home in Sandsli, in Bergen municipality. Charlotte Lygre told me that she started as a part-time caregiver. Slowly, she progressed to a 73 percent position, and after 14 years, she finally received a full-time position when Sandsli opened in 2020. Sandsli is the first nursing home in Norway where all healthcare workers and nurses work full-time. For Charlotte, that means security for her and other employees, and security for our elderly and their families. This year, we will convert even more part-time positions to full-time positions. And we will get even more people into work, with appropriate support when needed. New, young employees – they are a resource. So, welcome them warmly.
paragraph_summary: The speaker highlights the benefits of full-time employment in nursing homes and outlines plans to increase full-time positions and workforce participation.
reason: It highlights a specific domestic initiative regarding full-time employment in Norwegian nursing homes.
crisis_reason: The paragraph describes a routine policy initiative to improve workforce conditions and employment levels, which is presented as a positive development rather than an urgent threat or danger.

paragraph_text: In a time when the population is aging, with more pensioners, we need as many people as possible, everyone who can, to work and create value. Of course, there are some who cannot work, for good reasons. We, as a welfare state, must support them. Because our welfare state is not just for show; it is meant to be used.
paragraph_summary: The speaker emphasizes the necessity of workforce participation to sustain the welfare state while affirming support for those unable to work.
reason: It discusses the domestic welfare state's role in supporting the aging population and workforce.
crisis_reason: The paragraph frames the sustainability of the welfare state as a routine policy matter regarding workforce participation and social support, rather than presenting it as an urgent threat or a crisis.

paragraph_text: One of the strongest images that stays with me is from Ål in Hallingdal. Until August 8th, Olaug and Halgeir Viken lived in a house furthest west, by Strandafjorden. It rained heavily that day, and in the afternoon, there was a loud bang. Masses of earth came rushing down the hillside. Olaug told us, "I saw the kitchen window explode and felt the shockwave, and then I don't remember… until I suddenly found myself lying in a pile outside. " The couple found each other, made it to the main road, stopped a car, and managed to call 112. The house was crushed. Everything was gone. During the cleanup efforts, there was only one thing that hadn't been destroyed, one thing to take with them to a new home: a small piece of a wall, with three photographs: their wedding picture and their two sons as children.
paragraph_summary: The speaker recounts the survival of a couple whose home was destroyed by a landslide in Ål during the extreme weather in August.
reason: It recounts a specific local natural disaster event in Norway.
crisis_reason: The paragraph describes a specific, acute natural disaster event involving the destruction of a home, which constitutes a threat to safety and livelihoods.

paragraph_text: Let us show that trusting diversity counteracts polarization. Let us learn from the history, cultural heritage, and knowledge of the Sami people regarding coexistence with nature. Let us demonstrate that we build trust by creating space for a wide range of opinions, cultures, and religious communities in Norway. And that is what we, in Norway, have become over the past couple of generations. We need an expanded sense of "we" when faced with an ever-increasing and stronger flow of impressions from outside, transmitted at an increasingly rapid pace. At the same time, we must cherish what binds us together. There are fundamental values that are shared and remain constant – such as human dignity, democracy, equality, and freedom of expression. This also means that we must speak openly about what engages us and what upsets us. That all lives are precious and equally valuable. And that hate – directed at Jews, Muslims, or others – offends those targeted and weakens us as a society. This isn't about natural forces; these are forces that we ourselves control. Tolerance, attitudes, and trust determine how we, the Norwegian "we, " will navigate uncertainty and insecurity in the future.
paragraph_summary: The speech emphasizes fostering national unity, social trust, and shared democratic values to combat polarization and intolerance within a diverse Norwegian society.
reason: It focuses on domestic social cohesion, tolerance, and national values within Norway.
crisis_reason: The paragraph frames polarization and intolerance as active threats to the integrity of the democratic order and social cohesion, requiring a deliberate response to combat them.

paragraph_text: When we face adversity, it is important to put into words how we feel. Last year, many people showed extraordinary courage by sharing that they felt lonely. Several of our young people spoke about how difficult it was to continue with school and leisure activities after the pandemic. Some opened up about mental health challenges. Others spoke about harassment and racism. Many found the courage to admit they were struggling financially and asked openly and honestly for help. It can be difficult enough in one of the world’s wealthiest countries. Asking for help is not something we should be ashamed of, even though we often prefer to manage on our own and not be a burden. But we can’t do everything alone. We all need help with something. And even though no one can help everyone, everyone can help someone. When there is war in Europe, prices are rising, and there is economic uncertainty, more people will need support, encouragement, and help. Fortunately, we have each other. Many have support from family, neighbors, and colleagues. And together, we have Norway’s welfare state. It’s not just for show; it’s a collective effort that we have built up over generations. The welfare state is there to be used. If we are to get through these difficult times, we must take care of each other and utilize the resources within a strong community.
paragraph_summary: The speaker encourages citizens to seek help for mental health and financial struggles while emphasizing the importance of community support and the Norwegian welfare state.
reason: The paragraph focuses on domestic social challenges and the Norwegian welfare state.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on the availability of support systems and the strength of the welfare state rather than framing mental health and financial struggles as an urgent, existential threat or an active crisis requiring immediate emergency response.

paragraph_text: Last year, I met many Ukrainian refugees throughout Norway. Narek is one of them. After only a few weeks in Bergen, he got a job at a restaurant not far from the Bryggen wharf. Narek served me "perse-torsk" – a traditional Bergen dish. He proudly told me that he had just learned the recipe. "We haven't regretted for a second that we gave Narek a job, " the restaurant owner said. I hope that more people will find the opportunity to offer jobs to Ukrainians in our country. Meeting Narek reminded me of the importance of work, of being able to support yourself and earn your own money. Not everyone can do that, and we must take good care of those who cannot work. But many who are currently out of work both want and can work, and we should help them with that. In Trondheim, at the Scandic Nidelven hotel, I had the opportunity to spend time with Adrian and Robert during a shift in the kitchen early in December. They have a developmental disability and have found employment through the "Helt Med" program. They greeted me with a knowledge and professional pride that was deeply impressive. Of course, as a community, we must create opportunities for them to work. Work is important for taking care of yourself and your family, for mental health, and for feeling that you are contributing. Work shows that we demand and expect from each other. Work is important for keeping the business sector and the economy going, but it is also important for us to be able to pay for education, research, and knowledge – all of which allow us as a society to adapt, solve problems, and create new opportunities. Yes, it allows us to shape our own future.
paragraph_summary: The speaker emphasizes the importance of employment for personal well-being, social contribution, and the overall economic stability of Norwegian society.
reason: The focus is on domestic employment programs and the Norwegian labor market.
crisis_reason: The paragraph frames employment as a positive pillar of personal well-being and economic stability rather than as an urgent threat or a crisis requiring immediate intervention.

paragraph_text: Dear everyone, making Norway, Norway, is not something we ever finish. Norway is not something we achieve once and for all. Norway is created by you and me, through small and large choices, every single day. Not each of us individually, but together. Together. When we work. When we help those around us. When we take seriously a deeply rooted Norwegian value of fulfilling our duties and claiming our rights, in that order. And when we look away from the often brutal comment sections on social media, and meet each other face to face, as the diverse individuals we are. Following the mass shooting in Oslo on June 25th last year, where two people were killed and over 20 were injured, we did just that: we came together and stood united against hate and harassment. The staff at the establishments where the shooting took place supported those affected – they didn't shy away – but reopened the very next day. At the Pride parade in September, I met Karoline and Tina from Valdres. They experienced severe harassment because they had chosen to embrace their feelings, to each other. They chose to get married and have children. They shared their story, and as we walked together in the Pride parade, they told me, "If we back down, then they win. " Over the coming year, I want us all to do as Karoline and Tina did. Don't be afraid, don't back down, don't cower. Even if it’s sometimes uncomfortable, we invest in something of our best and strongest: our community and solidarity.
paragraph_summary: The speaker calls for national unity, solidarity, and courage in the face of hate and harassment to strengthen Norwegian society.
reason: It addresses internal social cohesion and domestic incidents of violence and harassment.
crisis_reason: The speaker frames hate and harassment as a direct threat to the integrity and cohesion of Norwegian society, necessitating an urgent response of unity and courage.

paragraph_text: Eleven-year-old Embla from Bergen gave the children's New Year's address yesterday. She visited me at my office a few weeks ago and gave me advice for my own New Year's address. I want to quote her, because she put it so well: "Why push when you can hug? Why curse when you can praise? Why hate when you can love?" It is the sum of our actions and attitudes that shapes who we are as a community. Even though we are different. Whether we are young or old. Whether we come from the city or the countryside. Whether we have different beliefs. Whether we vote for different political parties. And whether we experience challenging times in different ways. Together, we can choose solidarity around everything we have in common, rather than fear of what is different. Truth over fake news. And understanding over judgment. Let us unite around that. Let us put aside some of our differences. Let us be generous with each other and with ourselves. During the Christmas season, many people have stepped up to support those who find the holidays difficult. The homeless, people struggling with addiction, or those who don't find their place in a traditional Norwegian family Christmas. I would like to extend a special greeting to you, and to the many thousands who work while others are on vacation or sleeping – police officers, snowplow drivers, those working on platforms, employees in hospitals and elderly care, and others who work shifts. I would like to thank the men and women in uniform who contribute to the defense of Norway and stand up for the security of our allies. 2022 was the year of volunteering. As we begin a new year, I would like to greet all of you who contribute to Norway’s vibrant volunteer sector, and especially to the mothers and fathers who provide opportunities for our children in sports and culture. You are invaluable to our community. We are all grateful for the tireless work that the King and Queen do for the country and for the people. We send them and the rest of the royal family our very best wishes for the new year. And to each and every one of you at home: Happy New Year!
paragraph_summary: The speaker calls for national unity, solidarity, and kindness while expressing gratitude to volunteers, essential workers, and the royal family.
reason: The paragraph provides domestic holiday greetings and thanks various national public service sectors.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on gratitude, national unity, and ceremonial appreciation rather than identifying or warning against a specific threat.

paragraph_text: Dear everyone. Tonight and today, we have wished each other a happy new year. But perhaps it would be more fitting to wish each other a new and better year. Because 2021 was the year we so strongly hoped the pandemic would ease its grip on us, that we could reclaim our everyday lives. That didn't happen. Again, we have celebrated a different Christmas and New Year. With masks and social distancing, with many of our loved ones infected or in quarantine, and with more people being temporarily laid off or having lost their jobs. Nevertheless, I am impressed by how people in Norway have handled the pandemic. We have fought against the virus by standing together, following the advice, even the most intrusive measures. We have fought against the virus with our greatest strengths: community and solidarity. In many countries, people have taken to the streets in large protests and turned against each other. In many countries, people are ignoring the rules because they don't trust the authorities or each other. That is not the Norwegian way. Things are different here. But we are all thoroughly tired of this pandemic. I saw someone on social media who had embroidered a design where the virus was getting its comeuppance, with such colourful and strong swear words that I shouldn't use them on television. We are that tired – that we are embroidering swear words! Even though we would so dearly like to be finished with the virus, the virus isn't finished with us. I urge everyone to continue following the advice and regulations, and to get vaccinated, whether you are due for your first, second, or third dose. The vaccine protects you from serious illness. It protects the people you care about – and the people who care about them. It protects society. Make it a New Year's resolution: get vaccinated!
paragraph_summary: The speaker reflects on the ongoing challenges of the COVID-19 pandemic in Norway and urges citizens to maintain solidarity and get vaccinated.
reason: It discusses domestic pandemic management and national vaccination efforts.
crisis_reason: The speaker frames the pandemic as an ongoing challenge requiring active vigilance and specific actions like vaccination, which constitutes an active threat framing.

paragraph_text: There are crises that affect an entire society – such as war or a pandemic. And there are personal crises that affect us as individuals – such as losing someone we love. Such crises are mirrors – reflecting how vulnerable we truly are, and how dependent we are on others. But they can also be a mirror reflecting how strong we are. How strong we are when we stand together against adversity, how strong we are in community. And that fills me with optimism; it points the way towards everything we can achieve together – a just society, an environmentally friendly society, a safe everyday life for everyone. I wish each and every one of you a good – a much better – New Year.
paragraph_summary: The speaker reflects on how personal and societal crises reveal human vulnerability and strength, emphasizing the importance of community in building a better future.
reason: The text reflects on national resilience and domestic societal goals.
crisis_reason: The paragraph reflects on the nature of crises in a philosophical and retrospective manner rather than presenting an active, urgent threat that demands immediate action or sacrifice.

paragraph_text: The pandemic has taught us a great deal – it has reflected who we are – it has shown us our strengths as a community, but also our challenges as a society. I believe everyone has seen the value of a strong, public healthcare system and all the skilled people who work within it. But the pandemic has also revealed weaknesses in our healthcare system. We need to train more professionals. We need to be able to provide more intensive care in hospitals simultaneously. People struggling with mental health issues need better and faster support. Things will improve. It’s a major undertaking, and we are already working on it.
paragraph_summary: The speaker discusses lessons learned from the pandemic regarding the need to strengthen public healthcare, increase professional training, and improve mental health support.
reason: It addresses domestic healthcare system improvements and national policy needs.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on lessons learned and improvements to be made in the aftermath of the pandemic, which frames the content as a recovery and policy-oriented reflection rather than an active threat.

paragraph_text: One of the strengths of Norwegian society is the relatively small differences between people. For long stretches of our history, we have distributed power and opportunities more fairly than perhaps any other country – shifting power to ordinary people. Nevertheless, I have no doubt: the pandemic has made our society more unfair. Our lives are somewhat more disparate. Some have saved money. Others have worked from home. Some have enjoyed a little extra quality time with their families. Others have lost their jobs and income and are now facing a new, difficult winter. More people are lonely. And on top of everything, the energy crisis has added another burden for many. Many of us are part of strong communities: families who have time for each other, vibrant local communities. But inequality and increasing social and economic differences are eroding our sense of community. When our lives become increasingly different, it erodes trust, even in Norway. It weakens our sense of community. The fight against a divided society is therefore a fight about who we are, about who we want to be.
paragraph_summary: The speaker addresses how the pandemic and energy crisis have increased social and economic inequality, threatening Norway's sense of community and public trust.
reason: The focus is on domestic economic inequality and social cohesion within Norway.
crisis_reason: The speaker explicitly frames the pandemic and energy crisis as threats that are actively undermining social cohesion and public trust, which constitutes a chronic threat requiring urgent attention.

paragraph_text: When I think about the future and the major challenges that lie ahead, I think first and foremost about our young people. We have the best young people in the world! But even they need a community that supports them. We are stronger together, everyone is. In many of my meetings with young people, I see that communities have failed them – and the pandemic has made a bad situation even worse for many of them. I meet ordinary young people who are struggling, who need reliable adults to help them get their lives in order, to finish school, and eventually, to find a job. At the same time, I must say, there is so much drive and initiative in Norwegian young people. Frøya Valland Dale is one of the young people I remember well from last summer – a cheerful girl with lots of energy at Knarvik upper secondary school just outside Bergen. Frøya is studying technology and industrial subjects. She loves to weld! An unconventional and courageous choice, but as Frøya said, "I like that something is happening, learning with my hands. " One of the most meaningful things I do as a politician is to ensure that students like Frøya can succeed in a more practical school – with apprenticeships for everyone. It’s a fantastic investment in our young people – and in the future.
paragraph_summary: The speaker emphasizes the need for community support and practical education opportunities to help young people succeed in school and the workforce.
reason: It discusses domestic youth education and labor market integration policies.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on routine educational and social support initiatives rather than framing the situation as an urgent threat or danger to the state or society.

paragraph_text: I would like to send a big thank you to all of you who are on duty this New Year's weekend. Thank you for looking after us while we celebrate. I know that many of you are extra tired and fed up. That applies to many who have been on the front lines during the pandemic. Healthcare workers and teachers, childcare workers and cleaners, NAV employees, and so many others. We now know even more about how dependent we are on you, how essential you are to our society. For your expertise and professional pride, for your hard work and courage, we are eternally grateful to you. A heartfelt thank you also goes to all Norwegians living abroad. Our students around the world, our seafarers, our aid workers, and everyone who carries Norwegian values out into the world and brings a little bit of the world back home. You all enrich us. A warm thank you goes to our beloved royal family, who faithfully and tirelessly serve us all, both at home and abroad. And a special thank you to the Armed Forces, and to all of you who are on guard, ensuring Norway's safety – both here and abroad.
paragraph_summary: The speaker expresses gratitude to essential workers, Norwegians living abroad, the royal family, and the Armed Forces for their service and contributions to society.
reason: The paragraph thanks domestic workers and Norwegian citizens serving abroad.
crisis_reason: The paragraph is purely ceremonial and focused on expressing gratitude and recognition, which does not invoke a threat or an urgent situation.

paragraph_text: On New Year's Eve a year ago, we celebrated leaving a decade behind and embarking on a new one. It offered a glimmer of hope and sparked bigger dreams. However, the decade didn't begin as we had hoped or dreamed. 2020 proved to be a truly difficult year. At the beginning of the year, the coronavirus emerged. And at the very end of the year, we witnessed the devastation that natural forces can inflict. The community of Gjerdrum, in the region of Romerike, was struck by one of the worst quick clay landslides in recent times. Many people have been affected. Many have lost their homes. Many are still evacuated. Many are living in uncertainty. Our thoughts are with everyone affected by this disaster, and our gratitude goes to the rescue workers who have done a remarkable job. The coronavirus dominated our society for most of the year. The virus has taken lives. Many have become ill. Jobs have been lost. Many have experienced loneliness and isolation. Desires for closeness have been replaced by the need for distance.
paragraph_summary: The speech reflects on the challenges of 2020, specifically the COVID-19 pandemic and the devastating quick clay landslide in Gjerdrum.
reason: It addresses domestic natural disasters and the national impact of the pandemic.
crisis_reason: The paragraph frames the pandemic and the landslide as significant challenges that defined the year, focusing on the threats to safety and health that occurred.

paragraph_text: Many of the challenges we faced before the pandemic will still be present after it. Inclusion is particularly important. Work provides income, ensuring food on the table and a roof over one's head. But work is also more than just income; it's about community. I believe that everyone who has spent a lot of time working from home has learned to appreciate that. Work is also about achieving a sense of accomplishment and feeling useful to others. Many people in Norway have so much more to offer, but they are not given the opportunity. This could be due to an unusual name, gaps in their CV, or a disability. Moving forward, we must address this more effectively. My hope is that our society, after the pandemic, will become even better at seeing everyone and including even more people.
paragraph_summary: The speaker advocates for greater workplace inclusion to ensure that individuals with diverse backgrounds or disabilities can contribute to society and feel valued.
reason: The focus is on domestic labor market inclusion and social policy.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on promoting workplace inclusion as a positive social goal and a matter of valuing individuals, rather than framing the lack of inclusion as an urgent threat or a crisis requiring emergency intervention.

paragraph_text: During the holiday season, we think of those working abroad, unable to be with their families and friends. Many are doing a job on our behalf, to make the world a better place. This year, the coronavirus has created an even more special situation. I want to thank you all for your efforts. We are thinking of you during this holiday season. Many Norwegians have been in quarantine over the past year, including our beloved King and Queen. I would like to recall the King’s words in his address to the nation on March 15, 2020. His message was that we all need a little extra kindness during this time. Those are wise words. In difficult times, it’s easy to use stronger language than necessary when we disagree. I want to thank the King and Queen for the support and wisdom they have shown during a particularly challenging time. A little more kindness makes it easier for all of us to persevere.
paragraph_summary: The speaker expresses gratitude to Norwegians working abroad and emphasizes the importance of kindness and unity during the COVID-19 pandemic.
reason: It offers domestic holiday greetings and reflections on national social conduct.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on gratitude and the promotion of social values like kindness and unity rather than framing the pandemic as an active, ongoing threat requiring urgent intervention.

paragraph_text: There are many people who deserve our thanks after a year like this. I would like to highlight a few. Employees in hospitals and elderly care are in a unique position. Their efforts have meant a great deal to the patients they have treated and have given us all a sense of security and pride. All the dedicated individuals across our country, such as those at the Halden Volunteer Centre, who are making an enormous effort to provide information about infection control to those who do not understand Norwegian as well. Everyone who extends a helping hand to those who are lonely, for example, Karl Laugsand and the National Association for Public Health in Ytterøy, who call around to people living alone in the village and invite them to a shared meal. Employees in schools and kindergartens, who are ensuring that children and young people have the best possible start in life. They quickly adapted to digital learning and have persevered through yellow and red alert levels. Employees in transportation, cleaning, and retail, who have kept the wheels of society turning. Employees in government departments, directorates, agencies, counties, and municipalities, and especially those in the Institute of Public Health and the Directorate of Health. Many are still working weekends and late evenings, providing advice, regulations, compensation schemes, and much more. Without them, Norway’s response to the virus would not have been possible. I would also like to thank the other parties in the Parliament for their constructive and positive cooperation during the pandemic. Finally, I would like to express my sincere gratitude to all of you who follow the infection control advice and regulations. It’s not enjoyable, I know. But it works when we do it together. And we need to persevere a little longer. That's how we managed 2020. Together. And that's how we will overcome the beginning of 2021 as well. Happy New Year. Our time will come soon.
paragraph_summary: The speaker expresses gratitude to essential workers, volunteers, and the public for their collective efforts and cooperation during the COVID-19 pandemic.
reason: The paragraph thanks various domestic public sectors and national institutions for their pandemic response.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on expressing gratitude for past efforts and collective cooperation during the pandemic, which frames the situation as a period of recovery and reflection rather than an active, ongoing threat.

paragraph_text: The government has had to make decisions quickly, often under considerable uncertainty. Not everything went right on the first attempt, but we have adjusted our decisions as we learned more. Since March, two waves of infection have hit Europe. Both times, we in Norway have fared better than many other countries. We have had fewer people in hospitals and fewer deaths. Our economy has performed better. There are several reasons why we have, so far, managed to cope better. We implemented measures early on. Countries that waited were hit harder. And most Norwegians have stepped up to the plate and contributed to the collective effort. People have put their own interests and dreams on hold to protect the elderly and vulnerable groups. This has saved lives. I am deeply grateful, proud, and moved by the way the Norwegian people have handled the biggest challenge our society has faced since World War II. We stood together when it mattered most. Although 2020 was a difficult year, the efforts of the people will be something we can always look back on with pride.
paragraph_summary: The Prime Minister reflects on Norway's successful management of the COVID-19 pandemic and expresses gratitude for the public's collective efforts during the crisis.
reason: It evaluates the domestic response to the pandemic compared to other nations.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on the successful management of a past event and expresses gratitude for collective efforts, which frames it as a recovery and achievement rather than an active, ongoing threat.

paragraph_text: Twenty years ago, one of our resistance heroes, Tore Gjelsvik, published his memoirs from the war. The title of the book also expresses the hope I have for 2021: "Soon, our day will come. " Soon, the day will come when we can hug each other again. We can stand close together at concerts and sing at the top of our lungs. We can gather all generations of the family for holidays and birthdays. And the graduating class can celebrate again. Soon, our day will come. I wish I could say that day is tomorrow. Unfortunately, that is not the case. We must continue to live with the measures for a while longer. Most of us can manage to live somewhat unusually for a period. It's not enjoyable, and it takes its toll, but we can cope.
paragraph_summary: The Prime Minister encourages patience with ongoing COVID-19 restrictions while expressing hope for a future return to normal social gatherings and family celebrations.
reason: The text discusses domestic pandemic restrictions and the hope for a return to normal life.
crisis_reason: The paragraph frames the ongoing restrictions as a necessary measure to manage the pandemic, implying an active threat to public health that requires continued public compliance and patience.

paragraph_text: However, those who were already experiencing loneliness may have found it even worse. Therefore, I have one message for you this evening: Look around. If you think someone is lonely, give them a call. Start a conversation. See how they are doing. It costs so little, but it means so much. Soon, our day will come.
paragraph_summary: The speaker encourages citizens to reach out to those experiencing loneliness to provide support and foster social connection.
reason: It encourages domestic social support for lonely individuals within the community.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on social connection and community support as a positive, routine civic action rather than framing loneliness as an urgent threat to the state or social order.

paragraph_text: It has been almost 45 years since I became involved in politics. Much has changed in those years, and mostly for the better. The issues that have been most important to work on have therefore changed. But the belief that everyone should have a job to go to has always been a driving force for me. Therefore, it has been difficult to see how the coronavirus has affected so many businesses, particularly in the tourism, hospitality, culture, entertainment, and transportation sectors. I understand that many people are struggling right now. Soon, our day will come, and their day will come. Then we will once again meet at concerts, cafes, and restaurants. Let tourists enjoy the best that Norway has to offer. I look forward to that. In the meantime, we will support businesses that are struggling. The government cannot take over all the risk, but we will have good systems in place to help save many viable businesses. I would also like to encourage everyone who can to support their local businesses during this time.
paragraph_summary: The speaker discusses the economic impact of the coronavirus on Norwegian businesses and outlines government support measures to help them survive the pandemic.
reason: Focuses on domestic economic recovery and support for Norwegian businesses during the pandemic.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on the economic survival of businesses during the pandemic, framing the situation as an ongoing threat to livelihoods that requires active government intervention.

paragraph_text: The Sustainable Development Goals give us something to strive for here at home as well. Among the areas the government has identified are school dropout rates, mental health, and child poverty. Our goal is to create a society with opportunities for everyone. If we succeed, we will reduce social inequality and ensure that fewer people feel left behind. That is what we are working towards. From the new year, all municipalities must have a psychologist. We are increasing child benefits. More young people are completing upper secondary education. Currently, three out of four are doing so. We are on the right track, but we are not at the finish line.
paragraph_summary: The government is addressing social inequality through initiatives targeting school dropout rates, mental health services, and child poverty to ensure equal opportunities for all.
reason: Addresses domestic social policy goals regarding education, mental health, and poverty within Norway.
crisis_reason: The paragraph describes routine government policy initiatives aimed at improving social outcomes rather than framing these issues as an urgent, existential threat or a crisis requiring immediate emergency action.

paragraph_text: This autumn, the so-called NAV case has led many to question their trust in public institutions. I understand that. It is difficult to accept that such a serious error could occur over such a long period and affect so many people. On behalf of the government, I would like to express my sincere apologies to those who have been affected. We are now doing everything we can to rectify the injustice that has been committed. The case will be thoroughly investigated, so that we can learn from it. We must ensure that something like this never happens again. The trust that the public and the courts interpret laws and regulations correctly is fundamental to our society. Moving forward, we must work hard to earn that trust once again.
paragraph_summary: The Prime Minister apologizes for the NAV welfare scandal and commits to investigating the errors to restore public trust in government institutions.
reason: Addresses a specific domestic administrative scandal and the need to restore trust in Norwegian public institutions.
crisis_reason: The paragraph frames the welfare scandal as a threat to public trust in government institutions, which the speaker treats as a serious issue requiring an investigation and corrective action.

paragraph_text: I visited Vardal secondary school in Gjøvik. There, students gather in discussion groups twice a year. Students provide each other with a sense of security and are more likely to ask for help dealing with difficult thoughts and feelings. Teachers believe that this is increasingly important in a world where it’s easy to feel like an outsider or inadequate. Perhaps we don't talk enough about feelings? Perhaps we simply lack the words to express when we feel like we're not coping? The government is working on a new plan to promote the mental health of children and young people. We will examine how we can better prevent and support, before problems escalate. The professional support system needs to be improved. But perhaps the value of what each of us can do is just as great? I’m thinking of the boy who stands up for the one being bullied. The girl who calls to check if everything is okay when her friend withdraws. The adults who speak up when they realize a child isn't doing well at home. These small contributions mean so much. This is what determines the kind of society we have. Whether we can rally around each other.
paragraph_summary: The speaker discusses the importance of mental health support for youth and the role of individual community contributions in fostering a caring society.
reason: Discusses domestic school-based mental health initiatives and community support systems in Norway.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on the positive, routine goal of fostering a caring society and supporting youth mental health as a community value, rather than framing these issues as an urgent threat or emergency.

paragraph_text: The family is the most important and closest community we have. In the past, families provided care as people grew older. Now, municipalities and healthcare services are increasingly important. However, the solidarity between generations remains the same. Our society functions because adults care for children, and working-age people support the elderly. In the coming decades, we will face challenges with this model. The number of people having children in Norway is steadily declining. To maintain the population, each of us must, on average, have just over two children. Today, that number is only 1. 6. This means that relatively fewer young people will be responsible for supporting an increasingly large welfare state. Norway needs more children! I don't need to explain how to achieve that. I will also not be making any directives. Not everyone can or wants to have children, for various reasons. And more and more people are waiting longer to have children, also for various reasons. But waiting too long can also make it more difficult. An increasing number of children are now conceived with the help of healthcare services. Therefore, we must ask: Is it too difficult to have children early? We must create a supportive environment for young people to have children during their studies and early careers.
model_reasons: environment: refers to social and economic conditions for starting a family
paragraph_summary: The speaker addresses Norway's declining birth rate and the need to create a more supportive environment for young people to have children.
reason: Addresses domestic demographic challenges and the sustainability of the Norwegian welfare state model.
crisis_reason: The speaker frames the declining birth rate as a significant societal challenge that requires active intervention and a supportive environment, treating the demographic trend as an urgent threat to the nation's future.

paragraph_text: Throughout history, anxious parents have looked at their newborn babies and asked themselves: What future awaits you? It’s easy to think that things were better in the past. But that wasn't the case. As recently as the 1950s, we lost many children to accidents and illnesses – seven times as many as we do today. Children now have a safer upbringing. Studies show that children and young people are even satisfied with their parents. It’s not surprising. Parents coach their children’s skiing, bake waffles at youth group meetings, and organize social gatherings for the class. And young people are using fewer drugs, committing fewer crimes, and completing their education at higher rates. Most new parents can look at their newborn and envision a secure upbringing and incredible opportunities ahead.
paragraph_summary: The speaker highlights improvements in child safety, youth behavior, and educational outcomes to reassure parents about the future of their children in Norway.
reason: Reviews domestic historical progress in child safety and upbringing in Norway.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on positive developments, improvements, and reassurance regarding the future, which frames the situation as an achievement rather than an urgent threat.

paragraph_text: Although the vast majority of children experience the kind of life we hope and dream of, that unfortunately isn't the case for everyone. Some lack supportive adults who can provide comfort and care when needed. Some wounds are internal, and go unnoticed. Moving forward, the government will do more to identify and support these children. Our ambition is for Norway to be the society that is best at providing children with security and opportunities.
paragraph_summary: The Norwegian government intends to improve identification and support services for vulnerable children to ensure their security and opportunities.
reason: Outlines domestic government ambitions for child welfare and security within Norway.
crisis_reason: The paragraph describes a routine policy intention to improve services rather than framing the situation as an urgent threat or a crisis requiring immediate defensive action.

paragraph_text: The most vulnerable children are those who are exposed to violence and abuse. We have a new child protection law that strengthens children's rights and aims to ensure that more children have a safe and positive upbringing. When children cannot live with their parents, we will utilize family networks and support systems to which the child is connected. More children will receive the right support earlier, before it’s too late. And those who work most closely with the most vulnerable children must be the most competent.
paragraph_summary: The speech outlines improvements to child protection laws and support systems aimed at ensuring the safety and well-being of vulnerable children.
reason: Discusses domestic child protection laws and the strengthening of rights for children in Norway.
crisis_reason: The paragraph describes routine legislative improvements and support systems for child welfare rather than framing the situation as an urgent, active threat or a crisis requiring immediate emergency response.

paragraph_text: Christopher Davidsen won silver at the World Culinary Championships. His mother was a drug addict. At six years old, he was the one changing his baby sister’s diapers. He grew up in a foster home and has said to NRK: "I grew up in a really good foster home. (…) Without them, none of this would have been possible. " We have many such success stories. We must create even more.
paragraph_summary: The speaker highlights the importance of foster care in enabling personal success stories and advocates for expanding support for children in similar circumstances.
reason: Uses a domestic success story to highlight the importance of the Norwegian foster care system.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on the positive impact of foster care and advocates for policy expansion as a means of fostering success, rather than framing the situation as an urgent threat or a crisis.

paragraph_text: Many children spend a great deal of time online. Reports of online abuse are increasing dramatically. This includes the spread of nude images and children being manipulated or threatened into creating such images. In even more serious cases, there is also physical abuse. These are difficult cases for the police. In recent years, the police have been given increased resources. We are now organizing the police to better protect our children.
paragraph_summary: The government is increasing police resources and reorganizing law enforcement to better protect children from rising instances of online abuse and exploitation.
reason: Addresses domestic police efforts to combat online abuse of children in Norway.
crisis_reason: The paragraph frames the rising instances of online abuse and exploitation as a serious threat to the safety of children, necessitating an urgent government response through increased resources and reorganization.

paragraph_text: More children are growing up in families with low incomes. A major factor is the influx of immigrants to Norway who lack the knowledge and skills needed to succeed in our workforce. We must ensure that all children have the same opportunities, regardless of the challenges their parents face. Therefore, we have made it easier for these children to attend preschool. We are working towards the same goal with after-school programs. But the most important support is for parents to find employment. The government has launched an inclusion initiative and an integration program to help more people enter the workforce. Regardless, all children must have the opportunity to play with, learn from, and develop alongside other children. Therefore, we are working with voluntary organizations to ensure that membership fees and equipment requirements do not prevent children from participating.
paragraph_summary: The government is addressing child poverty by promoting workforce integration for immigrant parents and increasing access to preschool, after-school programs, and extracurricular activities.
reason: Discusses domestic integration programs and efforts to reduce child poverty among immigrant families in Norway.
crisis_reason: The paragraph describes routine policy measures and government initiatives to improve social outcomes rather than framing child poverty as an urgent, existential threat or an active crisis requiring immediate emergency intervention.

paragraph_text: Most young people in Norway are doing very well, and they behave better than my generation did. Nevertheless, the proportion struggling with mental health issues is increasing. More people report feeling lonely. Many struggle to cope with stress and pressure from various sources. Fifteen-year-old Julie Irene Svendgård expressed it well in Aftenposten: "I can't be everything everyone wants to be. That makes me angry with myself and makes me feel like a failure. " We can all become too focused on what others think, believe, or say about us. We should be more concerned with what feels right for ourselves, and strive to achieve our own goals, not those of others. To young people today, I would say this: Be proud of who you are. Appreciate your friends when they are themselves. Certainly, have ambitions and work hard, but be realistic and find time for friends and family.
paragraph_summary: The speaker addresses the rising mental health challenges, loneliness, and performance pressure experienced by young people in Norway.
reason: Addresses the mental health and social pressures faced by young people within Norwegian society.
crisis_reason: The speaker frames the mental health challenges, loneliness, and pressure faced by young people as a significant and urgent societal problem that requires attention and intervention.

paragraph_text: Unfortunately, some who are struggling do not receive the help they need when they need it. In recent years, more psychologists have been hired in municipalities, and there has been a significant increase in staff at health clinics and school health services. In schools, life skills will be a recurring theme across various subjects. Children who feel safe, thrive, and master their skills also learn more at school.
paragraph_summary: The speaker addresses gaps in mental health support and emphasizes the importance of school-based life skills and student well-being for academic success.
reason: Focuses on domestic improvements to school health services and mental health support for Norwegian students.
crisis_reason: The paragraph frames mental health support and student well-being as essential components of academic success rather than as an urgent threat or a crisis requiring immediate intervention.

paragraph_text: However, we have not solved all our problems. There are still people struggling to find work. Moving forward, our welfare system will cost more, while income growth will likely be less than we have been accustomed to. And, importantly, we must prepare for a completely new future – a future where modernization and digitalization in the workplace accelerate, and where growth must come from green and climate-friendly industries. We must ensure that the society we leave to our children and grandchildren is at least as good as the society we inherited from our parents. Therefore, we must create what we call a sustainable welfare society. A large public sector requires strong value creation in the private sector. Anything else is not sustainable. Therefore, for Norway moving forward, it is crucial to create more profitable jobs in the private sector. This requires a collective effort, and a significant one at that. But I believe in Norway. It will require a great deal, but we can achieve this together. It will take entrepreneurs, researchers, and talented people in today’s businesses to create ideas and opportunities. These ideas must increasingly lead to secure jobs for more people. We politicians must provide a good education system and a good vocational training program. We need a tax system that encourages investment in Norwegian jobs, and as little bureaucracy as possible. In addition, we need people who are willing to take risks and invest their savings in newly established businesses. And finally, we need an attitude that in Norway, it is both acceptable to fail and acceptable to succeed. As we enter a new year and a new era for our country, we should all reflect on how important jobs and value creation are. For successful integration, to combat inequality and poverty, and to pay for our welfare system. Work will become even more important in the coming decades if we are to create a sustainable welfare society.
paragraph_summary: The speaker emphasizes the necessity of private sector value creation, digitalization, and green industry growth to ensure a sustainable welfare society for future generations.
reason: Focuses on domestic economic growth, welfare, and national labor market policies.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on long-term economic development, modernization, and the sustainability of the welfare state as a positive goal rather than framing these as an urgent threat or an active crisis.

paragraph_text: In almost every job, tasks will change. There will be more computers and more robots. We, too, must adapt if we are to keep pace. Many of us will need to work longer, while the skills we acquire will become outdated more quickly. Many will therefore need to supplement their education, or perhaps even pursue entirely new fields of study. We will create the conditions to facilitate this. Our goal is clear: in the Norwegian workplace, no one should become obsolete. The future will bring more change. We must adapt to this. Not only because it is absolutely necessary, but also because new technology and new knowledge create a better society for everyone. We will see new businesses that do not pollute. We will have better jobs when robots and computers take over tasks that are dangerous and tedious. We will have fewer forms and less paperwork, freeing up labor for welfare. Yes, change is a challenge for those affected by it. But our willingness to change also makes Norway a better place.
paragraph_summary: The speaker discusses the necessity of adapting to technological change and lifelong learning to ensure workforce stability and societal progress in Norway.
reason: Discusses domestic workplace adaptation and technological changes within Norway.
crisis_reason: The paragraph frames technological change as a routine challenge for workforce development and societal progress rather than an urgent threat to stability or security.

paragraph_text: To do this, we also need free and independent media that can help us distinguish between what is important and unimportant, and between what is true and false. And that can help us understand that an issue often has multiple sides. Many of us grew up in homes where there was always a newspaper or two on the kitchen table. Today, there is often a mobile phone there instead. Will our children adapt to staying informed about the world around them in the same way as before? Will they adapt to being open to counterarguments and to trusting documented facts? This is perhaps even more important now that conspiracy theories and fake news spread so easily. To parents and to all adults, I would say: the best way to educate young people to become critical citizens is to be one yourself. We should remember this when we sit at the kitchen table and when we interact on social media. This creates an important safeguard for our values and our democracy.
paragraph_summary: The speaker emphasizes the importance of independent media, critical thinking, and digital literacy in safeguarding democracy against misinformation and conspiracy theories.
reason: Discusses the importance of media literacy and critical thinking for Norwegian citizens.
crisis_reason: The speaker frames misinformation and conspiracy theories as active, urgent threats to the integrity of democracy, necessitating a defensive response through media literacy and critical thinking.

paragraph_text: Everyone in our society should be able to benefit from the freedom that we hold so dear. But some must fight hard for it. And they may be sitting next to you on the bus, at school, or in the next office. Last year, more young women and women from minority backgrounds took courageous steps into the public discourse. This does not happen without cost. Many experience severe threats and harassment. Some of them call themselves "the shameless girls. " In some environments, shame – what we call negative social control – is a prison that particularly some young women are condemned to spend their entire lives in. We have fought against this for a long time, but changing culture is difficult. Now, we are seeing a new generation of young women from minority backgrounds standing up. A generation of freedom. A generation that wants to decide for themselves what they want to become, who they want to flirt with at parties, and who they want to share their lives with. They need to know that they have my full support, and our full support. There are probably some concerned parents watching. I know you love your children. Therefore, I also hope you can see how important it is to give them trust. Let them try, fail, and grow. Focus more on their well-being than on what others might think. Then, your children will thrive in Norwegian society. I would also like to mention Azra Gilani. She came to Norway in the 1970s and has raised four children here. She wrote in Aftenposten that: "There is nothing called honor associated with girls' sexuality. The only honor is that which we, as parents, achieve when we manage to raise our children to become decent human beings. " That is well said, and an inspiration to us all, regardless of background.
paragraph_summary: The speaker addresses the challenges of negative social control and harassment faced by young women from minority backgrounds while advocating for their personal freedom.
reason: Addresses domestic social issues regarding gender equality and cultural integration within Norway.
crisis_reason: The speaker frames the harassment and negative social control of young women as a serious threat to their personal freedom and social well-being, requiring urgent attention and advocacy.

paragraph_text: Change can be difficult, but often leads to something better. A society in transition needs something to unite around. In Norway, our beloved royal couple fulfills that role. Last year, the entire nation celebrated the royal couple's 80th birthdays. That is a remarkable age. At the same time, they are able to view today's society with such vision. They represent stability and encourage us to open ourselves up to, and enjoy, what is new. I extend my greetings to the royal family and thank them for the inspiration they provide to us all.
paragraph_summary: The speaker highlights the role of the Norwegian royal couple in providing national unity, stability, and inspiration during times of societal change.
reason: Refers to the role of the Norwegian royal family in national unity.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on the positive, stabilizing role of the monarchy and national unity, rather than framing societal change as an urgent threat or danger.

paragraph_text: Up to 60 percent of the elderly in institutions are undernourished. Now, the government is working to ensure that all elderly people have dinner later in the day. Simple changes in welfare services can lead to significant improvements. To improve the quality of our elderly care, we must work smarter. We need more ideas like that. Peer work is another form of personal initiative. It can involve former offenders helping released prisoners reintegrate into society, or people with serious illnesses helping others because they understand the emotional aspects of having a serious or chronic illness. In Tromsø, I recently met Jimmy Pedersen, one of the driving forces behind Café X. They offer drug-free activities for people struggling with addiction. Those who visit the café told about the sense of security they felt being able to talk with others who understood their experiences, challenges, and could offer a helping hand. There are many such organizations that are important. Retretten, Wayback, and many of the patient organizations in Norway are other examples. The knowledge gained from personal experience is invaluable.
paragraph_summary: The speaker advocates for improving elderly care and supporting peer-led initiatives to address social challenges through shared personal experience.
reason: Focuses on domestic elderly care and social support services within Norway.
crisis_reason: The paragraph describes a routine policy goal of improving social services and community support rather than framing these issues as an urgent threat or a crisis requiring immediate defensive action.

paragraph_text: The government is working on a reform of the child welfare system. Many families facing challenges are able to provide their children with a good upbringing with a little support. However, when children experience serious neglect, society must intervene and the child may be placed in foster care. We must give these children a better start in life, and the love and care they did not receive at home. Therefore, we are collaborating with the foundation Forandringsfabrikken. There, we meet children and young people who have firsthand experience with child welfare and mental health services, and who provide us with valuable advice. The experiences of these children will be central as we design the child welfare system of the future.
paragraph_summary: The government is reforming the child welfare system by incorporating the firsthand experiences and advice of children and young people.
reason: Discusses domestic reforms to the Norwegian child welfare system.
crisis_reason: The paragraph describes a routine policy reform aimed at improving a system through stakeholder engagement, which is presented as a positive governance development rather than an urgent threat or danger.

paragraph_text: The examples I have highlighted demonstrate what individuals can achieve through engagement, initiative, and courage. They inspire us and give us reason to believe in Norway. Norway is a small country with an open economy. We are accustomed to competing and adapting. So far, we have managed this well. Those who lose their jobs receive assistance in finding new employment. In the meantime, their income is secured, and we offer opportunities to enhance their skills. We are seeing new businesses being created. We are also seeing Norwegian companies investing more in research. For the first time, Norwegian students are performing above average in reading, mathematics, and science. We are on the right track, although much remains to be done.
paragraph_summary: The speaker highlights Norway's economic resilience, support systems for the unemployed, business innovation, and improvements in student academic performance.
reason: Reviews domestic economic performance, employment, and educational achievements.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on economic resilience, support systems, innovation, and academic improvements, which are framed as achievements and positive developments rather than urgent threats.

paragraph_text: In the future, we will need even more new, private companies. Let us use the new year to encourage those who are creating new jobs. In Norway, it should be both acceptable to fail and acceptable to succeed. And let us reflect on whether we ourselves can take the initiative – in volunteering, at work, or as entrepreneurs. In the future, we cannot rely as heavily on the oil industry. We must all lean forward a little more. Happy New Year.
paragraph_summary: The speaker encourages entrepreneurship, job creation, and personal initiative to transition Norway's economy away from its heavy reliance on the oil industry.
reason: Encourages domestic entrepreneurship and national economic transition.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on economic transition and future-oriented growth through entrepreneurship rather than framing the reliance on oil as an urgent, existential threat or a crisis requiring immediate emergency intervention.

paragraph_text: We are proud of what we achieve together, and with good reason. We cooperate well, we support each other, and we trust each other. We are good together. But we also need the many individuals who move our society forward. Politicians can create the conditions for new, private sector jobs, but it is ambitious women and men who must create them.
paragraph_summary: The speaker emphasizes the importance of collective cooperation and individual initiative in driving private sector job creation and societal progress.
reason: Emphasizes domestic cooperation and the role of individual initiative in national progress.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on positive societal progress and economic development through cooperation and initiative, which are framed as constructive achievements rather than urgent threats.

paragraph_text: We can allocate funds to research, but it is creative minds who must make the crucial breakthroughs. We can prohibit discrimination, but it is courageous individuals who must break taboos and barriers. Moving forward, we must create more space for personal initiatives, and we must encourage them, just as we encourage our athletes who inspire us. Let me highlight some individuals who have inspired me. One of them I met in the beautiful village of Ballstad in Lofoten. While working at the local doctor's office, Christian Mide recognized the need for a simple and safe way to detach used syringe tips. So, he created such a device. It will now be sold in several countries around the world. The potential is enormous. Billions of syringes are used annually. The company is headquartered in Ballstad and has a regional office in Oslo.
paragraph_summary: The speaker emphasizes the importance of individual initiative and creativity in driving innovation, illustrated by the success of a Norwegian medical device inventor.
reason: Highlights domestic innovation and individual contributions to the Norwegian economy.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on individual initiative, creativity, and success stories, which are framed as positive developments and achievements rather than threats or urgent dangers.

paragraph_text: Many of us must, in the years to come, do as Christian Mide has done. Another source of inspiration is the company BergenBio, and James Lorens. He led a research group at the University of Bergen, which conducted research that the company is now building upon. They recently tested their new cancer medicine, and the results for blood cancer were even better than expected – a significant breakthrough. Now, the medicine will be tested on other forms of cancer. An increasing number of people are affected by cancer. It is painful to see how cancer can devastate healthy individuals, and how those we care about are lost far too early. New and better medicines offer hope, giving cancer patients more valuable years with their loved ones. Therefore, the government has significantly increased its investment in research, and we will strive to make Norway a great place to create value. To create jobs, and to solve important challenges for people and for society.
paragraph_summary: The speaker highlights advancements in cancer research and medicine while emphasizing government investment in innovation to improve health outcomes and economic growth.
reason: Discusses domestic medical research and healthcare improvements for Norwegian citizens.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on positive developments, scientific advancements, and economic growth rather than framing health outcomes as an urgent threat or crisis.

paragraph_text: Our welfare system depends on funding, but there is also a great deal of well-being in having a job. This is something that the many thousands of people who are currently unemployed understand particularly well, especially at the beginning of the new year. We need enough jobs, and we need as many people as possible to be employed. Aisha Ali Mohammed has taken this to heart. She is a mother of three and Oslo's first Somali female bus driver. She has challenged attitudes within her community about women staying at home. She has faced criticism for doing a "man's job" and for wearing trousers. But she has also faced criticism from ethnic Norwegians for wearing a hijab while driving a bus. "I represent the company and must be polite. That's why I smile back, " she told Dagbladet. Barriers that may seem small can, in reality, be significant. If you are lucky enough to ride on her bus, I hope you will give her a warm smile. That way, you contribute to everyday integration.
paragraph_summary: The speaker discusses the importance of employment for welfare and social integration, highlighting the experiences of a bus driver overcoming cultural and social barriers.
reason: Focuses on domestic integration and social attitudes within the Norwegian workforce.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on the positive aspects of employment, social integration, and individual success stories rather than framing unemployment or social barriers as an urgent threat or crisis.

paragraph_text: Aisha Ali Mohammed represents the Norway that King Harald spoke of in Slottsparken in September. He said that his greatest hope is that we will be able to take care of each other, and that we will build this country further, on trust, community, and generosity. That we will feel that, despite all our differences, we are one people. That Norway is one. The speech made a strong impression, and it shows us the way forward. Now, it is up to us to follow that path.
paragraph_summary: The speaker emphasizes the importance of national unity, trust, and community as the guiding principles for Norway's future development.
reason: The paragraph focuses on national unity and the social values of Norway.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on positive, aspirational values like unity, trust, and community as guiding principles for the future, rather than framing any specific threat or danger.

paragraph_text: I would like to thank the King and Queen for being a unifying force and role models in their efforts for our country. Not everyone will become entrepreneurs. Equally important are the initiatives that engaged employees take in their jobs. One such initiative was taken by the nurse Mohsen Jamei, whom I recently met at the Bjørkelia co-living community in Gjøvik. He noticed that the elderly were not eating enough, and that several were undernourished. Before 2:30 pm, breakfast, lunch, and dinner were already served. Mohsen Jamei moved dinner to 4:00 pm and ensured that there was more time for each meal. The results are fantastic. The elderly have gained weight, sleep better at night, and are more active. His commitment has given other people a better and more dignified life.
paragraph_summary: The Prime Minister highlights the importance of individual initiative in the workplace by sharing a nurse's successful efforts to improve elderly nutrition and care.
reason: It discusses domestic healthcare improvements and the efforts of a specific nurse in a Norwegian community.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on individual initiative and positive professional achievements, which are framed as examples of success rather than threats or urgent problems.

paragraph_text: Many of those who have arrived need protection, but many also come from safe environments. Therefore, we have tightened our laws and regulations. Fewer people are arriving now, and we are returning those who do not qualify for residency more quickly. The work of the police and security services is fundamental to our security. We must maintain control of our borders and of who enters the country. A strict, but fair, asylum policy sends a signal that the asylum system is for those who need protection.
paragraph_summary: The speaker outlines stricter asylum regulations and border control measures to ensure the immigration system prioritizes those in genuine need of protection.
reason: It focuses on domestic asylum policy, border control, and national security regulations.
crisis_reason: The paragraph describes policy adjustments in routine, administrative terms without using language that frames immigration as an urgent threat, danger, or crisis to the state.

paragraph_text: Those who are granted residency will be welcomed in accordance with good Norwegian traditions. As Solbjørg Mikkola from the refugee services said when I met her in Sør-Varanger: "With humanity, respect, and cooperation. " Cooperation is essential for successful integration. New arrivals must be willing to follow our laws and regulations, to learn Norwegian, to find work, and to participate in society. Integration is never without challenges, but it is not hopeless either. In *Aftenposten*, a 16-year-old named Ali Al-Jabri from Stovner wrote that integration does not have to be difficult. He and other young people visit elderly residents, bringing mutual joy and benefit. This visiting program is a model for young people throughout the country. Ali Al-Jabri invites people to visit and see how integration works in practice. I accept that invitation. He is a great representative of many children of immigrants who are stepping forward with the ability to promote integration.
paragraph_summary: The speaker discusses the importance of mutual cooperation and active participation in society for the successful integration of new arrivals in Norway.
reason: It discusses the integration of immigrants into Norwegian society through local community efforts.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on the importance of cooperation and social participation as a constructive, routine aspect of societal integration rather than framing the presence of new arrivals as an urgent threat or danger.

paragraph_text: In many countries, we see a struggle between values, between our values and the values of extremists. In Norway, we have no ghettos, no places where young people only experience hopelessness. We must be able to hold onto that. Everyone who grows up in Norway must experience that our society provides opportunities for those who work hard and persevere. The Storting (Parliament), the government, and the municipalities cannot accomplish this task alone. If our values are to prevail, we must also achieve what we can call "everyday integration. " Here, we can all contribute. Can school classes organize friendship groups so that newly arrived children can experience more Norwegian homes? Do you have an extra seat in your car so that refugee children can also participate in football training? Do you have any good Norwegian children's books that you can pass on to a refugee family that has just arrived in the community? Do you have the opportunity to volunteer as a guardian or foster parent for a child? Can employers with foreign-sounding names be invited to job interviews? Our everyday choices are what drive everyday integration. Everyone will benefit if we succeed in integrating. Everyone will lose if we fail.
paragraph_summary: The speaker emphasizes the importance of individual contributions to everyday integration to ensure social cohesion and equal opportunity for all residents in Norway.
reason: It addresses domestic social integration and the role of Norwegian citizens in fostering inclusion.
crisis_reason: The paragraph focuses on the positive goal of social cohesion and equal opportunity as a standard societal aspiration rather than framing the current state as an urgent threat or danger.

paragraph_text: Our beloved royal couple has shown great commitment to inclusion. In 2016, we will celebrate the royal couple's jubilee as regents. For 25 years, they have contributed to the unity and community that characterize Norway with wisdom, insight, and consideration. We thank you for that. The royal couple visited the arrival center for refugees in Østfold last year. They saw how a large system was established in record time. I would like to thank all the employees of the Directorate of Immigration and the Police Immigration Unit. Few understand the crisis you are handling across the country. I am impressed by your perseverance and ability to deliver. The volunteers have also contributed to solving the challenges.
paragraph_summary: The Prime Minister honors the royal couple's jubilee and commends the efforts of immigration authorities and volunteers in managing the refugee crisis.
reason: It acknowledges the work of domestic immigration authorities and volunteers in handling refugee arrivals.

paragraph_text: Our next challenge is to support those who are starting new lives in a new municipality and entering the workforce, while we also face increasing unemployment. We are facing a demanding economic transition. The fall in oil prices means that the transition is happening more quickly. Many people are asking how we will manage this. The answer is that we must create more jobs. We will build the country in the years to come. We will build new roads, new railways, develop new ideas, create new products, and climate-friendly solutions. We will invest more in research and support more development and innovation in businesses. We have many unresolved tasks, and we have many available workers. Therefore, we must connect them. More of us must be willing to create our own jobs. More must invest in Norwegian jobs. The challenge may seem great, but we have a well-educated population, good technological expertise, rich natural resources, and good cooperation in the labor market. We have the best starting point. Therefore, I am confident that together we will overcome this challenge as well. The government's most important task in the years to come will be to ensure that more people have a job to go to.
paragraph_summary: The speaker addresses the need to combat rising unemployment and facilitate economic transition through job creation, innovation, and investment in infrastructure and research.
reason: It discusses domestic economic challenges, unemployment, and national investment strategies.
crisis_reason: The paragraph frames unemployment as a problem requiring active intervention and strategic response, treating it as a threat to economic stability and livelihoods.

paragraph_text: The jobs of the future will require skills. A labor market where many are pushed aside will lead to social inequality. I do not want that kind of society. Therefore, I repeat the call to employers: Give a refugee or someone with gaps in their resume a chance. Many employers have tried, and few have regretted it. Adults who do not meet the requirements of the labor market in terms of reading, writing, and arithmetic must receive better support. We must assess what each individual needs and make it easier to fill in what is missing. When we have this in place, we open up opportunities for everyone. It takes courage for an adult man to ask for help learning to read. But I have heard that men are brave. Whether you are a man or a woman, young or old: do not be afraid to ask for help. The help may be closer than you think. The labor market offers fantastic opportunities. We need you there too.
paragraph_summary: The speaker advocates for inclusive labor market policies, emphasizing the need for adult education and employer support to integrate marginalized individuals into the workforce.
reason: It focuses on domestic labor market policies and adult education initiatives.
crisis_reason: The paragraph describes a routine policy recommendation for labor market inclusion and education, lacking any language framing the situation as an urgent threat, danger, or instability.

paragraph_text: Tonight, I would like to send a warm greeting to the King and Queen. The respect from the Norwegian people isn't solely about the King's constitutional duties.
